description: Unique identifier for the biospecimen record. Primary key for the biospecimen transactional entity.

Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Biospecimen processing and storage generate vendor invoices from central labs, biobanks, and specialty testing facilities. Required for trial cost tracking, vendor payment processing, and per-protocol

Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Biospecimens are collected from enrolled subjects and may be analyzed by treatment arm for exploratory biomarker endpoints. Linking biospecimen to enrollment enables treatment arm stratification of bi

Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Biospecimen collection is documented via CRF forms (biospecimen collection CRF) in the EDC system. Linking biospecimen to crf_form enables traceability to the data collection instrument and supports s

Foreign key linking to discovery.assay. Business justification: Translational medicine programs run validated discovery assays (target engagement, PD biomarker assays) on clinical biospecimens. The biospecimen record must reference which discovery assay is planned

Foreign key linking to regulatory.dmf. Business justification: Bioanalytical methods, reference standards, and assay validation for biospecimens are documented in Drug Master Files (Type II or III). CMC regulatory teams must reference the appropriate DMF when bio
Reference to the investigational site where the biospecimen was collected. Links to the site master record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS).

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Biomarker studies, PK/PD analysis, and translational research require linking specimens to the product being tested. Essential for dose-response modeling, bioequivalence studies, and supporting produc
Self-referencing FK on biospecimen (parent_biospecimen_id)
Reference to the clinical trial subject from whom this biospecimen was collected. Links to the subject master record in the clinical trial management system.

Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Biospecimen collection procedures, timing, and handling are defined in the protocol. Linking biospecimen to protocol version enables verification that collection followed the protocol-specified proced

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Biospecimen processing uses reagent/consumable lots; GCP compliance (ICH E6) requires lot traceability for data integrity and result reproducibility. Role prefix distinguishes reagent lots from clinic

Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Biospecimens are shipped from investigational sites to central labs. Linking biospecimen to shipment supports chain-of-custody verification and specimen integrity documentation. shipment_tracking_numb

Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Biospecimens are stored at specific controlled locations (central labs, biobanks, -80°C freezers). Required for sample tracking, chain of custody, retrieval for analysis, temperature excursion monitor
Reference to the clinical trial under which this biospecimen was collected. Links to the trial master record.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Specimen volume uses standardized units (mL, µL). Required for lab processing (sufficient volume checks), aliquot planning, inventory management, and CDISC SDTM LB domain mapping. Essential for specim
Reference to the protocol-defined visit during which this biospecimen was collected. Links to the visit schedule in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system.
The anatomical location from which the specimen was collected, such as left arm, liver, or lung. Particularly important for tissue biopsies and tumor samples.

The name or code of the assay or test planned to be performed on this specimen, such as pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis, biomarker assay, or genomic sequencing. Supports laboratory planning and resource allocation.
Indicates whether the chain of custody for the specimen has been verified and documented. True if verified, False otherwise. Critical for regulatory compliance and data integrity.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2), 21 CFR Part 11, GLP

description: The date on which the biospecimen was collected from the trial subject. Critical for protocol compliance and time-to-event analysis.
The method or procedure used to collect the specimen, such as venipuncture, biopsy, or catheterization. Ensures protocol compliance and reproducibility.
The precise date and time when the biospecimen was collected. Required for pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) studies where timing is critical.
The protocol-defined name of the visit during which the specimen was collected, such as Baseline, Week 4, End of Treatment. Aligns with the visit schedule in the Case Report Form (CRF).
Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for long-term biobanking and future research use of this specimen. True if consent was provided, False otherwise.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2), 21 CFR Part 50, GDPR

description: Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for genetic or pharmacogenomic testing on this specimen. True if consent was provided, False otherwise.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2), 21 CFR Part 50, GDPR

description: The type of tube or container used for specimen collection, such as EDTA tube, serum separator tube, cryovial. Critical for ensuring proper specimen handling and assay validity.
The date and time when this biospecimen record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Detailed description of any protocol deviation that occurred during specimen collection, processing, or handling. Required for regulatory documentation.
Indicates whether the subject was fasting at the time of specimen collection. Critical for certain laboratory tests such as glucose and lipid panels.

business_glossary_term: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) Code
description: The standardized LOINC code representing the specimen type and associated test. Enables interoperability with laboratory information systems and regulatory submissions.
The date on which the specimen was processed in the laboratory. Used to track time from collection to processing for quality control.
The method used to process the specimen after collection, such as centrifugation, aliquoting, or fixation. Ensures traceability of specimen handling procedures.
Indicates whether the specimen collection deviated from the protocol-defined procedures. True if a deviation occurred, False otherwise.
The date on which the specimen was received at the central laboratory or biobank. Used to verify shipment integrity and processing timelines.
The date on which the specimen was shipped to the central laboratory or biobank. Used to track transit time and ensure cold chain compliance.
The standardized SNOMED CT code representing the specimen type or anatomical source. Supports clinical terminology standardization and regulatory compliance.
Unique business identifier for the biospecimen, typically a barcode or alphanumeric code assigned at collection. Used for chain-of-custody tracking and laboratory processing.
Assessment of the specimen quality upon receipt or processing. Identifies any conditions that may affect assay results or specimen usability.

The current lifecycle status of the biospecimen, indicating its stage in the collection, processing, storage, and analysis workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: collected|processed|stored|shipped|received|analyzed|depleted|discarded — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Further classification or detail of the specimen type, such as specific tissue origin or blood component. Provides granular categorization for specialized assays.

Classification of the biological specimen collected. Defines the nature of the sample for processing and analysis requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|serum|plasma|urine|tissue|csf|saliva|bone_marrow|tumor_biopsy — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: LOINC, SNOMED CT, CDISC SDTM LB Domain

description: The temperature condition under which the specimen is stored, such as room temperature, refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen at -20°C, frozen at -80°C, or in liquid nitrogen. Critical for specimen stability and assay integrity.
The day relative to the reference date (typically first dose or randomization) on which the specimen was collected. Negative values indicate pre-treatment, positive values indicate post-treatment.
The date and time when this biospecimen record was last modified in the system. Supports audit trail and change tracking for regulatory compliance.
The quantity of specimen collected, measured in the unit specified in volume_unit. Used to determine sufficiency for planned assays.
Unique surrogate identifier for each CRF form definition record in the Medidata Rave electronic data capture system. Primary key for the crf_form data product.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Reference to the specific trial protocol version under which this CRF form definition was created and approved. Ensures form-to-protocol traceability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.23

description: Reference to the clinical trial for which this CRF form definition was designed. Links the instrument definition to its parent study in the CTMS.
Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled visit with which this CRF form is associated in the study schedule of events. Null for common forms appearing across all visits.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CDISC SDTM TV Domain

description: Annotated CRF text describing the SDTM variable mappings, derivation rules, and coding instructions associated with each field on this form. Used by data management and programming teams for SDTM dataset creation.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: Current lifecycle approval state of the CRF form definition. 'draft' indicates design in progress; 'under_review' indicates pending sponsor/CRO review; 'approved' indicates GCP-compliant sign-off; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version; 'retired' indicates no longer in use.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Date and time when this CRF form definition received formal GCP-compliant approval. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on Electronic Records

business_glossary_term: Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization (CDASH) Domain
description: CDISC CDASH domain code indicating the data collection standard this form adheres to (e.g., 'AE', 'VS', 'CM'). Ensures harmonized data collection aligned with CDASH implementation guides.
reference: CDISC CDASH Implementation Guide v2.0; ICH E6(R2)

description: Detailed instructions for site staff on how to complete this CRF form, including definitions, coding conventions, and data source guidance. Embedded in the EDC and printed CRF booklet.

Specifies whether completion of this CRF form is mandatory for all subjects, optional, or conditional on specific criteria (e.g., only if a prior form field triggers it). Drives data completeness monitoring and query generation.
Date and time when this CRF form definition record was first created in the system. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; GDPR Article 5

description: Specifies the data entry mode configured for this CRF form in Medidata Rave. 'single' requires one entry; 'double' requires independent dual entry for critical data; 'auto' is populated programmatically from device or lab feeds.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; Medidata Rave Configuration Guide

description: Total number of programmed edit checks (validation rules) configured for this CRF form in Medidata Rave. Provides a measure of form complexity and data quality control coverage.
reference: CDISC Edit Check Specification; Medidata Rave Study Design

business_glossary_term: Case Report Form (CRF) Effective End Date
description: Date on which this CRF form version was retired or superseded. Null for currently active versions. Supports version lifecycle management and historical data traceability.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

business_glossary_term: Case Report Form (CRF) Effective Start Date
description: Date from which this CRF form version is active and available for data entry at investigational sites. Supports version management and ensures sites use the correct form version.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data; Medidata Rave Study Design

description: Total number of data fields (questions/items) defined on this CRF form. Used for form complexity assessment, site burden estimation, and data management planning.
reference: CDISC CDASH; Medidata Rave Study Design Specification

description: Short display label for the CRF form used in the EDC user interface and printed CRF booklets. May differ from form_name for brevity in UI contexts.
reference: CDISC CDASH; Medidata Rave Study Design

description: Human-readable name of the CRF form as displayed in Medidata Rave and study documentation. Examples: 'Adverse Event Form', 'Vital Signs', 'Concomitant Medications'.
Unique Object Identifier (OID) for the CRF form as defined in Medidata Rave and conforming to CDISC ODM standards. Used for cross-system referencing and SDTM mapping. Example: 'AE_FORM_V2'.
reference: CDISC ODM 1.3.2; CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide

description: Numeric sequence order of this form within its associated visit or study schedule of events. Determines the display order in the EDC and printed CRF booklet.
reference: CDISC ODM 1.3.2; Medidata Rave Study Design

description: Classification of the CRF form by its data collection pattern in Medidata Rave. 'scheduled' forms appear at predefined visits; 'unscheduled' are event-driven; 'common' appear across all visits; 'log' forms capture repeating events (e.g., AE log); 'repeating' forms allow multiple instances per visit.
reference: Medidata Rave Study Design Specification; CDISC CDASH

description: Version identifier of the CRF form definition, following semantic versioning (e.g., '1.0', '2.1', '3.0.1'). Tracks design changes over the trial lifecycle and supports audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Indicates whether this CRF form contains blinded data (e.g., treatment assignment, randomization code) that must be restricted from unblinded personnel during the trial. Supports GCP-compliant blinding controls.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether this CRF form allows multiple instances per subject per visit (e.g., an Adverse Event log form where each AE is a separate instance). Drives EDC configuration in Medidata Rave.
reference: CDISC ODM 1.3.2; Medidata Rave Study Design Specification

description: Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) is required for data entered on this CRF form. Critical forms (e.g., primary endpoints, safety data) typically require 100% SDV; others may be risk-based.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring; EMA Reflection Paper on Risk-Based Quality Management

description: Indicates whether an electronic signature (e-signature) is required on this CRF form by the investigator or site staff upon completion, as mandated by 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9; FDA Guidance on Electronic Records

description: Indicates whether this CRF form contributes data to the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population definition. ITT-relevant forms are critical for primary efficacy analysis and regulatory review.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on ITT Principle

description: Date and time when this CRF form definition record was last modified. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail, change control, and GCP compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether this CRF form captures data for the trial's primary efficacy or safety endpoint (e.g., ORR, PFS, OS). Primary endpoint forms receive heightened monitoring, SDV, and data review scrutiny.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: The internal Medidata Rave draft version number for this CRF form, distinct from the business version. Tracks incremental design changes within the Rave study build environment prior to promotion to production.
reference: Medidata Rave Study Design Specification; 21 CFR Part 11; CSV Validation

description: The study name as configured in Medidata Rave EDC system, used to identify the parent study context for this CRF form. Supports cross-referencing between the data lakehouse and the source EDC system.
reference: Medidata Rave Study Design Specification; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether data collected on this CRF form is included in regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA/MAA/IND). Drives SDTM dataset inclusion and eCTD packaging requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; ICH M4E; eCTD Specification

description: Indicates whether this CRF form is designated as a safety-critical form (e.g., Adverse Event, Serious Adverse Event, SUSAR reporting). Safety forms trigger expedited review workflows and pharmacovigilance data feeds.
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Two-letter CDISC SDTM domain code to which this CRF form maps for regulatory submission (e.g., 'AE' for Adverse Events, 'VS' for Vital Signs, 'CM' for Concomitant Medications, 'LB' for Laboratory). Drives SDTM dataset generation for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

description: The percentage of data points on this CRF form subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) under the risk-based monitoring plan. Ranges from 0.00 (no SDV) to 100.00 (full SDV). Supports risk-based monitoring strategy.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18.3; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

description: Native identifier of this CRF form record in the source EDC system (Medidata Rave). Enables lineage tracing from the Silver Layer back to the operational system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data; Data Lineage Best Practices

business_glossary_term: Case Report Form (CRF) Validation Rule Set
description: Identifier or name of the edit check / validation rule set applied to this CRF form in Medidata Rave. References the programmed edit checks that enforce data quality, range checks, and cross-form consistency rules.
reference: CDISC Edit Check Specification; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; 21 CFR Part 11

description: CDISC ODM-compliant OID of the study visit to which this form is assigned, as defined in Medidata Rave. Supports cross-system visit-form linkage without requiring a resolved FK at this stage.
reference: CDISC ODM 1.3.2; Medidata Rave Study Design

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each laboratory test result record in the clinical trial data product. Primary key for the lab_result entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to clinical.biospecimen. Business justification: Laboratory results are derived from biological specimens. Both tables have specimen_type, but the authoritative source is biospecimen. Linking lab_result to biospecimen enables traceability from test

Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Laboratory results are collected for enrolled subjects and must be analyzed by treatment arm. Linking lab_result to enrollment enables treatment arm stratification of lab data and filtering by analysi

Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Laboratory results may be captured via CRF forms (lab CRF) in addition to direct EDC integration from central labs. Linking lab_result to crf_form enables traceability to the data collection instrumen
Reference to the investigational site (clinical site) where the specimen was collected. Used for site-level lab data quality monitoring and GCP compliance oversight.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Central labs are registered business partners in pharma masterdata for vendor qualification, payment, and GLP/GCP audit trails. lab_name and lab_accreditation_number are denormalized plain-text attrib

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Safety labs and efficacy biomarkers must be attributable to specific products for regulatory submissions (ICH E3), product labeling (warnings/precautions), and post-marketing safety surveillance. Crit

Foreign key linking to quality.method_validation. Business justification: GCP/GLP requires analytical methods used in clinical trials to be validated. Linking lab_result to method_validation supports NDA/BLA regulatory submission (analytical methods section) and audit trail

Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) from whom the specimen was collected. Links to the subject/patient master in the CTMS or EDC system (Medidata Rave). Classified as restricted PII as it can be used to trace back to an individual trial participant.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA 45 CFR §164; GDPR Article 4; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Laboratory test panels, testing schedules, and normal ranges are defined in the protocol. Linking lab_result to protocol enables verification that the correct tests were performed at the correct timep
Reference to the clinical trial or study under which this laboratory result was collected. Corresponds to the protocol/study record in Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS or Medidata Rave.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Clinical Trials Regulation (EU) No 536/2014

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Lab results use standardized units. Required for CDISC SDTM LB domain mapping (standard units conversion), safety analysis, cross-study comparisons, and regulatory submissions. Essential for data stan
Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the specimen was collected. Aligns with the visit schedule defined in the trial protocol and captured in Medidata Rave CRF.

Boolean flag indicating whether the laboratory result has been assessed by the investigator as clinically significant. Corresponds to CDISC CDASH/SDTM LBCLSIG. A clinically significant abnormal result may trigger an adverse event report or protocol-defined action.
reference: CDISC CDASH LB domain (LBCLSIG); ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Guidelines

description: Date on which the biological specimen was collected from the trial subject. Used for study day calculation, protocol deviation assessment, and time-point alignment in efficacy and safety analyses. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBDTC (date portion).
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBDTC); ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Full date and time of specimen collection from the trial subject, including timezone offset. Used for precise PK/PD time-point calculations and timed lab assessments (e.g., fasting glucose, post-dose PK samples). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBDTC (full datetime).
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBDTC); ICH E6(R2) GCP; ISO 8601

description: Free-text comments or notes associated with the laboratory result, including laboratory annotations, investigator comments on abnormal results, or data management notes. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBCOMM. Used for contextual interpretation of results.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBCOMM); ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Status of the Case Report Form (CRF) data entry for this laboratory result in Medidata Rave EDC. Tracks the data management workflow from initial entry through verification and database lock. Used for data cleaning and trial master file completeness tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave data management workflow

description: Date and time when the laboratory result was entered or imported into the Medidata Rave EDC system. Used for audit trail compliance per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) to demonstrate timely data entry and GCP compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Medidata Rave audit trail

description: Indicates whether the subject was fasting at the time of specimen collection. Relevant for metabolic panels (glucose, lipids) where fasting status affects reference ranges and result interpretation. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBFAST.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBFAST); ICH E3

description: Indicates whether the laboratory result was generated by a central laboratory (contracted central lab with standardized methods across all sites) or a local laboratory (site-specific lab). Central lab results are preferred for primary efficacy and safety endpoints due to standardization.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC SDTM LB domain

business_glossary_term: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes (LOINC) Code
description: Standardized LOINC code identifying the laboratory test or observation. Enables cross-study, cross-site, and cross-system harmonization of lab test definitions. Required for regulatory submissions and interoperability with central lab data transfers.
reference: LOINC v2.77 (Regenstrief Institute); ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog; CDISC CDASH/SDTM LB domain

description: Flag indicating whether the result is within, below, or above the laboratory's normal reference range. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBNRIND. Values: normal, low, high, critically_low, critically_high. Used in safety data listings and shift tables for regulatory submissions.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBNRIND); ICH E3; CTCAE v5.0; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the result value falls outside the laboratory's defined reference range (either below lower limit or above upper limit). Distinct from clinical significance — a result can be out of range but not clinically significant. Used for automated data quality checks and safety signal detection.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain; ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the result meets the laboratory's criteria for a panic (critical) value requiring immediate notification to the investigator or site. Supports GCP-compliant safety monitoring and timely SAE/AE reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312.32; CTCAE v5.0; CAP (College of American Pathologists) guidelines

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this laboratory result is associated with a protocol deviation (e.g., specimen collected outside the protocol-defined time window, incorrect specimen type collected). Used for ITT/PP population analysis and regulatory submission deviation summaries.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations

description: Boolean flag indicating whether an open data query (discrepancy) exists for this laboratory result record in Medidata Rave EDC. Used by data managers to track unresolved data issues requiring site clarification before database lock.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave query management

description: Source system record identifier from Medidata Rave EDC for this laboratory result entry. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail back to the originating EDC system.

Upper bound of the laboratory's normal reference range for this test, applicable to the subject's demographic group (age, sex). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTNRHI. Used for normal/abnormal flagging and shift table generation.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBSTNRHI); ICH E3; CTCAE v5.0

description: Lower bound of the laboratory's normal reference range for this test, applicable to the subject's demographic group (age, sex). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTNRLO. Used for normal/abnormal flagging and shift table generation.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBSTNRLO); ICH E3; CTCAE v5.0

description: Date and time when the laboratory result was officially reported and transmitted by the laboratory to the sponsor or EDC system. Used for turnaround time monitoring, GCP compliance (timely reporting of critical values), and data transfer reconciliation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; CAP turnaround time standards

description: Current status of the laboratory result record reflecting its lifecycle in the EDC and central lab workflow. Values: preliminary (initial result), final (verified and released), corrected (amended after initial release), cancelled (result voided), pending (awaiting analysis). Corresponds to HL7 observation status.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain; HL7 FHIR Observation.status; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2)

description: Standardized unit of measure for the laboratory result after conversion to a common unit for cross-site and cross-study comparability. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESU. Enables pooled analyses and regulatory submission datasets.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBSTRESU); UCUM; ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: The reported result value for the laboratory test as a string to accommodate both numeric and character results (e.g., '5.4', '>100', 'POSITIVE', 'NEGATIVE', 'TRACE'). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBORRES. Stored as STRING to preserve original reported value including qualifiers.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBORRES); ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Numeric representation of the laboratory result value after standardization and unit conversion. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESN. Used for quantitative statistical analyses, shift tables, and outlier detection. Null for non-numeric results.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBSTRESN); ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Relative study day of specimen collection calculated from the subject's first dose date (Day 1). Negative values indicate pre-dose assessments. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBDY. Used for time-course analyses, PK/PD modeling, and protocol compliance assessment.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBDY); ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Broad classification of the laboratory test panel or category (e.g., hematology, chemistry, urinalysis, coagulation). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBCAT variable. Used for grouping and filtering in safety data review and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hematology|chemistry|urinalysis|coagulation|immunology|microbiology|pharmacokinetics|other — promote to reference product]
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBCAT); ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: Human-readable name of the laboratory test performed (e.g., Hemoglobin, Serum Creatinine, ALT, White Blood Cell Count). Sourced from the central lab data transfer file and mapped to the CRF lab panel definition in Medidata Rave.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBTEST); ICH E3; FDA Data Standards Catalog

description: More granular sub-classification of the laboratory test within its category (e.g., liver function tests within chemistry, CBC within hematology). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSCAT variable.
reference: CDISC SDTM LB domain (LBSCAT); ICH E3

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Toxicity Grade
description: Toxicity grade assigned to the laboratory abnormality per the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) grading scale (Grade 1=Mild, Grade 2=Moderate, Grade 3=Severe, Grade 4=Life-threatening, Grade 5=Death). Used for safety signal detection, dose modification decisions, and regulatory safety reporting.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E3; FDA Safety Reporting Guidance; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Date and time when the laboratory result was verified (source data verified, SDV) by the clinical monitor or data manager in Medidata Rave. Required for GCP audit trail and trial master file completeness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18.3; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each subject observation record in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Represents the atomic unit of clinical trial data collected per subject per visit per Case Report Form (CRF) field.
reference: CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Clinical observations are collected for enrolled subjects. Linking subject_observation to enrollment provides treatment arm context and enables filtering observations by analysis population (ITT, mITT
Reference to the CRF form definition from which this observation originates. Identifies the instrument (e.g., Vital Signs, Lab Results, Adverse Events) used to collect the data point.
reference: CDISC CDASH; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

description: Reference to the investigational site where the observation was collected. Supports site-level data quality monitoring, SDV tracking, and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Guidelines

description: Reference to the enrolled trial subject (patient) from whom this observation was collected. Enables subject-level data aggregation for ITT, PP, and safety analyses.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG; HIPAA; GDPR Article 4

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Clinical observations are collected according to protocol-defined data collection standards and edit checks. Linking subject_observation to protocol enables verification that observations were collect

Foreign key linking to quality.quality_deviation. Business justification: Protocol deviations identified in subject observations (protocol_deviation_flag=true) must be formally escalated to quality deviation records per GCP requirements. This FK enables direct traceability
Reference to the clinical trial (study) under which this observation was collected. Links the observation to the parent trial protocol, phase, and regulatory submission context.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Subject observations record numeric clinical measurements requiring standardized UoM for CDISC/SDTM regulatory submissions and cross-trial data analysis. unit and standard_unit are denormalized plain-
Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which this observation was collected. Supports visit-level data alignment and protocol deviation detection.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG

description: Indicates the analysis population(s) to which this observation contributes: Intent-to-Treat (ITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety population, or excluded. Drives statistical analysis dataset (ADaM) construction and primary/secondary endpoint derivation.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; CDISC ADaM IG; FDA Guidance on ITT Principle; EMA Guideline on Missing Data

description: Reference identifier linking this observation to its complete audit trail record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Enables traceability of all data changes, user actions, and timestamps per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 1.45; EMA Reflection Paper on Expectations for Electronic Source Data

description: The calendar date on which the observation was collected from the subject at the investigational site. Corresponds to SDTM --DTC variable. Critical for visit window compliance, protocol deviation assessment, and time-to-event endpoint calculations.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG (--DTC); ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312

description: The precise date and time at which the observation was collected, including time zone offset. Required for time-sensitive measurements (e.g., PK sampling, ECG) and for accurate SDTM --STDTC derivation.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG (--STDTC); ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

description: The name of the specific CRF field (data point) within the form from which this observation was collected (e.g., SYSBP, WEIGHT, AESTDTC). Corresponds to the EDC field label in Medidata Rave.
reference: CDISC CDASH IG; CDISC SDTM IG; ICH E6(R2)

description: Indicates whether this observation record has been locked in the EDC system as part of the database lock process, preventing further modifications. Locked records are used for final statistical analysis and regulatory submission datasets.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Guidelines; FDA Guidance on Database Lock

description: Native record identifier assigned by the EDC system (Medidata Rave) for this observation. Used for source system traceability, audit trail reconciliation, and data migration validation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave Data Model

description: The date and time at which the data was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave) by the site user. Used for data entry timeliness monitoring, audit trail compliance, and GCP inspection readiness per ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave Audit Trail

description: Indicates whether this observation represents the baseline measurement for the subject, used as the reference value for change-from-baseline calculations in efficacy endpoint derivation (e.g., tumor size at baseline for ORR). Corresponds to SDTM ABLFL flag in ADaM datasets.
reference: CDISC ADaM IG (ABLFL); ICH E9 Statistical Principles; FDA Guidance on Efficacy Endpoints

description: Indicates whether this observation was collected during an unscheduled visit (not defined in the protocol schedule of assessments). Unscheduled observations may be excluded from primary efficacy analyses per the statistical analysis plan.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC SDTM IG; Protocol Schedule of Assessments

description: The date and time of the most recent modification to this observation record in the EDC system. Supports audit trail tracking, data change monitoring, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave Audit Trail

description: The upper bound of the reference (normal) range for this observation type as defined by the central laboratory or clinical standard. Used for out-of-range flagging, safety signal detection, and SDTM STNRHI derivation.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG (STNRHI); ICH E3 Clinical Study Report; FDA Safety Reporting Guidelines

description: The lower bound of the reference (normal) range for this observation type as defined by the central laboratory or clinical standard. Used for out-of-range flagging, safety signal detection, and SDTM STNRLO derivation.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG (STNRLO); ICH E3 Clinical Study Report; FDA Safety Reporting Guidelines

description: The numeric representation of the observation value after unit standardization, corresponding to the SDTM STRESN variable. Used for quantitative analyses, endpoint derivation (ORR, PFS, OS), and statistical modeling in ADaM datasets.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG (STRESN); CDISC ADaM IG; ICH E9 Statistical Principles

description: Current workflow status of the observation record within the EDC system lifecycle. Drives data cleaning workflows, lock readiness assessment, and database lock milestones. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|verified|locked|queried|resolved|deleted — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; Medidata Rave Workflow

description: Indicates whether the numeric observation value falls outside the defined normal reference range (low or high). Triggers safety review workflows, potential AE/SAE reporting, and DSMB notification processes.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E3; FDA Safety Reporting Guidelines; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Indicates whether this observation is associated with a protocol deviation (e.g., collected outside the visit window, wrong procedure performed). Flagged observations may be excluded from Per Protocol (PP) analysis populations.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations

description: Status of any data query (discrepancy) raised against this observation in the EDC system. Open queries must be resolved prior to database lock. Supports data cleaning metrics and site performance monitoring.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: The text of the most recent data query raised against this observation, describing the discrepancy or clarification required from the site. Supports data management review and site communication workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC Data Management Guidelines

description: The verbatim data value as entered by the site user in the EDC system (Medidata Rave), prior to any derivation or standardization. Stored as STRING to accommodate all data types (numeric, text, coded). Represents the source data for SDV and audit trail purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 11; CDISC SDTM ORRES variable convention

description: The documented reason provided by the site user when modifying a previously entered observation value in the EDC system. Mandatory per 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP requirements for any post-entry data correction. Stored as the most recent change reason.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Section 11.10(e); ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9.3; EMA GCP Guidelines

description: CDISC SDTM domain code to which this observation maps (e.g., VS for Vital Signs, LB for Laboratory, AE for Adverse Events, EX for Exposure). Critical for SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission packages (NDA/BLA/MAA).
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide; EMA Data Standards

business_glossary_term: Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) Variable Name
description: The specific CDISC SDTM variable name to which this CRF field maps (e.g., VSSTRESN, LBORRES, AEDECOD). Enables automated SDTM dataset generation and submission-ready data transformation.
reference: CDISC SDTM IG v3.4; FDA Study Data Technical Conformance Guide

description: The date on which Source Data Verification (SDV) was completed for this observation. Used for monitoring visit planning, SDV coverage reporting, and GCP audit trail documentation.

Status of Source Data Verification (SDV) for this observation, indicating whether the EDC-entered value has been verified against the original source document (e.g., medical record, lab report) by the CRA or site monitor. Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Risk-Based Monitoring

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Toxicity Grade
description: The NCI CTCAE toxicity grade assigned to this observation when it represents an adverse finding (Grade 1=Mild, Grade 2=Moderate, Grade 3=Severe, Grade 4=Life-threatening, Grade 5=Death). Critical for safety endpoint reporting, dose modification decisions, and regulatory safety narratives.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E3; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting; CDISC SDTM AE Domain

description: The username or identifier of the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) or monitor who performed Source Data Verification (SDV) for this observation. Supports GCP compliance documentation and monitoring visit reports.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Risk-Based Monitoring Guidance

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each trial endpoint assessment record in the clinical lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Clinical endpoints are assessed for enrolled subjects and must be analyzed by treatment arm. Linking trial_endpoint to enrollment provides treatment arm context and enables filtering by analysis popul

Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Clinical endpoints are captured via specific CRF forms (endpoint assessment CRF, tumor assessment CRF, response evaluation CRF). Linking trial_endpoint to crf_form enables traceability to the data col

Foreign key linking to market.hta_submission. Business justification: HTA bodies evaluate specific clinical endpoints (primary, PRO, surrogate) for reimbursement decisions. Direct traceability from endpoint results to HTA submission is required for HTA response document
Reference to the investigational site at which the endpoint assessment was performed.

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Endpoint results must be linked to products for efficacy claims in regulatory applications (BLA/NDA/MAA), product labeling (indications/clinical studies section), and health technology assessments. Co
Reference to the enrolled trial subject (patient) for whom this endpoint is being assessed. Constitutes protected health information under HIPAA and GDPR.
reference: HIPAA 45 CFR §164; GDPR Article 9; ICH E6(R2)

description: Reference to the specific protocol version governing this endpoint assessment, ensuring traceability to the approved study design.
Reference to the parent clinical trial under which this endpoint assessment was conducted. Links to the trial master record in the CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Trial endpoint results require standardized UoM for CDISC ADaM/SDTM submissions, cross-trial efficacy comparisons, and HTA dossier preparation. result_unit is a denormalized plain-text attribute; a FK

Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: Clinical endpoints are assessed at specific protocol-defined visits. Linking trial_endpoint to visit provides the complete visit context including planned vs actual date, visit window compliance, and
Date on which the independent adjudication committee completed its review and issued a final determination for this endpoint assessment.
reference: FDA Guidance on Independent Radiological Review; ICH E6(R2)

description: Indicates whether this endpoint assessment requires review by an independent adjudication committee (e.g., BICR, DSMB, IRC) due to discordance between investigator and central read, or per protocol mandate.
reference: FDA Guidance on Independent Radiological Review; ICH E6(R2)

description: Final response category or concordance determination issued by the independent adjudication committee after review. Overrides investigator assessment when discordant, per protocol rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CR|PR|SD|PD|NE|concordant|discordant — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Guidance on Independent Radiological Review; RECIST 1.1

description: Current status of the independent adjudication committee review for this endpoint assessment. Tracks workflow from pending through completed adjudication, supporting BICR and IRC processes.
reference: FDA Guidance on Independent Radiological Review; ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Calendar date on which the endpoint assessment was performed. This is the principal real-world event date for the endpoint record, distinct from data entry or audit timestamps.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Standardized methodology used to evaluate the endpoint (e.g., RECIST 1.1 for solid tumor response, iRECIST for immunotherapy, BICR for blinded independent central review, RANO for CNS tumors). Critical for regulatory submission credibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECIST_1.1|iRECIST|BICR|investigator|RANO|mRECIST|PCWG3|Cheson|IWG — promote to reference product]
reference: RECIST 1.1 (Eisenhauer et al. 2009); FDA Guidance on Radiological Imaging; EMA Oncology Guidelines

description: Current workflow status of the endpoint assessment record: planned (scheduled but not yet performed), completed (data captured and verified), missed (visit occurred but assessment not done), not_done (subject not available), or invalid (data quality issue).
reference: ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC SDTM

description: Baseline measurement value for the endpoint at study entry (e.g., sum of longest diameters at baseline for RECIST). Used to calculate percent change from baseline and determine response categories.

Best overall response achieved by the subject across all assessment timepoints up to and including this assessment, per RECIST or applicable criteria. Used in ORR and DOR calculations for regulatory submissions.
reference: RECIST 1.1; FDA Guidance on Oncology Endpoints; ICH E9(R1)

description: Reason for censoring a subject in time-to-event analyses when the endpoint event has not occurred (e.g., lost to follow-up, withdrawal of consent, initiation of new anticancer therapy, end of study). Required for regulatory-grade survival analyses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lost_to_followup|withdrawal|new_anticancer_therapy|end_of_study|administrative|death_other_cause — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Survival Analysis; CDISC SDTM

description: Indicates whether the response (CR or PR) has been confirmed by a subsequent assessment at least 4 weeks later, as required by RECIST 1.1 for ORR determination in regulatory submissions.
reference: RECIST 1.1; FDA Guidance on Oncology Endpoints

description: Timestamp when this endpoint assessment record was first created in the clinical data system. Required for GCP audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 5

description: Indicates whether this endpoint record has been locked as part of the clinical database lock process prior to unblinding and statistical analysis. Locked records are immutable for regulatory submission purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Database Lock

description: Source system record identifier from Medidata Rave EDC, enabling traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the originating EDC record for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC

description: Classification of the endpoint as primary, secondary, exploratory, or safety per the study protocol. Primary endpoints drive the primary efficacy analysis for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the endpoint definition within the study (e.g., EP001, PFS_PRIMARY). Sourced from the study protocol and EDC configuration in Medidata Rave.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Full descriptive name of the clinical endpoint being assessed (e.g., Objective Response Rate, Progression-Free Survival, Overall Survival, Duration of Response, Quality of Life). Aligns with protocol-defined endpoint nomenclature.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints

description: Functional type of the endpoint indicating the nature of the measurement: efficacy (ORR, PFS, OS), safety (AE rate), pharmacokinetic (PK), pharmacodynamic (PD), biomarker, or patient-reported outcome (PRO/QoL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|safety|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic|biomarker|patient_reported|composite — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Biomarker Qualification

description: Indicates whether the subject is considered evaluable for this specific endpoint at this timepoint, based on protocol-defined evaluability criteria (e.g., minimum number of prior assessments, no major protocol deviations).
reference: ICH E9(R1); Study Protocol Evaluability Criteria

description: Date on which the endpoint event (progression, death, response) occurred. For censored subjects, this is the last known event-free date. Used in time-to-event analyses for PFS and OS.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Survival Analysis; CDISC SDTM

description: Indicates whether the endpoint event (e.g., disease progression, death) has occurred for time-to-event endpoints such as PFS and OS. False indicates censoring. Critical for Kaplan-Meier and Cox regression analyses.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Survival Analysis; CDISC SDTM

description: Indicates whether this subject's endpoint assessment is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population, the primary analysis population for most regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on ITT Principle; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Lowest (nadir) measurement value recorded for the subject across all prior assessments. Used in RECIST PD determination (≥20% increase from nadir) and DOR calculations.
Percentage change in the endpoint measurement from the baseline value to the current assessment value. Directly used in waterfall plots and response determination per RECIST criteria.
reference: RECIST 1.1; FDA Guidance on Oncology Endpoints; ICH E9(R1)

description: Indicates whether this subject's endpoint assessment is included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population, used as a sensitivity analysis alongside ITT in regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Populations

description: Indicates whether there is an open data query (discrepancy) raised against this endpoint record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Open queries must be resolved before database lock.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Categorical tumor response classification per the applicable assessment criteria: Complete Response (CR), Partial Response (PR), Stable Disease (SD), Progressive Disease (PD), or Not Evaluable (NE). Directly feeds ORR calculation for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: RECIST 1.1; iRECIST; FDA Guidance on Oncology Endpoints; ICH E9(R1)

description: Numeric result of the endpoint assessment (e.g., tumor size in mm, PFS duration in months, QoL score). This is the principal quantitative outcome for the endpoint record. Null for categorical-only endpoints.
reference: ICH E9(R1); Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC SDTM

description: Duration in days from the study reference date (e.g., randomization date) to the endpoint event or censoring date. Used directly in PFS, OS, and DOR analyses for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Survival Analysis; CDISC SDTM

description: Timestamp when this endpoint assessment record was most recently modified. Required for GCP audit trail compliance and data integrity monitoring under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each investigational medicinal product (IMP) or comparator record in the clinical trial supply master. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Guideline; 21 CFR Part 312

Document reference number or system identifier for the IMP accountability log maintained at the investigational site or centrally. Links to the eTMF document in Veeva Vault per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.15.

description: Manufacturer-assigned batch or lot number for the specific IMP batch supplied to the trial. Essential for GMP traceability, recall management, and IMP accountability per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.

reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles; ICH E6(R2) Section 1.7; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether this IMP requires cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled storage and transport) throughout the supply chain from manufacturer to investigational site. Drives GDP-compliant distribution planning.

description: Timestamp when this investigational product record was first created in the system of record (Oracle CTMS or SAP). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).

description: Unit of measure used for IMP dispensation, return, and destruction quantities (e.g., tablet, capsule, vial, mL). Ensures consistent accountability across all quantity fields. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|ampoule|mL|mg|patch|sachet|unit — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU GMP Annex 13; EDQM Standard Terms

description: Expiration date of the IMP batch beyond which the product must not be dispensed to trial subjects. Mandatory field for IMP accountability and GCP compliance per ICH E6(R2).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.137

description: Reference to the GMP certificate or Qualified Person (QP) release certificate confirming the IMP batch was manufactured in compliance with current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP). Required for EU CTA and IND.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; PIC/S GMP Guide; 21 CFR Parts 210-211; ICH Q7

description: Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this IMP or comparator within the trial supply system. Used for dispensation tracking and accountability logs in Oracle CTMS and SAP MM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU IMPD Guideline; 21 CFR Part 312.23

description: Reference number or document identifier for the Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier (IMPD) submitted to EU competent authorities. Supports EMA/MHRA CTA submissions.
reference: EU Directive 2001/20/EC; EMA IMPD Guideline; Veeva Vault RIM

description: FDA-assigned Investigational New Drug (IND) application number under which this IMP is authorized for clinical investigation in the United States. Links to the regulatory.application record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; FDA IND Guidance; ICH E6(R2)

description: Unique identifier for the IMP dispensation kit as labeled and shipped to the investigational site. Used for kit-level accountability tracking and IWRS-driven dispensation in Oracle CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU GMP Annex 13; Oracle CTMS IMP Module

description: Date on which the IMP batch was manufactured. Used to calculate shelf life, retest dates, and to verify GMP compliance of the batch at time of dispensation.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.137; ICH Q1A Stability Guideline

description: IMP code of the matching placebo formulated to be indistinguishable from the active investigational product in appearance, taste, and smell. Required for double-blind trial integrity and IMP accountability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; EU GMP Annex 13; FDA Blinding Guidance

description: Narrative description of the mechanism of action (MOA) of the IMP's active pharmaceutical ingredient (API), describing how it produces its pharmacological effect at the molecular or cellular level.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA IND Guidance; EMA Scientific Advice

description: Classification of the product role in the trial: investigational (test article), comparator (active control), placebo (matching or non-matching), rescue medication, or auxiliary medicinal product per ICH E6(R2) definitions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 1.33; EU IMPD Guideline; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Total quantity of this IMP destroyed at site or centrally, expressed in the dispensation unit. Required for complete IMP accountability reconciliation and destruction certificate documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.15; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Total quantity of this IMP dispensed to trial subjects from this batch, expressed in the dispensation unit (e.g., tablets, vials, mL). Core field for IMP accountability log per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.15; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Total quantity of this IMP returned by trial subjects or sites, expressed in the dispensation unit. Used to calculate compliance rates and reconcile IMP accountability per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.15; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Date by which the IMP batch must be retested to confirm continued suitability for use, particularly for drug substances with defined retest periods rather than expiry dates. Supports stability management.
reference: ICH Q1A(R2) Stability Guideline; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 211.137

description: Required storage conditions for the IMP as specified in the CoA and IMPD/IND (e.g., 2-8°C refrigerated, 15-25°C controlled room temperature, -20°C frozen, protect from light). Critical for cold-chain management and GDP compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: refrigerated_2_8c|controlled_room_temp|frozen_minus20c|frozen_minus80c|protect_from_light|ambient — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q1A(R2); EU GDP Guidelines; 21 CFR Part 211.142; WHO TRS 961

description: Current status of this IMP record in the trial supply lifecycle: available (at site, ready for dispensation), dispensed (issued to subject), returned (received back from subject), destroyed, quarantine (hold pending investigation), or expired.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU GMP Annex 13; SAP MM Inventory Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this investigational product record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; EU GMP Annex 11

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the investigational site record in the clinical data lakehouse. Primary key for this master record.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Sites are physically located in countries. Essential for regulatory authority jurisdiction determination, IRB/IEC requirements, site selection strategy, country-level enrollment tracking, and GCP comp

Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Sites receive IMP through specific distribution nodes (depots/warehouses); GDP compliance requires documented supply chain routing. Clinical supply management uses this for shipment planning, temperat

Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: An investigational site is hosted by a healthcare organization. Linking to hco_master enables site feasibility analysis, HCO-level performance benchmarking, contracting, and Sunshine Act reporting at

Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Sites must comply with jurisdiction-specific regulations and inspection requirements. Site qualification, GCP inspection readiness, and regulatory compliance tracking require knowing which health auth

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Investigational sites are managed by regional or country clinical operations org units responsible for site monitoring, budget oversight, and performance reporting. Pharma clinical ops teams require t

Foreign key linking to masterdata.address. Business justification: Sites have standardized physical addresses. Required for site management, investigational product shipments, monitoring visit planning, regulatory documentation (site lists in submissions), and geoloc

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Investigational sites are registered as business partners (vendors) in pharma masterdata for site fee payments, investigator grant processing, and vendor qualification audits. This is a standard accou

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Sites incur costs tracked via internal orders for site activation, monitoring, patient recruitment, and investigator fees. Essential for trial budget management, site payment processing, and cost-per-

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Sites are owned/operated by legal entities (hospitals, research institutions, private practices). Required for clinical trial agreements (CTAs), payment processing, insurance/liability coverage, and r
Date on which the site close-out visit was completed and the site was formally closed. All essential documents must be archived in eTMF (Veeva Vault) by this date.
Timestamp when the investigational site record was first created in the clinical data lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; GDPR Article 5

description: Actual number of subjects who have been randomized and enrolled at this site to date. Compared against enrollment_target to assess site performance and trigger enrollment support actions.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; CTMS Oracle/Siebel

description: Planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site as agreed between the sponsor and principal investigator. Used for enrollment forecasting, site performance benchmarking, and IMP supply planning.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) Site Folder ID
description: Reference identifier for the site-level folder in the Veeva Vault eTMF system. Links the site master record to its essential document archive for GCP inspection readiness and regulatory submission support.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Date on which the site feasibility assessment was completed, confirming the site's capability, patient population, and infrastructure to conduct the trial. First milestone in the site activation timeline tracked in CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.19; CTMS Oracle/Siebel Site Milestone

business_glossary_term: First Patient First Visit (FPFV) Actual Date
description: Actual date on which the first patient first visit (FPFV) occurred at this site. Compared against fpfv_target_date to measure site activation performance and enrollment timeline adherence.

business_glossary_term: First Patient First Visit (FPFV) Target Date
description: Planned date for the first patient first visit (FPFV) at this site, representing the target enrollment start. Used for enrollment forecasting and site performance tracking against the clinical development timeline.
reference: ICH E6(R2); CTMS Oracle/Siebel Enrollment Milestone

description: Current GCP inspection status of the investigational site by a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Critical for site risk assessment, regulatory submission support, and pre-approval inspection (PAI) readiness.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.68; EMA GCP Inspection Procedure; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.19.3; PIC/S

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the investigational site within the clinical program. Drives CRA monitoring schedules, IMP shipment eligibility, and enrollment reporting. Values: selected (identified for participation), activated (SIV complete, ready to enroll), enrolling (actively screening/randomizing subjects), on_hold (enrollment paused), closed (no further activity), terminated (early discontinuation).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; 21 CFR Part 312.62; CTMS Oracle/Siebel

description: Date on which the current IRB/IEC approval for the trial protocol at this site expires. Triggers renewal workflow; enrollment must be suspended if approval lapses without renewal.

Official approval reference number issued by the IRB/IEC for the clinical trial protocol at this site. Mandatory for site activation and must be filed in the eTMF (Veeva Vault) prior to first patient first visit (FPFV).

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Name
description: Name of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) of record responsible for ethical oversight of clinical trial activities at this site. Required for GCP compliance and eTMF documentation in Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3; 21 CFR Part 56; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Training Confirmed Indicator
description: Indicates whether all required site staff have completed GCP training as documented in the eTMF (Veeva Vault) prior to site activation. Mandatory pre-condition for site initiation per ICH E6(R2).
Indicates whether this site is designated as the lead or coordinating site for a multi-center trial. Lead sites often host the coordinating investigator and serve as the primary regulatory contact.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.19; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Last Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Inspection Date
description: Date of the most recent GCP inspection conducted at this site by a regulatory authority. Used to assess inspection recency and site compliance history for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.68; ICH E6(R2); EMA GCP Inspection Procedure

business_glossary_term: Last Patient Last Visit (LPLV) Actual Date
description: Actual date on which the last patient last visit (LPLV) occurred at this site, marking the end of active patient participation. Triggers site close-out visit scheduling and IMP reconciliation.

Primary regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the investigational site's clinical trial activities. Determines applicable GCP guidelines, inspection readiness requirements, and submission standards (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|NMPA|WHO|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Parts 312, 314; EMA Regulation (EU) No 536/2014; ICH E6(R2)

description: Number of subjects who were screened but failed to meet eligibility criteria at this site. Used to calculate screen failure rate and assess protocol feasibility and patient population fit.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; CTMS Oracle/Siebel

description: Date on which the sponsor formally selected the site for participation in the clinical trial, following feasibility review. Marks transition to site status 'selected' in CTMS.

Classification of the investigational site by institutional category, used for site selection strategy, feasibility analysis, and enrollment benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic_medical_center|community_hospital|private_clinic|research_center|cro_managed|veterans_affairs|pediatric_center — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2); CTMS Oracle/Siebel Site Classification

description: Date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was conducted by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA). Confirms site readiness, staff training, and IMP accountability procedures. Required before first patient enrollment.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.19.2; 21 CFR Part 312.62; CTMS Oracle/Siebel

description: IANA time zone identifier for the investigational site (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for accurate timestamp alignment of EDC data from Medidata Rave, adverse event reporting timelines, and CRA scheduling.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; ICH E2B(R3) ICSR Reporting

description: Timestamp when the investigational site record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data pipeline processing.

Unique surrogate identifier for the principal investigator or sub-investigator master record in the clinical domain. Serves as the primary key for this entity across all downstream clinical data products.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice (GCP); 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to hcp.contract. Business justification: Each principal investigator's trial participation is governed by a signed investigator agreement (contract). Direct FK enables financial disclosure tracking, FMV compliance validation, and OIG/SAM scr

business_glossary_term: Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) Investigator ID
description: Source system identifier for this investigator as recorded in Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing back to the operational CTMS record.
reference: Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS; ICH E6(R2)

description: FK to hcp.hcp_master.hcp_master_id — Principal investigators are HCPs — linking PI records to HCP master enables unified view of an HCPs relationship with the company across clinical, commercial, and medical affairs activities.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Principal investigators are registered with and subject to debarment checks by specific health authorities (FDA debarment list, EMA disqualification register). Regulatory compliance teams must link ea

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_site. Business justification: A Principal Investigator (PI) record in a CTMS is fundamentally associated with a specific investigational site — the PI conducts the trial at that site. The principal_investigator table carries per-s

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Principal investigators receive investigator grants and honoraria requiring registration as business partners for payment processing, financial disclosure compliance (Sunshine Act), and vendor qualifi

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PIs hold medical licenses in specific countries. Required for regulatory compliance verification, license validation against country-specific medical boards, Form FDA 1572 validation, and investigator

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.reporter. Business justification: Principal Investigators are primary reporters of trial SAEs/SUSARs to sponsors. Linking PI identity to PV reporter records enables causality assessment tracking, follow-up correspondence, and regulato

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Site feasibility assessments and investigator qualification processes match PIs to trials by therapeutic area expertise. Plain 'therapeutic_area_expertise' text column denormalizes the master. Clinica

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the investigator primarily practices and conducts clinical trial activities. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, EMA, PMDA, NMPA, etc.).
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; ICH E6(R2); EMA Clinical Trial Regulation (EU) 536/2014

description: Reference identifier or URL pointing to the investigator's current curriculum vitae stored in Veeva Vault eTMF. The CV is a mandatory eTMF document (TMF Reference Model artifact 01.01) required for regulatory submissions and site qualification.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.2.10; TMF Reference Model v3.0 (DIA); Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Reference identifier pointing to the investigator's delegation of authority log stored in Veeva Vault eTMF or site trial master file. The delegation log documents which trial tasks have been assigned to sub-investigators and site staff, as required by GCP.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1.5, 8.3.22; TMF Reference Model v3.0; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Primary professional email address of the investigator used for trial-related communications, protocol amendments, and safety notifications. Distinct from personal email; should be institutional or practice-based.
reference: ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 4; HIPAA Privacy Rule

description: Date on which the investigator was last verified against the FDA Debarment List and the Office of Inspector General (OIG) exclusion database. Mandatory pre-engagement check and periodic re-verification per sponsor SOPs.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.70; FDA Debarment List; OIG Exclusion Database; FDCA Section 306

description: Date on which the investigator's financial disclosure form (FDA Form 3454 or 3455) was most recently submitted or updated. Required for NDA/BLA submissions to FDA.

Status of the investigator's financial disclosure form (FDA Form 3454/3455) as required under 21 CFR Part 54. Indicates whether the investigator has disclosed financial interests that could affect trial integrity. 'Disclosed' means the form has been submitted and reviewed.
reference: 21 CFR Part 54 (Financial Disclosure by Clinical Investigators); FDA Guidance on Financial Disclosure

description: Legal given name of the principal investigator or sub-investigator as it appears on their medical license and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; 21 CFR Part 312.53; GDPR Article 4

description: Date on which the investigator signed FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator) for the most recent trial engagement. Form 1572 is a legal commitment by the investigator to comply with FDA regulations and GCP. Required for all IND-regulated trials in the US.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53(c); FDA Form 1572; ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Training Certification Date
description: Date on which the investigator most recently completed GCP training as required by ICH E6(R2). Mandatory for trial participation; triggers re-training alerts when approaching the validity window (typically 2-3 years).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1.3; 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC

business_glossary_term: Good Clinical Practice (GCP) Training Expiry Date
description: Date on which the current GCP training certification expires. Investigators with expired GCP training must not conduct trial activities. Drives compliance monitoring and site qualification workflows.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1.3; FDA GCP Guidance; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC

description: Name of the organization or platform that delivered the GCP training (e.g., CITI Program, TransCelerate, sponsor-internal training). Supports audit trail for regulatory inspections.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA GCP Guidance; MHRA GCP Inspection findings

description: GCP-defined role of the individual at the investigational site: Principal Investigator (PI) who bears overall responsibility, Sub-Investigator (sub-I) who assists under PI supervision, or Coordinating Investigator who coordinates multi-site activities.

Current lifecycle status of the investigator record. 'Active' indicates the investigator is qualified and currently engaged in trial conduct. 'Disqualified' reflects FDA or regulatory authority debarment or disqualification actions.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312.70 (Disqualification of Clinical Investigators); FDA Debarment List

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board (IRB) / Ethics Committee Registration Number
description: Registration number of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) with which the investigator's site is registered. Required for FDA-regulated trials under 21 CFR Part 56 and ICH E6(R2).
reference: 21 CFR Part 56 (Institutional Review Boards); ICH E6(R2) Section 3; OHRP IRB Registration

description: Indicates whether the investigator is currently listed on the FDA Debarment List or OIG exclusion database. A value of True immediately disqualifies the investigator from participating in any FDA-regulated clinical trial.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.70; FDCA Section 306; OIG Exclusion Database

description: Legal family name of the principal investigator or sub-investigator as it appears on their medical license and regulatory submissions.
