reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; 21 CFR Part 312.53; GDPR Article 4

description: Date on which the investigator's medical license expires. Triggers re-qualification workflows to ensure continued GCP-compliant trial conduct. Null if the license has no expiry.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; 21 CFR Part 312.53; GCP compliance monitoring

description: Name of the regulatory or professional body that issued the medical license (e.g., California Medical Board, General Medical Council UK, Ordre des Médecins France). Required for cross-border trial conduct verification.

State or national medical license number issued by the relevant licensing authority. Required for regulatory submissions and site qualification. Uniquely identifies the investigator's right to practice medicine.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53(c); ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1.1; State Medical Board regulations

description: Primary board-certified or recognized medical specialty of the investigator (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Cardiology, Neurology). Used to match investigator expertise to trial therapeutic area and protocol requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; American Board of Medical Specialties (ABMS); UEMS (European Union of Medical Specialists)

description: Middle name or initial of the investigator as recorded on official credentials. Used for disambiguation when first and last names are not unique.

10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) assigned by CMS to US-based healthcare providers. Used for US-specific investigator identification, cross-referencing with HCP databases, and commercial engagement separation.
reference: HIPAA Administrative Simplification (45 CFR Part 162); CMS NPI Registry; NPPES

description: Persistent digital identifier (ORCID iD) assigned to the investigator by the ORCID registry. Used to uniquely identify researchers across publications, grant applications, and clinical trial registries (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov).
reference: ORCID Standard; ICH E6(R2); ClinicalTrials.gov registration requirements

description: Primary professional phone number for the investigator, used for urgent safety communications, protocol queries, and site monitoring contact. Includes country code for international trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 4; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Total number of GCP-regulated clinical trials in which the investigator has previously served as PI or sub-investigator. Key feasibility metric used by sponsors and CROs during site selection.
reference: ICH E6(R2); TransCelerate Investigator Registry; FDA Guidance on Site Selection

description: Highest relevant professional or academic degree held by the investigator (e.g., MD, PhD, PharmD). Used in regulatory submissions and delegation logs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|PhD|MD-PhD|PharmD|MBBS|MBChB|DVM|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.53

description: Date on which the sponsor or CRO last verified the investigator's qualifications, credentials, and GCP training as part of the site qualification or re-qualification process. Supports audit trail for regulatory inspections.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.6; 21 CFR Part 312.53; FDA Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) readiness

description: Timestamp when the principal investigator master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GCP-compliant record management per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); ICH E6(R2) Section 8; GDPR Article 5

description: Timestamp when the principal investigator master record was most recently modified. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; ALCOA+ data integrity principles

description: Identifier assigned by a national regulatory authority for the investigator's registration to conduct clinical trials (e.g., FDA investigator registration number, EudraCT investigator ID, MHRA registration). Required for IND/NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.53; EudraCT; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation (EU) 536/2014; MHRA

description: Secondary or sub-specialty area of clinical expertise (e.g., Hematologic Oncology, Pediatric Immunology). Provides finer-grained matching for complex or rare disease trials.
Number of years the investigator has been conducting GCP-regulated clinical trials. Used during site feasibility assessments to evaluate investigator experience relative to protocol complexity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.1; FDA Guidance on Investigator Qualifications; TransCelerate Site and Investigator Quality Tolerance Limits

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each informed consent event record in the clinical trial. Primary key for the trial_consent data product.

Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Informed consent is obtained during the enrollment process. Linking trial_consent to enrollment provides the complete enrollment context and enables verification that consent was obtained before enrol

Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Informed consent may be captured via CRF form (consent CRF) in the EDC system, especially for electronic consent (eConsent) workflows. Linking trial_consent to crf_form enables traceability to the dat

Foreign key linking to patient.informed_consent. Business justification: GCP compliance and GDPR/HIPAA data privacy audits require reconciliation between trial CRF consent records (trial_consent in eTMF/EDC) and patient-level consent management records (patient.informed_co
Reference to the investigational site where the consent event occurred. Links to the site master record in the CTMS.
Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) or sub-investigator who conducted the informed consent discussion and signed the consent form. Links to the investigator master record in the CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Reference to the trial subject (patient) who provided informed consent. Links to the subject master record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 50; GDPR Article 9

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Informed consent forms are version-controlled per protocol version. The trial_consent table has protocol_version as a string, but linking to protocol table provides the authoritative protocol version

Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: REMS programs mandate specific patient consent elements and consent form language (e.g., iPLEDGE pregnancy prevention counseling, Clozapine agranulocytosis risk disclosure). Regulatory and clinical te
Reference to the clinical trial for which this consent was obtained. Links to the trial master record in the CTMS.

Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: Informed consent is typically obtained at the screening visit. Linking trial_consent to visit provides the visit context and enables verification that consent was obtained before any protocol-specific

Free-text field for additional notes or comments related to the consent event, such as special circumstances, deviations from standard consent procedures, or clarifications noted by the investigator or study coordinator.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Date on which the subject (or LAR) signed and dated the informed consent form, confirming voluntary agreement to participate in the trial. This is the principal business event date for the consent transaction.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.8; 21 CFR Part 50.27(a); FDA 21 CFR Part 312

description: Version identifier of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) used for this consent event, as approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). Critical for regulatory traceability when protocol amendments trigger re-consent.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.2; 21 CFR Part 50.27; FDA Guidance on Informed Consent

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the ICF was presented and signed by the subject, ensuring the subject received information in their native language per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.6; 21 CFR Part 50.25; ISO 639-1

description: Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this consent event, used for regulatory traceability and audit purposes. Typically generated by the CTMS or EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave or Oracle Clinical).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 50.25

description: Current lifecycle state of the consent record. 'Active' indicates valid consent in force; 'withdrawn' indicates subject has revoked consent; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer consent version; 'pending' indicates awaiting subject signature; 'expired' indicates consent period has lapsed.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 7; 21 CFR Part 50

description: Precise date and time at which the consent was electronically captured or recorded in the EDC/CTMS system (e.g., Medidata Rave), supporting 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; FDA Electronic Records Guidance

description: Classification of the consent event: initial consent at enrollment, re-consent triggered by protocol amendment, assent from a minor subject, consent by a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR), or withdrawal of consent.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 50.25; GDPR Article 7

description: Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the system (EDC or CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+ Data Integrity Principles

description: Name or identifier of the eConsent platform used to capture electronic consent (e.g., Medidata Rave eConsent, Veeva Vault). Populated only when electronic_consent is True.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Use of Electronic Informed Consent (2016)

description: Indicates whether the consent was obtained via an electronic consent (eConsent) platform rather than a traditional paper-based process. Relevant for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and audit trail requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Use of Electronic Informed Consent (2016); ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) Document ID
description: Document identifier in the Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) system (e.g., Veeva Vault) for the scanned or electronically captured consent form. Enables direct linkage between the consent record and the regulatory-compliant document archive.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8; DIA eTMF Reference Model; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) or delegated sub-investigator has signed the informed consent form, confirming the consent discussion was conducted per GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.8; 21 CFR Part 50.27(a)

description: Date on which the IRB or IEC approved the ICF version referenced in this consent event. Consent must not be obtained prior to this date to maintain GCP compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3.1.2; 21 CFR Part 56.109; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board / Independent Ethics Committee (IRB/IEC) Approval Number
description: Reference number of the IRB or IEC approval that authorized the specific ICF version used in this consent event. Ensures GCP-compliant traceability between the consent form version and its ethics committee approval.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 3.1; 21 CFR Part 56; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

description: Indicates whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population. Subjects who consented and received at least one dose of investigational product are typically included in the ITT population.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; FDA Guidance on ITT Principle

description: Full legal name of the Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) who provided consent on behalf of the subject. Populated only when lar_required is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.12; 21 CFR Part 50.3(l); GDPR Article 9

description: Relationship of the Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) to the trial subject (e.g., parent, legal guardian, spouse). Required when lar_required is True to establish legal authority to consent.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.12; 21 CFR Part 50.3(l)

description: Indicates whether a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) was required to provide consent on behalf of the subject, applicable for minors, cognitively impaired subjects, or other legally incapacitated individuals.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.12; 21 CFR Part 50.3(l); GDPR Article 8

description: Indicates whether the Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) has signed and dated the informed consent form. Applicable only when lar_required is True.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.12; 21 CFR Part 50.27(b)

description: Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for long-term storage and future use of biological samples in a biobank. This is a separate optional consent element from the main trial consent.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 9; 21 CFR Part 50.25(b); ISBER Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the subject provided optional consent for genetic or genomic research using their biological samples. Separate from the main trial consent and biobank consent.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 9; 21 CFR Part 50.25(b); GINA (Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act)

description: Date on which the subject provided re-consent following a protocol amendment or updated ICF version. Null if no re-consent has been required for this subject.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.2; 21 CFR Part 50.25; FDA Guidance on Protocol Amendments

description: Reason that triggered the re-consent requirement, such as a protocol amendment, new safety information identified during the trial, or a regulatory authority request. Null if no re-consent was required.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.2; FDA Guidance on Informed Consent

description: Location reference for the original signed consent form source document, such as the eTMF folder path in Veeva Vault or physical file location at the investigational site. Supports GCP source data verification (SDV) and audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.9; 21 CFR Part 312.62; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Indicates whether the subject has personally signed and dated the informed consent form. False if consent was provided solely by a Legally Authorized Representative (LAR) without subject co-signature.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.8; 21 CFR Part 50.27(a)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this consent record in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data integrity monitoring per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; ALCOA+ Data Integrity Principles

description: Date on which the subject formally withdrew consent to participate in the trial. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Triggers subject discontinuation workflow in the CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.8; 21 CFR Part 50.25(a)(8); GDPR Article 7(3)

description: Free-text or coded reason provided by the subject or investigator for withdrawal of consent. Captured for regulatory reporting and subject safety follow-up purposes.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.8; 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Indicates whether an impartial witness was required to be present during the consent process, applicable when the subject or LAR is unable to read or when a short-form consent is used.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8.9; 21 CFR Part 50.27(b)(2)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each subject enrollment and randomization record within a clinical trial. Primary key for the enrollment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Subject-level IMP accountability requires knowing which inventory lot was dispensed to each enrolled subject. GCP IMP accountability logs and pharmacovigilance lot traceability per subject are regulat

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Enrolled subjects are assigned to specific investigational product kits at randomization. While enrollment has treatment_arm_code, linking to investigational_product provides the actual IMP kit assign
Reference to the investigational site where the subject was screened and enrolled. Corresponds to the site record in Oracle CTMS / Medidata Rave.

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Tracks which medicinal product each subject received. Required for product-specific safety reporting (ICH E2A), efficacy analysis by product, and regulatory queries about subject exposure in multi-pro
Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) record. Links to the subject master record sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.
reference: ICH E6(R2); HIPAA 45 CFR Parts 160 and 164; GDPR Article 9

description: Reference to the specific protocol version under which the subject was enrolled. A trial may have multiple protocol amendments; this captures the version active at enrollment.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: REMS programs (e.g., iPLEDGE, Clozapine REMS) require individual patient enrollment and compliance tracking. Each clinical enrollment in a REMS-governed trial must link to the REMS program for FDA-man
Reference to the parent clinical trial under which this subject was screened and enrolled. Links to the trial master record in Oracle CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Clinical Trials Regulation (EU) No 536/2014

description: The ratio of subjects allocated to each treatment arm as defined in the randomization scheme (e.g., '1:1', '2:1', '1:1:1'). Determines the probability of assignment to each arm. Sourced from the RTSM randomization schedule.
reference: ICH E9 Section 2.3; ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Indicates the current blinding state of the subject's treatment assignment. 'blinded' = treatment assignment concealed; 'open_label' = treatment known from outset; 'unblinded' = previously blinded assignment has been revealed. Critical for data integrity and regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Blinding

description: The number of subjects per randomization block used in the blocked randomization scheme. Confidential as knowledge of block size could compromise blinding. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM randomization parameters.
reference: ICH E9 Section 2.3; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; FDA Guidance on Randomization

description: Date on which the subject completed all protocol-required study procedures and follow-up visits. Populated only when enrollment_status = completed. Used for study close-out and final analysis datasets.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC CDASH Standard; ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration

description: Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the source system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) GCP guidelines.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

business_glossary_term: Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) Subject Key
description: The natural/business key for this subject enrollment record as assigned by Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation between CTMS and Medidata Rave EDC. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Flag indicating whether the subject's enrollment and randomization data has been locked for analysis (database lock). True = data locked and frozen for statistical analysis; False = data still open for query resolution or updates. Critical for regulatory submission readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data; CDISC Standards

description: Flag indicating whether the subject has been confirmed as meeting all inclusion criteria and none of the exclusion criteria as defined in the trial protocol. True = eligible; False = not eligible or assessment pending. Drives enrollment decision in RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Inclusion/Exclusion Criteria

description: Date on which the subject was formally enrolled into the clinical trial following eligibility confirmation. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the enrollment transaction. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the subject within the enrollment and randomization workflow. Drives Intent-to-Treat (ITT), modified ITT, and Per-Protocol (PP) population definitions. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screened|screen_failed|enrolled|randomized|withdrawn|completed|discontinued — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312

description: Flag indicating whether a valid, signed Informed Consent Form (ICF) has been confirmed for this subject prior to any study procedures. True = ICF confirmed; False = ICF not confirmed or pending. Mandatory GCP compliance check.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 50.25; EMA Regulation (EU) No 536/2014

description: Date on which the subject signed the Informed Consent Form (ICF), confirming voluntary participation in the trial. Must precede any study-specific procedures per GCP requirements. Critical for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 50; EMA Regulation (EU) No 536/2014 Article 29; GDPR Article 7

description: Flag indicating whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population. True = subject is part of the ITT population (all randomized subjects regardless of protocol adherence). Drives primary efficacy analysis per ICH E9.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.2; ICH E9(R1) Addendum on Estimands; 21 CFR Part 314; FDA Guidance on ITT Principle

description: Flag indicating whether the subject is included in the modified Intent-to-Treat (mITT) analysis population. Criteria for mITT inclusion are protocol-defined (e.g., received at least one dose). Supports secondary efficacy analyses.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.2; ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on ITT Principle; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Flag indicating whether the subject is included in the Per-Protocol (PP) analysis population. True = subject completed the trial per protocol without major deviations. Used for sensitivity analyses in NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.
reference: ICH E9 Section 5.2; ICH E9(R1); 21 CFR Part 314; EMA Guideline on Statistical Principles

description: Unique alphanumeric code assigned by the RTSM system at the point of randomization, linking the subject to their treatment arm assignment within the randomization schedule. Confidential during blinded phase; revealed only upon unblinding. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Adaptive Designs

description: Date on which the subject was randomized and assigned to a treatment arm via the RTSM system. May differ from enrollment_date in trials with a run-in period. Null for screen-failed or non-randomized subjects.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312

description: The natural/business key for this subject enrollment record as assigned by Medidata Rave EDC/RTSM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing back to the authoritative EDC source system.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; 21 CFR Part 11; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Coded or free-text reason why a subject failed screening and was not enrolled. Populated only when enrollment_status = screen_failed. Used for protocol feasibility analysis and site performance monitoring. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC screen failure CRF.
reference: ICH E6(R2); FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data; CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Date on which the subject first presented at the investigational site for screening assessments. Marks the start of the eligibility evaluation period. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a subject at the point of screening, prior to eligibility confirmation. Format is site-specific and assigned sequentially by the RTSM system in Medidata Rave. Used to track screen failures and pre-enrollment activity.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Value of the first stratification factor used to balance treatment arm assignments across key prognostic variables (e.g., disease stage, geographic region, biomarker status). Defined in the trial protocol and captured at randomization in RTSM.
reference: ICH E9 Section 2.3; ICH E6(R2); CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Value of the second stratification factor used to balance treatment arm assignments (e.g., prior therapy, performance status, age group). Null if the trial uses fewer than two stratification factors. Captured at randomization in RTSM.
reference: ICH E9 Section 2.3; ICH E6(R2); CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Value of the third stratification factor used to balance treatment arm assignments (e.g., country, mutation status, weight band). Null if the trial uses fewer than three stratification factors. Captured at randomization in RTSM.
reference: ICH E9 Section 2.3; ICH E6(R2); CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Unique identifier assigned to a subject upon successful enrollment into the trial. Typically formatted as site-number-sequential-ID (e.g., 001-001). Assigned by Medidata Rave RTSM upon eligibility confirmation and enrollment confirmation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Code identifying the treatment arm (e.g., active drug, placebo, comparator, dose level) to which the subject was assigned at randomization. Confidential in blinded trials. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM randomization schedule.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2); CDISC CDASH Standard; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Human-readable description of the treatment arm assignment (e.g., 'Drug A 100mg QD', 'Placebo', 'Drug B 200mg BID'). Confidential in blinded trials. Complements treatment_arm_code for reporting and analysis.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2); CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Date on which the subject's treatment assignment was unblinded. Populated only when blinding_status = unblinded. Emergency unblinding events must be documented per GCP. Null for blinded or open-label subjects.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Blinding; EMA Guideline on Blinding

description: Coded reason for the unblinding of the subject's treatment assignment. Emergency unblinding (e.g., for medical management of a Serious Adverse Event) must be documented and reported per GCP and pharmacovigilance requirements. Null for blinded or open-label subjects.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.13; ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on Blinding

description: Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified in the source system. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 8; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data

description: Date on which the subject withdrew from the trial or was discontinued. Populated only when enrollment_status = withdrawn. Used for survival analysis (censoring) and dropout rate reporting.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC CDASH Standard

description: Coded or free-text reason for subject withdrawal or discontinuation from the trial (e.g., adverse event, consent withdrawal, lost to follow-up, protocol deviation, investigator decision). Null for active or completed subjects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adverse_event|consent_withdrawal|lost_to_follow_up|protocol_deviation|investigator_decision|sponsor_decision|death|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E9; CDISC CDASH Standard; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each adverse event record reported by a subject during a clinical trial. Primary key for this transactional entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: SAE processing triggers vendor invoices for emergency labs, imaging, hospitalization, and specialist consultations. Required for trial cost reconciliation, safety budget tracking, and sponsor financia

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Adverse event surveillance requires brand-level aggregation for pharmacovigilance, periodic safety update reports (PSURs), label updates, and commercial risk assessment. Essential for post-marketing s

Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Adverse events are reported for enrolled subjects and must be analyzed by treatment arm for safety assessment. Linking trial_adverse_event to enrollment enables treatment arm stratification of AE data

Foreign key linking to clinical.crf_form. Business justification: Adverse events are captured via specific CRF forms (AE CRF) in the EDC system. Linking trial_adverse_event to crf_form enables traceability to the data collection instrument, supports source data veri

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.icsr. Business justification: EDC-to-safety reconciliation (ICH E2B, FDA 21 CFR 312.32) requires linking each trial AE to its corresponding ICSR in the PV system. argus_case_reference is a denormalized text reference to the ICSR t

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: SUSAR and SAE causality assessments require identifying the exact inventory lot dispensed to the subject. ICH E6 GCP and pharmacovigilance regulations mandate lot-level traceability for adverse events

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Adverse events require causality assessment to specific investigational products. While concomitant_medication field exists, there is no structured link to the IMP that may have caused the AE. This FK
Reference to the investigational site where the adverse event was reported and the subject is enrolled.

Foreign key linking to product.labeling. Business justification: Unexpected SAEs and SUSARs trigger label update workflows — the AE must be assessed against the current approved labeling to determine if it is 'unexpected'. Pharmacovigilance and regulatory affairs r

Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: Adverse events are often reported by treating physicians who are not the principal investigator. Regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA) require traceability of the reporting healthcare professional for cau

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: AE causality assessment requires product identification. Mandatory for pharmacovigilance (21 CFR 312.32), PSUR/PBRER preparation, product labeling updates, and regulatory safety reporting. Critical fo

Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance reconciliation requires linking trial CRF-captured AEs (trial_adverse_event) to patient safety database records (patient.adverse_event) for SUSAR/SAE reporting, signal detection, and
Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event. Links to the subject/patient master record in the CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS

description: Foreign key linking to quality.complaint. Business justification: Product complaints reported during clinical trials (packaging defects, appearance issues, administration problems) are tracked in quality complaint system per 21 CFR 820.198. Enables trend analysis an

Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Adverse event reporting criteria, grading scales (CTCAE), and expectedness assessments are defined in the protocol. Linking AE to protocol version enables correct application of protocol-defined AE as

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_adverse_reaction. Business justification: EDC-safety reconciliation (GVP Module VI) requires matching each trial AE verbatim term to its MedDRA-coded pv_adverse_reaction. meddra_preferred_term, meddra_pt_code, meddra_system_organ_class on tri

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Serious unexpected adverse events (SUSARs) trigger mandatory expedited regulatory reporting obligations. Pharmacovigilance teams must track which AEs resulted in expedited safety reports (15-day alert

Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: Regulatory AE reporting (MedWatch, EudraVigilance) requires identifying the reporting investigator by role, not just the HCP master. This FK enables investigator-level safety signal tracking, GCP comp
Reference to the clinical trial (study) in which the adverse event was reported. Links to the trial protocol master record.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: Adverse events are typically reported during specific patient visits. Linking AE to visit provides temporal and operational context for when the event was observed and documented. The onset_date captu

Action taken with the investigational medicinal product (IMP) in response to the adverse event (e.g., Dose not changed, Dose reduced, Drug interrupted, Drug withdrawn, Not applicable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Dose not changed|Dose reduced|Drug interrupted|Drug withdrawn|Not applicable|Unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Externally-known, human-readable identifier for the adverse event as assigned in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Used for cross-referencing in CRFs, safety narratives, and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Regulatory reporting classification of the adverse event: AE (non-serious), SAE (Serious Adverse Event), SUSAR (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction), or AESI (Adverse Event of Special Interest). Drives reporting timelines and workflows.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on Safety Reporting

description: Descriptive severity classification of the adverse event as recorded in the CRF. Corresponds to CTCAE grade but expressed as a categorical label for reporting and filtering.
reference: ICH E2A; NCI CTCAE v5.0; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Current lifecycle status of the adverse event, indicating whether it is still ongoing, has resolved, resolved with residual effects, or resulted in death.
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The exact verbatim term as reported by the investigator or subject in the CRF prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for audit and coding reconciliation purposes.
reference: ICH E2A; MedDRA; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

business_glossary_term: Adverse Event of Special Interest (AESI) Flag
description: Indicates whether the adverse event has been designated as an Adverse Event of Special Interest (AESI) per the trial protocol or regulatory guidance, requiring enhanced monitoring and reporting.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA Guidance on AESI; EMA Guideline on Safety Reporting

description: Investigator's assessment of the causal relationship between the adverse event and the investigational medicinal product (IMP). Used in safety reporting and benefit-risk evaluation.
reference: ICH E2A; WHO Causality Assessment Criteria; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Name or description of any concomitant medication administered to treat or manage the adverse event. Free-text field as captured in the CRF.
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; ICH E6(R2)

description: Timestamp when the adverse event record was first created in the EDC or source system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade
description: Severity grade of the adverse event per NCI CTCAE grading scale (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Mandatory for oncology trials and widely used across therapeutic areas.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E2A; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

description: Result of withdrawing or reducing the investigational product dose in response to the adverse event. Positive dechallenge (AE improved on withdrawal) supports causal relationship.
reference: ICH E2A; WHO Causality Assessment Criteria

description: The regulatory deadline by which the SAE or SUSAR must be reported to the relevant health authority (e.g., FDA 7-day or 15-day, EMA 7-day or 15-day). Calculated from sponsor_aware_date.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on SUSAR Reporting

description: The actual date on which the SAE or SUSAR was submitted to the health authority. Used to assess compliance with regulatory reporting timelines.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on SUSAR Reporting; FDA

description: The date on which the adverse event was first reported by the investigator or site to the sponsor. Day 0 for regulatory reporting clock calculations (7-day/15-day timelines).
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on Safety Reporting

description: Indicates whether the adverse event meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition).
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on Safety Reporting

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), triggering expedited 7-day or 15-day regulatory reporting obligations to health authorities.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on SUSAR Reporting; Directive 2001/20/EC

description: Indicates whether the adverse event is unexpected — i.e., not listed in the current Investigator's Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI). A serious, unexpected, related AE constitutes a SUSAR requiring expedited reporting.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on SUSAR Reporting

business_glossary_term: AE Led to Study Discontinuation Flag
description: Indicates whether the adverse event led to the subject's permanent discontinuation from the clinical trial. Critical for ITT and safety analysis populations.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Current status of the medical monitor's review of the adverse event record. Tracks the safety review workflow from initial entry through medical sign-off.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Medidata Rave EDC; Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS

description: Name or identifier of the sponsor medical monitor or physician who reviewed and assessed the adverse event record.
Free-text medical narrative describing the adverse event, its clinical course, management, and outcome. Used in ICSR submissions and clinical study reports (CSR).
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety / Oracle AERS; FDA MedWatch

description: The date on which the adverse event first began or was first observed by the subject or investigator. Principal real-world event date for the AE lifecycle.
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The final or current clinical outcome of the adverse event as assessed by the investigator. Used in ICSR generation and aggregate safety reporting.
reference: ICH E2A; Argus Safety / Oracle AERS; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA

description: The subject key as assigned in Medidata Rave EDC, used to cross-reference the adverse event record back to the source EDC system for data reconciliation and audit trail purposes.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 11

description: Result of re-administering the investigational product after the adverse event resolved, to assess causality. Positive rechallenge strengthens causal relationship.
reference: ICH E2A; WHO Causality Assessment Criteria

description: The date on which the adverse event resolved or returned to baseline. Null if the event is ongoing at the time of data cut.
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The specific ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by this SAE (e.g., Death, Life-threatening, Hospitalization required, Persistent disability, Congenital anomaly, Medically significant). Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Death|Life-threatening|Hospitalization required|Persistent disability|Congenital anomaly|Medically significant|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on Safety Reporting

description: The date on which the sponsor first became aware of the serious adverse event. Starts the regulatory reporting clock for SAE/SUSAR expedited reporting to health authorities.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on Safety Reporting; 21 CFR Part 314.81

business_glossary_term: Concomitant Treatment Given for AE Flag
description: Indicates whether concomitant medication or other treatment was administered to manage or treat the adverse event.
reference: ICH E2A; Medidata Rave EDC; ICH E6(R2)

description: Timestamp when the adverse event record was most recently modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GDPR Article 5

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each patient visit record within the clinical trial data product on the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice (GCP); 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.enrollment. Business justification: Patient visits occur within the context of a specific enrollment. While visit already has participant_id, trial_id, and site_id, linking to enrollment provides the complete enrollment context includin

Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: At each dispensing visit, the IRT system fulfills a clinical supply order. Linking visit to clinical_supply_order supports per-visit IMP dispensation accountability logs required under GCP and IMP acc
Reference to the investigational site where the visit took place. Links to the site master record in CTMS (Oracle/Siebel).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Reference to the principal investigator or sub-investigator who conducted or supervised this visit. Links to the investigator master record in CTMS (Oracle/Siebel).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4; 21 CFR Part 312.62; FDA Form 1572

description: Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who attended this visit. Links to the subject/patient master record in the EDC (Medidata Rave) and CTMS (Oracle/Siebel).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164

description: Reference to the clinical trial protocol version that governs the schedule of assessments for this visit. Links to the protocol master record.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E8; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the clinical study (trial) under which this visit was conducted. Links to the study master record in CTMS.
The date on which the visit actually occurred as recorded in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Corresponds to the CDISC CDASH VISDAT field. Null if the visit was missed or cancelled.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH VISDAT; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62

description: Indicates whether one or more Adverse Events (AEs) were reported at or since the previous visit (True) or not (False). Triggers downstream pharmacovigilance workflows in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: The name of the treatment arm or study arm to which the subject was assigned at the time of this visit (e.g., 'Drug A 100mg', 'Placebo', 'Drug A + Drug B Combination'). Sourced from CTMS and EDC randomization records.
reference: CDISC SDTM v3.3 TA Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E9

description: Indicates the blinding status of the subject and/or investigator at the time of this visit. Unblinded records are restricted to authorized personnel only and are critical for data integrity in double-blind trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.7; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Guideline on Blinding

description: The percentage of protocol-required assessments and procedures that were completed at this visit (0.00–100.00). Supports partial visit analysis and data completeness reporting for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the subject's concomitant medication list was reviewed and updated at this visit (True) or not (False). Supports safety and drug-drug interaction analysis.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH CM Domain; 21 CFR Part 312

description: The date and time when this visit record was first created in the source EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 5

description: The date and time when the visit data was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave) by the site staff. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Medidata Rave EDC

description: The date and time when the visit record was locked in the EDC (Medidata Rave), preventing further data entry or modification without an authorized unlock. Null if the record has not yet been locked.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC

description: The study day number calculated from the subject's reference date (typically first dose date or randomization date) to the actual visit date. Corresponds to the CDISC VISITDY variable. Negative values indicate pre-dose visits.
reference: CDISC SDTM v3.3 VISITDY; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Classification of the protocol deviation severity at this visit: major (could affect subject safety or data integrity), minor (unlikely to affect safety or data integrity), or administrative (procedural/documentation only).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations (2023); EMA GCP Inspections

description: Free-text description of the protocol deviation(s) noted at this visit, as recorded by the site in the EDC or CTMS. Null when deviation_flag is False.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations

description: Indicates whether one or more protocol deviations were recorded for this visit (True) or not (False). A True value triggers linkage to the protocol deviation register in the CTMS or eTMF (Veeva Vault).
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.5; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Protocol Deviations

description: The reason the subject discontinued the study at or before this visit, if applicable. Null for non-termination visits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adverse_event|withdrawal_of_consent|lost_to_follow_up|protocol_violation|sponsor_decision|death|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH DS Domain; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E9

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Capture (EDC) Visit Object Identifier (OID)
description: The unique object identifier (OID) assigned to this visit record within the Medidata Rave EDC system. Used for traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the source EDC system.
reference: Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC ODM v1.3; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The trial epoch during which this visit occurred, as defined in the CDISC SDTM Trial Design model (e.g., Screening, Run-In, Treatment, Follow-Up, Post-Study). Supports epoch-level analysis.
reference: CDISC SDTM v3.3 TE Domain; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether the subject's current informed consent form (ICF) was verified as valid and current at the time of this visit (True) or not (False). Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 4.8; 21 CFR Part 50; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC; GDPR Article 7

description: The date on which the visit was scheduled to occur per the protocol Schedule of Assessments and the subject's randomization or enrollment anchor date.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH VISDAT; 21 CFR Part 312

description: The number of data queries (discrepancies) currently open against this visit record in the EDC (Medidata Rave). A non-zero value indicates outstanding data cleaning actions required before database lock.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18; Medidata Rave EDC; CDISC Data Management

description: The unique randomization number assigned to the subject at the time of randomization, as recorded in the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) system. Confidential as it may reveal treatment assignment in blinded trials.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312; CDISC CDASH RNDM

description: Indicates whether one or more Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) were reported at this visit (True) or not (False). SAEs require expedited reporting to health authorities per ICH E2A and 21 CFR Part 312.32.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) has been completed for this visit by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) or monitor (True) or not (False). Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.18.3; 21 CFR Part 312.62; EMA GCP Directive 2005/28/EC

description: The date and time when this visit record was last modified in the source EDC system (Medidata Rave) or during Silver Layer processing. Supports audit trail and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; GDPR Article 5

description: Human-readable label for the visit as defined in the protocol Schedule of Assessments (e.g., 'Screening', 'Cycle 1 Day 1', 'Week 12 Follow-Up', 'End of Study'). Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC visit folder name.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH v2.0; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Sequential numeric identifier for the visit as defined in the protocol Schedule of Assessments (SoA). Unscheduled visits may carry a non-integer or supplemental numbering convention captured separately.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH v2.0; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Current lifecycle status of the visit record indicating whether the visit was completed, missed, partially completed, cancelled, or rescheduled. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC and CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH v2.0; Medidata Rave EDC

description: Classification of the visit according to its role in the trial timeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screening|baseline|treatment|follow_up|end_of_study|unscheduled|early_termination — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CDISC CDASH v2.0; 21 CFR Part 312

description: The number of days before the planned visit date that defines the lower boundary of the acceptable visit window per the protocol. Used to assess visit window compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312

description: The number of days after the planned visit date that defines the upper boundary of the acceptable visit window per the protocol. Used to assess visit window compliance.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Indicates whether the actual visit date fell within the protocol-defined visit window (True) or outside it (False). Derived at ingestion from planned_date, actual_date, window_lower_day, and window_upper_day; stored for analytical efficiency.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH E9; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each versioned clinical trial protocol record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the protocol data product.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Protocol design targets specific brand indications and label claims. Enables strategic alignment between clinical development programs and commercial planning, supports evidence generation strategy, a

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Protocol defines the specific indication being studied; IND/CTA submissions and protocol amendments formally reference the indication record. Plain 'indication' text column denormalizes the product.in

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Protocols drive R&D cost tracking via internal orders for trial execution budgets (personnel, materials, CRO costs). Standard pharma cost accounting requires protocol-level cost center assignment for

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Co-development and in-licensing agreements govern specific protocol conduct, endpoints, and data rights. In pharma partnerships, a licensee may run a protocol under a distinct licensing agreement from

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Protocols define study design for specific medicinal products. Required for IND/CTA submissions, regulatory dossiers (eCTD Module 5), and ensuring protocol design aligns with product CMC characteristi

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Novel protocol designs (adaptive trials, biomarker-driven selection, dosing regimens) may constitute patentable method-of-treatment inventions. Linking supports IP strategy, competitive intelligence,

Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_approval_commitment. Business justification: A specific protocol version fulfills a post-approval commitment study requirement. Regulatory teams must link the protocol to the PAC to report fulfillment status to FDA/EMA, confirm the study design

Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: Clinical protocols are authored against a specific discovery project. IND/CTA filings and regulatory submissions require tracing the protocol to its originating discovery project for portfolio governa

Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Protocols are written to support specific regulatory applications (IND, NDA, BLA). Regulatory teams must track which protocols support which applications for submission planning, health authority meet

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Clinical protocols must reference the approved IMP quality specification to define acceptable product parameters (potency, purity, storage) per IMPD/IND requirements. This link supports regulatory sub

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Protocol amendments must be submitted to health authorities (FDA IND amendment, EMA CTA amendment). Regulatory operations teams track which submission corresponds to each protocol version for amendmen

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Protocol design, endpoint selection, and statistical methodology are TA-specific. Plain 'therapeutic_area' text column denormalizes the master. Medical affairs and regulatory teams filter protocols by
Reference to the parent clinical trial to which this protocol version belongs. Links the protocol to the overarching study record in the CTMS.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS

description: Ratio of subjects assigned to each treatment arm (e.g., 1:1, 2:1, 1:1:1). Directly impacts sample size calculations and statistical power. Documented in the randomization section of the protocol.

Sequential integer counter for protocol amendments. Zero (0) indicates the original protocol; each subsequent amendment increments by one. Supports amendment history tracking and regulatory submission traceability.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; FDA Guidance on Protocol Amendments; 21 CFR 312.30

description: Documented justification for protocol changes introduced in this amendment version. Required by regulatory agencies to assess the impact of changes on subject safety, data integrity, and study validity. Null for original protocol (amendment_number = 0).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; 21 CFR 312.30; EMA Guideline on Protocol Amendments

description: Classification of the amendment's regulatory significance. 'Substantial' amendments require prior regulatory authority approval; 'non_substantial' require notification only; 'administrative' are minor corrections. Determines regulatory submission pathway.
reference: EU Clinical Trial Regulation 536/2014; EMA Guideline on Substantial Modifications; 21 CFR 312.30

description: Date on which the sponsor's internal governance (e.g., Clinical Development Committee) formally approved this protocol version for implementation. Distinct from IRB/IEC approval; triggers eTMF filing in Veeva Vault.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.11; Veeva Vault eTMF; SOPs

description: Level of masking applied to the trial to prevent bias. Double-blind masks both subject and investigator; open-label has no masking. Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory acceptance of efficacy data.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.7; ICH E9(R1); 21 CFR 312

description: National Clinical Trial (NCT) identifier assigned by ClinicalTrials.gov upon trial registration. Mandatory for FDA-regulated trials under FDAAA 801 and required for publication in peer-reviewed journals per ICMJE policy.
reference: FDAAA 801; ICMJE Clinical Trial Registration Policy; WHO ICTRP

description: Timestamp when this protocol record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; GDPR Article 5

description: Structural design of the clinical trial. Determines how treatment arms are organized and how subjects are allocated. Informs statistical analysis plan and regulatory review strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parallel|crossover|factorial|single_arm|basket|umbrella|platform — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E8(R1); ICH E9(R1); FDA Adaptive Design Guidance

description: Description of the investigational product dose, route of administration, frequency, and duration for each treatment arm (e.g., Drug X 200mg IV Q3W for 12 cycles). Directly linked to CMC and IND/NDA/BLA submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.6; ICH M4E (CTD Module 5); 21 CFR 312.23

description: Date from which this protocol version is operationally effective at investigational sites. For amendments, this is the date sites must implement the changes. Drives site notification and training requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; 21 CFR 312.30; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation

description: European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) registration number assigned by EMA for clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA. Required for MAA submissions and EU Clinical Trial Regulation compliance.
reference: EU Clinical Trial Regulation 536/2014; EMA EudraCT Database; EMA GCP Guideline

description: Conditions or characteristics that disqualify a subject from enrollment (e.g., prior therapy, comorbidities, contraindications). Complements inclusion criteria to define the eligible study population.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; 21 CFR 312.23; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation

description: Hypothesis-generating objectives not powering the study, such as biomarker analyses, translational research endpoints, or health economics and outcomes research (HEOR) assessments. Supports future study design and RWE generation.

Boolean flag indicating whether this protocol version has been formally attested as compliant with ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice guidelines. Required for regulatory submissions and audit readiness.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR 312; EMA GCP Guideline; PIC/S GCP Guide

description: Eligibility criteria that subjects must meet to be enrolled in the trial (e.g., age range, diagnosis, performance status, biomarker positivity). Defines the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) population. Stored as structured free text per protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.5; 21 CFR 312.23; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation

description: FDA-assigned Investigational New Drug (IND) application number under which this protocol is filed. Links the protocol to the regulatory submission record. Required for US clinical trial conduct under 21 CFR Part 312.
reference: 21 CFR 312.23; FDA IND Application; eCTD Module 5

description: Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) granted approval for this protocol version. Milestone date for site activation and GCP compliance documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3.1; 21 CFR 56.109; EU Clinical Trial Regulation 536/2014

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board (IRB) / Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) Approval Reference
description: Reference number or identifier of the IRB/IEC approval for this protocol version. Required for GCP compliance and site activation. Tracked in Veeva Vault eTMF under the Trial Master File structure.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 3; 21 CFR 56; EU Clinical Trial Regulation 536/2014

value_regex: Phase I|Phase I/II|Phase II|Phase II/III|Phase III|Phase IV
description: Regulatory development phase of the clinical trial as defined by ICH and FDA guidelines. Determines the study objectives, patient population size, and regulatory pathway (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA).
reference: ICH E8(R1); 21 CFR 312.21; FDA Guidance for Industry

description: Total number of subjects planned for enrollment as determined by the sample size justification in the protocol. Based on statistical power calculations for the primary endpoint. Drives site activation and enrollment planning.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.5; ICH E6(R2) Section 6.9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: The principal clinical outcome measure used to evaluate the primary objective (e.g., Overall Survival (OS), Progression-Free Survival (PFS), Objective Response Rate (ORR)). Drives sample size calculation and statistical analysis plan.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: The principal scientific and clinical objective of the trial as stated in the protocol. Defines the primary endpoint and the key question the study is designed to answer (e.g., demonstrate superiority in Overall Survival).
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.2; ICH E9(R1) Statistical Principles

description: Sponsor-assigned unique protocol identifier used across all regulatory submissions, eTMF filings, and CTMS records. Externally known identifier referenced in IND/NDA/BLA/MAA dossiers.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6; 21 CFR 312.23; Veeva Vault eTMF

description: Current lifecycle state of this protocol version. 'draft' indicates work-in-progress; 'under_review' indicates IRB/IEC or sponsor review; 'approved' indicates fully authorized for use; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer amendment; 'retired' indicates withdrawn.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Veeva Vault QualityDocs Lifecycle States

description: Method used to randomly allocate subjects to treatment arms. Stratified randomization accounts for prognostic factors; adaptive randomization adjusts allocation ratios based on accumulating data. Documented in the protocol and statistical analysis plan.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) Section 6.7; FDA Guidance on Randomization

description: Narrative description of the statistical assumptions and power calculations supporting the planned sample size, including assumed effect size, alpha level, power, and dropout rate. Required by ICH E9(R1) and regulatory agencies.
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 3.5; FDA Statistical Guidance; EMA Guideline on Clinical Trials

description: Document identifier or version reference for the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) associated with this protocol version. The SAP details the pre-specified statistical methods for analyzing primary and secondary endpoints per ICH E9(R1).
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) Section 6.9; FDA Statistical Guidance

description: Abbreviated or lay-language title of the protocol used in operational communications, CTMS displays, and site-facing materials. Distinct from the full regulatory title.
reference: ICH E6(R2); Oracle Clinical / Siebel CTMS

description: Clinical or demographic variables used to stratify randomization to ensure balanced treatment arms (e.g., disease stage, ECOG performance status, geographic region, biomarker status). Stored as free text per protocol specification.
reference: ICH E9(R1); FDA Guidance on Stratified Randomization

description: Broad classification of the study design indicating whether the trial involves an active intervention, passive observation, or special access program. Aligns with ClinicalTrials.gov and EudraCT registration categories.
reference: ICH E8(R1); ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; EudraCT

description: Full official title of the clinical trial protocol as it appears on the protocol document and regulatory submissions. Typically includes the investigational product name, indication, and study design descriptor.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this protocol record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity. Enables change detection in the Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; GDPR Article 5

description: Document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF where the authoritative protocol document is stored. Enables direct traceability from the data product record to the source document in the electronic Trial Master File.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8; Veeva Vault eTMF; TMF Reference Model

description: Sequential version identifier for this protocol document (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 2.1). Each approved amendment increments the version. Used to distinguish original protocol from subsequent amendments.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.1; Veeva Vault eTMF Document Versioning

description: High-level description of the Schedule of Assessments (SoA) including number of study visits, key assessment windows, and follow-up duration. Detailed SoA is maintained in the protocol appendix and Medidata Rave EDC configuration.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 6.4; Medidata Rave EDC; CTMS Visit Schedule

description: Primary key for the site_activation association
Foreign key linking to the investigational site participating in this trial activation
Foreign key linking to the clinical trial for which this site has been activated
Date on which the site close-out visit was completed and the site was formally closed for this specific trial. The same site may have different closure dates across different trials.
Actual number of subjects who have been randomized and enrolled at this site for this specific trial. Per-trial-per-site metric that cannot reside on the site master record.

Planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site for this specific trial, as agreed between the sponsor and site. Belongs to the site-trial pairing, not the site master, because the same site may have different enrollment targets across different trials.

Reference identifier for the site-level folder in the Veeva Vault eTMF system for this specific trial. Each trial generates a separate eTMF site folder per site, making this a site-trial-level reference.
reference: Veeva Vault eTMF; ICH E6(R2) Section 8

description: Actual date on which the first patient first visit occurred at this site for this trial. Used to calculate FPFV variance against target for operational reporting.
Planned date for the First Patient First Visit (FPFV) at this site for this trial. A key activation milestone specific to the site-trial combination.
Current GCP inspection status of this site for this specific trial by a regulatory authority. Inspection outcomes are trial-specific and must be tracked at the site-trial level.
Current operational lifecycle status of this site within this specific trial. A site may be actively enrolling in one trial while on-hold in another, confirming this is a site-trial-level attribute.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; CTMS industry standard

description: Date on which the current IRB/IEC approval for this trial protocol at this site expires. Renewal is required per trial, making this a site-trial-level attribute.
Official approval reference number issued by the IRB/IEC for this specific trial protocol at this site. Each trial requires a separate IRB/IEC approval at each site.
Name of the Institutional Review Board or Independent Ethics Committee that approved this trial at this site. May differ by trial if a site uses different ethics committees for different protocols.
Date of the most recent GCP inspection at this site for this trial. Trial-specific inspection history belongs to the site activation record.
Actual date of the Last Patient Last Visit at this site for this trial, marking the end of active enrollment and data collection at this site.
Number of subjects screened but failing eligibility criteria at this site for this specific trial. Varies by trial protocol and site population.
Date on which the sponsor formally selected this site for participation in this specific trial. Marks the beginning of the site activation lifecycle for this site-trial pairing.
Date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was conducted by the CRA for this site-trial combination. A mandatory GCP milestone before first patient enrollment.
Unique surrogate identifier for each clinical trial (study) record in the Pharmaceuticals data lakehouse. Primary key for the trial master record.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: Trials directly inform market access strategies for the investigational product/indication. Access teams reference trial design, endpoints, and results for HTA submissions, payer negotiations, and val

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Phase IV and post-approval confirmatory trials are mandated by a specific regulatory approval. Regulatory and clinical teams must link the trial to the approval that required it for post-approval life

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Clinical trials test specific branded products. Links trial portfolio to commercial brand for lifecycle planning, regulatory submission tracking, launch readiness, and clinical-commercial strategic al

Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: R&D budget planning and variance reporting require linking each clinical trial to its approved finance budget. Pharma finance controllers track trial spend vs. budget for capitalization decisions, por

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: CROs are registered business partners in pharma masterdata for vendor qualification, contract management, and payment processing. cro_name is a denormalized plain-text attribute; replacing it with a F

Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: Commercial supply planning uses Phase 3 trial enrollment forecasts and launch timelines to model post-approval demand. S&OP process requires linking trial data to demand planning for manufacturing cap

Foreign key linking to regulatory.designation. Business justification: Breakthrough therapy, orphan drug, fast-track, and PRIME designations directly govern trial design requirements, enrollment criteria, and FDA/EMA interaction frequency. Regulatory and clinical teams m

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Clinical trials should link to finance internal orders for trial budget tracking and cost allocation. This enables accurate R&D capitalization and trial cost management across the enterprise.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: The specific IND or CTA submission that authorized a clinical trial is a named regulatory operations record. Regulatory affairs teams must trace which submission enabled each trial for inspection read

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Trials are designed and registered for a specific formal indication. Regulatory submissions (IND, CTA), HTA dossiers, and clinical development plans require linking trial records to the product.indica

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: In-licensed compounds require tracking which licensing agreement governs each trial to manage milestone payments, royalty obligations, territory restrictions, and diligence requirements. Essential for

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Trials test specific medicinal products. Essential for regulatory submissions (CSRs, CTD Module 5), product lifecycle tracking, and linking clinical evidence to marketed products. Core relationship in

Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Portfolio reviews, competitive intelligence, and regulatory submissions require linking trials to their molecular targets. Essential for target validation reporting, strategic planning, and demonstrat

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Trials are owned by specific R&D or clinical operations org units for budget allocation, headcount reporting, and internal governance. Pharma domain experts expect every trial to be assigned to a spon

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: IP lifecycle planning and LOE (loss of exclusivity) analysis requires tracking which patent family covers each clinical trial program. IP teams use this to assess portfolio protection across jurisdict

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Clinical trials test investigational products covered by patents. Linking enables regulatory submissions (orange book listings), freedom-to-operate assessments, lifecycle management, and patent expiry

Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_approval_commitment. Business justification: FDA and EMA post-approval commitments (PMCs/PMRs) mandate specific clinical trials. Regulatory operations teams track which trial fulfills which PAC for agency status reporting. The post_approval_comm

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Trials have primary regulatory jurisdiction countries. Required for regulatory authority mapping, submission planning (FDA, EMA, PMDA), country-specific compliance requirements, and trial registry sel

Foreign key linking to discovery.discovery_project. Business justification: Stage-gate reviews, portfolio management, and budget tracking require linking clinical trials back to originating discovery projects. Essential for ROI analysis, resource allocation decisions, and int

Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Clinical trials must link to regulatory applications (IND) to track regulatory authorization. This is mandatory for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness. No visible redundant columns to

Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: In-licensed compounds under royalty agreements trigger milestone-based royalty accruals upon clinical events (Phase 3 initiation, first patient dosed). Finance must link trial records to the governing

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Trials are sponsored by legal entities. Essential for financial consolidation, regulatory submissions (sponsor identification in CTD), contract management, and liability tracking. Core business relati

Foreign key linking to regulatory.strategy. Business justification: Regulatory strategy documents define the clinical development pathway, target indication, and filing sequence. Regulatory affairs teams must trace which trials execute against which strategy for globa

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Trials are organized by therapeutic area for portfolio management, resource allocation, and regulatory strategy. Plain 'therapeutic_area' text column denormalizes the product.therapeutic_area master.

Actual number of patients enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) as tracked in Oracle/Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave EDC. Updated in real-time during trial execution. Used for enrollment rate monitoring and milestone tracking.
reference: ICH E6(R2); ClinicalTrials.gov results reporting; 21 CFR 312.33

business_glossary_term: Actual Primary Completion Date (Last Patient Last Visit)
description: Actual date on which the last patient completed the primary endpoint assessment (LPLV). Recorded in CTMS and Medidata Rave. Triggers database lock procedures and Clinical Study Report (CSR) preparation.
reference: FDAAA 801; ICH E3 Structure and Content of CSR; 21 CFR 312.33

business_glossary_term: Actual Trial Start Date (First Patient First Visit)
description: Actual date on which the first patient was enrolled and received the first dose (FPFV). Recorded in Oracle/Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave. Compared against planned_start_date to calculate enrollment delay variance.
reference: ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR 312.33 Annual Report; ClinicalTrials.gov results reporting

description: Statistical analysis population used for the primary endpoint analysis. Intent-to-Treat (ITT) is the regulatory gold standard for confirmatory trials. Per Protocol (PP) is used as a sensitivity analysis. Defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).
reference: ICH E9(R1) Section 5.2; FDA Guidance on ITT Principle; EMA Reflection Paper on Missing Data
