description: Level of blinding applied to the trial to minimize bias. Double-blind is the gold standard for Phase 3 confirmatory trials. Open-label is common in oncology and rare disease studies. Impacts GCP compliance requirements and unblinding procedures.
reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E6(R2) Section 4.7; 21 CFR 312.23

description: Type of control arm used in the trial. Placebo-controlled is standard for regulatory approval; active comparator is required when placebo is unethical. Historical control is used in rare disease single-arm studies.
reference: ICH E10 Choice of Control Group; 21 CFR 314.126; EMA Guideline on Choice of Non-Inferiority Margin

description: Timestamp when the trial master record was first created in the clinical data lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and data lineage purposes. Populated automatically on insert.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); ICH E6(R2) Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Date on which the Clinical Study Report (CSR) was finalized and approved per ICH E3 guidelines. The CSR is a mandatory component of NDA/BLA/MAA regulatory submissions. Tracked in Veeva Vault eTMF.
reference: ICH E3 Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports; 21 CFR 314.50; EMA Module 5 CTD

description: Regulatory authority-assigned Clinical Trial Authorisation reference number for trials conducted in EU/EEA, UK (MHRA), or other ex-US jurisdictions. Supports multi-regional trial tracking.
reference: EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014; MHRA Clinical Trials Regulations; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date on which the clinical trial database was locked in Medidata Rave EDC following data cleaning, query resolution, and medical review sign-off. Marks the point after which no further data changes are permitted without formal unblinding procedures. Critical milestone for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; FDA Guidance on Electronic Source Data; 21 CFR Part 11

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Capture (EDC) System Study Identifier

reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) Section 5.5; Medidata Rave Study Configuration

business_glossary_term: Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) Vault Document ID

reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 8 (Essential Documents); TMF Reference Model; 21 CFR Part 11

business_glossary_term: European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) Number

description: Current GCP compliance status of the trial based on internal quality oversight, audit findings, and regulatory inspection outcomes. Critical findings may trigger clinical holds. Tracked in MasterControl/TrackWise QMS and Veeva Vault.

description: FDA-assigned IND application number under which this trial is conducted. Links the trial to the regulatory submission record. Required for all Phase I–III trials in the US. May reference multiple INDs for combination studies.

Planned date for the pre-specified interim efficacy or futility analysis reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB). Relevant for adaptive trials and trials with early stopping rules. Defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan.

business_glossary_term: Institutional Review Board (IRB) / Ethics Committee Approval Status

description: Indicates whether the trial employs a pre-specified adaptive design allowing modifications to the trial or statistical procedures based on interim data analysis. Requires additional FDA/EMA guidance compliance per ICH E20.

description: Indicates whether the trial includes a pediatric population (patients under 18 years). Triggers Pediatric Study Plan (PIP/PSP) requirements under FDA PREA and EMA Paediatric Regulation. Impacts regulatory strategy and labeling.

description: Unique public registry identifier assigned by ClinicalTrials.gov (U.S. National Library of Medicine). Mandatory for trials subject to FDAAA 801 registration requirements. Used in regulatory submissions and public disclosure.

description: Regulatory development phase of the clinical trial per ICH and FDA/EMA classification. Phase 1/2 and 2/3 denote seamless adaptive designs. Phase 4 denotes post-marketing studies. business_glossary_term: Planned Primary Completion Date (Last Patient Last Visit)

reference: FDAAA 801; ClinicalTrials.gov Protocol Registration; ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: Planned Trial Start Date (First Patient First Visit)
description: Planned date for First Patient First Visit (FPFV) — the target date for the first patient to be enrolled and dosed. Used for milestone planning, CRO contracting, and supply chain scheduling. Tracked against actual FPFV for variance analysis.

description: The principal clinical outcome measure that the trial is powered to demonstrate (e.g., 'Overall Survival (OS)', 'Progression-Free Survival (PFS)', 'Objective Response Rate (ORR)', 'Complete Remission Rate'). reference: ICH E9(R1); ICH E8(R1); FDA Guidance on Clinical Trial Endpoints; EMA Guideline on Endpoints

description: Type of regulatory submission associated with this trial's data package. IND for US investigational studies; NDA/BLA for US marketing applications; MAA for EU; CTA for clinical trial authorisation. Links to the regulatory.submission domain. Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_term_extension. Business justification: Patent Term Extension (PTE/SPC) applications require precise regulatory review period dates derived from clinical trial milestones (IND filing, NDA approval). IP counsel directly references trial mile

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the candidate nomination record. Required for change tracking, audit compliance under 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data pipeline processing.
Unique surrogate identifier for the drug discovery research project record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for all downstream joins within the discovery domain.

Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: R&D portfolio budgeting: each discovery project has an allocated budget in finance for planning, variance reporting, and portfolio prioritization. Pharma finance controllers track project-level R&D sp

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Discovery projects are anchored to a drug substance (the active ingredient under development). Direct project→drug_substance linkage is required for CMC project tracking, regulatory strategy planning,

Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: Discovery projects require FTO clearance at stage gates (target validation, lead optimization, candidate nomination); analysis linkage supports portfolio decisions, risk assessment, and strategic plan

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Discovery projects are organized around specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level portfolio management, regulatory pathway planning, and alignment of discove

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: In SAP-based pharma R&D, a discovery project maps to a top-level internal order for cost settlement and program-level financial reporting. Child activities (HTS, SAR, synthesis) already carry internal

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Discovery projects often operate under collaboration or in-licensing agreements; linkage tracks licensed rights, diligence obligations, milestone triggers, and royalty terms for financial planning and

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Portfolio lineage tracking: connects research program to marketed medicine for ROI analysis, pipeline reporting, strategic planning. Essential for investment decisions, productivity metrics, and comme

Standardized identifier for the primary biological target (e.g., UniProt accession, HGNC gene symbol) the project is designed to modulate. Central to SAR analysis and MOA documentation in IDBS/Dotmatics ELN.
reference: UniProt; HGNC; ChEMBL target nomenclature; ICH S7A/S7B safety pharmacology

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Discovery projects are owned and managed by specific organizational units for governance, resource allocation, headcount planning, and portfolio management. Essential for matrix reporting and accounta

Foreign key linking to regulatory.strategy. Business justification: Every discovery project has a corresponding regulatory strategy defining its development pathway, designation targets, and submission sequence. Pharma portfolio governance requires tracing each discov

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Discovery projects are organized and budgeted by therapeutic area. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity is fundamental for portfolio management, strategic prioritization reporting, and TA-lev

Date on which the development candidate was formally nominated and the project transitioned to preclinical development. Nullable until nomination occurs. Used for cycle time analytics and portfolio performance measurement.
reference: ICH Q8(R2); 21 CFR 312; ISPE Drug Discovery guidelines

description: WHO ATC classification code assigned to the intended drug product based on its therapeutic target and mechanism. Used for market analysis, competitive intelligence, and regulatory classification.
reference: WHO ATC/DDD Index; EMA product classification; INN guidelines

description: Name of the primary external collaboration partner (CRO, academic institution, or partner pharma company) if external_collaboration_flag is true. Classified confidential as it reveals strategic partnership information.
reference: ICH Q10; GDPR Article 28; SOX Section 302

description: Classification of the external collaboration arrangement. Determines applicable contractual, IP, and regulatory frameworks governing data sharing and compound rights.
reference: ICH Q10; GDPR Article 28; IP licensing frameworks

description: Timestamp when the discovery project record was first created in the system of record (IDBS/Dotmatics or portfolio management system). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ (MHRA Data Integrity Guidance); ICH E6(R2)

description: Reference identifier for this discovery project in the IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics ELN system. Enables traceability from the lakehouse silver layer back to the authoritative source system for raw experimental data.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); ALCOA+ data integrity principles; IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Indicates whether the project involves external partners such as CROs, academic institutions, or other pharmaceutical companies. Triggers additional data governance, IP agreement, and confidentiality controls.
reference: ICH Q10; GDPR Article 28 (data processor agreements); 21 CFR 312

description: Indicates whether the project's studies are conducted under GLP regulations as required for regulatory submission. GLP-compliant studies are required for safety data submitted in IND applications.
reference: 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); OECD Principles of GLP; EMA GLP guidelines

description: Number of HTS campaigns executed under this discovery project. Provides a summary indicator of screening activity volume for portfolio reporting and resource utilization analysis.
reference: ISPE Drug Discovery guidelines; ICH Q8(R2)

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases 10th Revision (ICD-10) Code
description: Standardized ICD-10 disease classification code for the primary disease indication. Enables cross-domain linkage to clinical, regulatory, and medical affairs data and supports regulatory submissions.

Brief description of the IP strategy for the project, including patentability assessment, freedom-to-operate (FTO) status, and filing intentions. Classified confidential as it contains pre-filing IP strategy.
reference: USPTO; EPO; ICH Q10; SOX Section 302

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the discovery project record. Required for data lineage tracking, change control auditing, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ (MHRA Data Integrity Guidance); ICH E6(R2)

description: Scientific description of the proposed mechanism by which the drug candidate modulates the target to achieve therapeutic effect. Captured from ELN records in IDBS/Dotmatics. Critical for IND and NDA/BLA submissions.
reference: ICH M4 (eCTD); FDA IND 21 CFR 312.23; EMA scientific guidelines

description: Type of therapeutic modality being pursued in the project. Determines applicable regulatory pathway (NDA vs BLA), manufacturing approach (CMO vs CDMO), and GLP/GMP requirements. Distinguishes NCE from NBE programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|antibody|cell_therapy|gene_therapy|oligonucleotide|peptide — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR 312 (IND); EMA CHMP guidelines; ICH Q11 (biologics)

description: Current stage of the drug discovery workflow. Progresses from target identification through candidate nomination, representing the standard industry gate-based progression model. Used for portfolio stage-gate reporting and resource planning.
reference: ICH Q8(R2) Pharmaceutical Development; ISPE Drug Discovery guidelines

description: Most recent portfolio governance committee decision for the project. Recorded at each stage-gate review and drives resource reallocation and pipeline reporting.
reference: ICH Q10; ISPE Drug Discovery; SOX Section 302

description: Date of the most recent portfolio governance committee decision. Used for audit trail and decision history tracking in conjunction with portfolio_decision.

Externally-known alphanumeric project identifier assigned by the portfolio governance office (e.g., DISC-20240001). Used as the business key across IDBS/Dotmatics ELN, SAP project structures, and regulatory submissions. Aligns with the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category.
reference: ICH Q10; IDBS E-WorkBook naming conventions

description: Human-readable name of the drug discovery project (e.g., 'KRAS G12C Inhibitor Program'). Classified confidential as it may reveal pipeline strategy prior to public disclosure. Aligns with the IDENTITY_LABEL canonical category.
reference: ICH Q10; SOX Section 302 (pipeline disclosure controls)

description: Current lifecycle status of the discovery project as managed in the portfolio governance system. Drives portfolio dashboards and resource allocation decisions. Aligns with the LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category.
reference: ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System; FDA IND regulations 21 CFR 312

description: Classification of the discovery project by the nature of the entity being developed. NCE (New Chemical Entity) and NBE (New Biological Entity) drive distinct regulatory and IP strategies. Aligns with CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE canonical category.
reference: FDA PDUFA; EMA CHMP; ICH Q11; 21 CFR 314 (NDA) and 21 CFR 601 (BLA)

description: Target date by which a development candidate is expected to be nominated and handed off to preclinical development. Key milestone for pipeline planning, IND filing timelines, and portfolio forecasting.
reference: ICH Q8(R2); FDA IND 21 CFR 312; ISPE Drug Discovery guidelines

description: SAP Project System WBS element code that maps this discovery project to the financial controlling structure in SAP S/4HANA. Enables cost allocation, budget tracking, and financial reporting at the project level.
reference: SAP PS (Project System) module; SAP CO; SOX Section 302

description: Senior executive or therapeutic area head who sponsors and champions the project at the portfolio governance level. Distinct from the project leader who manages day-to-day execution.
Official date on which the discovery project was formally initiated and approved by portfolio governance. Used for project duration calculations, milestone tracking, and portfolio timeline reporting.

Portfolio governance classification indicating the strategic importance of the project (Tier 1 = highest priority, Tier 3 = exploratory). Drives resource allocation, budget prioritization, and executive reporting. Classified confidential as it reflects pipeline strategy.
reference: ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System; SOX Section 302

description: Primary biological system or organism used in discovery assays and preclinical models. Relevant for GLP study design, species selection justification in IND applications, and ADME/PK modeling.
reference: ICH S5(R3); ICH S6(R1); 21 CFR 58 (GLP); OECD GLP Principles

description: Documented reason for project termination or discontinuation (e.g., safety signal, lack of efficacy, strategic reprioritization, IP conflict). Nullable for active projects. Critical for lessons-learned analysis and portfolio retrospectives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety_signal|lack_of_efficacy|strategic_reprioritization|ip_conflict|resource_constraints|competitive_landscape|regulatory_concern — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q10; FDA IND 21 CFR 312.38 (IND withdrawal); EMA scientific advice

business_glossary_term: Absorption Distribution Metabolism Excretion (ADME) Profile ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each ADME profiling record in the discovery domain. Primary key for the adme_profile data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete ADME assay event with its own lifecycle and results.
reference: ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Reference to the compound under ADME evaluation, linking to the compound master record in the discovery compound library. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category (compound is the principal resource being profiled).

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: DMPK/ADME studies are routinely outsourced to CROs; regulatory submissions (IND) require identifying the performing laboratory as a qualified business partner. No existing CRO link on adme_profile; cr

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: ADME profiles are directly associated with drug substances for IND/NDA regulatory submissions (Module 2.6 pharmacokinetics). Regulatory affairs and CMC teams require direct adme_profile→drug_substance

Foreign key linking to discovery.lead_series. Business justification: ADME profiling is conducted specifically to support lead series optimization and progression decisions. adme_profile already has compound_id and project_id, but lacks a direct FK to the lead_series be

Foreign key linking to quality.method_validation. Business justification: ICH M3 and FDA BMV guidance require ADME data submitted in IND/NDA to reference validated bioanalytical methods. adme_profile already has submission_id; linking to method_validation enables regulatory
Reference to the drug discovery project under which this ADME profiling was conducted, enabling project-level aggregation of ADME data for compound progression decisions.

Composite numerical risk score (e.g., 0–100) summarizing overall ADME liability across solubility, permeability, metabolic stability, and DDI risk dimensions. Used for compound ranking and portfolio prioritization in discovery.
reference: ICH Q6A; internal compound scoring framework; FDA IND Guidance

description: Concentration of the compound used in the ADME assay incubation, expressed in micromolar (µM). Required for result interpretation and inter-assay comparability; standard concentrations are typically 1–10 µM.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LabWare LIMS; ICH M10

description: Calendar date on which the ADME assay panel was executed in the laboratory. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (day-level precision for assay scheduling and trending).

Unbound brain-to-plasma concentration ratio (Kp,uu,brain), the quantitative measure of CNS penetration accounting for plasma protein binding. Values >0.3 indicate meaningful CNS exposure; used in CNS drug discovery programs.
reference: FDA CNS Drug Development Guidance; EMA Guideline on Pharmacokinetics

description: Indicates whether the compound demonstrates blood-brain barrier penetration based on in vitro BBB assay (e.g., MDCK-MDR1, hCMEC/D3) or in vivo brain-to-plasma ratio. Critical for CNS-targeted and CNS-safety assessments.
reference: FDA CNS Drug Development Guidance; EMA Guideline on Pharmacokinetics; ICH S7A

description: Estimated oral bioavailability percentage (%F) derived from permeability, solubility, and metabolic stability data using predictive ADME models. Key compound progression criterion for oral drug candidates.
reference: FDA BCS Guidance; ICH M9; EMA BCS-based Biowaiver Guideline

description: Predicted in vivo hepatic clearance in mL/min/kg, scaled from in vitro microsomal intrinsic clearance using the well-stirred liver model. Used to estimate dose and dosing interval in early drug discovery.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Pharmacokinetics Guidance; EMA Guideline on Pharmacokinetics

description: Timestamp when this ADME profile record was first created in the system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records audit trail.
Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) for CYP2C9 enzyme inhibition in µM. CYP2C9 metabolizes warfarin and NSAIDs; inhibition is a critical DDI safety signal for regulatory submissions.
reference: FDA Drug Interaction Guidance (2020); EMA Guideline on Investigation of Drug Interactions; ICH M12

description: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) for CYP2D6 enzyme inhibition in µM. CYP2D6 metabolizes ~25% of marketed drugs; inhibition is a major DDI liability assessed by FDA and EMA.
reference: FDA Drug Interaction Guidance (2020); EMA Guideline on Investigation of Drug Interactions; ICH M12

description: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) for CYP3A4 enzyme inhibition in µM. CYP3A4 metabolizes ~50% of marketed drugs; inhibition predicts drug-drug interaction (DDI) liability. Key regulatory safety flag.
reference: FDA Drug Interaction Guidance (2020); EMA Guideline on Investigation of Drug Interactions; ICH M12

description: Composite boolean flag indicating whether the compound exhibits significant CYP inhibition risk (any CYP isoform IC50 below the regulatory concern threshold, typically <10 µM). Used for rapid compound triage and progression gating.
reference: FDA Drug Interaction Guidance (2020); EMA Guideline on Investigation of Drug Interactions; ICH M12

description: Ratio of basolateral-to-apical (B→A) over apical-to-basolateral (A→B) permeability in Caco-2 assay. Values >2 indicate active efflux transport (e.g., P-gp substrate), impacting oral bioavailability and CNS penetration.
reference: FDA BCS Guidance; ICH M9; EMA Guideline on Investigation of Drug Interactions

description: Reference identifier linking this ADME profile to the corresponding experiment entry in the Electronic Lab Notebook (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics), providing traceability to raw experimental data and GLP documentation.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; 21 CFR Part 11; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN

description: Predicted or measured plasma half-life of the compound in hours, derived from in vitro microsomal clearance data using allometric scaling or measured directly in in vivo PK studies. Informs dosing frequency predictions.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Pharmacokinetics Guidance; EMA Guideline on Pharmacokinetics

description: Intrinsic metabolic clearance measured in human liver microsomes (HLM), expressed as µL/min/mg protein. Used to predict in vivo hepatic clearance and half-life via well-stirred liver model. Critical for dose prediction.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Drug Metabolism Guidance; EMA Guideline on Drug Interactions

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Sample ID
description: Sample identifier assigned by the LIMS (LabWare or Thermo Fisher SampleManager) for the compound sample used in this ADME assay. Enables traceability from ADME result back to physical sample chain of custody.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LabWare LIMS; Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Categorical classification of compound metabolic stability based on microsomal half-life thresholds (e.g., low: t½ <30 min, medium: 30–60 min, high: >60 min). Used in compound progression scoring and SAR triage.
reference: ICH Q6A; FDA Drug Metabolism Guidance; internal SAR triage criteria

description: In vitro half-life of the compound in liver microsome incubation (human or rat), expressed in minutes. Derived from substrate depletion kinetics; used to classify metabolic stability as low/medium/high.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Drug Metabolism Guidance

description: Free-text field for ADME scientist observations, anomalies, assay deviations, or contextual notes relevant to interpreting the ADME profile results. Supports GLP documentation requirements.

Apparent permeability coefficient (Papp A→B) measured in Caco-2 cell monolayer assay, expressed in nm/s. Predicts intestinal absorption and oral bioavailability. Key ADME parameter for BCS classification.
reference: FDA BCS Guidance; ICH M9; EMA BCS-based Biowaiver Guideline

business_glossary_term: Parallel Artificial Membrane Permeability Assay (PAMPA) Permeability (nm/s)
description: Passive transcellular permeability measured by PAMPA assay in nm/s. Complements Caco-2 data by isolating passive diffusion component, informing SAR optimization for membrane permeability.

Indicates whether the compound is identified as a P-glycoprotein (P-gp / MDR1) efflux transporter substrate based on Caco-2 bidirectional permeability or dedicated P-gp inhibition assay. Impacts CNS penetration and oral bioavailability predictions.
reference: FDA Drug Interaction Guidance; EMA Guideline on Investigation of Drug Interactions; ICH M12

description: Percentage of compound bound to plasma proteins (albumin, alpha-1-acid glycoprotein) measured by equilibrium dialysis or ultrafiltration. Determines free fraction available for pharmacological activity and clearance.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance

description: Analytical method used to determine plasma protein binding percentage. Rapid equilibrium dialysis (RED) is the industry standard; method choice affects result interpretation and cross-study comparability.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this ADME profiling run, used in ELN (IDBS/Dotmatics) and LIMS cross-referencing. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; LabWare LIMS

description: Current workflow state of the ADME profile record from data entry through scientific review and approval. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|completed|reviewed|approved|invalidated — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; 21 CFR Part 11; MasterControl / TrackWise QMS

description: Scientific recommendation for compound progression based on the overall ADME profile assessment. Drives hit-to-lead and lead optimization decisions. Assigned by the ADME scientist upon profile review and approval.
reference: ICH Q6A; internal drug discovery stage-gate criteria; FDA IND Guidance 21 CFR Part 312

description: Intrinsic metabolic clearance measured in rat liver microsomes (RLM), expressed as µL/min/mg protein. Supports cross-species PK scaling and preclinical-to-clinical translation of metabolic stability data.
reference: ICH S7A; FDA Preclinical Safety Guidance; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Analytical method used to determine aqueous solubility, distinguishing kinetic from thermodynamic measurements which have different interpretive implications for drug development.

Measured aqueous solubility of the compound in micromolar (µM), typically from kinetic or thermodynamic solubility assay. Critical for assessing oral bioavailability and formulation feasibility. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.
reference: ICH Q6A; FDA Biopharmaceutics Classification System (BCS)

description: Biological species from which the biological matrix (microsomes, plasma, cells) was derived for this ADME assay. Enables cross-species PK comparison and allometric scaling for preclinical-to-clinical translation.
reference: ICH S7A; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; EMA Preclinical Guideline

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this ADME profile record. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.
Predicted or measured apparent volume of distribution (Vd) in L/kg, reflecting the extent of compound distribution into tissues versus plasma. Informs PK/PD modeling and dose prediction.
reference: ICH M10; FDA Pharmacokinetics Guidance; EMA Guideline on Pharmacokinetics

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key for each compound record in the discovery compound library. Primary key for the compound master data product.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: ATC classification of discovery compounds drives formulary management, reimbursement strategy, and pharmacovigilance signal detection. 'atc_code' is a denormalized reference to the masterdata ATC hier

Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Compounds (APIs) are covered by DMFs for regulatory submissions; linkage supports IND/NDA filings, tracks manufacturing site authorization, and enables CMC strategy coordination between discovery and

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Regulatory CMC requirement: IND/NDA submissions must trace marketed API back to discovery compound for chemistry documentation. Critical for candidate nomination handoff, regulatory filing preparation

Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO clearance on specific compounds is a mandatory IP process before synthesis scale-up and IND filing. IP counsel runs compound-level FTO to confirm freedom to use the chemical structure. Every pharm

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Linking compounds to the authoritative INN registry is fundamental for regulatory submissions (IND, NDA), WHO naming conventions, and pharmacovigilance reporting. 'inn' plain attribute is a denormaliz

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Compounds progressing to development/manufacturing are registered as materials in ERP for procurement, inventory management, batch tracking, and supply chain planning. Critical integration point betwe

Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: compound has a STRING field 'primary_target' which is a denormalized text reference to the compound's primary molecular target. In drug discovery, compounds are designed and optimized against specific

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_claim. Business justification: Compounds must link to patent claims to track IP protection. This is essential for pharmaceutical asset valuation and freedom-to-operate analysis. No visible redundant columns to remove.

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Compounds belong to patent families covering composition-of-matter globally; family-level tracking supports international IP strategy, prosecution coordination, and portfolio valuation for business de

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Compounds are patented chemical matter requiring direct patent linkage for IP lifecycle management, orange book listing preparation, patent term extension applications, and generic entry risk assessme

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Compounds are classified by therapeutic area for portfolio management, IP strategy, and compound library organization. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enforces consistency across discove

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Vendor qualification and approved vendor list (AVL) compliance requires linking purchased/sourced compounds to their business partner record. 'vendor_name' is denormalized; the FK enables vendor audit

Globally unique Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) registry number assigned by the American Chemical Society to uniquely identify the chemical substance. Used for regulatory submissions, procurement, and cross-system compound lookup.
reference: CAS Registry, ICH Q6A, FDA Substance Registration System (SRS)

description: Broad classification of the compound by molecular modality. Drives regulatory pathway selection (NDA vs BLA), manufacturing strategy (chemical synthesis vs biologics), and analytical method requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|peptide|adc|oligonucleotide|cell_therapy|rna_therapy|gene_therapy — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q6A (small molecules), ICH Q6B (biologics), FDA CDER/CBER classification

description: Internal working name or code name assigned to the compound during discovery (e.g., project code name such as 'ABC-1234'). Used as the primary human-readable label in ELN, LIMS, and CTMS systems before an INN or USAN is assigned.

Current lifecycle status of the compound within the discovery pipeline. 'Active' indicates the compound is under active research; 'promoted' indicates advancement to preclinical or clinical development; 'discontinued' indicates the compound has been deprioritized or terminated.
reference: ICH Q8, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics, 21 CFR Part 312

description: U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) controlled substance schedule classification for the compound (Schedule I–V or not controlled). Determines handling, storage, procurement, and regulatory reporting requirements under the Controlled Substances Act.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act (21 USC 812), 21 CFR Part 1308, DEA

description: System-generated timestamp recording when the compound record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11, ICH Q10, GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Current stage of the compound within the drug discovery pipeline. Tracks progression from target identification through hit identification, hit-to-lead, lead optimization, and candidate selection before IND filing. Used for portfolio reporting and stage-gate decision making.
reference: ICH Q8, ICH M3(R2), FDA IND Guidance 21 CFR Part 312, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Reference identifier linking this compound record to the originating experiment or notebook entry in the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Provides traceability from the compound master record back to the primary research data and synthesis records.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11, OECD GLP Principles, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

business_glossary_term: New Chemical Entity / New Biological Entity (NCE/NBE) Type
description: Regulatory classification of the compound as a New Chemical Entity (NCE), New Biological Entity (NBE), biosimilar, fixed-dose combination, or prodrug. Determines regulatory exclusivity periods, patent strategy, and submission pathway under FDA, EMA, and ICH guidelines.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.108, EMA Guideline on NCE/NBE, ICH Q8

description: Indicates whether the compound and its associated research data have been generated under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) conditions as required for regulatory submissions. GLP compliance is mandatory for safety pharmacology and toxicology studies supporting IND applications.
reference: OECD Principles of GLP, 21 CFR Part 58, EMA GLP Directive 2004/10/EC

description: Globally Harmonized System (GHS) hazard classification for the compound covering health, physical, and environmental hazards (e.g., 'Acute Toxicity Cat. 2, Carcinogen Cat. 1A'). Required for Safety Data Sheet (SDS) preparation, occupational health risk assessment, and EHS compliance.
reference: GHS (UN Purple Book), OSHA HazCom 2012, REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006, ICH Q3C

description: Number of hydrogen bond acceptors (HBA) in the compound structure. Key Lipinski Rule of Five parameter (threshold: ≤ 10 HBAs) used in drug-likeness assessment and oral bioavailability prediction during hit-to-lead and lead optimization.
reference: Lipinski Rule of Five, ICH Q6A, ADME Guidelines

description: Number of hydrogen bond donors (HBD) in the compound structure. Key Lipinski Rule of Five parameter (threshold: ≤ 5 HBDs) used in drug-likeness assessment and oral bioavailability prediction during hit-to-lead and lead optimization.
reference: Lipinski Rule of Five, ICH Q6A, ADME Guidelines

description: IUPAC International Chemical Identifier (InChI) string providing a standardized, layered representation of the compound's chemical structure. Used for cross-system compound deduplication, registry lookups, and interoperability with external databases (PubChem, ChEMBL). Classified confidential as it encodes proprietary structural IP.
reference: IUPAC InChI Trust, ICH Q6A, FDA SRS

description: Fixed-length 27-character hashed representation of the InChI string (InChIKey). Used as a compact, searchable identifier for compound lookup in external databases (PubChem, ChEMBL, CAS SciFinder) and for deduplication across compound registries.
reference: IUPAC InChI Trust, FDA SRS, PubChem

business_glossary_term: International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) Name
description: Systematic chemical name of the compound following International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) nomenclature rules. Provides an unambiguous, internationally recognized chemical identity for regulatory submissions and scientific publications.
reference: IUPAC 2013 Recommendations, ICH Q6A, FDA SRS

description: Date on which the compound record was last reviewed by a qualified scientist or data steward for accuracy, completeness, and continued relevance in the discovery portfolio. Supports data governance and periodic compound library curation activities.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11, ICH Q10, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Designation of the compound library or collection to which this compound belongs within the corporate compound library management system. Drives screening campaign design, compound availability, and resource allocation in HTS and fragment-based drug discovery programs.
reference: ISPE GAMP 5, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics, LIMS - LabWare / SampleManager

description: Logarithm of the octanol-water partition coefficient (LogP), a measure of the compound's lipophilicity. Critical SAR and ADME parameter used to predict membrane permeability, oral bioavailability, and CNS penetration. Lipinski Rule of Five threshold: LogP ≤ 5.
reference: Lipinski Rule of Five, ICH Q6A, ADME Guidelines, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Described mechanism of action (MOA) of the compound — the biochemical interaction through which the compound produces its pharmacological effect (e.g., 'selective inhibitor of EGFR tyrosine kinase'). Critical for target validation, patent claims, and regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E8, FDA Pharmacology Review Guidance, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Empirical molecular formula of the compound expressed using Hill notation (e.g., C21H30N2O5). Represents the elemental composition and is used in regulatory submissions, analytical method development, and structural verification.
reference: IUPAC Hill System, ICH Q6A, FDA SRS

description: Average molecular weight of the compound expressed in grams per mole (g/mol). Critical physicochemical property used in SAR analysis, ADME profiling, formulation development, and Lipinski Rule of Five drug-likeness assessment.
reference: ICH Q6A, Lipinski Rule of Five, IUPAC

description: Physical state of the compound at standard conditions (solid, liquid, gas, semi-solid). Determines handling procedures, formulation approach, and storage requirements. Captured in the ELN and LIMS at time of compound registration.
reference: ICH Q6A, GMP 21 CFR Part 211, LIMS - LabWare / SampleManager

description: Measured chemical purity of the compound expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00%). Determined by analytical methods such as HPLC or NMR. Critical quality attribute (CQA) for assay reliability, GLP compliance, and advancement decisions in hit-to-lead progression.
reference: ICH Q6A, GLP (OECD Principles), 21 CFR Part 58, LIMS - LabWare / SampleManager

description: Date on which the compound was formally registered in the corporate compound registry (IDBS/Dotmatics). Marks the official entry of the compound into the discovery compound library and initiates the compound lifecycle tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11, OECD GLP Principles, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Internally assigned unique registration number for the compound within the corporate compound registry (e.g., IDBS/Dotmatics compound registration system). Serves as the primary business identifier used across discovery, CMC, and regulatory workflows.
reference: ICH Q6A, 21 CFR Part 312, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Number of rotatable bonds in the compound structure. Veber rule parameter (threshold: ≤ 10) used alongside TPSA to predict oral bioavailability and molecular flexibility in SAR and lead optimization analyses.
reference: Veber Rules, ICH Q6A, ADME Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System (SMILES) Notation
description: Canonical SMILES (Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System) string representing the two-dimensional chemical structure of the compound. Used in cheminformatics, HTS data analysis, SAR modeling, and AI/ML-based drug discovery workflows. Classified confidential as it encodes proprietary structural IP.
reference: Daylight SMILES Specification, ICH Q6A, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Measured or predicted aqueous solubility of the compound expressed in micromolar (µM). Key ADME physicochemical property used in SAR analysis, formulation feasibility assessment, and drug-likeness profiling (Lipinski, Veber rules).
reference: ICH Q6A, Lipinski Rule of Five, ADME Guidelines, LIMS

description: Origin classification of the compound indicating how it was obtained. Synthesized compounds are produced in-house; purchased compounds are acquired from external vendors or CROs; natural products are derived from biological sources. Impacts IP strategy and CMC documentation.
reference: ICH Q11, FDA CMC Guidance, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Recommended storage conditions for the compound (e.g., '-20°C, under nitrogen, protected from light, desiccated'). Ensures compound stability and integrity in the compound library. Derived from stability data and referenced in CoA and compound library management SOPs.
reference: ICH Q1A (Stability Testing), ICH Q6A, GMP 21 CFR Part 211.68, LIMS

description: Topological Polar Surface Area (TPSA) of the compound expressed in square angstroms (Å²). Used to predict intestinal absorption, blood-brain barrier penetration, and oral bioavailability. Veber rule threshold: TPSA ≤ 140 Å² for oral drugs.
reference: Veber Rules, ICH Q6A, ADME Guidelines, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: System-generated timestamp recording when the compound record was last modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and enables change detection for downstream data consumers and AI/ML pipelines.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11, ICH Q10, GDPR Article 5(2)

description: United States Adopted Name (USAN) assigned by the USAN Council for the compound. Used in US regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA) and US commercial labeling. May differ slightly from INN.

Vendor-assigned catalog or product number for purchased compounds. Used for reorder, procurement traceability, and cross-referencing with vendor CoA and SDS documentation. Applicable only when source_type is 'purchased'.
reference: ICH Q10, SAP S/4HANA MM Module, GMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each compound synthesis record in the discovery domain. Primary key for the compound_synthesis data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete synthesis event with its own lifecycle, reagents, conditions, and outcomes.
reference: ICH Q11 (Development and Manufacture of Drug Substances)

description: Reference to the compound entity that was synthesized in this experiment. Links the synthesis record to the master compound registry (NCE/NBE catalogue).
reference: ICH Q11; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registry

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Synthesis activities consume reagents, labor, and equipment charged to cost centers for project costing, resource planning, and financial reporting. Essential for medicinal chemistry budget management

Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO on synthesis routes is a standard pharma IP process — IP teams assess whether a synthesis method infringes third-party process patents before scale-up. This is especially critical for API manufact

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Individual synthesis activities are charged to internal orders for cost-per-compound tracking, essential for portfolio economics, make-vs-buy decisions, and CRO cost benchmarking in pharmaceutical dis

Foreign key linking to discovery.lead_series. Business justification: Compound synthesis efforts by medicinal chemistry teams are organized by lead series. Each synthesis produces an analog within a specific chemical series. The synthesis table currently has series_code

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.master_batch_record. Business justification: GMP-compliant preclinical supply synthesis is governed by a master batch record. Pharma domain experts expect compound_synthesis records for GMP batches to reference the controlling MBR for regulatory

Reference to the biological target (protein, enzyme, receptor, or pathway) against which this compound was synthesized. Links synthesis provenance to target identification and MOA studies in the discovery domain.
reference: ChEMBL target classification; UniProt; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Process patent families protect synthesis routes globally across jurisdictions. IP teams track synthesis-to-patent-family linkage for global manufacturing FTO and licensing strategy. Family-level trac

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Synthesis routes are protected by process patents. IP teams track which synthesis methods are covered by patents for manufacturing freedom-to-operate and licensing. Pharma IP experts routinely link sy
Reference to the drug discovery project under which this synthesis was conducted. Enables project-level portfolio reporting, resource tracking, and compound series management.
reference: ICH Q10; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics project management

description: Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Kilo-scale and GMP preclinical supply synthesis is performed at designated manufacturing sites (pilot plants, kilo labs). Pharma operations track which manufacturing site executed each synthesis batch

Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Synthesized compound storage location tracking is required for chain of custody, GMP compliance, and controlled substance scheduling. 'storage_location' plain attribute is a denormalized reference to

business_glossary_term: Synthesis Cro Business Partner Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Contract synthesis outsourced to CROs/CMOs requires linking synthesis records to the qualified business partner for vendor management, GMP audit trails, and regulatory batch record requirements. Disti

Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: GMP batch records and regulatory submissions require knowing which plant performed compound synthesis. Role prefix 'synthesis_' distinguishes from manufacturing plant context. No existing plant FK on

Timestamp when the synthesis record received formal electronic approval in the ELN by the supervising scientist. Distinct from record creation; captures the GLP-required second-person review event for data integrity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); OECD Series on Principles of GLP

description: Alphanumeric batch identifier assigned to the synthesized compound lot. Used for inventory tracking, biological testing assignment, and downstream traceability through the discovery pipeline. Aligns with SAP MM batch management.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.68; ICH Q7; SAP S/4HANA MM module

description: Name and loading of the catalyst or catalytic system used in the reaction (e.g., Pd(PPh3)4, Pd2(dba)3/XPhos, DMAP). Null if the reaction is uncatalysed. Critical for SAR and process chemistry knowledge management.

Classification of the synthesized compound by modality. Distinguishes New Chemical Entities (NCE), New Biological Entities (NBE), peptides, oligonucleotides, PROTACs, fragments, and natural product derivatives. Drives downstream development pathway and regulatory strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCE|NBE|peptide|oligonucleotide|PROTAC|fragment|natural_product_derivative — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH M9; FDA CDER Drug Classification; EMA Guideline on the Investigation of Bioequivalence

description: Timestamp when this synthesis record was first created in the system (ELN or data lakehouse). Mandatory audit field for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and GLP data integrity requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Unique experiment identifier from the Electronic Lab Notebook system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics) that documents the full experimental procedure, observations, and raw data for this synthesis. Provides traceability back to the authoritative source record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Free-text description of the reason for synthesis failure or cancellation when synthesis_status is 'failed' or 'cancelled'. Captures root cause information (e.g., no reaction observed, product decomposition, purification failure, starting material unavailable) to support process learning and SAR knowledge management.
reference: ICH Q10 (Pharmaceutical Quality System); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Indicates whether this synthesis was conducted in full compliance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP-compliant syntheses are required for compounds entering formal preclinical safety studies. True = GLP compliant; False = non-GLP (exploratory research).
reference: OECD Principles of GLP; 21 CFR Part 58; OECD Series on Principles of GLP and Compliance Monitoring

description: GHS/CLP hazard classification of the synthesized compound based on its physicochemical and toxicological properties. Governs safe handling, storage, and disposal procedures in the laboratory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_hazardous|flammable|toxic|corrosive|oxidising|highly_toxic|environmental_hazard — promote to reference product]
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonised System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals); EU CLP Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008; OSHA HazCom 2012

description: Purity of the synthesized compound as determined by High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC), expressed as area percentage. A Critical Quality Attribute (CQA) for compound registration and screening eligibility. Compounds typically require ≥95% purity for HTS submission.
reference: ICH Q6A (Specifications: Test Procedures and Acceptance Criteria); GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); USP <621>

description: Empirical molecular formula of the synthesized compound (e.g., C18H21N3O2). Derived from structural confirmation data. Used for compound identity verification, registry matching, and regulatory documentation.
reference: IUPAC nomenclature; ICH Q6A; Dotmatics / IDBS compound registry

description: Calculated molecular weight of the synthesized compound in grams per mole (g/mol), derived from the confirmed chemical structure. Used for stoichiometric calculations, Lipinski rule-of-five assessment, and compound library profiling.
reference: ICH Q6A; IUPAC; Dotmatics / IDBS compound registry

description: Purity of the synthesized compound as determined by quantitative Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectroscopy, expressed as percentage. Orthogonal purity assessment to HPLC; used for structural confirmation and identity verification per GLP requirements.
reference: ICH Q6A; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); USP <761>

description: Actual mass of isolated compound product in milligrams. Used to determine availability for biological screening, ADME assays, and compound library registration. Directly linked to synthesis scale and yield.
reference: ICH Q11; LIMS (LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager)

description: Method used to purify the crude reaction product to achieve the required purity specification (e.g., column chromatography, preparative HPLC, recrystallisation, trituration). Influences final yield and purity outcomes and informs process development decisions.
reference: ICH Q11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Total elapsed time of the synthesis reaction in hours. A Critical Process Parameter (CPP) used in reaction optimization and SAR/process correlation studies.
reference: ICH Q8; ICH Q11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP)

description: Temperature at which the synthesis reaction was conducted, expressed in degrees Celsius. A Critical Process Parameter (CPP) that directly influences reaction selectivity, yield, and purity. Captured from ELN experimental conditions.
reference: ICH Q8 (Pharmaceutical Development); ICH Q11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP)

description: Free-text or structured description of the key reagents and their stoichiometric equivalents used in the synthesis (e.g., 'K2CO3 (2.0 eq), Pd(OAc)2 (5 mol%), SPhos (10 mol%)'). Supports reproducibility and GLP documentation requirements.
reference: GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); 21 CFR Part 58; ICH Q11

description: Indicates whether the synthesized compound has been registered in the corporate compound library for HTS and biological screening. True = registered and available for screening; False = not yet registered or failed registration criteria (e.g., purity < 95%).
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registry; LIMS (LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager)

description: Canonical SMILES (Simplified Molecular-Input Line-Entry System) string representing the two-dimensional chemical structure of the synthesized compound. Machine-readable format used for cheminformatics, SAR analysis, and compound database registration in Dotmatics.
reference: Daylight Chemical Information Systems SMILES specification; ICH Q6A; Dotmatics / IDBS

description: Name of the primary solvent or solvent system used in the synthesis reaction (e.g., DMF, THF, DCM, EtOH, water). Influences reaction kinetics, selectivity, and downstream purification strategy. Relevant to ICH Q3C solvent classification for later-stage development.
reference: ICH Q3C (Residual Solvents); ICH Q11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP)

description: Required storage condition for the synthesized compound to maintain stability and integrity (e.g., room temperature, 2–8°C, −20°C, −80°C, nitrogen atmosphere, desiccator). Derived from compound physicochemical properties and stability data.
reference: ICH Q1A (Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products); LIMS (LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager)

description: Analytical technique(s) used to confirm the chemical structure of the synthesized compound (e.g., 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, LCMS, HRMS, IR, X-ray crystallography). Structural confirmation is mandatory for compound registration and GLP compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1H-NMR|13C-NMR|LCMS|HRMS|IR|X-ray|multiple — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q6A; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); 21 CFR Part 58

description: Calendar date on which the synthesis reaction was initiated. Principal business event date for this transaction record. Used for timeline tracking, GLP compliance dating, and SAR trend analysis.
reference: GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); 21 CFR Part 58; ICH Q11

description: Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for this synthesis event, typically assigned by the ELN (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Follows site-specific numbering conventions (e.g., MED-SYN-000123). Used for cross-referencing in lab notebooks, SAR databases, and regulatory submissions.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; 21 CFR Part 11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP)

description: Scientific purpose for which this compound was synthesized. Classifies the synthesis within the drug discovery workflow (e.g., hit confirmation from HTS, lead optimisation, SAR exploration, ADME probe, tool compound, library synthesis). Enables portfolio analytics and resource allocation reporting.
reference: ICH Q8; FDA Guidance for Industry: Drug Interaction Studies; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Molar scale of the synthesis reaction expressed in millimoles (mmol). Determines reagent quantities, expected yield mass, and suitability for downstream biological screening (e.g., HTS vs. focused assay).
reference: ICH Q11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP)

description: Current lifecycle state of the synthesis experiment. Tracks progression from planned through execution to completion or failure. Drives workflow routing in the medicinal chemistry pipeline.
reference: ICH Q10 (Pharmaceutical Quality System); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Name or classification of the synthetic methodology applied (e.g., Suzuki coupling, reductive amination, Buchwald-Hartwig amination, solid-phase synthesis). Enables SAR analysis by correlating method with yield and purity outcomes.
reference: ICH Q11; IUPAC nomenclature; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this synthesis record. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance and data integrity monitoring in GLP environments.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); ICH Q10

description: Isolated yield of the target compound expressed as a percentage of the theoretical maximum based on the limiting reagent. A primary quantitative outcome metric for synthesis efficiency and SAR optimization. Range: 0.00–100.00.
reference: ICH Q11; GLP (OECD Principles of GLP); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the lead series record. Primary key for the lead_series data product in the discovery domain.
reference: ICH M10, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics internal entity model

description: Reference to the compound record representing the best-performing compound within this lead series at the current stage, used as the benchmark for potency, selectivity, and ADME profiling comparisons.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registry; ICH Q8

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: Lead series advancement requires formal FTO clearance as a stage-gate requirement. IP teams conduct series-level FTO before lead optimization investment. lead_series has ip_landscape_assessment as a t
Reference to the originating High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaign from which the initial hits in this series were identified. Provides traceability from lead series back to the screening event.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics HTS module; ICH Q8

description: Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Lead series are optimized for specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level pipeline tracking and aligns lead optimization decisions with the product indication

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Lead optimization programs in pharma are funded via dedicated internal orders covering medicinal chemistry, in vivo studies, and CRO costs. Finance tracks lead series spend separately from HTS campaig

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Lead series may be in-licensed or out-licensed; agreement linkage tracks licensed rights, royalty obligations, milestone payments, territory restrictions, and field-of-use limitations for portfolio ma
Reference to the primary molecular target (e.g., protein, enzyme, receptor) against which this lead series is designed to act. Central to target-based drug discovery and SAR analysis.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics target registry; ICH Q8; UniProt

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_claim. Business justification: Lead series scaffolds are covered by specific composition-of-matter patent claims. IP teams track which claims protect the lead scaffold for prosecution strategy and licensing negotiations. lead_serie

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Lead series represent patentable chemical scaffolds; patent family linkage tracks composition-of-matter protection, supports stage gate IP assessments, and enables portfolio prioritization based on IP

Foreign key linking to discovery.discovery_project. Business justification: Lead series are chemical series progressing through hit-to-lead and lead optimization stages within discovery projects. The series table currently has program_code as a denormalized STRING attribute.

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Lead series are developed within therapeutic area programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level pipeline reporting, stage-gate review preparation, and strategic portfolio pr

business_glossary_term: Absorption Distribution Metabolism Excretion (ADME) Profile Summary
description: Summary of the Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, and Excretion (ADME) profile for the lead series, including key in vitro ADME data such as microsomal stability, permeability (Caco-2/PAMPA), plasma protein binding, and CYP inhibition flags.
reference: ICH Q8; ICH M3(R2); GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS E-WorkBook ADME module

description: Total number of discrete compounds synthesized and registered within this lead series. Indicates the breadth of SAR exploration and medicinal chemistry effort invested in the series.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registry; ICH Q8

description: Timestamp when this lead series record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GLP/21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); ICH Q8

description: Current stage of the lead series within the drug discovery pipeline, from hit confirmation through candidate nomination. Distinct from series_status in that it reflects the scientific progression stage rather than the governance lifecycle state.
reference: ICH Q8; company discovery stage-gate framework; IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Date on which the lead series was formally deprioritized or stopped. Null if the series is still active. Used for portfolio analytics and resource reallocation tracking.
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP

description: Composite drug-likeness score for the best-in-class compound in the series, typically based on Lipinski Rule of Five parameters or QED (Quantitative Estimate of Drug-likeness). Ranges from 0 to 1; higher values indicate better drug-likeness.
reference: Lipinski Rule of Five; QED (Bickerton et al.); ICH Q8; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics cheminformatics

description: Reference identifier linking this lead series to the primary Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) experiment or project in IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics where the originating research data is recorded. Ensures data traceability and GLP audit trail.
reference: GLP (21 CFR Part 58); 21 CFR Part 11; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN

description: Indicates whether the key studies supporting this lead series have been conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) conditions as required for regulatory submission. True = GLP-compliant studies on record.
reference: GLP (21 CFR Part 58); OECD GLP Principles; FDA GLP regulations

description: Narrative assessment of the Intellectual Property (IP) landscape for the lead series, including freedom-to-operate (FTO) status, known competitor patents covering the scaffold, and patentability assessment. Classified as confidential due to competitive sensitivity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; ICH Q8; company IP governance policy; USPTO/EPO patent databases

description: Current IP status of the lead series scaffold, indicating whether freedom-to-operate has been established, patents have been filed, or IP encumbrances exist. Classified as confidential.
reference: USPTO; EPO; ICH Q8; company IP governance SOP

description: Description of the Mechanism of Action (MOA) for the lead series, explaining how compounds in the series interact with the molecular target to produce the desired pharmacological effect.
reference: ICH Q8; IDBS E-WorkBook pharmacology module

description: Drug modality classification for the lead series, indicating the type of therapeutic molecule (e.g., small molecule, biologic, peptide). Drives CMC strategy, manufacturing approach, and regulatory pathway selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|peptide|oligonucleotide|cell_therapy|gene_therapy|antibody_drug_conjugate|radioligand — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q8; FDA CDER/CBER modality classification; EMA guidelines

description: Molecular weight of the core scaffold or best-in-class compound in Daltons (Da). Key physicochemical property used in drug-likeness assessment (Lipinski Rule of Five: MW ≤ 500 Da for oral drugs).
reference: Lipinski Rule of Five; ICH Q8; IUPAC; Dotmatics cheminformatics

business_glossary_term: New Chemical Entity / New Biological Entity (NCE/NBE) Classification
description: Classification of the lead series as a New Chemical Entity (NCE), New Biological Entity (NBE), or New Molecular Entity (NME). Determines regulatory pathway, IP strategy, and data exclusivity eligibility.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314 (NCE); 21 CFR Part 601 (NBE); FDA CDER/CBER classification; ICH Q8

description: Date on which the lead series was formally nominated as a development candidate, marking the transition from discovery to preclinical development. Null if the series has not yet been nominated.
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP; IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Best-in-class oral bioavailability percentage (%F) for the lead series from preclinical in vivo PK studies. Key ADME metric for assessing drug-likeness and oral dosing feasibility.
reference: ICH Q8; ICH M3(R2); GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

description: Summary of in vivo Pharmacokinetics (PK) data for the lead series, including key parameters such as oral bioavailability (%F), half-life (t½), clearance (CL), and volume of distribution (Vd) from preclinical species studies.
reference: ICH Q8; ICH M3(R2); GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS E-WorkBook PK module

description: Type of biochemical or cellular assay used to determine the potency benchmark value (e.g., IC50, Ki, EC50, Kd). Provides context for interpreting the potency_benchmark_nm value.
reference: ICH Q8; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Best-in-class potency value (IC50 or Ki) for the lead series expressed in nanomolar (nM) concentration. Represents the principal quantitative performance metric for the series and is used in stage-gate go/no-go decisions.
reference: ICH Q8; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS E-WorkBook assay data

description: Indicates whether any significant safety liabilities (e.g., genotoxicity, hERG inhibition, reactive metabolites, phototoxicity) have been identified for compounds in this lead series. True = known safety liability requiring mitigation.
reference: ICH S2(R1); ICH S7B; ICH M7; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS E-WorkBook safety profiling

description: Narrative description of identified safety liabilities for the lead series, including the nature of the liability, affected compounds, and proposed mitigation strategies. Populated when safety_flag is True.
reference: ICH S2(R1); ICH S7B; ICH M7; GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

description: Qualitative assessment of the maturity of the Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) understanding for the lead series, from early (limited data) to mature (well-defined SAR with predictive models). Used in stage-gate assessments.
reference: ICH Q8; IDBS E-WorkBook SAR module; company medicinal chemistry SOP

description: Textual description of the core chemical scaffold or chemotype defining the lead series, including key structural features relevant to Structure-Activity Relationship (SAR) analysis. Captured from ELN records.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH Q8; SAR analysis standards

description: Narrative summary of the selectivity profile for the lead series, describing off-target activity, selectivity ratios against related targets, and any known liabilities identified through selectivity panels (e.g., kinase selectivity panel, GPCR panel).
reference: ICH Q8; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS E-WorkBook selectivity data

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lead series within the compound governance system (e.g., ONCO-SER-00042). Used as the business identifier across ELN, LIMS, and medicinal chemistry platforms.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registration standards; ICH Q8

description: Human-readable name assigned to the lead series, typically reflecting the chemotype or scaffold class (e.g., 'Pyrimidine Kinase Inhibitor Series A'). Used in medicinal chemistry reports and stage-gate presentations.

Date on which the lead series was formally initiated following hit confirmation from the originating HTS campaign. Marks the beginning of the hit-to-lead phase and is used for timeline and cycle-time analytics.
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP; IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Current lifecycle status of the lead series within the drug discovery pipeline. Governs stage-gate decisions and resource allocation. Values: hit_to_lead (active hit confirmation and early profiling), lead_optimization (active medicinal chemistry optimization), deprioritized (paused or stopped), nominated (candidate nominated for development), on_hold (temporarily paused pending decision).
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP; IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System (SMILES) string representing the core scaffold structure of the lead series. Enables cheminformatics analysis, substructure searching, and computational chemistry workflows.
reference: IUPAC SMILES notation standard; Dotmatics / IDBS E-WorkBook cheminformatics module

description: Date on which the most recent stage-gate committee decision was made for this lead series. Used for tracking progression timelines and portfolio reporting.
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP

description: Most recent stage-gate committee decision for the lead series, indicating whether the series has been approved to progress (go), stopped (no_go), placed on hold, or conditionally approved pending specific criteria.
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP; IDBS E-WorkBook

description: Documented rationale for the most recent stage-gate decision, capturing the scientific, strategic, and risk-based reasoning of the decision committee. Supports audit trail and portfolio governance.
reference: ICH Q8; company stage-gate governance SOP; GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

description: Timestamp when this lead series record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); ICH Q8

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the SAR study record. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH M10, IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN internal object model

description: Foreign key linking to discovery.assay. Business justification: SAR (Structure-Activity Relationship) studies use specific assays to measure potency and selectivity of synthesized analogs. The study table currently has assay_type as a denormalized STRING attribute

Reference to the single best-performing compound identified from this SAR study, based on the combination of potency, selectivity, and ADME profile. Candidate for progression to the next discovery stage.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registry; ICH M4 CMC

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO analyses are conducted on the chemical space explored in SAR studies to identify third-party IP blocking the series. IP and medicinal chemistry teams jointly review FTO before advancing a series.
Reference to the originating High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaign from which the hit compounds for this SAR study were identified. Provides lineage from HTS to lead optimization.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; LIMS; ICH M4

description: Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: SAR studies are conducted to optimize compounds for specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level medicinal chemistry portfolio reporting and aligns SAR findings

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: SAR studies are discrete R&D activities requiring dedicated cost tracking via internal orders for financial planning, cost-per-analog analysis, and stage-gate decision support in medicinal chemistry o
Reference to the lead chemical series under investigation in this SAR study. Links to the compound series master record in the compound library.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound library; ICH M4 CMC

description: Reference to the biological molecular target (e.g., protein, receptor, enzyme) that the SAR study is designed to modulate. Links to the target identification master record.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; UniProt; ChEMBL target ontology

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: SAR studies generate composition-of-matter IP filed as patent families covering the chemical series. IP teams link SAR findings to patent families for prosecution strategy and portfolio reporting. sar
Reference to the parent drug discovery project under which this SAR study is conducted. Enables roll-up reporting at the project level.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics project hierarchy; ICH M4

description: Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: SAR studies are conducted within therapeutic area medicinal chemistry programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level medicinal chemistry resource reporting and portfolio gove
Date on which the SAR study was actually completed and final data locked in the ELN. Compared against target completion date for schedule variance analysis.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH M4; GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

business_glossary_term: Absorption Distribution Metabolism Excretion (ADME) Impact Summary
description: Narrative summary of how structural modifications in this SAR study impacted ADME properties including metabolic stability, permeability, solubility, plasma protein binding, and CYP inhibition. Sourced from LIMS and ELN data.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; LIMS (LabWare/SampleManager); ICH M4 CMC; FDA ADME Guidance

description: Total count of chemical analogs synthesized and tested within this SAR study. Key productivity metric for medicinal chemistry resource planning and portfolio reporting.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics compound registration; ICH M4 CMC

description: Timestamp when this SAR study record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); FDA Data Integrity Guidance; ALCOA+

description: Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the completeness and reliability of the experimental data captured in this SAR study, based on assay reproducibility, data completeness, and ELN audit trail integrity. Used for data governance and AI/ML model training quality gates.
reference: FDA Data Integrity Guidance (2018); 21 CFR Part 11; ALCOA+ Data Integrity Principles

description: Reference identifier or URL to the corresponding study record in the IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN). Provides traceability to the primary research data source.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); FDA Data Integrity Guidance

description: Indicates whether this SAR study was conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. True = GLP-compliant study; False = non-GLP exploratory research. Relevant for regulatory submission packages.
reference: 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); OECD GLP Principles; FDA GLP Guidance

description: Free-text or structured description of the key chemical structural features (pharmacophores, functional groups, scaffolds, substituents) analyzed in this SAR study to drive potency, selectivity, or ADME properties.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH M4 CMC; SAR best practices

description: Description of the biochemical mechanism by which the compounds in this series exert their pharmacological effect on the target (e.g., competitive inhibition, allosteric modulation, covalent binding).
reference: ICH M4; FDA Pharmacology Review Guidance; ChEMBL MOA ontology

description: Indicates whether the lead compounds in this SAR study met the predefined metabolic stability criteria (e.g., microsomal half-life threshold). True = criteria met; False = criteria not met.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; LIMS; ICH M4; FDA ADME Guidance

business_glossary_term: New Chemical Entity / New Biological Entity (NCE/NBE) Type
description: Classification of the compound series as a New Chemical Entity (NCE, small molecule) or New Biological Entity (NBE, biologic/large molecule) or hybrid. Determines regulatory pathway and IP strategy.
reference: FDA NCE/NBE Classification; ICH M4; 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA) / Part 601 (BLA)

description: Indicates whether a patent application has been initiated for compounds or methods arising from this SAR study. True = patent filing in progress or completed; False = no patent action taken.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; USPTO Patent Filing; ICH M4; IP Management Best Practices
