description: Indicates whether the lead compounds demonstrated acceptable membrane permeability (e.g., Caco-2 or PAMPA assay thresholds). True = acceptable permeability achieved; False = not achieved.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; LIMS; ICH M4; FDA ADME Guidance

description: Type of biochemical potency measurement used to characterize compound activity in this SAR study (e.g., IC50 for inhibitory concentration, Ki for inhibition constant, EC50 for effective concentration).
reference: IUPAC pharmacology nomenclature; ChEMBL bioactivity standards; ICH M4

description: Highest (least potent) IC50 or Ki value observed across all analogs in this SAR study, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Together with the minimum, defines the potency range of the series.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics assay results; ICH M4 Pharmacology; ChEMBL bioactivity standards

description: Lowest (most potent) IC50 or Ki value observed across all analogs in this SAR study, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Represents the best-in-series potency achieved.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics assay results; ICH M4 Pharmacology; ChEMBL bioactivity standards

description: Outcome decision made at the conclusion of the SAR study regarding whether the lead series should progress to the next stage of drug discovery (e.g., lead optimization, candidate selection) or be deprioritized.
reference: ICH M4; ISPE Drug Discovery Stage Gate Model; FDA IND Guidance

description: Scientific hypothesis describing the proposed relationship between structural features of the compound series and the observed or expected biological activity. Captured from the ELN study plan.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; ICH M4 CMC; GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

description: Summary of selectivity data for the lead compounds against off-target proteins or related family members. Describes selectivity ratios, panel screening results, and any observed off-target liabilities.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH M4 Pharmacology; FDA Pharmacology/Toxicology Guidance

description: Quantitative ratio of the compound's potency against the primary target versus the most relevant off-target (e.g., 100-fold selective over related kinase). Key metric for candidate progression decisions.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH M4 Pharmacology; FDA Pharmacology Guidance

description: Date on which the SAR study was formally initiated, corresponding to the first experimental activity recorded in the ELN. Used for timeline tracking and portfolio planning.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH M4; GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

description: Descriptive title of the SAR study, typically including the lead series name and therapeutic target (e.g., 'Series-7 EGFR Kinase Inhibitor Optimization'). Used for display and search in ELN and project management tools.
Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the SAR study at initiation (e.g., SAR-ONC-202401). Used for cross-system referencing in ELN, LIMS, and project tracking systems.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics study numbering convention; ICH M4 eCTD

description: Stage of the drug discovery funnel at which this SAR study is conducted. Aligns with the hit-to-lead and lead optimization progression framework used in medicinal chemistry.
reference: ICH M4; ISPE Drug Discovery Stage Gate Model

description: Document identifier or Veeva Vault reference for the formal SAR study report summarizing findings, conclusions, and progression recommendations. Provides traceability to the official scientific record.
reference: Veeva Vault QualityDocs / eTMF; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH M4

description: Current lifecycle state of the SAR study. Drives workflow routing and portfolio reporting. 'terminated' indicates early discontinuation; 'archived' indicates data locked in ELN.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics study lifecycle; ICH M4

description: Planned date by which the SAR study is expected to be completed and key findings reported. Used for project milestone tracking and resource scheduling.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH M4; ISPE Drug Discovery Stage Gate

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SAR study record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); FDA Data Integrity Guidance; ALCOA+

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the assay master record in the discovery domain. Primary key for the assay data product. Role classification: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN internal object model

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Assay development and validation costs are tracked against cost centers for R&D budget reporting and financial controlling. Pharma finance teams require assay-level cost center assignment separate fro

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: CRO vendor qualification and approved vendor list (AVL) management requires linking outsourced assays to their business partner record. 'cro_vendor_name' is a denormalized representation; the FK enabl

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Assay technologies are frequently licensed from third parties (e.g., licensed cell lines, detection platforms, HTRF technology). The licensing agreement governs usage rights and royalty obligations. P

Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Assays are designed to screen against specific molecular targets. The assay table currently has target_name and target_class as denormalized STRING attributes. Adding molecular_target_id FK allows joi

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Assay ownership by org unit is required for GLP compliance scope assignment, budget allocation, and departmental capacity planning. 'owning_department' is a denormalized org unit reference that should

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Proprietary assay methods are protected by method patents. IP teams track which patents cover which assays for licensing, FTO, and CRO outsourcing decisions. A pharma IP expert would expect assay-to-p

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Assays are designed and maintained within therapeutic area programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level assay resource allocation, capacity planning, and portfolio reportin

Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to the assay within the ELN or LIMS (e.g., ASY-HTS0042). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used for cross-system referencing between IDBS/Dotmatics, LabWare LIMS, and SAP QM.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; LabWare LIMS; ICH Q2(R1)

description: Full descriptive name of the assay as registered in the ELN (e.g., 'EGFR Kinase Inhibition HTRF Assay'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used in scientific reports, regulatory submissions, and compound screening dashboards.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; ICH M10 (Bioanalytical Method Validation)

description: Primary scientific classification of the assay based on its biological or chemical mode of measurement. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Drives downstream SAR analysis workflows and compound triage decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: biochemical|cell_based|phenotypic|ADME|toxicity|binding|functional — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH S7A/S7B (Safety Pharmacology); FDA Guidance on Drug Interaction Studies; IDBS Dotmatics assay taxonomy

description: Indicates whether the assay has been adapted and validated for automated liquid handling platforms (e.g., Hamilton, Tecan, Beckman Coulter). Determines eligibility for inclusion in HTS screening campaigns and impacts throughput capacity planning.
reference: ISPE GAMP 5; ANSI/SLAS Microplate Standards; IDBS E-WorkBook automation integration

description: Name of the cell line used in cell-based assays (e.g., HEK293, HeLa, Jurkat, A549, MCF-7). Null for biochemical or cell-free assays. Required for assay reproducibility and cross-site comparability. Cell line authentication records are maintained in the ELN.
reference: ATCC cell line standards; ISPE GAMP 5; ICH Q5D (Cell Substrates); LabWare LIMS biological material registry

description: Percentage coefficient of variation (%CV) of the assay signal across replicate measurements, representing assay precision and reproducibility. Typically required to be ≤ 15% for HTS assays and ≤ 20% for bioanalytical assays per ICH Q2(R1). Stored from the assay validation run.
reference: ICH Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance 2018

description: Unit of measurement for compound concentrations used in the assay (e.g., nM, µM, mM). Standardizes concentration reporting across compound screening results and SAR analysis. Required for dose-response curve fitting and IC50/EC50 calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nM|uM|mM|mg_mL|ug_mL|ng_mL|pM — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q2(R1); IUPAC nomenclature; LabWare LIMS unit registry

description: Estimated reagent and consumable cost per assay well in US dollars. Used for screening campaign cost modeling, budget planning, and make-vs-buy decisions for outsourcing to CROs. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM procurement data and lab supply records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Cost Center Accounting); ICH Q10; ISPE Cost of Goods guidance

description: Timestamp when the assay master record was first created in the ELN or data platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and data lineage tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); ICH Q10; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: Physical or chemical detection principle used to measure the assay signal (e.g., fluorescence, luminescence, radioactivity, mass spectrometry). Distinct from technology_platform — platform is the instrument system, detection_method is the underlying measurement principle. Used for assay validation documentation and GLP compliance records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fluorescence|luminescence|absorbance|radioactivity|mass_spectrometry|NMR|SPR|calorimetry — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures; 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance

description: Identifier of the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) experiment or notebook entry in IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics where the assay development and validation data are recorded. Provides direct traceability from the data product to the source scientific record.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP)

description: Date when the assay was first deployed for compound screening or profiling activities. Marks the operational start of the assay lifecycle. Used for assay age tracking, periodic revalidation scheduling, and portfolio lifecycle management.
reference: 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook audit trail

description: Operational format describing how the assay is run in the screening workflow (e.g., High-Throughput Screening (HTS) primary screen, dose-response, selectivity panel). Determines throughput capacity planning and compound consumption requirements.
reference: ISPE GAMP 5; IDBS E-WorkBook assay format taxonomy; ICH Q2(R1)

description: Indicates whether the assay is conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations as required for regulatory submissions (IND/NDA/BLA). GLP-compliant assays require full audit trails, validated equipment, and certified personnel. Critical for preclinical safety study data submitted to FDA/EMA.
reference: 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); OECD Principles of GLP; EMA GLP Directive 2004/10/EC

description: Unit of measurement for the hit_threshold_value (e.g., percent inhibition, nM IC50). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of the hit-calling criterion across different assay types and readout formats.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH Q2(R1); IUPAC nomenclature

description: Numerical threshold used to classify a compound as a 'hit' in primary screening (e.g., ≥ 50% inhibition at 10 µM). Defined during assay validation and used by automated hit-calling algorithms in the HTS data analysis pipeline. Critical for hit-to-lead progression decisions.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics hit-calling configuration; ICH Q2(R1); FDA HTS Guidance

description: Standard incubation duration in hours for the assay reaction or cell treatment step. Critical for assay reproducibility and scheduling of automated liquid handling workflows. Stored from the validated assay protocol.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook protocol parameters; ISPE GAMP 5

description: Date of the most recent formal validation or requalification of the assay per ICH Q2(R1) or fit-for-purpose qualification criteria. Used to trigger periodic revalidation workflows and to assess whether assay data is current for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance 2018

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Assay ID
description: Cross-reference identifier for this assay in the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) — LabWare or Thermo Fisher SampleManager. Enables bidirectional traceability between the discovery data product and the LIMS assay execution records.
reference: LabWare LIMS / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH Q10

description: Indicates whether the assay measures multiple analytes or endpoints simultaneously in a single well or reaction (multiplexed assay). Multiplexed assays increase data density per compound and reduce reagent consumption. Relevant for assay cost modeling and data analysis pipeline configuration.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook multiplex assay configuration; LabWare LIMS

business_glossary_term: Outsourced to Contract Research Organization (CRO) Flag
description: Indicates whether this assay is currently outsourced to an external Contract Research Organization (CRO) for execution. When true, the cro_vendor_name field captures the CRO identity. Impacts data governance, IP ownership, and regulatory audit trail requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; FDA Guidance on Oversight of Clinical Investigations (adapted for preclinical CRO oversight)

description: Microplate format used in the assay (e.g., 96-well, 384-well, 1536-well). Determines reagent volumes, automation compatibility, and throughput capacity. Critical for liquid handling robot programming and compound dispensing protocols.
reference: ANSI/SLAS Microplate Standards; ISPE GAMP 5; LabWare LIMS plate management

description: Document identifier or URL reference to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) or assay protocol document stored in the ELN (IDBS/Dotmatics) or document management system (Veeva Vault QualityDocs). Ensures traceability from assay execution to the governing protocol version.
reference: 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); Veeva Vault QualityDocs; ISPE GAMP 5; ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System

description: Version number of the assay protocol document in effect at the time of assay execution (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Required for GLP audit trails and regulatory submission traceability. Distinct from protocol_reference which is the document identifier.
reference: 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); ICH Q10; Veeva Vault QualityDocs version control

description: Primary quantitative output metric reported by the assay (e.g., IC50, EC50, Ki, Kd, percent inhibition, percent activation). Determines how compound activity data is interpreted in SAR analysis and hit-to-lead progression decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IC50|EC50|Ki|Kd|percent_inhibition|percent_activation|ratio|count|viability_pct — promote to reference product]
reference: IUPAC pharmacology nomenclature; ChEMBL data standards; ICH M10

description: Indicates whether assay results generated using this assay are eligible for inclusion in regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). Eligibility requires full validation per ICH Q2(R1) and GLP compliance where applicable. Governs data governance workflows for regulatory data packages.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA); ICH Q2(R1); EMA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Date when the assay was formally retired and removed from active use. Null for active assays. Retirement is triggered by target deprioritization, supersession by a superior assay, or platform decommissioning. Required for data governance and historical data archival.
reference: ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System; 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); IDBS E-WorkBook lifecycle management

description: Ratio of the maximum signal (positive control) to the minimum signal (negative control), quantifying the dynamic range of the assay. A signal window ≥ 2 is typically required for HTS qualification. Used in assay validation reports and Z-factor calculations.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); Zhang JH et al. (1999) SLAS Discovery — Z-factor definition; FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance

description: Biological species from which the assay target, cells, or biological material is derived (e.g., human, mouse, rat, recombinant). Critical for translational relevance assessment and cross-species selectivity profiling. Impacts regulatory acceptability of preclinical data for IND submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human|mouse|rat|rabbit|dog|monkey|in_vitro_recombinant — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH S5(R3) Reproductive Toxicology; ICH S7A/S7B; FDA IND Guidance

description: Detection technology or instrument platform used to execute the assay (e.g., HTRF, AlphaScreen, Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR), NMR, FRET, TR-FRET, Luminescence, Flow Cytometry). Critical for assay reproducibility assessment and equipment scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HTRF|AlphaScreen|SPR|NMR|FRET|TR-FRET|luminescence|flow_cytometry|mass_spectrometry|fluorescence_polarization — promote to reference product]
reference: ISPE GAMP 5; ICH Q2(R1); LabWare LIMS instrument registry

description: Standard incubation or reaction temperature in degrees Celsius for the assay. Critical for assay reproducibility across sites and instruments. Deviations from this value are flagged as out-of-specification (OOS) events in the LIMS.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); LabWare LIMS environmental monitoring

description: Maximum number of compounds or samples that can be processed per day using this assay under standard HTS conditions. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. Used for compound library screening campaign planning and resource scheduling in the discovery lab.
reference: ISPE GAMP 5; IDBS E-WorkBook capacity planning module

description: Current lifecycle state of the assay with respect to its scientific and regulatory validation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. 'validated' indicates full ICH Q2(R1) compliance; 'qualified' indicates fit-for-purpose qualification; 'development' indicates the assay is still being optimized. Governs whether assay results can be used in regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures; 21 CFR Part 58 (GLP); FDA Bioanalytical Method Validation Guidance 2018

description: Statistical measure of assay quality (Z') calculated from positive and negative control variability. Values ≥ 0.5 indicate an excellent assay suitable for HTS. Stored as the validated Z' from the assay qualification run. Used to gate assay deployment for compound screening campaigns.
reference: Zhang JH et al. (1999) A Simple Statistical Parameter for Use in Evaluation and Validation of High Throughput Screening Assays; ICH Q2(R1)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the HTS campaign record in the discovery data product. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics internal entity model; ICH Q10 Pharmaceutical Quality System

description: Foreign key linking to discovery.assay. Business justification: HTS campaigns execute screening using specific assays. The campaign currently has assay_format, assay_technology, target_name, target_class as denormalized attributes. Adding assay_id FK establishes t

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: HTS campaigns are major cost events requiring cost center allocation for financial tracking, budget management, and project costing. Enables campaign cost analysis, resource planning, and financial re

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Outsourced HTS campaigns require linking to the qualified CRO business partner for contract management, AVL compliance, and vendor performance tracking. 'cro_name' is a denormalized business partner n

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: HTS campaigns are cost-tracked R&D projects requiring internal order assignment for budget control, variance analysis, and cost-per-screen metrics essential to pharmaceutical screening operations and

Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: hts_campaign has denormalized STRING fields target_name and target_class that duplicate data from the molecular_target master record. While hts_campaign already links to assay (which has molecular_tar
Reference to the parent drug discovery project under which this HTS campaign is executed. Links the campaign to the broader target identification and hit-to-lead program for portfolio tracking.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics project hierarchy; ICH Q10

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: HTS campaigns are executed at specific screening facilities/plants; facility tracking is required for GMP compliance, capacity planning, and regulatory audit trails. Role prefix 'screening_' distingui

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: HTS campaigns are executed within therapeutic area programs and budgets. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level campaign portfolio reporting, cost tracking, and strategic resou
Date on which the HTS campaign screening execution was completed. Used to calculate campaign duration, throughput metrics, and to trigger downstream hit confirmation workflows.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Date on which the HTS campaign screening execution actually commenced. Compared against planned_start_date to measure schedule variance and operational efficiency.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Scientific format of the assay used in the campaign, indicating whether the screen measures biochemical activity (purified protein), cellular response, whole-organism phenotype, direct binding (biophysical), or fragment-based interactions. Critical for interpreting hit quality and SAR analysis.
reference: NIH NCATS HTS Assay Guidance; ISPE Drug Discovery Guidelines

description: Specific detection technology or instrumentation platform used in the assay (e.g., HTRF, AlphaScreen, FRET, TR-FRET, FP, luminescence, absorbance, imaging). Determines data interpretation rules and quality thresholds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: HTRF|AlphaScreen|FRET|TR-FRET|FP|luminescence|absorbance|imaging|SPR|ITC — promote to reference product]
reference: SLAS (Society for Laboratory Automation and Screening) Technology Standards; ISPE GAMP 5

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the HTS campaign, assigned by the ELN or LIMS system (e.g., HTS-EGFR-2024). Used for cross-system referencing in Dotmatics, LabWare, and project management tools.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics campaign numbering convention; LIMS - LabWare SampleManager

description: Free-text scientific description of the HTS campaign objectives, biological rationale, assay design, and any special conditions or deviations from standard protocols. Provides context for data interpretation and scientific review.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the HTS campaign, typically including the target name and campaign purpose (e.g., 'EGFR Kinase Primary Screen Q1 2024'). Used in reports, dashboards, and scientific communications.
Current lifecycle state of the HTS campaign, tracking progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation. Drives operational dashboards and resource allocation decisions.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics workflow states; ICH Q10

description: Percentage coefficient of variation of the neutral control wells across all campaign plates, measuring intra- and inter-plate reproducibility. Acceptable CV% thresholds are defined per assay type and are a key quality acceptance criterion.
reference: SLAS Assay Quality Standards; ICH Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures

description: Total number of unique compounds tested in this HTS campaign. Key operational metric for campaign scope, resource planning, and hit rate denominator calculation.
reference: SLAS HTS Metrics Guidelines; ISPE Drug Discovery Guidelines

description: Timestamp when the HTS campaign record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GLP data integrity regulations.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GLP 21 CFR Part 58; ICH Q10

description: Indicates whether a Contract Research Organization (CRO) was engaged to execute or support this HTS campaign. Drives vendor management, data transfer agreement requirements, and IP ownership tracking.
reference: ICH Q10; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; GDPR Article 28 (data processor agreements)

description: Overall data quality assessment for the HTS campaign based on Z' factor, CV%, plate failure rate, and control performance. Determines whether campaign data is accepted for hit identification and downstream SAR analysis.
reference: SLAS Assay Quality Standards; ICH Q2(R1); GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Reference identifier linking this HTS campaign to the corresponding experiment record in the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Provides traceability to raw experimental data, protocols, and scientist sign-off records.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Number of assay plates that failed quality control criteria and were excluded from hit identification analysis. High failed plate counts may trigger campaign re-screening decisions.
reference: SLAS Assay Quality Standards; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; ICH Q10

description: Indicates whether this HTS campaign was conducted under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP compliance is required for studies intended to support regulatory submissions (IND/NDA). Non-GLP campaigns are exploratory research.
reference: GLP - 21 CFR Part 58; OECD GLP Principles; FDA GLP Regulations

description: Number of compounds that met or exceeded the hit threshold criteria in this campaign. Used to calculate hit rate and to scope confirmatory screening workload.
reference: SLAS HTS Metrics Guidelines; ISPE Drug Discovery Guidelines

description: Percentage of screened compounds that qualified as hits (hit_count / compounds_screened_count × 100). Key campaign quality metric used to assess assay selectivity, library quality, and target druggability. Stored as a raw business measurement, not a derived KPI.
reference: SLAS HTS Metrics Guidelines; NIH NCATS HTS Best Practices

description: Percentage inhibition or activation threshold used to define a 'hit' in this campaign (e.g., ≥50% inhibition at screening concentration). Determines the stringency of hit identification and directly impacts hit count and hit rate.
reference: SLAS HTS Best Practices; NIH NCATS Hit Criteria Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Batch ID
description: Batch or run identifier assigned by the LIMS (LabWare or Thermo Fisher SampleManager) for the sample processing associated with this HTS campaign. Enables traceability of compound samples, plate preparation, and analytical results.
reference: LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; 21 CFR Part 11; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Scheduled start date for the HTS campaign as defined in the project plan. Used for resource scheduling, capacity planning, and tracking schedule adherence.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics project planning; ICH Q10

description: Total number of assay plates processed during the campaign. Used for capacity planning, reagent consumption tracking, and quality control review scope.
Microplate format used in the HTS campaign, defining the number of wells per plate. Determines throughput capacity, liquid handling requirements, and data volume per plate.

Name or identifier of the reference compound used as the positive control in the assay (known active compound). Defines the 100% inhibition/activation benchmark for data normalization and Z' factor calculation.
reference: SLAS HTS Best Practices; GLP (Good Laboratory Practice) 21 CFR Part 58

description: Classification of the screening campaign by its scientific purpose in the HTS funnel. Primary screens test the full compound library; confirmatory screens re-test hits; counter-screens eliminate false positives; dose-response screens establish potency; selectivity screens assess off-target activity.
reference: ISPE Drug Discovery Guidelines; NIH NCATS HTS Best Practices

description: Single-point screening concentration in micromolar (µM) at which compounds are tested in the primary screen. Defines the activity threshold context for hit identification and is a critical parameter for hit rate comparability across campaigns.
reference: SLAS HTS Best Practices; NIH NCATS Assay Guidance

description: Ratio of the positive control signal to the background (negative control) signal, measuring the dynamic range of the assay. A higher S/B ratio indicates a more robust assay window. Complementary to Z' factor for assay quality assessment.
reference: SLAS Assay Quality Standards; NIH NCATS HTS Assay Guidance

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the HTS campaign record. Required for data lineage tracking, change control auditing, and Silver layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GLP 21 CFR Part 58; ICH Q10

description: Statistical measure of assay quality (Z' factor) calculated from positive and negative control signals, ranging from 0 to 1. A Z' ≥ 0.5 indicates an acceptable HTS assay window. Critical quality gate for campaign data acceptance per SLAS and NIH NCATS standards.
reference: Zhang JH et al. (1999) J Biomol Screen; SLAS Assay Quality Standards; NIH NCATS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the molecular target record in the discovery data product. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer — Internal PK Convention

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.fto_analysis. Business justification: FTO analysis is conducted on molecular targets before committing R&D investment. IP teams run target-level FTO to assess freedom to work on a biological target. A pharma IP expert would expect this li

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Molecular targets are validated for specific disease indications. Linking to product.indication enables indication-level target portfolio analysis, competitive intelligence reporting, and alignment of

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Target ownership by research org unit drives portfolio reporting, resource allocation, and therapeutic area governance. Pharma domain experts expect molecular targets to be formally assigned to a depa

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Target-level IP strategy is managed at the patent family level for global coverage assessment. IP portfolio reports group target protection by family. molecular_target already links to a single patent

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Molecular targets are patented inventions requiring patent protection tracking for freedom-to-operate analysis, portfolio management, and competitive intelligence in target selection decisions.

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_claim. Business justification: Specific patent claims cover target modulation methods; claim-level tracking required for FTO clearance, licensing negotiations, and assessing blocking IP in target validation stage gates.

Foreign key linking to product.therapeutic_area. Business justification: Molecular targets are classified and prioritized within therapeutic area programs. Linking to the master therapeutic_area entity enables TA-level target portfolio reporting, competitive landscape anal

Summary of known competitive programs targeting this molecular target, including approved drugs, clinical-stage compounds, and disclosed research programs from competitor organizations. Business-confidential intelligence used for portfolio strategy.
reference: Internal Competitive Intelligence; FDA Orange Book; ClinicalTrials.gov

description: Timestamp when this molecular target record was first created in the discovery data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Databricks Lakehouse Audit

description: Internal program or project code under which this target is being investigated. Links the target to the broader drug discovery program for portfolio management, resource allocation, and P&L tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling / Project System); Internal Portfolio Management

description: Specific disease or condition(s) associated with this molecular target, as supported by published literature or internal research data. May reference ICD-10/ICD-11 disease codes or MeSH disease terms.
reference: WHO ICD-11; MeSH Disease Ontology; Open Targets Platform; DisGeNET

description: Scientific assessment of the target's amenability to small molecule or biologic modulation based on structural, biochemical, and computational analyses. Key input to portfolio prioritization and NCE/NBE strategy decisions.
reference: ISPE Drug Discovery Guidelines; Hopkins & Groom Druggability Framework (Nature Reviews Drug Discovery)

description: Reference identifier to the primary Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) experiment record in IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics that documents the original target identification and characterization data. Provides full scientific audit trail.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records)

description: Ensembl stable gene identifier (e.g., ENSG00000146648) for the gene encoding the molecular target. Used for genomic cross-referencing, variant analysis, and bioinformatics pipelines.
reference: Ensembl Genome Browser (EMBL-EBI); ICH E15 Pharmacogenomics Definitions

description: Primary tissue(s) or cell type(s) where this molecular target is expressed at therapeutically relevant levels, as determined by transcriptomic or proteomic profiling (e.g., liver, lung epithelium, T-cells). Informs tissue selectivity and delivery strategy.
reference: Human Protein Atlas; GTEx; FANTOM5; ICH S6 Preclinical Safety for Biologics

description: Date on which this molecular target was first formally disclosed or documented in the internal ELN or target identification database. Establishes the scientific priority date for IP purposes.
reference: USPTO Patent Priority Date Rules; EPC Article 54 (Novelty); IDBS/Dotmatics ELN

description: Official HGNC (HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee) approved gene symbol encoding the molecular target (e.g., EGFR, TP53, BRCA1). Standard identifier used in regulatory submissions and scientific publications.
reference: HGNC — HUGO Gene Nomenclature Committee; FDA Pharmacogenomics Guidance

description: Quantitative score (0.0000–1.0000) representing the strength of genetic evidence linking this target to the associated disease, derived from GWAS, Mendelian randomization, or Open Targets Platform data. Higher scores indicate stronger genetic validation.
reference: Open Targets Platform; GWAS Catalog (EMBL-EBI); ICH E15 Pharmacogenomics

description: Date of the most recent scientific or portfolio review of this target record. Ensures data currency and supports periodic target triage processes.
reference: Internal Target Review SOP; GLP (21 CFR Part 58) Data Integrity Requirements

description: Narrative description of the molecular mechanism by which modulation of this target is expected to produce a therapeutic effect. Captures agonism, antagonism, inhibition, activation, or allosteric modulation details as documented in ELN.
reference: ICH E8 General Considerations for Clinical Studies; FDA Pharmacology Review Guidance

description: Categorical classification of the pharmacological mechanism of action for compounds acting on this target. Used to group targets by intervention strategy in SAR analysis and portfolio reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agonist|antagonist|inhibitor|activator|allosteric_modulator|degrader|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IUPHAR/BPS Guide to Pharmacology MOA Classification; ICH E8

description: Preferred drug modality for targeting this molecular target based on its structural and biochemical properties. Determines the NCE (New Chemical Entity) or NBE (New Biological Entity) classification of resulting compounds. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|antibody|peptide|oligonucleotide|cell_therapy|gene_therapy|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA CDER/CBER Modality Classification; ICH Q6A/Q6B; EMA Biologics Guidelines

description: Classification of the target's novelty relative to the competitive landscape. Novel targets represent first-in-class opportunities; established targets have existing approved drugs; repurposed targets are being explored in new indications.
reference: FDA Innovation and Drug Discovery Guidance; IP Strategy — Patent Filing (21 CFR Part 314)

description: Biological organism in which the molecular target is expressed and studied. Typically Homo sapiens for human targets, but may include model organisms used in preclinical validation studies.
reference: NCBI Taxonomy Database; ICH S5 Reproductive Toxicology; GLP Guidelines (21 CFR Part 58)

description: High-level summary of the patent landscape around this molecular target, including freedom-to-operate (FTO) considerations and known composition-of-matter or method-of-use patents. Supports IP strategy decisions.
reference: USPTO; EPO; ICH Q11; 21 CFR Part 314 (NDA IP Certification)

description: Name of the primary biological signaling or metabolic pathway in which this target participates (e.g., PI3K/AKT/mTOR, RAS/MAPK, JAK/STAT). Supports pathway-based target clustering and polypharmacology analysis.
reference: KEGG Pathway Database; Reactome Pathway Knowledgebase; WikiPathways

description: RCSB Protein Data Bank accession code for the primary 3D structure of this molecular target. Used by computational chemistry and structure-based drug design (SBDD) teams for molecular docking and virtual screening.
reference: RCSB Protein Data Bank (PDB); wwPDB Guidelines

description: Portfolio prioritization tier assigned to this target by the Target Selection Committee, reflecting strategic importance, scientific confidence, and commercial potential. Tier 1 targets receive maximum resource allocation.
reference: Internal Portfolio Review Process; ICH E8 General Considerations for Clinical Studies

description: Indicates whether a resolved 3D protein structure (X-ray crystallography, cryo-EM, or NMR) is available for this target in the Protein Data Bank (PDB) or internal structural biology repository. Directly impacts structure-based drug design (SBDD) feasibility.
reference: RCSB Protein Data Bank (PDB); ISPE Drug Discovery Guidelines

description: Narrative description of identified safety liabilities associated with modulation of this target (e.g., hERG channel activity, hepatotoxicity risk, immunosuppression). Populated only when safety_concern_flag is True.
reference: ICH S7A/S7B; FDA Safety Pharmacology Guidance; EMA Preclinical Safety Guidelines

description: Indicates whether known safety liabilities or toxicological concerns have been identified for this target based on literature, genetic association studies, or internal preclinical data. Triggers mandatory safety review before progression.
reference: ICH S7A/S7B Safety Pharmacology; FDA Preclinical Safety Evaluation Guidance; GLP (21 CFR Part 58)

description: Assessment of the target's selectivity relative to related family members or off-target proteins. Informs compound design strategy to minimize off-target toxicity and maximize therapeutic index.
reference: ICH S7A Safety Pharmacology Studies; FDA Pharmacology/Toxicology Review Guidance

description: Pharmacological class of the molecular target based on its biochemical function and structural family. Drives druggability assessment strategy and compound screening cascade design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GPCR|kinase|nuclear_receptor|ion_channel|protease|phosphatase|epigenetic_regulator|transcription_factor|transporter|other — promote to reference product]
reference: IUPHAR/BPS Guide to Pharmacology Target Classification; ChEMBL Target Classification

description: Internally assigned alphanumeric business identifier for the molecular target, used as the externally-known unique code across systems (ELN, LIMS, CTMS). Distinct from the surrogate PK and from external database accession numbers.
reference: Internal Master Data Management (MDM) Standard; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN

description: Name or identifier of the primary biochemical or biophysical assay used to measure compound engagement with this target (e.g., HTRF binding assay, SPR, ITC, NanoBRET). Registered in LIMS (LabWare/SampleManager) and ELN.
reference: LIMS — LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); IDBS/Dotmatics ELN

description: Human-readable scientific name of the molecular target (e.g., EGFR, BRAF, PD-L1, CDK4/6). Serves as the primary identity label used by research scientists and in ELN records within IDBS/Dotmatics.
reference: IUPHAR/BPS Guide to Pharmacology; UniProt Protein Nomenclature Guidelines

description: Biological molecule type of the target (e.g., protein, enzyme, receptor, ion channel, nucleic acid). Determines the modality of drug intervention and screening assay design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protein|enzyme|receptor|ion_channel|nucleic_acid|lipid|carbohydrate|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IUPHAR/BPS Guide to Pharmacology; ChEMBL Target Type Ontology

description: Universal Protein Resource (UniProt) accession number uniquely identifying the protein target in the global UniProtKB database. Enables cross-referencing with external biological databases and regulatory submissions.
reference: UniProt Knowledgebase (UniProtKB); ICH M4 CTD Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this molecular target record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GDPR Article 5; Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake

description: Date on which target validation studies were completed and the target was formally declared validated (in vitro or in vivo). Marks the transition from target identification to hit-to-lead progression stage.
reference: ICH S7A/S7B; GLP (21 CFR Part 58); Internal Stage-Gate Process

description: Current lifecycle stage of target validation, reflecting the level of scientific evidence supporting the target's role in disease. Drives go/no-go decisions for hit-to-lead progression. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: identified|hypothetical|validated_in_vitro|validated_in_vivo|clinically_validated|deprioritized — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH S7A/S7B Safety Pharmacology Guidelines; FDA Target Validation Guidance; IDBS/Dotmatics ELN Workflow

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each individual screening result record in the HTS or dose-response assay campaign. Primary key for the screening_result data product in the discovery domain.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics ELN; ICH M10 Bioanalytical Method Validation

description: Reference to the assay definition used to generate this screening result, including assay type, target, and protocol version.
reference: LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; GLP (Good Laboratory Practice) 21 CFR Part 58

description: Reference to the High-Throughput Screening (HTS) campaign or screening project under which this result was generated. Enables grouping of results by campaign for SAR analysis.
Reference to the biological molecular target (e.g., protein, enzyme, receptor) against which the compound was screened. Central to target identification and MOA studies.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH S9 Nonclinical Evaluation for Anticancer Pharmaceuticals

description: Reference to the compound (NCE or NBE) that was screened in this assay run. Links to the compound master record in the compound library.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH Q6A Specifications for New Drug Substances

description: Coefficient of variation (CV%) for replicate measurements of this compound in the assay run. Measures intra-assay reproducibility; high CV% triggers data quality review.
reference: ICH Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Concentration of the compound tested in the assay well, expressed in micromolar (µM). Critical for dose-response curve fitting and potency determination.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Timestamp when this screening result record was first created in the system. Required for GLP audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; GDPR Article 5

description: Coefficient of determination (R²) from the dose-response curve fitting model. Values close to 1.0 indicate a high-quality fit. Used as a data quality gate for potency value acceptance.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Data quality status assigned to this screening result based on automated QC rules and manual review. 'flagged' indicates a potential issue requiring review; 'rejected' excludes the result from downstream analysis.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; 21 CFR Part 11; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; ICH Q2(R1)

description: Half-maximal effective concentration (EC50) derived from dose-response curve fitting, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Key potency metric for agonist/activator compounds.
reference: ICH S9; ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Reference identifier linking this screening result to the corresponding experiment entry in the Electronic Lab Notebook (ELN) system (IDBS E-WorkBook or Dotmatics). Provides traceability to the original experimental record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Hill slope (cooperativity coefficient) from the four-parameter logistic (4PL) dose-response curve fit. Values near 1 indicate simple binding; values significantly different from 1 may indicate cooperativity, aggregation, or assay artifacts.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Half-maximal inhibitory concentration (IC50) derived from dose-response curve fitting, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Key potency metric for inhibitory compounds in SAR analysis and hit-to-lead progression.
reference: ICH S9; ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Identifier of the laboratory instrument (e.g., plate reader, liquid handler, mass spectrometer) used to generate the raw signal. Supports instrument qualification tracking and systematic error investigation per GLP.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; 21 CFR Part 11; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; ISPE GAMP 5

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this compound result meets the predefined criteria for progression into the hit-to-lead (H2L) phase, based on potency, selectivity, and data quality thresholds.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH Q6A; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Inhibition constant (Ki) representing the binding affinity of the compound to the target, expressed in nanomolar (nM). Derived from competitive binding or enzyme kinetics assays. Used in mechanistic SAR studies.
reference: ICH S9; ICH Q2(R1); IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Reference identifier from the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) for this screening result record. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational LIMS source system.
reference: LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; 21 CFR Part 11; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Maximum observed response (top asymptote) from the dose-response curve, expressed as a percentage of control. Indicates the efficacy ceiling of the compound at the target.
Minimum observed response (bottom asymptote) from the dose-response curve, expressed as a percentage of control. Used to assess baseline signal and curve quality.

Boolean flag indicating whether the compound has been identified as a Pan-Assay Interference Compound (PAINS) — a compound that produces artifactual activity signals across multiple assays due to non-specific mechanisms. PAINS compounds are deprioritized in hit-to-lead.
reference: Baell & Holloway (2010) J Med Chem; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Calculated percentage activation of the target relative to controls. Primary activity metric for agonist/activator HTS campaigns. Null for inhibition assays.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); GLP 21 CFR Part 58; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Calculated percentage inhibition of the target activity relative to positive and negative controls on the same plate. Primary activity metric for inhibitory HTS campaigns. Null for activation assays.
reference: ICH Q2(R1); GLP 21 CFR Part 58; IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics

description: Unique barcode identifier of the physical assay plate on which the compound was tested. Used for traceability back to the physical plate in the LIMS and for QC investigations.
reference: LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; ISPE GAMP 5

description: Numeric column designation (1–24) of the well on the assay plate. Used for spatial analysis of plate effects and systematic error detection.
reference: ANSI/SBS 1-2004 Microplate Standard; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Row designation (A–P) of the well on the assay plate. Used for spatial analysis of plate effects and edge-effect QC.
reference: ANSI/SBS 1-2004 Microplate Standard; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Free-text or coded reason explaining why a data quality flag was applied (e.g., 'edge well effect', 'reagent lot failure', 'instrument malfunction', 'outlier replicate'). Required when data_quality_flag is 'flagged' or 'rejected'.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; 21 CFR Part 11; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Unprocessed instrument signal reading from the assay detector (e.g., fluorescence units, luminescence counts, absorbance). Preserved as the primary measurement for audit and reanalysis.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; 21 CFR Part 11; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Lot identifier of the key assay reagent (e.g., enzyme, antibody, cell line passage) used in this assay run. Enables reagent lot correlation analysis for systematic variability investigation.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; cGMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: Sequential replicate index for this compound-assay combination within the same run (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Enables statistical aggregation across replicates and assessment of intra-assay variability.
reference: ICH Q2(R1) Validation of Analytical Procedures; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Scientific classification of the screening result based on activity thresholds and confirmatory data. Drives hit-to-lead progression decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|inconclusive|toxic|hit|confirmed_hit|false_positive|pan_assay_interference — promote to reference product]
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH Q6A; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Calendar date on which the assay run was executed. Used for temporal trending, reagent lot correlation, and GLP audit trail compliance.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Precise date and time when the assay instrument reading was captured. Supports intra-day sequencing, instrument log correlation, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Records and Signatures; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Quantitative selectivity score measuring the compound's specificity for the primary target versus off-target activities in a selectivity panel. Higher scores indicate greater selectivity. Used in SAR-driven lead optimization.
reference: IDBS E-WorkBook / Dotmatics; ICH S9; GLP 21 CFR Part 58

description: Ratio of the positive control signal to the background (negative control) signal on the assay plate. A key plate-level QC metric; low S/B ratios indicate assay degradation or reagent failure.
reference: GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager; ISPE GAMP 5

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this screening result record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; GDPR Article 5

description: Alphanumeric well position on the assay plate (e.g., A01, H12, P24) indicating the exact location of the compound in the plate layout. Standard SBS/ANSI plate format.
reference: ANSI/SBS 1-2004 Microplate Standard; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Z' factor for the assay plate, a dimensionless statistical metric (range -∞ to 1) measuring assay quality based on signal window and variability. Z' ≥ 0.5 is the accepted threshold for a valid HTS assay plate.
reference: Zhang JH et al. (1999) J Biomol Screen; GLP 21 CFR Part 58; LIMS - LabWare / Thermo Fisher SampleManager

description: Unique identifier for the electronic batch record (eBR). Primary key for the batch record entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key for the batch manufacturing record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.188 - Batch Production and Control Records

business_glossary_term: Batch Size Unit Of Measure Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: Batch size UoM in GMP batch records must be standardized for yield calculations, regulatory submissions, and cross-site comparisons. batch_size_uom is a plain string; normalizing to masterdata.unit_of

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Every batch is manufactured for a specific brand. Critical for serialization compliance, track-and-trace systems, product complaint investigations, recall management, and commercial supply chain visib

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: GMP batch records must be traceable to the commercial brand plan authorizing production campaigns. Supports supply-commercial audit trails, regulatory inspection readiness, and annual product review (

Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Batches manufactured using DMF-registered API, excipients, or processes must reference the DMF for regulatory traceability, CMC compliance verification, letter of authorization validation, and support

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Batch records document manufacturing of specific drug products. Essential for batch disposition, regulatory traceability, product genealogy, and linking manufacturing execution to product specificatio

Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: GMP requires each batch record to document the approved formulation version manufactured. Regulatory inspections, batch disposition decisions, and annual product quality reviews all require this direc

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Batch production costs for validation, clinical trials, and development batches are allocated to internal orders for proper project accounting, cost tracking, and capitalization assessment.

Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: Clinical supply chain management requires tracking which principal investigator received each investigational batch. FDA 21 CFR Part 312 and ICH E6 GCP require traceability of investigational product

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_line. Business justification: Batch records are executed on a specific manufacturing line. The batch_record table has production_line_code (STRING) which should be normalized to a FK to manufacturing_line. This enables access to l

Reference to the approved master batch record (MBR) that defines the standard manufacturing procedure, formula, and process parameters for this product. The MBR is the template from which this batch record is executed.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.186 - Master Production and Control Records

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.masterdata_ndc_code. Business justification: Batch records for US-marketed products require NDC code for FDA lot distribution reports, DSCSA serialization compliance, and drug recall traceability. Linking batch_record to masterdata_ndc_code enab

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: GMP batch records (21 CFR Part 211.188, EU GMP Annex 11) must document the specific material being manufactured including material number, grade, and specifications. Linking batch_record to material m

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Batch records are released against the approved medicinal product registration. QP certification, batch disposition, and regulatory batch certificates reference the medicinal product. Annual product q

Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Batch records for finished products must document the packaging configuration used for serialization compliance, labeling verification, and batch release. Regulatory batch certificates and QP release

Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: SAP MM batch master records are plant-specific. Batch records require plant assignment for lot release decisions, GMP inspection scope, and regulatory lot distribution reporting. Plant-level batch tra

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.process_validation. Business justification: Validation batches are executed as part of a formal process validation study (Stage 2 — Process Performance Qualification per FDA Process Validation Guidance 2011). Each batch_record that is a validat

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Batch records are the execution records for production orders. Each batch_record is created to document the manufacturing execution of a specific production_order. This is a standard child→parent rela

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: Batch disposition decisions require knowing the pv_product's REMS requirements, expedited reporting flags, and RMP commitments. PV teams trace safety signals back to specific manufactured batches via

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_site. Business justification: Batch records are executed at a specific manufacturing site. Currently batch_record has manufacturing_site_code and manufacturing_site_name (STRING) which are redundant with the manufacturing_site mas

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: GMP regulatory requirement (21 CFR Part 211, EU GMP): every executed batch record must reference the approved product specification it was manufactured against. Essential for batch release, regulatory

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Manufacturing batch records must link to supply inventory lots to enable end-to-end traceability from production to distribution. This is critical for product recalls, quality investigations, and supp

Foreign key linking to finance.transfer_price. Business justification: Inter-site/inter-entity batch manufacturing requires transfer pricing for OECD/tax compliance. Each batch record must reference the applicable transfer price to support intercompany valuation, tax fil

The actual quantity of product produced at the end of the manufacturing process. Compared against theoretical yield to calculate yield percentage and identify process inefficiencies or material losses.

The unique business identifier assigned to the manufacturing batch. This is the externally-known batch identifier used across all manufacturing, quality, and regulatory documentation. Must be unique and traceable per cGMP requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.188 - Batch Production and Control Records

description: The planned or target quantity of product to be manufactured in this batch. Expressed in the unit of measure specified in batch_size_uom. Used for yield calculations and material planning.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.186 - Master Production and Control Records

description: Current lifecycle status of the batch. In-process indicates active manufacturing; Released indicates QA approval for distribution; Rejected indicates failed quality specifications; Quarantine indicates pending investigation or testing; On-hold indicates temporary suspension; Expired indicates past shelf life.

Classification of the batch by manufacturing stage. Drug Substance (DS) refers to API production; Drug Product (DP) refers to formulated finished dosage form (FDF). Intermediate refers to in-process materials.
reference: ICH Q7 - Good Manufacturing Practice Guide for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients

description: Indicates whether a formal CAPA investigation was required for this batch due to deviations, out-of-specification (OOS) results, or quality issues. True indicates CAPA was initiated; False indicates no CAPA required.

The number of manufacturing deviations or non-conformances documented during the production of this batch. Deviations require investigation and impact assessment per cGMP. Zero indicates no deviations occurred.
The name of the qualified quality unit personnel who approved the final disposition decision. Must be an authorized individual per the quality management system (QMS).
The date on which the final disposition decision was made by the quality unit. Marks the completion of batch review and approval process.

The final quality decision on the batch. Release indicates approval for distribution; Reject indicates failure to meet specifications; Rework indicates batch will be reprocessed; Destroy indicates batch will be disposed of. This is the authoritative disposition outcome.

The documented justification for the disposition decision. For rejections, includes the specific quality failures or deviations. For releases, confirms all specifications and cGMP requirements were met. Required for regulatory compliance and audit trail.

Indicates whether environmental monitoring results (viable and non-viable particulates, temperature, humidity) were within acceptable limits throughout the manufacturing process. Critical for sterile and aseptic manufacturing per cGMP.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.42 - Design and Construction Features

description: The date after which the batch should not be used or distributed. Calculated based on manufacture date plus approved shelf life from stability studies. Required on all drug product labels per regulatory requirements.

The version number of the product formulation used for this batch. Tracks changes to the formula, process parameters, or specifications. Critical for change control and regulatory submissions (CMC section).
reference: ICH Q7 - Good Manufacturing Practice Guide for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients

description: The GMP classification under which this batch was manufactured. cGMP (current Good Manufacturing Practice) is required for commercial and late-stage clinical batches. GMP applies to earlier development batches. Non-GMP may apply to research-only materials.
reference: 21 CFR Parts 210 and 211 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice

description: The calendar date on which the batch manufacturing was completed. Used for regulatory labeling, shelf-life calculations, and expiry date determination. Distinct from manufacturing_end_timestamp which includes time precision.

The date and time when manufacturing operations for this batch were completed. Marks the end of the production cycle before quality testing and release. Used to calculate cycle time and production efficiency.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.188 - Batch Production and Control Records

description: The date and time when manufacturing operations for this batch commenced. Marks the beginning of the batch production cycle. Critical for batch genealogy and process timeline documentation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.188 - Batch Production and Control Records

description: Indicates whether Process Analytical Technology (PAT) tools were used for real-time monitoring and control of critical quality attributes (CQA) during this batch. PAT enables continuous quality verification per FDA PAT guidance.
reference: FDA PAT Guidance - Process Analytical Technology

business_glossary_term: Quality by Design (QbD) Design Space Compliance
description: Indicates whether all critical process parameters (CPP) remained within the approved QbD design space during manufacturing. True indicates full compliance; False indicates deviation requiring investigation. Applicable for products developed under QbD principles per ICH Q8.

Status of the material reconciliation process comparing input materials consumed against output product and documented waste. Discrepancies require investigation per cGMP. Reconciled indicates all materials are accounted for within acceptable limits.

The date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance. Distinct from manufacturing_start_timestamp which reflects the physical production start.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 - Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures

description: The date and time when this batch record was last modified in the system. Audit trail field for data governance and compliance. Updated whenever any field in the batch record is changed.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 - Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures

description: Reference to the regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) under which this batch was manufactured. Links the batch to the approved regulatory dossier and ensures manufacturing is conducted per approved CMC specifications.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314 - Applications for FDA Approval to Market a New Drug

description: The date by which the batch must be re-examined to ensure it still meets quality specifications. Primarily applicable to drug substances (API) and intermediates. If testing confirms specifications are met, the material may continue to be used.
reference: ICH Q7 - Good Manufacturing Practice Guide for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients

description: The probability of a single viable microorganism being present on a product unit after sterilization. Expressed as SAL-10^-N (e.g., SAL-10^-6 means one in a million probability). Required for sterile products. Not applicable for non-sterile products.
reference: ISO 11137 - Sterilization of Health Care Products

description: The sterilization method applied to this batch, if applicable. Required for sterile drug products and certain medical devices. Not applicable for non-sterile products. Method must be validated per regulatory requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.113 - Control of Microbiological Contamination

description: The expected quantity of product that should be produced based on the master batch record formula and input materials, assuming 100% efficiency with no losses. Used as the baseline for yield percentage calculation.

The ratio of actual yield to theoretical yield expressed as a percentage. Calculated as (actual_yield_quantity / theoretical_yield_quantity) * 100. Deviations from expected yield ranges trigger investigations per cGMP.
Unique identifier for the manufacturing deviation record. Primary key.
Reference to the manufacturing batch affected by this deviation.
Reference to the associated CAPA record initiated to address the root cause and prevent recurrence.

Reference to the drug product (DP) or drug substance (DS) affected by the deviation.

Specific manufacturing process step or operation during which the deviation occurred (e.g., blending, granulation, tablet compression, sterile filtration, lyophilization).

Pre-defined acceptance criteria that the validation study must meet to be considered successful. Includes Critical Quality Attributes (CQA) specifications, Critical Process Parameters (CPP) ranges, yield targets, impurity limits, and other quality metrics. Must be established before validation execution.
reference: ICH Q8, ICH Q6A, FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011)

description: Maximum batch size validated in the study, expressed in the unit of measure appropriate for the product. Defines the upper bound of the validated batch size range.

Minimum batch size validated in the study, expressed in the unit of measure appropriate for the product (kg for API, number of units for finished dosage form). Defines the lower bound of the validated batch size range.
Reference to the change control record that authorized this validation or revalidation activity. Links validation to the change management process per ICH Q10.
Additional comments, notes, or observations related to the validation study. May include summary of key findings, lessons learned, or special considerations.

List of Critical Process Parameters (CPP) monitored during the validation study. CPPs are process parameters whose variability has an impact on a CQA and therefore should be monitored or controlled to ensure the process produces the desired quality. Examples include temperature, pressure, mixing time, pH, flow rate.

List of Critical Quality Attributes (CQA) monitored during the validation study. CQAs are physical, chemical, biological, or microbiological properties that should be within an appropriate limit, range, or distribution to ensure the desired product quality. Examples include assay, impurities, dissolution, content uniformity, sterility, endotoxin.
Timestamp when this validation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.

Indicates whether a design space was defined as part of the validation. Design space is the multidimensional combination and interaction of input variables and process parameters that have been demonstrated to provide assurance of quality per ICH Q8. True if design space was established through QbD approach.
Number of deviations from the validation protocol that occurred during execution. Deviations must be documented, investigated, and assessed for impact on validation outcome per cGMP requirements.
Date when validation execution was completed (last validation batch or run completed and all testing finished).
Date when validation execution began (first validation batch or run initiated).
Timestamp when this validation record was last modified. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.

Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the validation. Per ICH Q10 and continued process verification principles, validations must be periodically reviewed to ensure the process remains in a state of control. Typically reviewed annually or based on risk assessment.
reference: ICH Q10, ICH Q12, FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011)

description: Number of validation batches or runs executed as part of the validation study. Typically minimum 3 consecutive successful batches for process validation per FDA guidance. May vary based on validation type and risk assessment.
reference: FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011), ICH Q7

description: Number of Out of Specification (OOS) results encountered during validation execution. OOS results require investigation per FDA OOS guidance and may impact validation outcome.

Indicates whether Process Analytical Technology (PAT) tools were implemented as part of the validation. PAT is a system for designing, analyzing, and controlling manufacturing through timely measurements of critical quality and performance attributes with the goal of ensuring final product quality. True if PAT tools (e.g., NIR, Raman, in-line sensors) were used.

Specific manufacturing process step or unit operation being validated (e.g., API synthesis, granulation, blending, compression, coating, fill-finish, lyophilization, sterilization, packaging). For cleaning validation, this is the equipment or system being cleaned. For CSV, this is the computerized system being validated.
reference: ICH Q8, FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011)

description: Date when the validation protocol was approved by Quality Assurance and authorized for execution. Represents the start of the validation lifecycle.
Unique protocol number assigned to the validation study. Serves as the externally-known business identifier for this validation activity.
Name of the Quality Assurance representative who reviewed and approved the validation protocol and report. QA approval is required per cGMP regulations.

Reference to the regulatory filing (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD section) where this validation data is or will be submitted. Validation data is typically included in Module 3 (Quality) of the Common Technical Document (CTD).
Date when the validation report was reviewed and approved by Quality Assurance. This is the official validation completion date and triggers the validated state for the process.

Event or condition that triggered the need for revalidation. Examples include: process changes, equipment changes, facility changes, raw material supplier changes, scale-up or scale-down, transfer to new site, out-of-specification results, quality issues, regulatory inspection findings, or periodic review schedule. Supports change control and lifecycle management per ICH Q12.
reference: ICH Q10, ICH Q12, 21 CFR Part 211.100

description: Validation approach methodology. Traditional approach uses fixed process parameters and extensive testing. Quality by Design (QbD) approach uses design space and process understanding per ICH Q8. Hybrid combines traditional and QbD elements. Risk-based approach uses ICH Q9 risk management principles to focus validation efforts.
reference: ICH Q8, ICH Q9, ICH Q10

description: Name of the person responsible for leading the validation study. Typically a validation engineer, process engineer, or quality assurance specialist.

Overall outcome of the validation study. Pass indicates all acceptance criteria were met. Fail indicates one or more acceptance criteria were not met. Conditional indicates validation passed with minor deviations that require corrective action but do not invalidate the study. Inconclusive indicates validation could not be completed or results were ambiguous.
reference: FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011), ICH Q10

description: Stage of process validation per FDA lifecycle approach. Stage 1 (Process Design) establishes commercial manufacturing process based on development knowledge. Stage 2 (Process Qualification) confirms process design is capable of reproducible commercial manufacturing. Stage 3 (Continued Process Verification) provides ongoing assurance that process remains in control. Also includes legacy approaches: prospective (validation before routine production), concurrent (validation during routine production), and retrospective (validation using historical data).
reference: FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011), ICH Q10

description: Current lifecycle status of the validation study. Draft indicates protocol is being prepared. In Progress indicates validation execution is underway. Under Review indicates validation report is being reviewed by Quality Assurance. Approved indicates validation successfully completed and approved. Rejected indicates validation failed to meet acceptance criteria. Conditional indicates validation passed with minor deviations requiring follow-up. Expired indicates validation has exceeded its periodic review date and requires revalidation. Superseded indicates validation has been replaced by a newer study. Type of validation being performed. Process validation covers manufacturing processes, cleaning validation covers equipment cleaning procedures, computerized system validation covers MES/SCADA/LIMS systems per 21 CFR Part 11, analytical method validation covers laboratory test methods, equipment validation covers production equipment qualification, and sterilization validation covers sterilization cycles.
reference: FDA Process Validation Guidance (2011), ICH Q7, 21 CFR Part 11

reference: GVP Module VI B.5; ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety Duplicate Detection

description: Status of SAE reconciliation between the pharmacovigilance database (Argus Safety) and the clinical trial EDC system (Medidata Rave). Applicable when report_type = 'clinical_trial'. Ensures consistency between safety database and clinical database per ICH E6(R2) GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 5.17; 21 CFR Part 312.62; GVP Module VI

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ICSR record in the source system. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance and Silver layer incremental data loading.

Assessment of whether the adverse drug reaction is listed (expected) in the current Reference Safety Information (RSI) — Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS) or approved product labeling. Unlisted + serious = SUSAR (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction), triggering expedited reporting. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2D; ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i; GVP Module VI B.3; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: PubMed Identifier (PMID) or Digital Object Identifier (DOI) for literature-sourced ICSRs. Populated when report_type = 'literature'. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.4.r.1. Supports systematic literature review (SLR) traceability.
