reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.4.r.1; GVP Module VI B.2; ICH E2D

description: Indicates whether the ICSR meets the four ICH E2B minimum criteria for a valid case: (1) identifiable reporter, (2) identifiable patient, (3) suspect drug, (4) adverse event. Cases not meeting minimum criteria are tracked but not submitted. Corresponds to ICH E2D minimum information requirements.
reference: ICH E2D; ICH E2B(R3); GVP Module VI B.1

description: Date of the most recent follow-up information received for this case, resetting the regulatory clock for follow-up submissions. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.5.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.5; GVP Module VI B.4

description: Unit of measurement for patient_age_value. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.2.2b. Supports neonatal and pediatric cases where age is expressed in days, weeks, or months.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) D.2.2b; ICH E11 (Pediatric Studies)

description: De-identified patient age at the time of the adverse event onset, expressed as a numeric value. Must be used in conjunction with patient_age_unit. Stored per GDPR/HIPAA de-identification standards. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.2.2a.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) D.2.2a; GDPR Article 9; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; GVP Module VI

description: Biological sex of the patient as reported. De-identified demographic field per GDPR/HIPAA. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.5. Used in signal detection and benefit-risk assessment stratification.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) D.5; GDPR Article 9; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

description: Patient body weight in kilograms at the time of the adverse event, as reported. De-identified per GDPR/HIPAA. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) D.3. Used for dose-weight relationship analysis in signal detection.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) D.3; GDPR Article 9; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164

business_glossary_term: Primary Adverse Event MedDRA Preferred Term (PT)
description: MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) Preferred Term (PT) coding for the primary adverse event/reaction reported. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.2.1b. Standardized coding enables cross-case signal detection and regulatory submission.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) E.i.2.1b; MedDRA; ICH M1; GVP Module VI

business_glossary_term: Primary Adverse Event MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC)
description: MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) for the primary adverse event, derived from the MedDRA PT hierarchy. Supports aggregate signal detection by organ system and PSUR/PBRER tabulation requirements.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) E.i.2.1a; MedDRA; ICH E2C(R2) PBRER; GVP Module VII

description: Date the ICSR was first received by the company (MAH/sponsor), establishing the regulatory clock start date (Day 0) for 7-day expedited and 15-day expedited reporting obligations. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.4.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.4; 21 CFR Part 314.81(b)(1); GVP Module VI B.4

description: Outcome of rechallenge — whether the adverse event recurred upon re-administration of the suspect drug after dechallenge. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.3b. Strengthens causality evidence and is critical for SUSAR assessment in clinical trials.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.3b; ICH E2A; WHO-UMC Causality Assessment

description: Calculated deadline date by which the ICSR must be submitted to the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, NMPA, TGA). Derived from receipt_date plus applicable reporting window (7 or 15 calendar days for expedited reports; 90 days for non-expedited). SLA-critical field for compliance monitoring.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.81(b)(1); GVP Module VI B.4; ICH E2A; PMDA Pharmacovigilance Regulations

description: Current submission status of the ICSR to the applicable health authority. Tracks compliance against 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting deadlines. Overdue status triggers escalation per PV SLA management procedures.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.81(b)(1); GVP Module VI B.4; ICH E2A; PMDA Pharmacovigilance Regulations

description: Classification of the ICSR source channel per ICH E2B(R3) C.1.3. Drives regulatory reporting obligations and processing workflows. Spontaneous reports from HCPs/patients, literature-sourced cases (PMID/DOI), clinical trial SAEs, solicited reports from patient support programs, and other sources.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.3; 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA Guideline on Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (GVP) Module VI

description: Internal case number assigned by the company (MAH/sponsor) in the source system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.8.1. Used for internal tracking and reconciliation with the worldwide unique case ID.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.8.1; Argus Safety Case Number

description: Indicates whether the adverse event meets ICH E2A seriousness criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or medically significant). True = Serious Adverse Event (SAE). Drives expedited 7-day or 15-day regulatory reporting obligation. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.3.2.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3) E.i.3.2; 21 CFR Part 312.32; GVP Module VI B.3

description: Specific ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by the adverse event. Multiple criteria may apply. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) E.i.3.2a-f. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3) E.i.3.2a-f; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the adverse event occurred. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.17.1. Determines applicable regulatory authority for submission routing (FDA, EMA, PMDA, NMPA, TGA, MHRA).
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.17.1; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; GVP Module VI

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether the case qualifies as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) — i.e., serious, unexpected (unlisted), and with a reasonable causal relationship to the investigational medicinal product. True = SUSAR requiring 7-day (fatal/life-threatening) or 15-day expedited reporting to health authorities and ethics committees.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014; GVP Module VI

description: Globally unique case identifier assigned at intake, conforming to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.1 format (sender identifier + case number). Used for cross-regional deduplication and regulatory submission tracking across FDA MedWatch, EudraVigilance, J-ICSR, and NMPA.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.1; 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA EudraVigilance Technical Specification

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each adverse reaction record within the pharmacovigilance system. Primary key for the pv_adverse_reaction data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 314; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: PV-coded adverse reactions must be reconciled against EDC-captured adverse events per GVP Module VI. A direct FK from pv_adverse_reaction to patient.adverse_event enables reaction-level EDC reconcilia

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Linking adverse reactions to ATC therapeutic class enables signal detection by drug category, benefit-risk analysis, and PSUR aggregate reporting. Medical coding teams perform MedDRA-to-ATC cross-tabu

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Adverse reactions aggregated by brand for safety surveillance, label update decisions, and competitive safety positioning. Essential for brand risk management committees and periodic benefit-risk eval

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Reaction-to-product traceability: pv_adverse_reaction must reference the specific drug_product for signal detection across formulations, labeling update decisions, and expedited regulatory reporting.

Reference to the parent Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) to which this adverse reaction belongs. One ICSR may contain multiple adverse reaction records. Supports header-detail navigation for case processing.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA EudraVigilance; Argus Safety

description: Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-level benefit-risk: adverse reactions must be evaluated against the approved indication for listedness determination and PSUR benefit-risk summaries. EMA/FDA require indication-specific saf

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Adverse reactions are coded against specific active substances (INN) for signal detection and aggregate safety analysis. Linking pv_adverse_reaction to inn_registry enables substance-level disproporti

Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Listedness determination for each adverse reaction requires reference to the current approved label. Linking pv_adverse_reaction to regulatory.label enables automated listedness_determination against
Reference to the suspect medicinal product (drug product) associated with this adverse reaction. Links to the master product record for the drug under pharmacovigilance surveillance.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.4; FDA NDA/BLA; EMA EudraVigilance product dictionary; Argus Safety product field

description: Action taken with the suspect medicinal product in response to the adverse reaction: dose reduced, drug withdrawn, dose not changed, not applicable, unknown, or dose increased. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.4.k.8 and is required for regulatory ICSR submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.4.k.8; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance; Argus Safety action taken

description: Free-text medical narrative describing the clinical course of this adverse reaction, including relevant medical history, concomitant medications, treatment, and outcome. Required for regulatory ICSR submissions and medical review. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field H.1.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) H.1; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance; Argus Safety narrative field

description: Assessment of the causal relationship between the suspect drug and the adverse reaction, using WHO-UMC or company standard causality categories. Drives listedness determination, SUSAR classification, and signal detection weighting.
reference: WHO-UMC Causality Assessment System; ICH E2B(R3) B.4.k.18; FDA MedWatch; Argus Safety causality

description: The methodology or algorithm used to perform the causality assessment (e.g., WHO-UMC scale, Naranjo algorithm, company-specific algorithm). Supports audit trail and methodological consistency across cases.
reference: WHO-UMC Causality Assessment; Naranjo Algorithm; ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety

description: User identifier of the medical coder or system process that performed or confirmed the MedDRA coding. Required for audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance. Stores the user login or system account name.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Argus Safety audit trail; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Date on which the MedDRA coding was completed for this adverse reaction. Used for coding turnaround time metrics, audit trail, and compliance with regulatory submission timelines.
reference: MedDRA Term Selection: Points to Consider; ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the MedDRA coding was performed automatically by the safety system (auto-coded), manually by a medical coder (manual review), or auto-coded and subsequently confirmed by manual review. Supports coding quality metrics and audit trail requirements.
reference: MedDRA Term Selection: Points to Consider; ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety coding workflow; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp when this adverse reaction record was first created in the pharmacovigilance safety database. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module VI. Supports case processing timeline metrics.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety audit trail

business_glossary_term: Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) Grade
description: Severity grade assigned per the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE), ranging from 1 (mild) to 5 (death). Applicable primarily for oncology clinical trial adverse events. Null for non-oncology or post-market cases where CTCAE grading is not applicable.
reference: NCI CTCAE v5.0; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA Oncology guidance; Medidata Rave CTCAE grading

description: Outcome of dechallenge (withdrawal of the suspect drug) on the adverse reaction. Positive dechallenge (reaction improved or resolved after withdrawal) supports causality. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.4.k.18.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.4.k.18; WHO-UMC Causality Assessment; Argus Safety dechallenge field

description: Indicates whether this adverse reaction meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria. True if any seriousness criterion is met. Drives expedited reporting timelines (7-day or 15-day) to health authorities.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.3; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance; 21 CFR Part 314

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Indicator
description: Indicates whether this adverse reaction has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR). True when the reaction is serious, unexpected (unlisted), and there is a reasonable causal relationship to the investigational medicinal product. Triggers mandatory expedited reporting to health authorities and ethics committees.
reference: ICH E2A; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA SUSAR reporting guideline

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this adverse reaction record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11. Used to track follow-up updates and case amendments in the safety database.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety audit trail

description: Determination of whether this adverse reaction is listed (expected) or unlisted (unexpected) in the current Reference Safety Information (RSI), such as the Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS) or Investigator Brochure (IB). Unlisted serious reactions in clinical trials constitute Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) requiring expedited reporting.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2D; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA SUSAR reporting guideline; Argus Safety listedness

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) High Level Group Term (HLGT) Code
description: MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) code, one level above HLT in the MedDRA hierarchy. Supports broader therapeutic area grouping for signal detection and regulatory submissions.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH E2B(R3); ICH E2C(R2)

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) High Level Term (HLT) Code
description: MedDRA High Level Term (HLT) code representing the grouping level above the Preferred Term. Used for aggregate signal detection and periodic safety update report (PSUR) tabulations.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH E2B(R3); ICH E2C(R2) PSUR

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Code
description: MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) numeric code assigned to this adverse reaction after coding. The PT is the primary level used for regulatory reporting, signal detection, and line listing generation. Mandatory for ICSR submissions to FDA and EMA.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.2a; EMA EudraVigilance; FDA MedWatch

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) Name
description: Human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) label corresponding to the meddra_pt_code. Stored denormalized for reporting and line listing generation without requiring a join to the MedDRA reference table.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.2b

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Code
description: MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code representing the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for regulatory line listings, PSUR summary tabulations, and risk management plan (RMP) categorization.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.2c; ICH E2C(R2); EMA RMP guideline

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Version
description: Version of the MedDRA dictionary used to code this adverse reaction (e.g., 26.0, 26.1). Critical for regulatory submissions and recoding exercises when MedDRA versions are updated. Required for reproducibility of signal detection analyses.
reference: MedDRA Introductory Guide; ICH E2B(R3); EMA EudraVigilance technical specification

description: Indicates whether the adverse reaction has been medically confirmed by a healthcare professional (HCP). Medically confirmed cases carry greater weight in signal detection and regulatory reporting. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field A.1.7.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.1.7; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance; Argus Safety

description: Date on which the adverse reaction first began. Used to calculate time-to-onset from drug exposure, supports causality assessment, and is required for ICSR regulatory submissions. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.2.i.4.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.4; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA EudraVigilance; Argus Safety

description: Standardized outcome of the adverse reaction at the time of reporting. Values align with ICH E2B(R3) outcome codes: recovered/resolved, recovering/resolving, not recovered/not resolved, fatal, unknown, recovered with sequelae. Mandatory for regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.7; FDA MedWatch; EMA EudraVigilance; Argus Safety outcome field

description: Duration of the adverse reaction in days, calculated from onset to resolution. Stored as a business attribute when explicitly reported by the reporter or calculated during case processing. Supports time-to-resolution analytics and benefit-risk assessments.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.5; Argus Safety duration field; ICH E2C(R2) PSUR

description: Sequential line number of this adverse reaction within the parent ICSR, enabling ordering and identification of multiple events within a single case. Corresponds to the reaction/event sequence in ICH E2B(R3) message structure.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section B.2; Argus Safety reaction sequence

description: Current workflow processing status of this adverse reaction record within the pharmacovigilance case management system: new (received, not yet processed), under review, coded (MedDRA coding complete), medically reviewed, closed (finalized), or deleted (voided). Drives case management workflow in Argus Safety.
reference: EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; Argus Safety case workflow; ICH E2B(R3)

description: Outcome of rechallenge (re-administration of the suspect drug after withdrawal) if performed. Positive rechallenge (reaction recurred) strengthens causality. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.4.k.19.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.4.k.19; WHO-UMC Causality Assessment; Argus Safety rechallenge field

description: Classification of the regulatory reporting obligation triggered by this adverse reaction: 7-day expedited (unexpected fatal/life-threatening SUSAR), 15-day expedited (other serious unexpected), periodic (PSUR/PBRER), non-expedited, or not reportable. Drives submission workflow and deadline tracking.
reference: ICH E2A; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Classification of the original source from which this adverse reaction was reported: spontaneous (unsolicited), clinical trial, published literature, regulatory authority, or other. Determines applicable reporting timelines and regulatory obligations. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field A.1.4.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.1.4; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA EudraVigilance; ICH E2D

description: Date on which the adverse reaction resolved or ended. Null if the event is ongoing, fatal, or outcome is unknown. Used to calculate event duration and supports outcome determination. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) field B.2.i.6.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.6; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; Argus Safety

description: Pipe-delimited list of applicable seriousness criteria met by this adverse reaction per ICH E2A: death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability/incapacity, congenital anomaly, medically significant. Supports regulatory reporting classification and expedited reporting decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|prolonged_hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3) B.1.i.3; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA EudraVigilance

description: Narrative severity classification of the adverse reaction using standard WHO/ICH terminology: mild, moderate, severe, life-threatening, or fatal. Complements CTCAE grade for non-oncology cases and post-market surveillance where CTCAE grading is not used.
reference: WHO Adverse Reaction Terminology; ICH E2B(R3); FDA MedWatch; Argus Safety severity field

description: Indicates whether this adverse reaction record has been flagged as contributing to a potential pharmacovigilance signal under review. Set by the signal detection process in the safety database. Supports signal management workflow and regulatory signal reporting obligations.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA Guideline on Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (GVP) Module IX; FDA Pharmacovigilance guidance

description: Number of days from the first dose of the suspect drug to the onset of the adverse reaction. A key pharmacovigilance metric used in signal detection, causality assessment, and benefit-risk evaluation. Derived from drug start date and reaction onset date during case processing.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); WHO-UMC Signal Detection; FDA Pharmacovigilance guidance; Argus Safety

description: The exact adverse event term as reported by the original reporter (patient, healthcare professional, or literature source) before MedDRA coding. Preserved verbatim for coding audit trail and signal detection. Required per ICH E2B(R3) field B.2.i.1.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) B.2.i.1; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; Argus Safety verbatim term

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the SAE report record in the pharmacovigilance safety database. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH E2D; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: SAE reports in PV are directly derived from serious adverse events in the clinical EDC system. sae_report.edc_case_reference and edc_reconciliation_status confirm this reconciliation process; a direct

Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: SAE reports under clinical trials are filed under the IND/CTA application. Linking sae_report to regulatory.application enables application-level SAE tracking for annual safety reports and regulatory

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: SAEs in clinical trials linked to investigational brand for development safety database, regulatory submission packages (NDA/BLA), and brand-level safety profile characterization. Required for integra

Foreign key linking to clinical.clinical_enrollment. Business justification: SAE reports from blinded trials require the enrollment record to identify treatment arm for unblinding and causality assessment. sae_report has patient_id and trial_id but cannot identify the specific

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: SAE-to-product traceability: sae_report documents a serious adverse event for a specific drug product. Linking to drug_product enables product-level SAE tracking for regulatory submissions, labeling u

Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: SAE reports originate from clinical trial sites (HCOs). Linking sae_report to hco_master enables site-level SAE aggregation for IND/CTA regulatory submissions and site performance monitoring — a stand

Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: sae_report.submission_authority is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK to health_authority enables authority-specific SAE reporting compliance dashboards, deadline tracking, and submission por

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.icsr. Business justification: An SAE report is a primary source document that feeds into an ICSR (Individual Case Safety Report). In PV operations, an SAE report from a clinical trial is processed and converted into an ICSR for re

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: SAE investigations require budget allocation for additional medical reviews, site follow-up visits, and causality assessments. Internal orders capture these incremental costs, enabling trial financial

Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: SAE reports during clinical trials reference the investigational compound directly. For pre-approval compounds not yet registered as pv_products, a direct FK to discovery.compound is required for IND

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: SAE reports from clinical trials must reference the specific IMP batch for causality assessment and potential product recall decisions. suspect_drug_dose and suspect_drug_route on sae_report are denor

Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: IND/CTA SAE reporting regulations require identification of the responsible investigator. The investigator record carries GCP certification, protocol deviation history, and 1572 status — all required

Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: SAE reports in clinical trials identify the site investigator who reported the event. Essential for query resolution, site communication, medical review, and regulatory inspection readiness per GCP re
Reference to the patient or clinical trial subject associated with this SAE. Links to the master patient/subject record for demographic and medical history context.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); ICH E6(R2) GCP; HIPAA; GDPR Article 9

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: SAE reports are processed and owned by specific PV org units (regional safety teams). Org unit assignment is required for GCP audit trails, regulatory submission accountability, workload management, a

Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: SAE reports must be associated with the discovery/development project for aggregate safety analysis, DSUR preparation, and clinical program oversight. Safety operations teams routinely query SAEs by p
Reference to the suspect medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) associated with the SAE. Links to the product master for INN, ATC code, formulation, and regulatory approval details.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.4; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 314.81

description: Reference to the healthcare professional, patient, or other reporter who submitted the SAE. Links to the reporter master record for qualification, contact, and reporter type details.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element A.2; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Foreign key linking to commercial.sales_rep. Business justification: SAE reports received via field sales reps must be tracked to the collecting rep for follow-up obligations, AE reporting compliance audits, and rep training records. Pharma companies are required to tr

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SAE reports under clinical trials are filed under the IND/CTA regulatory submission. Linking sae_report to regulatory.submission enables IND safety reporting compliance tracking per 21 CFR 312.32, tyi

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.susar. Business justification: The sae_report table has an is_susar boolean flag indicating that certain SAE reports qualify as SUSARs. When is_susar=true, the SAE report should be directly linked to the corresponding SUSAR record
Reference to the clinical trial or study in which the SAE occurred. Links to the clinical trial master record for protocol, phase, and IND/CTA details. Null for spontaneous post-market reports.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Foreign key linking to clinical.visit. Business justification: SAE reports from clinical trials are identified at a specific visit. Linking sae_report to the visit enables EDC-safety reconciliation and source data verification (SDV), a GCP requirement. sae_report

Externally-known alphanumeric case identifier assigned by the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety/Oracle AERS) upon initial receipt of the SAE. Used for cross-system tracking, regulatory submissions, and reconciliation with EDC systems such as Medidata Rave.
reference: ICH E2D; Argus Safety Case Number; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Medical assessment of the causal relationship between the suspect medicinal product and the SAE, based on WHO-UMC or investigator assessment criteria. Drives SUSAR classification and regulatory reporting obligations.
reference: WHO-UMC Causality Categories; ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Timestamp when the SAE report record was first entered into the pharmacovigilance safety database (Argus Safety/Oracle AERS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Outcome observed when the suspect drug was withdrawn or dose reduced (dechallenge). A positive dechallenge (event resolved on withdrawal) supports a causal relationship. Required field in ICH E2B(R3) ICSR submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.4.k.18.1; ICH E2A; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Case or subject reference number from the clinical trial EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave subject ID or AE sequence number) used to reconcile the SAE record between the safety database and the clinical database.
reference: ICH E2D; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave

description: Status of reconciliation between this SAE record in the pharmacovigilance safety database (Argus Safety) and the corresponding record in the clinical trial EDC system (Medidata Rave). Required per ICH E2D guidance to ensure consistency between clinical and safety databases.
reference: ICH E2D; ICH E6(R2) GCP; Medidata Rave-Argus Safety reconciliation SOP

description: Indicates whether the SAE resulted in the death of the patient. Triggers mandatory expedited 7-day reporting to health authorities and escalation to senior medical review.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2D; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Indicator
description: Indicates whether this SAE has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited 7-day (fatal/life-threatening) or 15-day reporting to health authorities and notification to investigators.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CT Regulation 536/2014

description: Assessment of whether the adverse reaction is listed (expected) in the current Reference Safety Information (RSI) such as the Investigator Brochure or SmPC. Determines whether the event qualifies as unexpected for SUSAR classification.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 312.32

description: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term code assigned to the primary SAE event term. Standard coding used for regulatory submissions, signal detection, and aggregate safety analysis.
reference: MedDRA v26+; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; FDA FAERS

description: Human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term label corresponding to the meddra_pt_code. Displayed in safety reports, regulatory submissions, and signal detection outputs.
reference: MedDRA v26+; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI

description: MedDRA System Organ Class code representing the highest-level anatomical or physiological classification of the SAE. Used for aggregate safety reporting and PSUR tabulations.
reference: MedDRA v26+; ICH E2B(R3); ICH E2C(R2) PSUR

description: Current status of the medical officer review of the SAE case. Tracks progression from initial triage through physician causality assessment and case narrative approval.
reference: ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety workflow

description: Free-text medical narrative summarizing the SAE case including patient history, event description, clinical course, treatment, and outcome. Contains protected health information (PHI). Submitted as part of the ICSR to health authorities.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element H.1; ICH E2D; HIPAA; GDPR Article 9; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the serious adverse event first began or was first observed by the patient or healthcare professional. Used to calculate time-to-onset from first drug exposure.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element A.1.7; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Clinical outcome of the SAE at the time of reporting or case closure. Drives case narrative, aggregate safety reporting, and benefit-risk assessment in PSUR/PBRER.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.2.i.7.2; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

business_glossary_term: Patient Age at Adverse Event Onset
description: Age of the patient in years at the time the serious adverse event began. Used for demographic analysis, signal detection, and population-specific risk assessment.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.1.2.2; HIPAA; GDPR Article 9

description: Biological sex of the patient as recorded at the time of the SAE. Used for demographic stratification in signal detection and aggregate safety reporting (PSUR/PBRER).
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.1.2.1; HIPAA; GDPR Article 9

description: Date on which the company first received valid minimum information about the SAE (Day 0 for regulatory reporting clock). SLA-critical: initial SAE notification must be processed within 24 hours of site awareness per ICH E2D.
reference: ICH E2D; ICH E2A; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Outcome observed when the suspect drug was re-administered after the initial adverse event (rechallenge). A positive rechallenge (event recurred) strongly supports causality. Required field in ICH E2B(R3) ICSR submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.4.k.18.2; ICH E2A; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Actual date on which the ICSR/SUSAR was submitted to the health authority. Compared against regulatory_submission_due_date to assess compliance with reporting timelines.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EudraVigilance

description: Deadline by which the SAE/SUSAR must be submitted to the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, etc.). Calculated from receipt_date based on applicable reporting timeline (7-day for fatal/life-threatening SUSAR; 15-day for other SUSARs; 30-day for non-expedited).
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; EU CT Regulation 536/2014

description: Current workflow state of the SAE report from initial receipt through medical review, causality assessment, and regulatory submission. Drives SLA monitoring and submission queue management.
reference: ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety workflow states

description: Classification of the SAE report by its originating source. Determines applicable regulatory reporting timelines and submission pathways (e.g., 7-day vs 15-day expedited reporting).
reference: ICH E2D; ICH E2A; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: One or more ICH E2A seriousness criteria met by this event: death, life-threatening, hospitalization (initial or prolonged), disability/incapacity, congenital anomaly/birth defect, or medically important event. Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|prolonged_hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_important — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2D; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the investigational site or sponsor first became aware of the SAE. Used to calculate the 24-hour initial notification SLA and the 7/15-day regulatory reporting clock per ICH E2A and GCP requirements.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Number of days between the first dose of the suspect medicinal product and the onset of the SAE. Key pharmacovigilance metric used in signal detection, aggregate analysis, and benefit-risk evaluation.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; FDA FAERS

description: Free-text description of the treatment(s) administered to manage the SAE, including concomitant medications, procedures, or interventions. Captured in the case narrative.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether specific treatment was administered to the patient in response to the SAE. Relevant for case narrative, medical review, and aggregate safety reporting.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) element B.2.i.8; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SAE report record. Supports audit trail, version control, and reconciliation workflows per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Globally unique case identifier formatted per ICH E2B(R3) standard (country code + organisation identifier + local case number). Required for electronic ICSR submissions to FDA, EMA, and other health authorities.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA GVP Module VI

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for the SUSAR record in the pharmacovigilance data product. Primary key for the silver-layer lakehouse entity.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA Guideline on SUSAR Reporting

description: Foreign key linking to patient.adverse_event. Business justification: A SUSAR classification originates from a clinical adverse event. Linking susar directly to patient.adverse_event enables expedited reporting traceability — regulators (EMA, FDA) require the source cli

Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: SUSARs are reported under a specific IND/CTA application. Linking susar to regulatory.application enables IND-level SUSAR tracking and annual IND safety report compilation per 21 CFR 312.33, tying eac

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: SUSARs tracked by brand for expedited regulatory reporting (FDA, EMA) and brand-specific safety monitoring during clinical development. Critical for development safety update reports (DSURs) and inves

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level SUSAR aggregation: SUSARs are reported per investigational product but aggregated by active substance across trials for development safety update reports (DSURs). Linking to drug_subst

Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: SUSARs are reported to specific health authorities (FDA via IND, EMA via EudraCT/CTIS). A proper FK to health_authority enables authority-specific SUSAR reporting compliance tracking, deadline managem
Reference to the parent Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) from which this SUSAR was identified. Links the SUSAR to its originating safety case in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: SUSAR investigations in clinical trials generate specific costs for expedited assessments, unblinding procedures, and regulatory submissions. Finance tracks these via internal orders linked to trial b

Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: SUSARs are filed specifically for investigational compounds under an IND. Direct FK to discovery.compound is required for expedited 7/15-day IND safety reporting and replaces the denormalized investig

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: SUSARs reference the specific IMP batch and formulation for causality assessment and regulatory submission. investigational_product_name, investigational_product_dose, investigational_product_route on

Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: ICH E2A and GCP require sponsors to notify the responsible investigator of SUSARs within defined timelines. Linking susar to investigator enables automated investigator notification workflows and prov

Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: SUSARs involving investigational products require manufacturing plant identification for quality/manufacturing investigations and batch recall decisions. Linking SUSAR to the manufacturing plant of th

Foreign key linking to patient.patient. Business justification: SUSARs (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions) in clinical trials must be traceable to the specific trial subject for expedited regulatory reporting to FDA (IND safety reports), EMA (EudraVig

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: SUSARs require expedited regulatory reporting (7/15-day clock) and are owned by specific PV org units. Org unit assignment supports submission deadline tracking, regulatory accountability, and GCP ins

Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: SUSARs are filed under a specific IND which belongs to a discovery project. Regulatory teams must associate SUSARs with the development project for IND annual reports, DSUR preparation, and clinical p

Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: SUSARs must be reported against the specific protocol version active at the time of the event per FDA 21 CFR 312.32 and EMA GVP Module VI. susar has trial_id but not protocol_id; the protocol version

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: A SUSAR is always associated with a specific investigational medicinal product under PV surveillance. The susar table captures investigational_product_name as a free-text string but lacks a structured

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SUSARs must be expedited-reported under the IND/CTA regulatory submission. Linking susar to regulatory.submission enables IND safety reporting compliance tracking per 21 CFR 312.32 and EudraCT require
Reference to the clinical trial in which the SUSAR occurred. Used to associate the SUSAR with the relevant Investigational New Drug (IND) application and trial protocol.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation (EU) No 536/2014

description: The unique case number assigned by the Argus Safety / Oracle AERS pharmacovigilance system for this SUSAR. Serves as the system-of-record identifier for cross-system traceability.
reference: Argus Safety System; ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: The treatment arm to which the subject was assigned (e.g., investigational product, comparator, placebo), populated after unblinding. Required for causality assessment and regulatory reporting when unblinding has occurred.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

business_glossary_term: Trial Blinding Status at Time of SUSAR
description: Indicates whether the clinical trial was blinded at the time the SUSAR was identified, and if so, whether unblinding occurred for this case. Unblinding for SUSAR reporting is a critical regulatory and trial integrity decision.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2) Section 4.6; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The timestamp when this SUSAR record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GCP Audit Trail Requirements

description: The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the EMA via the EudraVigilance Clinical Trial Module (EVCTM). Null if not yet submitted or not applicable.
reference: EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline; EU Clinical Trial Regulation No 536/2014; EudraVigilance EVCTM

description: The sponsor's determination of whether the adverse reaction is expected or unexpected based on comparison against the current Investigator's Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI). An 'unexpected' determination is a prerequisite for SUSAR classification.
reference: ICH E2A Section I; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

business_glossary_term: FDA Investigational New Drug (IND) Safety Report Submission Date
description: The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the FDA as an IND safety report under 21 CFR Part 312.32. Null if not yet submitted or not applicable. Used for SLA compliance tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); FDA Guidance on IND Safety Reporting

description: Sequential number of the follow-up report for this SUSAR (e.g., 1 for first follow-up, 2 for second). Zero or null for initial reports. Used to track the version history of regulatory submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section A.1.12; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The date on which the sponsor first received valid minimum information about the adverse reaction, triggering the start of the expedited reporting clock. Day 0 for 7-day and 15-day SLA calculations per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Section III; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The treating investigator's assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse reaction, as reported on the Case Report Form (CRF). Captured separately from sponsor causality per ICH E2A requirements.
reference: ICH E2A Section I; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: Indicates whether the adverse reaction resulted in the death of the subject. A fatal outcome triggers the 7-day expedited reporting clock and is a primary driver of reporting urgency.
reference: ICH E2A Section II; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1)(i); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: Indicates whether the adverse reaction was life-threatening at the time of occurrence (i.e., the subject was at immediate risk of death). Together with is_fatal, determines applicability of the 7-day reporting clock.
reference: ICH E2A Section II; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c)(1)(i); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The timestamp when this SUSAR record was most recently modified in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and change tracking for regulatory submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); GCP Audit Trail Requirements

description: The date on which the qualified medical reviewer completed the medical assessment of this SUSAR. Part of the audit trail for regulatory compliance.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the UK Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA). Null if not applicable.
reference: MHRA Guidance on SUSAR Reporting; UK Clinical Trials Regulations 2004 (SI 2004/1031)

description: The free-text clinical narrative describing the SUSAR, including the sequence of events, medical history, treatment, and outcome. Required for all regulatory submissions per ICH E2B(R3). Classified as confidential due to sensitive clinical content.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section H.1; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The date on which the SUSAR was submitted to the Japan Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA). Null if not applicable.
reference: PMDA Pharmacovigilance Regulations; Japanese Pharmaceutical Affairs Law

business_glossary_term: Adverse Reaction MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) Code
description: The MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) Preferred Term code representing the primary adverse reaction. Mandatory for regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, MHRA, and PMDA.
reference: MedDRA Version Current; ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

business_glossary_term: Adverse Reaction MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) Name
description: The human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term name corresponding to the reaction_meddra_pt_code. Stored for reporting clarity and to avoid repeated lookups against the MedDRA dictionary.
reference: MedDRA Version Current; ICH E2B(R3); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

business_glossary_term: Adverse Reaction MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) Name
description: The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) under which the primary adverse reaction is classified. Used for signal detection, aggregate analysis, and PSUR/PBRER reporting by therapeutic area.
reference: MedDRA Version Current; ICH E2B(R3); ICH E2C(R2) PBRER

description: The date on which the suspected unexpected serious adverse reaction first manifested in the subject. Used for temporal causality assessment and time-to-onset analysis.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section G.k.4.1; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The outcome of the adverse reaction at the time of reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recovered|recovering|not_recovered|recovered_with_sequelae|fatal|unknown — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section G.i.8; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: Current lifecycle status of the SUSAR record within the pharmacovigilance workflow. Drives workflow routing in Argus Safety and regulatory submission tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|submitted|follow_up_pending|closed|invalidated — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A; Argus Safety Workflow; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: Indicates whether this SUSAR is subject to a 7-day expedited reporting clock (fatal or life-threatening) or a 15-day reporting clock (all other serious unexpected reactions). Drives SLA enforcement and regulatory deadline calculation.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline; MHRA Guidance on SUSAR Reporting

description: The calculated calendar date by which the SUSAR must be submitted to all applicable health authorities, derived from initial_receipt_date plus the applicable reporting clock (7 or 15 calendar days). SLA-critical field with criminal liability for non-compliance.
reference: ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32(c); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline; MHRA Guidance

description: The ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by this adverse reaction, which qualifies it as serious. Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fatal|life_threatening|hospitalization_required|prolonged_hospitalization|persistent_disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2A Section II; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The sponsor's assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse reaction. A causality of 'related' or 'not assessable' (where benefit of doubt applies) is required for SUSAR classification.
reference: ICH E2A Section I; 21 CFR Part 312.32(a); EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

business_glossary_term: Subject Age at Time of Reaction
description: The age of the clinical trial subject in years at the time the adverse reaction occurred. Required for regulatory submissions and demographic analysis. Classified as restricted PHI.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section D.2.2; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; GDPR Article 9

description: The biological sex of the clinical trial subject as recorded in the case report. Required for ICH E2B(R3) regulatory submissions and demographic safety analysis. Classified as restricted PHI.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section D.5; HIPAA 45 CFR Part 164; GDPR Article 9

description: Indicates whether this SUSAR record represents an initial report, a follow-up report with additional information, or a final report. Tracks the reporting lifecycle across multiple submissions to health authorities.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

value_regex: Phase I|Phase II|Phase III|Phase IV|Not Applicable
description: The phase of the clinical trial in which the SUSAR occurred (Phase I through Phase IV). Influences regulatory reporting obligations and signal assessment context.
reference: ICH E8(R1); 21 CFR Part 312.21; EMA Clinical Trial Regulation (EU) No 536/2014

description: The date on which the treatment assignment was unblinded for this SUSAR case, if applicable. Null for open-label trials or cases where unblinding was not performed. Critical for audit trail and trial integrity documentation.
reference: ICH E6(R2) Section 4.6; ICH E2A; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA SUSAR Reporting Guideline

description: The globally unique case identifier assigned per ICH E2B(R3) standards, formatted as country code + organisation identifier + local case number. Used for regulatory submissions and cross-authority deduplication.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Section A.1.0.1; EudraVigilance; FDA FAERS

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the suspect or concomitant drug record within an Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR). Primary key of this entity.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA Guideline on Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (GVP) Module VI

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: suspect_drug.drug_authorization_number is a denormalized attribute mapping to a marketing authorization. A proper FK to regulatory.approval enables traceability from adverse event case to the specific

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Suspect drugs in case reports linked to brand for causality assessment, brand-specific safety profiling, and competitive safety benchmarking. Supports brand safety narrative development and medical af

Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: The suspect drug in an ICSR for an investigational compound must link directly to the discovery compound for case processing, duplicate detection, and aggregate analysis. suspect_drug links to pv_prod

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Core PV traceability: suspect_drug must link to the exact drug_product formulation for signal detection, batch-level safety analysis, and regulatory submissions. PV experts expect this link to identif

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level signal detection: the same API appears in multiple formulations. Linking suspect_drug to drug_substance enables cross-product signal aggregation by active ingredient, a standard PV and
Reference to the parent Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) to which this suspect or concomitant drug record belongs. One ICSR may contain multiple drug records.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: PV case processing requires tracing a suspect drug's implicated batch to the actual inventory_lot for distribution scope assessment, cold chain compliance review, and batch recall decisions. Regulator

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: The suspect drug in a trial ICSR is the investigational product. Linking enables batch-level traceability for causality assessment and potential recall decisions per ICH E2B R3. suspect_drug has verba

Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Listedness assessment in ICSR processing requires knowing the current approved label for the suspect drug. Linking suspect_drug to regulatory.label enables automated listedness determination against t

Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: Tracking prescribing physician for suspect drugs enables prescriber-pattern analysis in signal detection, targeted DHPC distribution, and prescriber follow-up for dechallenge/rechallenge outcomes. Cri

Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: US ICSRs require NDC-level product identification for FDA submissions. Enables precise product matching, regulatory compliance, and integration with FDA Structured Product Labeling (SPL) for listednes

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Suspect drugs must link to material master for batch traceability, quality investigations, and manufacturing deviation correlation. Enables root cause analysis when batch_lot_number indicates potentia

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: Suspect drugs in ICSRs should link to the PV product master for authoritative product data. Add pv_product_id FK. Remove product_name, inn_name, atc_code as they normalize to pv_product (verified pv_p

Foreign key linking to patient.treatment_exposure. Business justification: Batch/lot-level safety investigations require tracing a PV suspect drug record to the specific treatment_exposure (lot_number, dose_administered, administration_date). This link is essential for batch

value_regex: drug withdrawn|dose reduced|dose increased|dose not changed|unknown|not applicable
description: Action taken with the suspect or concomitant drug in response to the adverse event (e.g., drug withdrawn, dose reduced, dose not changed, unknown). Critical for dechallenge assessment and causality evaluation. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.8.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Status of the WHO Drug Dictionary coding process for this drug record: coded (coding complete), pending (awaiting coding), not coded (unable to code), auto-coded (coded by automated system), or manually coded (coded by a pharmacovigilance specialist).
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); Argus Safety; EMA GVP Module VI

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the drug was administered to the patient. Used for regional signal detection and regulatory reporting stratification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Total cumulative dose of the drug received by the patient from start to stop date (or to the date of the adverse event). Used in dose-response analyses and exposure-based signal detection. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.5a.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.5a; EMA GVP Module IX

description: Unit of measurement for the cumulative dose amount (e.g., mg, g, IU). Must be interpreted in conjunction with cumulative_dose_amount. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.5b.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.5b; UCUM; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Outcome observed after the drug was withdrawn or dose reduced (dechallenge): whether the adverse event improved or resolved. A positive dechallenge supports causality. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.9.i.3.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.3; WHO-UMC Causality Assessment; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Physical form of the drug product administered to the patient (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, patch, solution). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.9.2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|solution|patch|cream|powder|suspension|inhaler|suppository — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.9.2; EDQM Standard Terms; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Numeric quantity of the drug administered per dose as reported. Must be interpreted in conjunction with dose_unit. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.1a.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.1a; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Unit of measurement for the dose amount (e.g., mg, mcg, g, mg/kg, IU, mL). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.1b. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|mcg|g|mg/kg|IU|mL|mg/mL|% — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.1b; UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Frequency at which the drug was administered (e.g., once daily, twice daily, weekly). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.3. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: once daily|twice daily|three times daily|four times daily|weekly|biweekly|monthly|as needed|continuous|every 8 hours — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.3; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Role of the drug in the adverse event case: suspect (primary drug under investigation), concomitant (taken alongside but not suspected), interacting (suspected drug-drug interaction), or treatment (used to treat the adverse event). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.1.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.1; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Sequential number identifying the position of this drug record within the parent ICSR, used for ordering and referencing multiple drugs in a single case (e.g., drug 1, drug 2). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.1.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.1; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Expiry date of the drug product batch administered to the patient. Used in product quality investigations to assess whether an expired product may have contributed to the adverse event. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.8.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.8; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.137; cGMP

description: Medical condition or disease for which the drug was prescribed or taken, coded using MedDRA terminology. Supports indication-stratified signal detection and benefit-risk assessment. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.7.r.2b.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.7.r.2b; MedDRA; EMA GVP Module IX

description: Indication for drug use as reported verbatim by the reporter, prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for traceability and coding audit. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.7.r.2a.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.7.r.2a; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the drug identity is blinded (True) in the context of a clinical trial (e.g., randomized controlled trial where the patient or investigator does not know the assigned treatment). Affects SUSAR reporting and unblinding procedures.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.2.5; ICH E6(R2) GCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the drug is a product owned or marketed by the reporting company (True) or a competitor/third-party product (False). Determines regulatory reporting obligations and case ownership.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA GVP Module VI

description: WHO Drug Dictionary parent drug code representing the active moiety or parent compound, used to group drug products sharing the same active substance for aggregate signal detection and PSUR analyses.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module IX

description: Outcome observed after the drug was reintroduced following dechallenge (rechallenge): whether the adverse event recurred. A positive rechallenge is strong evidence of causality. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.9.i.4.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.9.i.4; WHO-UMC Causality Assessment; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Timestamp when this suspect or concomitant drug record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module VI.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; GDPR Article 5

description: Timestamp when this suspect or concomitant drug record was last modified in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module VI.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; GDPR Article 5

description: Route by which the drug was administered to the patient (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical). Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.10.2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.10.2; EDQM Standard Terms; FDA 21 CFR Part 314

description: Identifier of this drug record in the originating pharmacovigilance source system (e.g., Argus Safety case drug ID or Oracle AERS drug sequence identifier). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record.
reference: Argus Safety / Oracle AERS; ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 11

description: Date on which the patient started taking the drug. Used to calculate drug exposure duration and temporal relationship to adverse event onset. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.4.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.4; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the patient stopped taking the drug. Nullable if the drug was ongoing at the time of the report. Used to calculate exposure duration and assess dechallenge timing. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.4.r.5.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.4.r.5; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Number of days between the drug start date and the onset of the adverse event. A key temporal metric for causality assessment and signal detection algorithms (e.g., disproportionality analysis).
reference: ICH E2B(R3); WHO-UMC Causality Assessment; EMA GVP Module IX

description: Drug name exactly as reported by the reporter (patient, healthcare professional, or other source) before any coding or standardization. Preserved for traceability and audit. Maps to ICH E2B(R3) element G.k.2.2.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k.2.2; FDA 21 CFR Part 314; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Unique alphanumeric code assigned by the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) to the coded medicinal product. Used for product-level case aggregation, line listing, and signal detection analytics.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); ICH E2B(R3) G.k.2.3.r.1

business_glossary_term: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) Coded Drug Name
description: Standardized drug name as coded against the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD), representing the preferred term for the medicinal product. Enables cross-case aggregation and signal detection.
reference: WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD); ICH E2B(R3) G.k.2.3; EMA GVP Module IX

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the PSUR/PBRER/PADER/DSUR master record in the pharmacovigilance data lakehouse.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA Guideline on PSUR

description: Reference to the marketing authorisation application (NDA/BLA/MAA) or investigational application (IND) under which this periodic safety report is submitted.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: PSURs are tied to specific marketing approvals. The approval record contains approval_date (international birth date reference), approved_indication, and conditions the PSUR must address. Direct link

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: PSURs are structured around ATC classifications for benefit-risk assessment and PSUSA grouping. ATC code is a core PSUR data element per ICH E2C(R2) and EMA PSUSA procedures. Linking psur to atc_class

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: ICH E2C regulatory requirement: PSUR is a substance-based periodic safety report. Linking psur to drug_substance is fundamental to PSUR structure — the report covers all formulations of the active sub
Reference to the primary health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) to which this PSUR is submitted. Determines applicable regulatory requirements and submission format.
reference: FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-level PSUR benefit-risk: EMA and FDA require PSUR benefit-risk evaluation structured by approved indication. Linking psur to indication enables indication-specific cumulative safety summari

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: PSURs are written for a specific active substance identified by INN — a core ICH E2C(R2) requirement. The INN is the primary identifier for PSUR scope definition and PSUSA procedures. Linking psur to

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: PSUR preparation is a discrete, budgeted PV activity (medical writing, data analysis, regulatory affairs). Finance tracks actual vs. planned costs per PSUR cycle via internal orders. Pharma finance co

Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: PSURs assess whether the current label accurately reflects the benefit-risk profile. Linking psur to the label version under review enables tracking of label_update_required decisions against the spec

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: PSUR is submitted by specific MAH legal entity. Determines signatory authority, regulatory obligations, and submission routing. Critical for regulatory compliance and multi-entity portfolio management

Foreign key linking to finance.milestone_payment. Business justification: In pharma licensing and co-development agreements, PSUR data lock points or favorable benefit-risk conclusions trigger contractual milestone payments. Finance milestone_payment tracks these triggering

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: PSURs reference patent status for benefit-risk assessment context, especially when evaluating whether formulation changes covered by new patents affect safety profile. Patent expiry impacts market dyn

Foreign key linking to regulatory.post_approval_commitment. Business justification: PSURs are frequently submitted as fulfillment of post-approval commitments. Linking psur to post_approval_commitment enables tracking of PSUR-type commitment fulfillment status, a key regulatory compl
Reference to the medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) that is the subject of this periodic safety report.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2; EMA PSUSA Procedure

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: PSURs are authored and owned by specific PV/regulatory org units. Org unit assignment is required for PSUR schedule management, resource planning, QPPV accountability, and regulatory audit — a standar

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PSURs are submitted to health authorities in specific countries/regions. Country is a core PSUR scheduling and submission tracking attribute per ICH E2C(R2). A proper FK to country enables regional PS

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: PSURs are submitted as formal regulatory submissions in the eCTD dossier. Linking psur to regulatory.submission enables tracking of PSUR submission sequence numbers, validation status, and eCTD operat

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: PSURs must reference ongoing and completed clinical trials contributing safety data in the reporting interval per GVP Module VII. A direct FK to the primary trial enables the PSUR author to pull trial

Date on which the QPPV (or designated signatory) formally approved the PSUR for regulatory submission. Represents the final internal milestone before submission and is an auditable event per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: EMA GVP Module I; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 23; 21 CFR Part 11; SOPs

description: Overall benefit-risk evaluation conclusion for the product as determined by the medical reviewer for this PSUR period. A finding of unfavourable or inconclusive may trigger regulatory action, label update, or risk management plan revision.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 17; EMA PSUSA Guideline; FDA PADER Guidance; ICH E2F

description: Timestamp when this PSUR record was first created in the pharmacovigilance data lakehouse, providing the audit trail creation event per 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR data governance requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5; SOX; ICH E2C(R2)

business_glossary_term: Cumulative Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) Count
description: Total number of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) received from the IBD through the data lock point across all reporting intervals, providing the full post-marketing safety case history for the product.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 9; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA PSUSA Guideline

description: Total cumulative patient-years of exposure to the product from IBD through data lock point, calculated from treatment duration data. Used as the primary denominator for incidence rate calculations in benefit-risk assessment.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 6.2; EMA PSUSA Guideline; ICH E2E Pharmacovigilance Planning

description: Estimated cumulative number of patients exposed to the product from the IBD through the data lock point, derived from post-marketing surveillance, clinical trials, and sales data. Used as denominator for cumulative incidence rate calculations.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 6; FDA PADER Guidance; EMA PSUSA Guideline

description: The cut-off date up to which all safety data (adverse events, literature, clinical studies) are included in this PSUR. No new data arising after this date is incorporated into the current report period.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2.3; FDA 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2); EMA PSUSA Guideline

description: Date on which the initial draft of the PSUR was completed by the lead author and made available for internal review. Used to track preparation milestones against the submission deadline.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA GVP Module VII; SOPs; Veeva Vault RIM Workflow

description: Outcome of the health authority's assessment of this PSUR, indicating any regulatory action required. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|no_action|label_update|rmp_update|referral|suspension|withdrawal — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]
reference: EMA PSUSA Procedure; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; ICH E2C(R2) Section 17; EMA GVP Module VII

description: The date of first marketing authorisation for the product in any country worldwide, used as the anchor date for calculating PSUR submission deadlines and reporting intervals per ICH E2C(R2). SLA-critical field.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2.2; EMA PSUSA Guideline; WHO IBD Definition

business_glossary_term: Interval Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) Count
description: Total number of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) received during the current reporting interval (between interval_start_date and data_lock_point), including both spontaneous and solicited reports.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 9; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA PSUSA Guideline; ICH E2B(R3)

description: End date of the current PSUR reporting interval, which is typically equal to the data lock point. Defines the close of the reporting window for interval case counts and exposure data.

Number of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) reported during the current reporting interval. SAEs include fatal, life-threatening, hospitalisation-requiring, or permanently disabling events per ICH E2A criteria.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2C(R2) Section 9; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004

description: Start date of the current PSUR reporting interval (the period covered by this report). Together with interval_end_date defines the reporting window for interval case counts and exposure data.

business_glossary_term: Interval Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Count
description: Number of Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) identified during the current reporting interval. SUSARs require expedited reporting and are tracked separately for signal detection purposes.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E2C(R2); FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 17

description: Indicates whether the safety findings in this PSUR require an update to the product label (SmPC, Package Insert, or Patient Information Leaflet). A True value initiates a label change management workflow.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 17; FDA 21 CFR 314.70; EMA SmPC Guideline; MHRA Guidance on Labelling

description: Date on which the medical reviewer completed the clinical and benefit-risk review of the PSUR draft. Milestone in the PSUR preparation workflow tracked against the submission deadline.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 17; EMA GVP Module VII; SOPs

description: Number of new safety signals identified during the reporting interval through signal detection activities (disproportionality analysis, literature review, spontaneous reports). Drives benefit-risk re-evaluation.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 16; EMA Guideline on Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (GVP) Module IX; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

description: EMA-assigned procedure number for the PSUR Single Assessment (PSUSA) process applicable to centrally authorised products in the EU. Used to track the PSUSA workflow and outcome in Veeva Vault RIM and RIMS.
reference: EMA PSUSA Procedure; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 107e; EMA GVP Module VII

description: Date on which quality control review of the PSUR was completed, verifying accuracy of case counts, exposure data, and regulatory formatting prior to QPPV approval and submission.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA GVP Module VII; MasterControl/TrackWise QMS; SOPs; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to this PSUR/PBRER/PADER/DSUR submission, used in regulatory correspondence and tracking systems such as Argus Safety and Veeva Vault RIM.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA PSUR Single Assessment (PSUSA); FDA 21 CFR 314.81

description: Current lifecycle workflow state of the PSUR record, tracking progression from initial drafting through medical review, QC, approval, and regulatory submission. Drives SLA monitoring and escalation workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|qc_review|approved|submitted|accepted|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA PSUSA Procedure; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; Veeva Vault RIM Workflow

description: Classification of the periodic aggregate safety report: PSUR (Periodic Safety Update Report), PBRER (Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report per ICH E2C R2), PADER (Periodic Adverse Drug Experience Report per FDA), DSUR (Development Safety Update Report for investigational products), or PSUSA (PSUR Single Assessment for EU centralised procedure).
reference: ICH E2C(R2); ICH E2F; FDA 21 CFR 312.32; EMA PSUSA Guideline

description: Regulatory-mandated submission frequency for this PSUR, which varies by product age, risk profile, and authority requirements. Drives automated SLA deadline calculation from the IBD.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2.4; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 107b; FDA 21 CFR 314.81

description: Sequential ordinal number of this PSUR reporting period for the product (e.g., 1st, 2nd, 3rd PSUR), enabling chronological tracking of the full PSUR history for a product.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2.3; EMA PSUSA Procedure

business_glossary_term: Risk Management Plan (RMP) Update Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the benefit-risk findings in this PSUR necessitate an update to the product's Risk Management Plan (RMP). A True value triggers a formal RMP revision workflow in the pharmacovigilance system.
