reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 17; EMA GVP Module V (Risk Management Systems); EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004

description: Native record identifier from the originating pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety, Oracle AERS, or Veeva Vault RIM) for this PSUR record, enabling traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2C(R2); EMA GVP Module II (Pharmacovigilance System Master File); SOX

description: Actual date on which the PSUR was submitted to the health authority. Compared against submission_deadline to determine on-time or late submission for regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA PSUSA Procedure; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Mandatory regulatory deadline by which this PSUR must be submitted to the applicable health authority, calculated from the IBD and reporting frequency. SLA-critical field monitored for compliance.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 107b; FDA 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2)

description: Electronic or paper format used for regulatory submission of this PSUR. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the standard for FDA and EMA submissions; NeeS is a non-eCTD electronic submission format.
reference: FDA eCTD Guidance; EMA eCTD Guideline; ICH M8 eCTD Specification; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PSUR record in the data lakehouse, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5; SOX; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacovigilance action record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete regulatory/safety action with a defined lifecycle triggered by a validated signal, PSUR finding, RMP update, or health authority request.
reference: ICH E2E Pharmacovigilance Planning; 21 CFR Part 314; EMA GVP Module V

Reference to the Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) or Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER) finding that triggered this action. Nullable when the action is not PSUR-driven.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA GVP Module VII; 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_agreement. Business justification: PV actions include partner notification actions (notified_authorities field, dhpc_distribution_scope) that are governed by PV agreements. When a safety action requires notifying a partner (licensee, c
Reference to the medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) subject to this pharmacovigilance action. Links to the master product record for the affected compound or finished dosage form.
reference: EMA XEVMPD; FDA NDC Directory; ICH E2B(R3)

Reference to the Risk Management Plan (RMP) associated with or updated by this pharmacovigilance action. Nullable when the action is not RMP-driven.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; ICH E2E; FDA REMS Guidance

description: Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.sae_report. Business justification: SAE events can trigger PV actions (regulatory notifications, safety measures). Add sae_report_id FK (nullable - only when action is triggered by SAE). The triggering_event_type field indicates the tri
Reference to the validated safety signal that triggered this pharmacovigilance action. Links the action to its upstream signal detection record for traceability and regulatory reporting.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; FDA Guidance on Signal Detection

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.susar. Business justification: SUSAR events can trigger PV actions (label changes, regulatory notifications). Add susar_id FK (nullable - only when action is triggered by SUSAR). The triggering_event_type field indicates the trigge

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Regulatory PV actions (label updates, DHPC, protocol amendments) are often triggered by safety findings from a specific clinical trial. Linking pv_action to trial enables traceability from regulatory

Foreign key linking to regulatory.variation. Business justification: Safety-driven PV actions result in Type II variations (EU) or Prior Approval Supplements (US). Linking pv_action to the resulting variation enables tracking of safety-driven variation timelines and re

Detailed narrative description of the pharmacovigilance action, including the specific risk minimization measures to be implemented, rationale, and expected patient safety impact. Captured from Argus Safety case narrative or Veeva Vault RIM action record.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Externally-known business identifier for the PV action, formatted as PVA-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in regulatory correspondence, authority notifications, and cross-system tracking within Argus Safety/Oracle AERS and Veeva Vault RIM.
reference: ICH E2E; EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Current lifecycle state of the pharmacovigilance action, tracking progression from initiation through completion or cancellation. Drives workflow routing in safety management systems.
reference: ICH E2E; EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Classification of the regulatory or safety action being taken. Includes labeling updates (CCDS/SmPC/USPI changes), Dear Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC), restricted indication, market suspension/withdrawal, Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) modification, Risk Management Plan (RMP) update trigger, and Post-Authorization Safety Study (PASS) initiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: labeling_update|dhpc|restricted_indication|market_suspension|market_withdrawal|rems_modification|rmp_update|pass_initiation — promote to reference product]
reference: EMA GVP Module V; FDA REMS Guidance; ICH E2C(R2); 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Date on which the pharmacovigilance action was fully implemented and closed. Compared against target_completion_date to assess timeliness and regulatory compliance. Nullable until action is completed.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314.81; ICH E2E

description: Source system case or action number from Argus Safety/Oracle AERS, the operational system of record for pharmacovigilance. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source PV system.
reference: Argus Safety/Oracle AERS; 21 CFR Part 11; EMA GVP Module I

description: Date on which the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA, etc.) was formally notified of this pharmacovigilance action. Nullable when notification is not required or not yet submitted.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA GVP Module V; MHRA Guidance on PV

description: Indicates whether this pharmacovigilance action requires formal notification to one or more health authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, etc.) prior to or upon implementation. Drives regulatory submission workflows.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA GVP Module V; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Assessment of how this pharmacovigilance action affects the overall benefit-risk balance of the medicinal product. Informs PSUR/PBRER benefit-risk conclusions and regulatory authority communications.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA GVP Module VII; FDA Benefit-Risk Framework

description: Timestamp when this PV action record was first captured in the system (Argus Safety/Oracle AERS or Veeva Vault). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E6(R2); EMA GVP Module I

business_glossary_term: Dear Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC) Distribution Scope
description: Geographic scope of the Dear Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC) distribution when action_type is dhpc. Indicates whether the communication is national, regional, or global. Not applicable for non-DHPC actions.
reference: EMA GVP Module XV; 21 CFR Part 200.5; MHRA DHCP Guidance

description: Date on which the effectiveness assessment of this pharmacovigilance action was completed or is scheduled. Supports periodic benefit-risk evaluation and regulatory reporting cycles.
reference: EMA GVP Module XVI; FDA REMS Assessment Guidance; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Outcome of the post-implementation effectiveness assessment, indicating whether the pharmacovigilance action successfully minimized the identified risk. Informs subsequent signal management and RMP update decisions.
reference: EMA GVP Module XVI; FDA REMS Assessment Guidance; ICH E2E

description: Status of the post-implementation effectiveness assessment for this pharmacovigilance action. Tracks whether the action achieved its intended risk minimization objective, as required by EMA GVP Module XVI and FDA REMS assessment requirements.
reference: EMA GVP Module XVI; FDA REMS Assessment Guidance; ICH E2E

description: Date on which this pharmacovigilance action was escalated to senior management or the QPPV. Nullable when escalation_flag is false. Supports governance and oversight reporting.
reference: EMA GVP Module I; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Indicates whether this pharmacovigilance action has been escalated due to missed deadlines, critical safety findings, or authority pressure. Triggers executive and QPPV notification workflows.
reference: EMA GVP Module I; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Document reference number or Veeva Vault document ID for the evidence package demonstrating that the pharmacovigilance action has been implemented (e.g., updated label version, DHPC distribution confirmation, REMS modification approval letter).
reference: EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314.81; Veeva Vault RIM

description: The date on which the pharmacovigilance action was formally initiated or triggered. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp (day-level precision) for regulatory timeline tracking and compliance reporting.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314.81; ICH E2E

description: Type of labeling document affected when action_type is labeling_update. Identifies whether the Company Core Data Sheet (CCDS), Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC), US Prescribing Information (USPI), Patient Information Leaflet (PIL), or package insert is being updated. Null or not_applicable for non-labeling actions.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA SmPC Guideline; 21 CFR Part 201; Directive 2001/83/EC

description: Specific section(s) of the labeling document being updated (e.g., Section 4.4 Special Warnings, Section 4.8 Undesirable Effects, Boxed Warning, Contraindications). Applicable only for labeling_update action type.
reference: EMA SmPC Guideline; 21 CFR Part 201.57; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Comma-separated list of health authority codes (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) that have been or must be notified of this action. Supports multi-jurisdictional regulatory tracking.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314.81; PMDA Pharmacovigilance Regulations

description: Risk-based priority classification of the pharmacovigilance action, determining escalation urgency and resource allocation. Critical actions typically involve immediate patient safety risks requiring expedited regulatory response.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; ICH E2E; FDA Guidance on Signal Detection

description: Mandatory deadline imposed by a health authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) for completion or notification of this pharmacovigilance action. Distinct from the internally-set target_completion_date. Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.81; EMA GVP Module V; MHRA Guidance on PV; PMDA Regulations

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Name
description: Name of the FDA-approved Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program being modified when action_type is rems_modification. Nullable for non-REMS actions.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.520; FDA REMS Guidance; FDAAA Section 505-1

description: Organizational unit or party accountable for implementing and completing this pharmacovigilance action. Determines workflow ownership and escalation paths within the safety management system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pv_team|regulatory_affairs|medical_affairs|manufacturing|commercial|external_cro|external_cmo — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EMA GVP Module I; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 314

description: Planned date by which the pharmacovigilance action must be fully implemented. Drives SLA monitoring, regulatory deadline tracking, and escalation workflows. Distinct from actual completion date.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; 21 CFR Part 314.81; ICH E2E

description: Free-text or structured reference number identifying the specific triggering event (e.g., signal case number, PSUR submission number, authority correspondence reference). Provides cross-system traceability.
reference: EMA GVP Module V; ICH E2C(R2); 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Classification of the upstream event or finding that necessitated this PV action. Distinguishes between signal validation outcomes, PSUR/PBRER findings, RMP periodic reviews, health authority requests, SUSAR clusters, out-of-specification (OOS) trends, and literature review findings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: signal_validation|psur_finding|rmp_review|authority_request|susar_cluster|oos_trend|literature_review — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this PV action record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module I.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EMA GVP Module I; ICH E6(R2)

description: Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM or QualityDocs for the regulatory submission package, labeling document, or DHPC associated with this action. Supports document management and regulatory submission traceability.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; 21 CFR Part 11; EMA GVP Module V

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each pharmacovigilance regulatory submission record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR Part 314; EMA EudraVigilance Technical Specifications

description: Reference to the regulatory application (NDA, BLA, MAA, IND) under which this pharmacovigilance submission is filed. Links to the regulatory.application product. Enables traceability between safety submissions and the marketing authorisation or investigational application.
reference: 21 CFR 314.81; 21 CFR 312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Periodic safety reports (PSURs, PADERs) and signal-driven submissions pertain to specific marketing approvals. Linking pv_submission to approval enables authority-level benefit-risk tracking tied to t

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Regulatory safety submissions (ICSRs, PSURs, SUSARs) must reference commercial brand for health authority tracking, compliance audit trails, and brand-level submission history. Essential for regulator

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Submission-level product tracking: pv_submission records regulatory submissions to health authorities. Linking to drug_product enables product-level submission tracking for regulatory intelligence, co

Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Every PV submission targets a specific health authority. pv_submission.health_authority_code is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK enables authority-specific PV submission compliance dashboar

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original pv_submission_id when this record represents a resubmission, follow-up, or amendment. Null for original submissions. Enables reconstruction of the full resubmission history chain.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.7; EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide

description: Reference to the Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) that is the subject of this submission, when submission_type is an ICSR variant. Links to the source safety case record in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS. Null for aggregate report submissions (PSUR, PBRER, DSUR).
reference: ICH E2B(R3); 21 CFR 314.81; EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety Data Model

description: Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.psur. Business justification: Some PV submissions are for PSUR reports (submission_type indicates report type). Add psur_id FK (nullable - only populated for PSUR submission types). This links the submission transaction to the PSU
Reference to the medicinal product (Drug Product / Drug Substance) that is the subject of the safety report or aggregate submission. Links to the master product record.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) G.k; EMA Product Dictionary; FDA NDC Directory

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: PV submissions are executed by specific regulatory affairs or PV org units. Org unit ownership is required for submission workload reporting, regulatory deadline accountability, and inspection readine

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.rmp. Business justification: Some PV submissions are for RMP reports. Add rmp_id FK (nullable - only populated for RMP submission types). Remove rmp_version as it normalizes to rmp.version_number.

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.sae_report. Business justification: SAE reports generate regulatory submissions. Add sae_report_id FK (nullable - only populated for SAE submission types). This links the submission transaction to the SAE master record.

Reference to the pharmacovigilance safety signal record that prompted or is associated with this submission, where applicable. Links to the signal detection and management record. Null for routine ICSR submissions not linked to a formal signal.
reference: EMA GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; FDA Guidance on Safety Reporting

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Submission target country determines format (E2B R2/R3, CIOMS), gateway (EudraVigilance, FDA ESG, MHRA Yellow Card), and timelines. Operational requirement for submission routing and compliance tracki

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: PV submissions (E2B transmissions, PSURs, RMPs) are formal regulatory submissions tracked in the regulatory dossier. Linking pv_submission to regulatory.submission enables reconciliation of PV transmi

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: The legal entity making a PV submission is a core regulatory requirement — health authorities require MAH legal entity identification on all submissions. sender_organisation and sender_identifier are

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.susar. Business justification: SUSAR records generate regulatory submissions (expedited reporting). Add susar_id FK (nullable - only populated for SUSAR submission types). One SUSAR can have multiple submissions (initial + follow-u

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Expedited IND safety reports (15-day reports) are submitted to health authorities in the context of a specific clinical trial per FDA 21 CFR 312.32. Linking pv_submission to trial enables regulatory t

Foreign key linking to regulatory.variation. Business justification: Safety-driven variations (urgent safety restrictions, Type II variations) generate pv_submission records for authority notification. Linking pv_submission to variation enables end-to-end tracking of s

Technical acknowledgement code returned by the health authority gateway per HL7 v2 / E2B messaging standards. AA = Application Accept; AE = Application Error; CA = Commit Accept; CE = Commit Error; TA = Technical Accept; TE = Technical Error. Drives automated error handling and resubmission workflows.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) Implementation Guide; HL7 v2 Acknowledgement Codes; EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules

description: Timestamp when the technical acknowledgement (ACK) message was received from the health authority gateway confirming successful receipt of the submission. Distinct from substantive acceptance. Used for SLA compliance evidence.
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Gateway Technical Specifications; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E2B(R3) Implementation Guide

description: The case number from the Argus Safety / Oracle AERS pharmacovigilance system that is the source of record for this submission. Enables direct traceability from the submission record back to the full safety case in the operational PV system.
reference: Argus Safety / Oracle AERS; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI

description: The case or report reference number assigned by the receiving health authority upon acceptance of the submission (e.g., EudraVigilance case number, FDA FAERS case ID). Used for follow-up queries and regulatory correspondence. Populated after authority acceptance.
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; PMDA E2B Transmission Guide

description: Timestamp when this pharmacovigilance submission record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and 21 CFR Part 11 / Annex 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU Annex 11; GDPR Article 5(1)(f); ICH Q10

description: The date the company first received the minimum valid safety information constituting a reportable case (Day 0 for regulatory clock purposes). This is the anchor date from which the 7-day or 15-day expedited reporting deadline is calculated for ICSRs. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.4.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.4; ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(1)

description: Number of calendar days remaining between the current date and the regulatory_deadline at the time of last record update. Negative values indicate overdue submissions. Used for SLA monitoring dashboards and automatic escalation triggers. Note: this is a snapshot value captured at record update time, not a live calculation.
reference: ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 314.81

description: Structured error code returned by the health authority gateway or E2B validation engine upon rejection or technical failure (e.g., EudraVigilance business rule error code, FAERS conformance error code). Enables automated error categorisation and routing to the appropriate remediation team.
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E2B(R3) Validation Rules

description: The specific electronic gateway or web portal used to transmit this submission to the health authority. Determines technical format and protocol requirements. EudraVigilance Gateway and EVWEB are EMA channels; FAERS Gateway is the FDA channel for ICSRs; MHRA Sentinel is the UK post-Brexit channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EUDRAVIGILANCE_GATEWAY|EUDRAVIGILANCE_EVWEB|FAERS_GATEWAY|MHRA_SENTINEL|PMDA_GATEWAY|NMPA_GATEWAY|VIGIBASE_WHO|DIRECT_EMAIL|PAPER — promote to reference product]
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Technical Specifications; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; MHRA Sentinel System Guidance; PMDA E2B Transmission Guide

description: Indicates whether this submission is subject to expedited regulatory reporting requirements (7-day or 15-day clock). True = expedited; False = periodic/non-expedited. Drives SLA escalation logic and compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH E2D; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(1); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the submission was transmitted on or before the regulatory_deadline. True = submitted on time; False = submitted late. Key compliance KPI field populated at time of submission. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and CAPA tracking.
reference: ICH E2D; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 314.81; PIC/S GVP Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this submission relates to a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) from a clinical trial, which carries specific expedited reporting obligations to both health authorities and investigators under ICH E2A and GCP regulations.
reference: ICH E2A; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 312.32; EU Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014

description: The technical format standard used for the submission message. E2B_R2_XML = ICH E2B(R2) XML format (used by some national authorities); E2B_R3_XML = ICH E2B(R3) XML format (required by EMA EudraVigilance and FDA from 2022); ECTD = Electronic Common Technical Document format (for aggregate reports); PDF = portable document format for ad-hoc communications; PAPER = legacy paper submission.
reference: ICH E2B(R2); ICH E2B(R3); ICH M8 eCTD; EMA EudraVigilance Technical Specifications; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide

description: The mandatory regulatory deadline date by which this submission must be transmitted to the health authority, calculated from the date of first receipt (Day 0) of the safety information. For expedited ICSRs: Day 7 (fatal/life-threatening) or Day 15 (serious). For PSURs/PBRERs: per approved data lock point schedule. SLA-critical field used for automatic escalation.
reference: ICH E2D; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(1); EMA GVP Module VI; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Free-text or structured description of the reason(s) for rejection returned by the health authority gateway or authority reviewer when submission_status is REJECTED. Includes E2B validation error codes and human-readable explanations. Used for root cause analysis and CAPA.
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E2B(R3) Implementation Guide

business_glossary_term: Report Period End Date (Data Lock Point)
description: End date of the data collection period (Data Lock Point, DLP) for aggregate reports (PSUR, PBRER, PADER, DSUR). Defines the cut-off date for safety data included in the report. Null for individual case (ICSR) submissions.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); ICH E2F; EMA GVP Module VII; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2)

description: Start date of the data collection period covered by this submission. Applicable primarily to aggregate reports (PSUR, PBRER, PADER, DSUR) defining the International Birth Date (IBD) or period start. Null for individual case (ICSR) submissions.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); ICH E2F; EMA GVP Module VII; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2)

description: Qualifier indicating whether this is an initial report, a follow-up update to a previously submitted case, or a nullification (withdrawal) of a previously submitted report. Corresponds directly to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2 field.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2; EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide

description: Name or identifier of the Qualified Person for Pharmacovigilance (QPPV) responsible for this submission under EU GVP regulations. The QPPV bears legal accountability for the completeness and accuracy of pharmacovigilance submissions to the EMA and EU member states.
reference: EMA GVP Module I; EU Directive 2001/83/EC Article 104; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Number of times this submission has been resubmitted to the health authority following a rejection or technical error. Zero indicates the original submission was accepted without resubmission. Used to track submission quality and identify systemic data quality issues.
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E2B(R3) Implementation Guide

description: The principal real-world timestamp when the pharmacovigilance report was transmitted to the health authority gateway or portal. This is the legally significant submission event time used for regulatory deadline compliance calculations.
reference: 21 CFR 314.81; EMA GVP Module VI; ICH E2D; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: Current lifecycle state of the pharmacovigilance submission workflow. DRAFT = in preparation; PENDING = queued for transmission; SUBMITTED = transmitted to health authority gateway; ACKNOWLEDGED = technical acknowledgement received from gateway; ACCEPTED = substantively accepted by authority; REJECTED = rejected with errors requiring correction; RESUBMITTED = corrected and resubmitted after rejection; WITHDRAWN = voluntarily withdrawn by the company. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|PENDING|SUBMITTED|ACKNOWLEDGED|ACCEPTED|REJECTED|RESUBMITTED|WITHDRAWN — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E2B(R3) Implementation Guide

description: Classification of the pharmacovigilance submission by regulatory report type. ICSR_EXPEDITED_7DAY = fatal/life-threatening Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) requiring 7-calendar-day reporting; ICSR_EXPEDITED_15DAY = serious ICSR requiring 15-calendar-day reporting; ICSR_NON_EXPEDITED = non-expedited periodic ICSR; PSUR = Periodic Safety Update Report; PBRER = Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report; PADER = Periodic Adverse Drug Experience Report; DSUR = Development Safety Update Report. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ICSR_EXPEDITED_7DAY|ICSR_EXPEDITED_15DAY|ICSR_NON_EXPEDITED|PSUR|PBRER|PADER|DSUR|AD_HOC_SAFETY_COMM|REMS_ASSESSMENT|SUSAR — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E2D; ICH E2C(R2); ICH E2F; 21 CFR 314.81; EMA Guideline on Good Pharmacovigilance Practices (GVP) Module VI

description: Sequential version number of this submission for a given safety case or aggregate report. Version 1 = initial submission; Version 2+ = follow-up or amendment submissions. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2 (report type: initial/follow-up/nullification).
reference: ICH E2B(R3) C.1.2; EMA EudraVigilance Business Rules; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide

description: Unique message transmission identifier assigned by the health authority gateway at the point of receipt (e.g., EudraVigilance message ID, FAERS transmission reference). Distinct from the authority_case_number; this is the technical envelope identifier used for gateway-level tracing and acknowledgement matching.
reference: EMA EudraVigilance Gateway Technical Specifications; FDA FAERS Technical Conformance Guide; ICH E2B(R3) A.1.0.1

description: Timestamp when this pharmacovigilance submission record was most recently modified in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and 21 CFR Part 11 / Annex 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU Annex 11; GDPR Article 5(1)(f); ICH Q10

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Risk Management Plan record in the pharmacovigilance data lakehouse.
reference: EU GVP Module V; ICH E2E

description: Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: RMP risk minimization commitments (REMS, additional monitoring) are mandatory inputs to market access strategy formulation. HTA bodies and payers require RMP alignment before granting reimbursement. M
Reference to the regulatory application (NDA, BLA, MAA) to which this RMP is attached or submitted as a condition of approval.
reference: EU GVP Module V; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004

description: Reference to the marketing authorization or approval record that mandated or is associated with this RMP.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: RMPs are approved by health authorities in specific countries. Country of the approving authority is a core RMP tracking attribute per EU GVP Module V. A proper FK enables country-specific RMP version

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: RMP safety specification: EMA GVP Module V requires the RMP safety specification to characterize the active substance's known and potential risks. Linking rmp to drug_substance enables substance-level

Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: RMPs are submitted to and assessed by specific health authorities (EMA PRAC, national competent authorities). rmp.health_authority_code is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK enables authority

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-specific risk minimization: RMP risk minimization measures (educational materials, REMS, restricted access) are defined per approved indication. EMA GVP Module V requires indication-level r

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: RMPs are written for a specific active substance identified by INN — a core EU GVP Module V requirement. The INN defines the RMP scope and is required for EMA submission. Linking rmp to inn_registry i

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: RMP preparation, maintenance, and effectiveness evaluation are significant, budgeted PV activities. Each RMP version update and submission cycle incurs costs (consultants, regulatory affairs, medical

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Risk Management Plan ownership by legal entity determines regulatory accountability, update obligations, and PRAC submission responsibility. Essential for lifecycle safety management and regulatory co

Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: RMP risk minimization measures frequently mandate patient access programs (controlled distribution, pregnancy prevention registries, educational programs). The market patient_access_program operationa
Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding version of this RMP, enabling full version chain reconstruction and audit trail for regulatory inspections.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA version control guidance; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: RMPs are managed by specific PV/regulatory org units responsible for risk minimization activities. Org unit ownership is required for RMP update scheduling, effectiveness evaluation accountability, an

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: RMPs are submitted as part of the regulatory dossier (eCTD Module 1.8.2). Linking rmp to regulatory.submission enables tracking which submission sequence included the RMP version, supporting variation

Current lifecycle status of the RMP document with the relevant health authority: draft (in preparation), submitted (filed with authority), approved (accepted by authority), rejected (not accepted), superseded (replaced by newer version), or withdrawn.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011 Rev 2; FDA REMS guidance

business_glossary_term: Argus Safety RMP Source System ID
description: Source system identifier for this RMP record as stored in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS, used for data lineage, reconciliation, and integration with the pharmacovigilance safety database.
reference: Argus Safety / Oracle AERS; 21 CFR Part 11; EU GVP Module I

description: Date on which the health authority formally approved or accepted this version of the RMP. Null if not yet approved.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; FDA REMS guidance

description: Overall benefit-risk conclusion documented in this RMP version, reflecting the current assessment of the product's benefit-risk balance as evaluated by the marketing authorization holder and/or health authority.
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II; ICH E2E; EMA benefit-risk methodology

description: Timestamp when this RMP record was first created in the pharmacovigilance data system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GVP Module I.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU GVP Module I; ICH E6(R2)

description: Date from which this version of the RMP becomes operationally effective and binding for pharmacovigilance activities and risk minimization measures.
reference: EU GVP Module V; ICH E2E; FDA REMS guidance

description: Description of the plan to evaluate the effectiveness of risk minimization measures, including key performance indicators, data sources, and evaluation timelines as required by EU GVP Module XVI.
reference: EU GVP Module XVI; EMA/204715/2012; FDA REMS assessment guidance

description: Number of important identified risks documented in the safety specification of this RMP version. An identified risk is one for which there is adequate evidence of an association with the medicinal product.
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II Safety Specification; ICH E2E Section 2.2

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this RMP record in the data system, used for change tracking, data lineage, and regulatory audit trail compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU GVP Module I; GDPR Article 5(1)(e)

description: Number of missing information items documented in the safety specification, representing gaps in knowledge about the safety of the medicinal product (e.g., use in pregnancy, pediatric use).
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II Safety Specification; ICH E2E Section 2.4

description: Planned date for the next periodic review and potential update of the RMP, as agreed with the health authority or per internal PV schedule.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011 Rev 2

description: Number of Post-Authorization Safety Study (PASS) commitments included in this RMP version, representing studies imposed as conditions of marketing authorization or undertaken voluntarily.
reference: EU GVP Module VIII; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 9(4)(cb); EU GVP Module V Part III

description: Number of important potential risks documented in the safety specification of this RMP version. A potential risk is one for which there is some basis for suspicion but insufficient evidence to confirm.
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II Safety Specification; ICH E2E Section 2.3

description: Indicates whether the pharmacovigilance plan for this RMP includes only routine PV activities (spontaneous reporting, PSUR/PBRER) or also includes additional PV activities such as PASS studies, registries, or enhanced surveillance.
reference: EU GVP Module V Part III; ICH E2E Section 3

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Name
description: Official name of the FDA-approved REMS program associated with this RMP, if applicable (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin, TIRF REMS Access Program).
reference: FDA REMS 21 CFR 505-1; FDA REMS database

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) is required by the FDA as part of the US risk management plan for this product. Applicable only for FDA submissions.
reference: FDA REMS 21 CFR 505-1; FDA Guidance on REMS

description: Indicates whether risk minimization measures in this RMP are limited to routine measures (product labeling, SmPC, PIL) or also include additional risk minimization measures (educational materials, controlled access programs, REMS for US).
reference: EU GVP Module XVI; FDA REMS 21 CFR 505-1; ICH E2E Section 4

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the RMP document, used in regulatory submissions and authority correspondence (e.g., RMP-2024-001).
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011 Rev 2; FDA REMS guidance

description: Classification of the RMP format based on the regulatory authority template: EU GVP Module V (EMA/MHRA), FDA Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS), PMDA RMP (Japan), or WHO RMP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EU_RMP|FDA_REMS|PMDA_RMP|MHRA_RMP|WHO_RMP — promote to reference product]
reference: EU GVP Module V; FDA REMS 21 CFR 505-1; PMDA RMP Guidance; ICH E2E

description: Narrative summary of the safety specification section of the RMP, covering the epidemiology of the indication, non-clinical safety findings, clinical trial safety data, and post-marketing experience.
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II; ICH E2E Section 2

description: The approved or proposed therapeutic indication(s) covered by this RMP, as defined in the marketing authorization or clinical development program (e.g., treatment of relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma).
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II; ICH E2E Section 2

description: Description of the target patient population covered by this RMP, including age groups, special populations (pediatric, geriatric, renal/hepatic impairment), and any population restrictions.
reference: EU GVP Module V Part II; ICH E2E Section 2; EMA Pediatric Regulation

description: Reference identifier(s) to pharmacovigilance signal detection records or PRAC signal assessments that triggered or informed this RMP version update.
reference: EU GVP Module IX (Signal Management); EMA PRAC signal assessment; Argus Safety

description: Date on which this version of the RMP was formally submitted to the health authority as part of a regulatory dossier or standalone submission.
reference: EU GVP Module V; FDA 21 CFR 314.81; EMA submission guidelines

description: Date on which this RMP version was superseded by a newer version. Null for the currently active version.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA version control guidance

description: Full descriptive title of the Risk Management Plan document, typically including the product name, indication, and version.
reference: EU GVP Module V; ICH E2E

description: Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) where the RMP document is stored and managed as part of the regulatory dossier.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; eCTD Module 1.8.2; EU GVP Module V

description: Documented rationale for updating this RMP version, including the trigger (e.g., new safety signal, PSUR outcome, authority request, new indication, PASS completion) and summary of changes from the previous version.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011 Rev 2; ICH E2E

description: Version identifier of the RMP document (e.g., 1.0, 2.3), incremented with each regulatory submission or significant update. Supports version history tracking.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011 Rev 2

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacovigilance agreement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_AGREEMENT.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: PV agreements between MAH and licensee/partner define PV responsibilities for a specific approved product. Linking pv_agreement to approval enables MAH to track which approvals have active PV agreemen

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: PV agreements with co-promotion or licensing partners govern safety data exchange obligations tied to specific brand plans. Commercial and PV teams need this link to align PSUR responsibilities, signa

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: The counterparty in a PV agreement is a registered business partner in the MDM system (licensee, co-marketing partner, CRO). Linking to business_partner enables counterparty data quality management, a

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: PV agreements are negotiated as part of or directly reference licensing agreements — ICSR exchange timelines, SUSAR reporting obligations, and PSUR responsibilities are defined within the licensing de

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance agreements are contracts between legal entities defining safety data exchange obligations. MAH legal entity linkage foundational for contract management, audit trail, and regulatory

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: PV agreements are managed by specific PV org units (alliance management, PV operations). Org unit ownership is required for compliance review scheduling, ICSR exchange monitoring, and PV agreement aud

Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: PV agreements with partners/licensees are negotiated per development program and govern safety data exchange obligations for a specific project. Compliance teams need to associate PV agreements with d

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: A PV agreement governs safety data exchange obligations for specific medicinal products. The pv_agreement table has territory_scope and counterparty fields but no structured FK to the product it cover

Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance agreements with licensors/licensees for in-licensed or out-licensed products often reference the same commercial relationship as royalty agreements. Linking enables tracking of safet

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: PV agreements with CMO/CDMO partners specify which manufacturing sites are covered by ICSR exchange and SUSAR reporting obligations (ICH E2D, EU GVP Module I). Linking pv_agreement to the manufacturin

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: PV agreements have a defined territory scope and governing law country. counterparty_country and governing_law_country are plain-text denormalizations of country. A proper FK enables territory-based P

Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the pharmacovigilance agreement (e.g., SDEA-2024-0042). Used in regulatory correspondence, audit trails, and partner communications. Maps to the document number in Veeva Vault RIM or Argus Safety agreement registry.
reference: GVP Module I; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Current lifecycle state of the pharmacovigilance agreement. DRAFT = under negotiation; ACTIVE = in force and binding; SUSPENDED = temporarily paused by mutual consent or regulatory action; TERMINATED = permanently ended before expiry; EXPIRED = reached end date without renewal; UNDER_REVIEW = undergoing periodic review or amendment.

Classification of the pharmacovigilance agreement by its legal and operational nature. SDEA = Safety Data Exchange Agreement; PVA = Pharmacovigilance Agreement; CO_VIGILANCE = co-vigilance arrangement; CO_PROMOTION = co-promotion partner PV agreement; AFFILIATE = local affiliate PV agreement. Determines the regulatory obligations and reporting obligations applicable to each party.
reference: GVP Module I; ICH E2E; EMA/873138/2011

description: Date of the most recent amendment to the pharmacovigilance agreement. Nullable if no amendments have been made since original execution. Used to track agreement evolution and trigger re-review workflows.
reference: GVP Module I; 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2E

description: Reference identifier used in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS to link this pharmacovigilance agreement to the partner configuration for automated ICSR routing and exchange. Enables traceability between the agreement record and the operational safety system configuration.
reference: Argus Safety; Oracle AERS; GVP Module VI; ICH E2B(R3)

description: Current compliance monitoring status of the pharmacovigilance agreement based on periodic reviews, audits, and KPI tracking. COMPLIANT = all obligations met; NON_COMPLIANT = one or more obligations breached; UNDER_REVIEW = compliance assessment in progress; PENDING_AUDIT = scheduled for compliance audit. Supports GVP Module I oversight requirements.
reference: GVP Module I; GVP Module II; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Email address of the designated pharmacovigilance contact at the counterparty organisation. Used for operational safety data exchange communications and escalation notifications.
reference: GVP Module I; GDPR Article 4; ICH E2E

description: Name of the designated pharmacovigilance contact person at the counterparty organisation responsible for safety data exchange under this agreement. Required for operational communication and escalation.

Classification of the counterparty by their operational role in the pharmaceutical value chain. CRO = Contract Research Organization; CMO = Contract Manufacturing Organization; CDMO = Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization; LICENSEE = product licensee; CO_PROMOTER = co-promotion partner; AFFILIATE = local affiliate entity; DISTRIBUTOR = distribution partner. Drives applicable GVP obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CRO|CMO|CDMO|LICENSEE|CO_PROMOTER|AFFILIATE|DISTRIBUTOR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GVP Module I; GVP Module VI; ICH E2E

description: Timestamp when this pharmacovigilance agreement record was first created in the system. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per model conventions. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GVP Module I.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GVP Module I; ICH E2E

description: Specifies the technical format used for exchanging Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) between parties. E2B_R2 = ICH E2B(R2) XML format; E2B_R3 = ICH E2B(R3) FHIR-based format; PAPER = paper-based exchange; PROPRIETARY = system-specific format; NOT_SPECIFIED = format not yet defined. Drives technical integration requirements with Argus Safety/Oracle AERS.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA/EMEA/H/20665/04; FDA 21 CFR Part 314

description: Technical transmission method used for exchanging safety data between parties. EVWEB = EMA EudraVigilance Web Trader; GATEWAY = automated gateway-to-gateway; SFTP = secure file transfer; EMAIL = encrypted email; MANUAL = manual entry; API = direct API integration. Determines system configuration in Argus Safety.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA EudraVigilance; FDA FAERS; GVP Module VI

description: Date on which the pharmacovigilance agreement becomes legally binding and operational obligations commence. Determines the start of mandatory safety data exchange timelines. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.
reference: GVP Module I; 21 CFR Part 314.81; ICH E2E

description: Date on which the pharmacovigilance agreement ceases to be in force unless renewed or extended. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance alerts. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.

business_glossary_term: Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) Exchange Timeline (Days)
description: Number of calendar days within which Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) must be exchanged between the MAH and the counterparty following initial receipt. Typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening SUSARs and 15 days for other serious cases per GVP Module VI. Drives compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.
reference: GVP Module VI; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA/873138/2011

business_glossary_term: GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA) In Place Flag
description: Indicates whether a GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with the counterparty covering the personal data exchanged as part of pharmacovigilance activities. Mandatory for EU-based safety data exchange under GDPR Article 28.
reference: GDPR Article 28; GVP Module I; EMA/873138/2011

description: ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code of the governing language of the pharmacovigilance agreement document (e.g., EN for English, DE for German, FR for French). Relevant for legal interpretation and translation obligations.

Date of the most recent periodic compliance review conducted for this pharmacovigilance agreement. Used to track review frequency and identify overdue reviews per GVP Module I requirements for oversight of outsourced PV activities.
reference: GVP Module I; GVP Module II; ICH E2E

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pharmacovigilance agreement record. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per model conventions. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GVP Module I; ICH E2E

description: Date on which the next periodic compliance review of this pharmacovigilance agreement is scheduled. Drives workflow scheduling and resource planning for the PV compliance team.
reference: GVP Module I; GVP Module II; ICH E2E

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, derogations, or operational notes relevant to this pharmacovigilance agreement that are not captured in structured fields. Used by PV operations and regulatory affairs teams.

business_glossary_term: Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) Responsible Party
description: Designates which party bears primary responsibility for authoring and submitting Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSURs) / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Reports (PBRERs) to regulatory authorities. MAH = Marketing Authorisation Holder; COUNTERPARTY = partner organisation; JOINT = shared responsibility; NOT_APPLICABLE = PSURs not in scope of this agreement.
reference: GVP Module VII; ICH E2C(R2); 21 CFR Part 314.81

business_glossary_term: Qualified Person for Pharmacovigilance (QPPV) Contact Name
description: Name of the Qualified Person Responsible for Pharmacovigilance (QPPV) designated by the MAH under this agreement. The QPPV is a mandatory regulatory role under EU GVP legislation responsible for the PV system. Captured for regulatory compliance and audit purposes.
reference: GVP Module I; Directive 2001/83/EC Article 8(3)(ia); EMA/873138/2011

description: Designates which party is responsible for submitting safety reports (ICSRs, SUSARs, PSURs, RMPs) to national competent authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, etc.) for products in scope. Determines accountability in regulatory inspections and audits.
reference: GVP Module I; GVP Module VI; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA/873138/2011

description: Number of calendar days before the agreement expiry date that a renewal notification must be issued to the counterparty. Drives automated alerting in the PV agreement management system to prevent lapses in safety data exchange coverage.

Designates which party is responsible for maintaining and updating the Risk Management Plan (RMP) for products covered under this agreement. Relevant for EU-authorised products where RMP is mandatory per GVP Module V.
reference: GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011; ICH E2E

description: Designates which party is responsible for signal detection, assessment, and management activities for products covered under this agreement. MAH = Marketing Authorisation Holder; COUNTERPARTY = partner; JOINT = shared; NOT_APPLICABLE = signal management not in scope. Aligns with GVP Module IX obligations.
reference: GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; EMA/873138/2011

business_glossary_term: Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) Exchange Timeline (Days)
description: Number of calendar days within which Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) must be exchanged between parties. Typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening and 15 days for non-fatal/non-life-threatening SUSARs per GVP Module VI and ICH E2D. Separate from general ICSR exchange timelines.
reference: GVP Module VI; ICH E2D; 21 CFR Part 312.32; EMA/873138/2011

description: Date on which the pharmacovigilance agreement was formally terminated prior to its scheduled expiry date. Nullable if the agreement has not been terminated. Triggers wind-down procedures for safety data exchange and regulatory notification obligations.
reference: GVP Module I; ICH E2E; 21 CFR Part 314

description: Free-text description of the reason for early termination of the pharmacovigilance agreement (e.g., product withdrawal, partnership dissolution, regulatory action, mutual agreement). Required for regulatory audit trail and compliance documentation.

Description of the geographic territories or regulatory jurisdictions covered by this agreement (e.g., specific countries, regions, or global). Determines which national competent authorities are in scope for regulatory reporting obligations under this agreement.
reference: GVP Module I; ICH E2E; EMA/873138/2011

description: Reference identifier of the executed pharmacovigilance agreement document stored in Veeva Vault (RIM or QualityDocs module). Enables direct navigation to the source document for audit and review purposes. Aligns with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault RIM; GVP Module I

description: Version identifier of the pharmacovigilance agreement document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented upon each amendment or renewal. Supports document lifecycle management in Veeva Vault and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GVP Module I; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacovigilance product master record. Primary key for the pv_product entity within the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ICH E2D; EU GVP Module VI

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: pv_product.authorization_number is a denormalized attribute mapping to a marketing authorization. A proper FK to regulatory.approval enables the QPPV to track which approved products are under active

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: ATC classification is mandatory for WHO Drug Dictionary coding, PSUR scope definition, signal detection stratification, and regulatory submissions. Replaces text atc_code with proper master data refer

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Product authorization country determines regulatory jurisdiction, reporting requirements (expedited timelines, formats), and applicable safety regulations. Essential for routing ICSRs to correct healt

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Core link mapping pharmacovigilance product entity to commercial brand for integrated safety-commercial decision making, launch planning, lifecycle management, and cross-functional brand team coordina

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: The PV product record must align with the brand plan for PSUR scheduling, signal management timelines, and commercial lifecycle planning. A direct FK enables period-based PV planning queries without t

Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: The current approved label is the reference document for listedness assessments across all PV activities. Linking pv_product to the current label version enables centralized listedness reference manag

Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: Core traceability for safety signal investigation. When adverse events emerge, pharmacovigilance teams must access the original discovery compound structure, preclinical safety data, and SAR to assess

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Formulation-level PV surveillance: pv_product already links to medicinal_product but needs the formulation-level drug_product for PSMF maintenance, expedited reporting configuration, and formulation-s

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level PV monitoring: PSMF and PSUR are organized around the active substance. Linking pv_product to drug_substance enables substance-level safety surveillance across all formulations and mar

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Listedness and benefit-risk per indication: pv_product must reference approved indications to determine whether an AE is listed (expected) or unlisted (unexpected), driving expedited reporting obligat

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: International Nonproprietary Name (INN) is WHO standard for substance identification in pharmacovigilance. Required for duplicate case detection, substance-level signal detection, and regulatory submi

Foreign key linking to product.labeling. Business justification: Listedness determination: PV teams must reference the current approved label to assess whether an adverse reaction is listed. Linking pv_product to labeling enables direct traceability to the label ve

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) is the legal entity responsible for pharmacovigilance obligations. Required for regulatory submissions, liability determination, and safety reporting accountabilit

Foreign key linking to masterdata.ndc_code. Business justification: US market products require NDC linkage for FDA FAERS submissions, product identification in US ICSRs, and recall coordination. Essential for US pharmacovigilance operations and regulatory compliance.

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Core bridge between PV product master and commercial product master. Required for regulatory identifier reconciliation (NDC, EMA product number), lifecycle status alignment, product discontinuation im

Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Products are defined by their molecular targets. Essential for class effect analysis, mechanism-based safety monitoring, and portfolio-level risk assessment. When safety signals emerge for one product

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: PV products must track primary patents for lifecycle management, regulatory submissions, and understanding formulation/composition when assessing adverse events. Essential for innovator product safety

Foreign key linking to discovery.discovery_project. Business justification: Links marketed products to originating discovery projects for institutional knowledge and retrospective analysis. When serious safety issues arise, teams review original project rationale, target sele

Foreign key linking to regulatory.rems. Business justification: When pv_product.rems_required=true, the product is enrolled in a REMS program. Linking pv_product to the specific rems record enables PV teams to access REMS program requirements during signal assessm

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Each PV product is managed by a specific PV org unit (therapeutic area safety team). Product-to-org-unit assignment is required for PSMF maintenance, QPPV delegation, signal detection ownership, and P

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: pv_product represents the PV view of a product under a marketing authorization. Linking to the primary regulatory submission enables PSMF maintenance and product-level submission history tracking, a c

Native product identifier from the Argus Safety / Oracle AERS pharmacovigilance system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and traceability between the Silver Layer PV product master and the operational PV case processing system.
reference: Oracle Argus Safety Product Configuration; ICH E2B(R3) integration

description: Type of regulatory marketing authorisation application under which the product is approved (e.g., NDA — New Drug Application, BLA — Biologics License Application, MAA — Marketing Authorisation Application). Determines submission pathway and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NDA|BLA|MAA|ANDA|NDS|other — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR 314; 21 CFR 601; EU Directive 2001/83/EC; ICH M4

description: Indicates whether the product contains a controlled substance subject to DEA scheduling requirements. True = controlled substance; False = not a controlled substance. Impacts PV reporting and distribution controls.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act; 21 CFR 1308; EU Directive 2011/62/EU

description: Timestamp when the PV product master record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GVP Module II.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU GVP Module II; ICH E6(R2)

description: EMA-assigned product number for centrally authorised products in the EU. Used for EudraVigilance submissions, PSUR repository submissions, and EU regulatory correspondence.
reference: EU GVP Module VI; EudraVigilance Access Policy; EMA/816573/2011

description: EudraVigilance Medicinal Product Dictionary (EVMPD) identifier assigned by EMA for the product. Required for electronic ICSR submissions to EudraVigilance and EU regulatory reporting.
reference: EU GVP Module VI; EudraVigilance Data Analysis System; Regulation (EC) 726/2004

description: Indicates whether this product is subject to expedited (7-day or 15-day) Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) reporting obligations to health authorities. Drives case processing timelines in Argus Safety.
reference: ICH E2D; EU GVP Module VI Section 6.3; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(1); 21 CFR 312.32

description: International Birth Date (IBD) — the date of first marketing authorisation granted worldwide for the active substance. Serves as the reference date for PSUR/PBRER data lock point scheduling per ICH E2C(R2).
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2.1; EU GVP Module VII; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2)

description: Indicates whether the product holds an orphan drug designation for a rare disease indication. True = orphan designation granted; False = no orphan designation. Affects PV reporting thresholds and PSUR requirements.
reference: EU Regulation 141/2000; 21 CFR 316; FDA Orphan Drug Act

description: Classification of the medicinal product by its scientific/regulatory category (e.g., small molecule, biologic, vaccine, gene therapy, cell therapy, combination product). Drives applicable regulatory framework and PV requirements.
reference: ICH S6(R1); EU GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 3.2(e)

description: Reference identifier linking this product to the applicable Pharmacovigilance System Master File (PSMF) that describes the PV system governing this product. Mandatory for EU GVP Module II compliance.
reference: EU GVP Module II; Directive 2001/83/EC Article 104; EMA/816573/2011

business_glossary_term: Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) Next Due Date
description: Next scheduled due date for submission of the Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) or Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER) to the relevant health authority. Calculated from IBD and submission frequency.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Section 2.1; EU GVP Module VII; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2)(ii)

business_glossary_term: Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) Submission Frequency
description: Regulatory-mandated frequency for Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER) submission for this product. Drives the PSUR scheduling calendar.
reference: ICH E2C(R2); EU GVP Module VII; 21 CFR 314.81(b)(2)(ii)

description: Current lifecycle status of the product within the pharmacovigilance system. Drives case processing eligibility, signal detection inclusion, and PSUR scheduling. Active = currently under PV surveillance; Suspended = temporarily halted; Withdrawn = marketing authorisation withdrawn; Under_review = under regulatory review; Archived = historical record only.
reference: EU GVP Module VI; 21 CFR 314.81; Argus Safety product configuration

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) is required by the FDA for this product. True = REMS is mandated; False = REMS not required. Applicable to US-authorised products only.

Indicates whether a Risk Management Plan (RMP) is required for this product by the applicable health authority. True = RMP is mandated; False = RMP not required. Drives risk minimisation activity tracking.
reference: EU GVP Module V; EMA/838713/2011; 21 CFR 505-1 (REMS)

description: Indicates whether this product is currently included in the active signal detection and management programme. True = included in signal detection runs; False = excluded (e.g., archived or withdrawn products).
reference: EU GVP Module IX; ICH E2E; FDA Guidance on Signal Detection

description: Timestamp when the PV product master record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GVP Module II.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU GVP Module II; SOX

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacovigilance reporter record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the reporter master data entity.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety Reporter Entity

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Reporter country determines follow-up feasibility, data privacy regulations (GDPR vs others), contact protocols, and language requirements. Operational necessity for case management and regulatory com

Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: Reporters (pharmacists, hospital physicians) are affiliated with HCOs. Linking reporter to hco_master supports institutional-level reporting pattern analysis, follow-up management, and GDPR data stewa

Foreign key linking to hcp.hcp_master. Business justification: When reporter is HCP (is_hcp flag), linking to hcp_master enables license verification, specialty validation for causality assessment, NPI matching for regulatory submissions, and structured follow-up

Reference to the canonical/master reporter record when this record has been identified as a duplicate. Null if this record is itself the master. Supports deduplication and case re-linking workflows in the PV system.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety Duplicate Management

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.address. Business justification: Structured address linkage enables standardized contact management, duplicate reporter detection, and GDPR compliance (right to erasure). Replaces fragmented address fields with master data reference

Privacy-compliant pseudonymous identifier assigned to the reporter in lieu of personal identifiers, enabling case aggregation and follow-up management without exposing PII. Mandatory under GDPR and HIPAA for PV processing.
reference: GDPR Article 4 & Article 25; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514; ICH E2B(R3) A.1.7

description: Source system identifier for the reporter as assigned by Argus Safety / Oracle AERS. Used for lineage tracing and deduplication against the operational PV system.
reference: Argus Safety / Oracle AERS Reporter Master; ICH E2B(R3) A.1.7

description: Date on which the reporter provided or withdrew consent to be contacted for follow-up. Required for GDPR audit trail demonstrating lawful basis for processing personal data.

Indicates whether the reporter has provided explicit consent to be contacted for follow-up queries regarding the adverse event report. Mandatory consent gate under GDPR Article 7 before any follow-up communication is initiated.
reference: GDPR Article 7; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.508; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Preferred channel for follow-up communication as indicated by the reporter. Drives the follow-up workflow in Argus Safety. 'no_contact' indicates the reporter has declined further contact, which must be respected per GDPR consent obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety Follow-up Workflow

description: Timestamp when the reporter record was first created in the PV system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 for electronic records in regulated pharmaceutical environments.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; GDPR Article 30

description: Department or unit within the reporter's organization (e.g., Oncology Ward, Clinical Pharmacology, Drug Safety). Supports routing of follow-up queries to the correct organizational unit.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.4; Argus Safety Reporter Master

description: Indicates whether this reporter record has been identified as a potential or confirmed duplicate of another reporter record. Duplicate reporters must be resolved before case processing to prevent double-counting in aggregate safety analyses.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR §314.81; Argus Safety Duplicate Detection

description: Primary email address of the reporter for electronic follow-up correspondence. Classified as restricted PII. Used only for PV case follow-up; not shared with commercial systems. Consent-gated per GDPR.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.6; GDPR Article 4 & Article 6; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514

description: Last/family name of the individual reporter. Stored in restricted classification per GDPR and HIPAA. Used for follow-up correspondence and duplicate detection. May be withheld or anonymized per reporter consent.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.3b; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514

description: Fax number of the reporter or their institution, used for formal follow-up correspondence in jurisdictions where fax remains a regulatory-accepted communication channel (e.g., Japan PMDA submissions).
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.6; PMDA Pharmacovigilance Guidelines

description: Date on which the reporter submitted their first adverse event report to the company. Used for longitudinal reporter relationship analysis and tenure-based reliability assessment.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety Reporter Master; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Target date by which a follow-up response from the reporter is required to meet regulatory submission timelines (e.g., 15-day expedited reporting for SUSARs and SAEs). Calculated based on initial receipt date and applicable regulatory clock.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); FDA 21 CFR §312.32; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR Part 314.81

description: Indicates whether outstanding follow-up queries have been sent to this reporter and a response is pending. Drives the follow-up management workflow in Argus Safety to ensure timely case completion within regulatory timeframes.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR §312.32; Argus Safety Follow-up Workflow

description: Date on which the GDPR Right to Erasure request was processed and PII fields were anonymized. Null if no erasure request has been received. Required for GDPR compliance audit trail.
reference: GDPR Article 17 & Article 30; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Indicates whether the reporter has submitted a Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten) request under GDPR Article 17. When true, PII fields must be anonymized while retaining the anonymized case record for pharmacovigilance regulatory obligations under GDPR Article 17(3)(c).
reference: GDPR Article 17; EMA GVP Module VI; ICH E2B(R3)

description: First/given name of the individual reporter. Stored in restricted classification per GDPR and HIPAA. Used for follow-up correspondence and duplicate detection. May be withheld or anonymized per reporter consent.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.3a; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514

description: Indicates whether the reporter is a qualified Healthcare Professional (HCP). HCP-reported cases carry higher regulatory weight and may trigger expedited 15-day reporting obligations to FDA, EMA, and other health authorities.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.2; FDA 21 CFR §314.81; EMA GVP Module VI; 21 CFR Part 312.32

description: ISO 639-1 or ISO 639-2 language code representing the reporter's preferred communication language. Used to route follow-up correspondence to appropriately qualified multilingual PV staff and to select translated follow-up letter templates.
reference: ISO 639-1; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Date of the most recent adverse event report submitted by this reporter. Used to identify active versus dormant reporters and to support follow-up prioritization.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety Reporter Master; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Professional license or registration number of the HCP reporter (e.g., medical license, pharmacy registration). Used to verify reporter qualification and credibility. Stored as confidential business data.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.2; FDA MedWatch 3500A; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Free-text field for PV case processor notes regarding the reporter, such as communication history, special handling instructions, or context about the reporter's relationship to the case. Not transmitted in regulatory submissions.
reference: Argus Safety Reporter Master; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Name of the institution, hospital, clinic, regulatory authority, or company from which the reporter submitted the adverse event report. Relevant for institutional reporters and regulatory authority submissions.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.4; EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR §314.81

description: Primary telephone number of the reporter for follow-up contact. Classified as restricted PII. Stored in E.164 international format where possible. Used exclusively for PV case follow-up activities.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.6; GDPR Article 4; HIPAA 45 CFR §164.514

description: Professional qualification of the reporter, particularly relevant for Healthcare Professional (HCP) reporters. Determines the clinical credibility weighting of the report and regulatory classification. ICH E2B(R3) field A.2.1.2. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: physician|pharmacist|nurse|dentist|other_health_professional|consumer|unknown — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.2; EMA GVP Module VI; FDA MedWatch 3500A

description: Identifier of the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, NMPA) when the reporter is a regulatory body. Used to route mandatory ICSR submissions and track authority-initiated follow-up requests.
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.1.4; FDA 21 CFR §314.81; EMA GVP Module VI; MHRA Yellow Card; PMDA Guidelines

description: Internal PV team assessment of the reporter's historical reliability based on completeness and accuracy of prior submissions. Used to weight signal detection analysis and prioritize follow-up resources. Assigned by the PV case processor.
reference: EMA GVP Module VI; ICH E2B(R3); Argus Safety Reporter Assessment

description: Current operational status of the reporter record within the PV system. Inactive or blocked reporters are excluded from new case assignments. Supports duplicate detection and data quality management.
reference: Argus Safety Reporter Master; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI

description: Classification of the reporter by their relationship to the adverse event. Drives regulatory reporting obligations and follow-up workflows. ICH E2B(R3) field A.2.1.1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: healthcare_professional|patient_consumer|lawyer|regulatory_authority|literature|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.2.1.1; FDA 21 CFR §314.81; EMA GVP Module VI

description: Classification of the original source through which the adverse event report was received. Determines regulatory handling requirements; e.g., literature-sourced reports require medical literature monitoring per GVP Module VI. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spontaneous|literature|clinical_trial|regulatory_authority|solicited|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]
reference: ICH E2B(R3) A.1.4; EMA GVP Module VI; FDA 21 CFR §314.81

description: Medical or clinical specialty of the HCP reporter (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Supports signal detection analysis by therapeutic area and enables targeted follow-up by medically qualified personnel.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety Reporter Master

description: Cumulative count of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) submitted by this reporter. Used for duplicate detection, reporter profiling, and reliability assessment. Updated upon each new case linkage.
reference: ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; Argus Safety Reporter Aggregation

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the reporter record. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GDPR Article 30 record-keeping obligations.
