reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH E2B(R3); EMA GVP Module VI; GDPR Article 30

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the safety signal record in the pharmacovigilance system. Primary key for the safety_signal data product.
reference: ICH E2C(R2) Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report; GVP Module IX Signal Management

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Signal detection and prioritization by therapeutic class enables risk-benefit assessment, class-effect evaluation, and regulatory communication strategy. Supports signal management workflows and PRAC

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Validated safety signals that require label changes or risk minimization directly impact brand plans. Signal management teams formally notify brand plan owners; this link enables commercial impact ass

Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: Safety signals during clinical development are compound-specific before a pv_product is established. Signal management teams need direct compound traceability for investigational product signal assess

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: Substance-level signal aggregation: safety signals are frequently detected at the active substance level across multiple formulations and markets. Linking to drug_substance enables cross-product signa

Foreign key linking to product.indication. Business justification: Indication-specific benefit-risk: safety signals are evaluated per approved indication to determine whether the benefit-risk balance is affected. PRAC signal assessments and PSUR referrals require ind

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Safety signals are detected for specific active substances (INN). Signal management databases (EudraVigilance, FAERS) track signals by INN/substance across products. Linking safety_signal to inn_regis

Foreign key linking to regulatory.label. Business justification: Safety signals with label_change_recommended=true must reference the specific label version to be updated. This enables signal management workflow to directly identify and queue the affected label for

Foreign key linking to discovery.molecular_target. Business justification: Safety signal validation requires molecular target review. Signal assessment committees evaluate target biology, pathway, tissue expression, and known class effects to determine if the signal is mecha

Foreign key linking to masterdata.org_unit. Business justification: Safety signals are assessed and owned by specific PV org units (signal management team, therapeutic area safety team). Org unit ownership is required for signal triage workflow, PRAC notification acco

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Safety signal management requires IP teams to assess whether a label change triggered by a signal affects patent claims or exclusivity. Signal closure reports and regulatory action decisions reference

Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: Safety signals are managed at the development project level for portfolio safety reviews, DSUR signal sections, and go/no-go decisions on clinical progression. Signal management teams routinely aggreg
Reference to the medicinal product (drug substance or drug product) associated with this safety signal. Links to the master product entity for the implicated compound.
reference: GVP Module IX; ICH E2C(R2); EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Safety signals requiring regulatory action result in formal submissions (label changes, variations, PSUR referrals). Linking safety_signal to the resulting regulatory submission enables end-to-end sig

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Safety signals detected from clinical trial data must be traceable to the originating trial for PRAC notification and signal triage (GVP Module IX). safety_signal has source_reference_number as text b
End date of the data analysis period used for signal detection. Together with analysis_period_start_date, defines the cumulative or interval data window reviewed.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Start date of the data analysis period used for signal detection. Defines the temporal window of adverse event data reviewed to identify the signal.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Narrative summary of the formal signal assessment findings, including methodology applied, data sources reviewed, clinical interpretation, and conclusions. Supports regulatory dossier preparation and PRAC submissions.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2C(R2); EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Overall benefit-risk impact determination resulting from the signal assessment. Indicates whether the signal changes the benefit-risk balance of the product as favorable, unfavorable, uncertain, or neutral.
reference: GVP Module IX; ICH E2C(R2) PBRER; EMA Benefit-Risk Methodology; GVP Module I

description: Date on which the safety signal was formally closed following completion of all assessment activities and regulatory actions. Represents the end of the signal management lifecycle.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Timestamp when the safety signal record was first created in the pharmacovigilance system (Argus Safety / Oracle AERS). Used for audit trail and data lineage compliance per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GVP Module IX; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Name of the specific pharmacovigilance database or data source from which the signal was detected (e.g., FDA FAERS, WHO VigiBase, EudraVigilance, company spontaneous database, published literature). Supports traceability of signal origin.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; FDA FAERS

description: Methodology used to identify the safety signal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disproportionality_analysis|clinical_review|machine_learning|literature_review|spontaneous_report|regulatory_authority — promote to reference product]
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Date and time when the safety signal was first detected or identified, representing the principal real-world event time. Critical for SLA tracking of PRAC rapporteur notification timelines and regulatory reporting deadlines.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; 21 CFR 314.81; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Empirical Bayes Geometric Mean (EBGM) — a Bayesian disproportionality metric used in the FDA FAERS database analysis. EBGM ≥2 is a commonly applied threshold for signal detection.
reference: GVP Module IX; FDA FAERS Signal Detection; DuMouchel EBGM Method

description: Number of fatal outcome cases among the cases contributing to this safety signal. Fatal cases receive highest priority in signal triage and may trigger expedited regulatory notification.
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; GVP Module IX; 21 CFR 312.32

description: Information Component (IC) — a Bayesian disproportionality metric used by the WHO Uppsala Monitoring Centre (UMC) VigiBase. Positive IC values indicate a drug-event association stronger than expected by chance.
reference: GVP Module IX; WHO UMC VigiBase IC Method; Bate et al. IC Algorithm

description: Indicates whether the signal assessment has resulted in a formal recommendation to update the product label (SmPC, USPI, or equivalent). Triggers regulatory submission activities.
reference: GVP Module IX; 21 CFR 314.70; EMA SmPC Guideline; ICH E2C(R2)

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term Code
description: MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code representing the primary adverse event or medical concept associated with this safety signal. Standardized coding per MedDRA hierarchy for regulatory submissions and signal tracking.
reference: MedDRA Version Current; ICH M1 MedDRA Guideline; GVP Module IX; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term Name
description: Human-readable MedDRA Preferred Term name corresponding to the meddra_pt_code. Provides the standardized medical terminology label for the safety signal event.
reference: MedDRA Version Current; ICH M1 MedDRA Guideline; GVP Module IX

business_glossary_term: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) Code
description: MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code for the primary adverse event, providing the highest-level classification in the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for signal clustering and therapeutic area analysis.
reference: MedDRA Version Current; ICH M1 MedDRA Guideline; GVP Module IX

business_glossary_term: Pharmacovigilance Risk Assessment Committee (PRAC) Rapporteur Notification Date
description: Actual date on which the PRAC rapporteur was notified of the safety signal. Compared against prac_notification_due_date to assess SLA compliance.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; Regulation (EU) No 1235/2010

business_glossary_term: Pharmacovigilance Risk Assessment Committee (PRAC) Rapporteur Notification Due Date
description: SLA-critical deadline by which the PRAC rapporteur must be notified of the validated safety signal per EMA regulatory requirements. Drives escalation workflows and compliance monitoring.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; Regulation (EU) No 1235/2010

description: Triage priority assigned to the safety signal based on clinical seriousness, signal strength, and public health impact. Drives SLA timelines for assessment completion and regulatory notification.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; FDA PDUFA VI Commitments

description: Proportional Reporting Ratio (PRR) — a disproportionality analysis metric quantifying the ratio of the proportion of reports for a drug-event combination versus all other drugs. Values >2 with chi-squared >4 and ≥3 cases typically indicate a signal.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; Evans et al. PRR Method; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

business_glossary_term: Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) Referral Flag
description: Indicates whether this safety signal has been referred for inclusion and evaluation in the next Periodic Safety Update Report (PSUR) / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER).
reference: GVP Module VII PSUR; ICH E2C(R2); EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Indicates whether the safety signal assessment has determined that a regulatory action is required (e.g., label update, DHPC, REMS modification, product withdrawal). Drives downstream regulatory affairs workflows.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; 21 CFR 314.81; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Type of regulatory action determined to be required based on the signal assessment outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: label_update|rems_modification|dhpc|psur_referral|product_withdrawal|contraindication_addition|none — promote to reference product]
reference: GVP Module IX; 21 CFR 314.70; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; FDA REMS Guidance

business_glossary_term: Risk Management Plan (RMP) Update Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the signal assessment outcome requires an update to the product's Risk Management Plan (RMP). Triggers regulatory affairs and pharmacovigilance team coordination.
reference: GVP Module V Risk Management Systems; EMA/838713/2011 Rev 2; Regulation (EU) No 1235/2010

description: Reporting Odds Ratio (ROR) — a disproportionality analysis metric representing the odds of a specific adverse event being reported for a drug versus all other drugs. Complements PRR in signal detection.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; van Puijenbroek ROR Method; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Number of Serious Adverse Event (SAE) cases among the total cases contributing to this signal. Serious cases include fatal, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, or congenital anomaly outcomes per ICH E2A.
reference: ICH E2A Clinical Safety Data Management; GVP Module IX; 21 CFR 312.32

description: Detailed narrative description of the safety signal including the nature of the concern, clinical context, patient population affected, and initial evidence summary. Supports signal triage and assessment documentation.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2C(R2); EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the safety signal upon creation in Argus Safety / Oracle AERS. Used for cross-system referencing, regulatory correspondence, and PRAC rapporteur notifications.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Classification of the data source from which the safety signal was detected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spontaneous_database|literature|clinical_trial|real_world_data|social_media|health_authority — promote to reference product]
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2C(R2); FDA Guidance on Signal Detection

description: Current lifecycle state of the safety signal per GVP Module IX workflow: new (detected, not yet triaged), under_evaluation (active assessment in progress), validated (confirmed as a genuine safety concern), refuted (assessed and dismissed), closed (final disposition reached and documented).
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; ICH E2C(R2)

description: Concise, human-readable title describing the safety signal, typically formatted as [Drug Name] and [Adverse Event Term]. Used in regulatory correspondence, PRAC submissions, and internal safety committee communications.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Classification of the signal as new (not previously known), strengthened (existing signal with increased evidence), weakened (existing signal with decreased evidence), or refuted (previously identified signal now dismissed).
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Reference number or identifier from the originating data source (e.g., FAERS case number, EudraVigilance report ID, literature PubMed ID, clinical trial NCT number) that triggered or contributed to the signal detection.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; FDA FAERS; EudraVigilance; WHO VigiBase

description: Date on which the safety signal was formally triaged by the pharmacovigilance team to determine priority and assign for full assessment. Marks the transition from detection to active evaluation.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the safety signal record. Supports audit trail requirements and tracks signal assessment progression through the lifecycle.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GVP Module IX; ICH E6(R2) GCP

description: Date on which the safety signal was formally validated as a genuine safety concern following initial assessment. Triggers downstream regulatory notification and benefit-risk evaluation activities.
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2; ICH E2C(R2)

business_glossary_term: Signal Case Series - Signal Case Series Id
description: Primary key for the signal_case_series association
Foreign key linking to the Individual Case Safety Report contributing to the signal

business_glossary_term: Signal Case Series - Safety Signal Id
description: Foreign key linking to the safety signal that this ICSR contributes to

Indicates whether this ICSR is a contributing (numerator) case in the disproportionality analysis for the signal, as opposed to a background reference case. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.

Total number of Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) or adverse event cases contributing to this safety signal at the time of detection or most recent assessment. Used as a threshold criterion alongside disproportionality metrics. [Moved from safety_signal: The aggregate case_count on safety_signal is a derived summary of the signal_case_series records. While the aggregate may be retained on safety_signal for performance/reporting purposes, the authoritative case-level data now lives in signal_case_series. If retained, it should be treated as a denormalized cache, not the source of truth.]
reference: GVP Module IX Signal Management; ICH E2C(R2); EMA/873138/2011 Rev 2

description: Date of the specific signal detection run during which this ICSR was identified as contributing to the safety signal. Belongs to the relationship, not to the signal or the ICSR alone. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.

Per-case contribution score to the aggregate disproportionality metric (PRR/ROR/EBGM/IC) for this signal. Quantifies how much this individual ICSR influences the signal strength. Belongs to the relationship, not to either entity alone. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.

The specific MedDRA Preferred Term code from the ICSR that matched the signal's MedDRA PT during the detection run. An ICSR may have multiple MedDRA terms; this records which one drove the case's inclusion in this signal's case series. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.

The unique manufacturing batch or lot number assigned per GMP batch numbering convention. Business identifier for traceability.

The unit of measure for the batch size (e.g., kg, L, units, tablets, vials).

description: Number of open CAPA records associated with this batch or its manufacturing process that may impact disposition decision.

Number of open Out of Specification (OOS) investigations linked to this batch that must be closed before disposition can be finalized.

The date on which the disposition decision was reported to the relevant regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.).
Indicates whether the disposition decision (e.g., rejection, reprocessing) must be reported to regulatory authorities per regional requirements.
The quantity of the batch rejected and segregated for destruction or reprocessing due to non-conformance with specifications.
The quantity of the batch approved for release and distribution, which may be less than the total batch size if partial rejection or rework occurred.
The date by which the batch must be retested to confirm continued compliance with specifications, applicable for drug substances and intermediates.
The operational system of record from which this disposition record was sourced, typically SAP QM or MasterControl/TrackWise.

Foreign key linking to product.drug_substance. Business justification: API manufacturers issue CoAs for drug substance batches supplied to drug product manufacturers. ICH Q7 GMP for APIs requires CoA issuance per substance batch. Direct drug_substance link enables API Co

Foreign key linking to hcp.hco_master. Business justification: COAs are routinely issued to hospital pharmacies and HCOs receiving drug shipments. Linking COA to HCO enables traceability for product release to institutional customers, required for GMP compliance

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: CoA is the quality release document enabling inventory availability for distribution. Each CoA certifies a specific inventory lot for commercial release. Critical for batch release workflow and custom

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: COAs are issued by a GMP-authorized legal entity (the QP-releasing manufacturer). The 'issuing_site_name' plain attribute is a denormalized legal entity reference. Regulatory market authorization and

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: COAs are issued for specific materials/batches. The 'material_type' plain attribute is a denormalized material reference. Customer-facing COA generation, material specification retrieval, and GMP rele
Reference to the product (drug substance, drug product, excipient, or raw material) for which this CoA is issued.

business_glossary_term: Last Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Assessment Outcome
description: FDA's determination following review of the most recently submitted REMS assessment report. Indicates whether the program is meeting its goals or requires modification.

business_glossary_term: Last Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Assessment Submission Date
description: Date on which the most recent REMS assessment report was submitted to FDA via eCTD. Used to verify compliance with the assessment schedule.
reference: 21 CFR 314.520(c); eCTD Guidance; Veeva Vault RIM

business_glossary_term: Last Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Modification Date
description: Date of the most recent FDA-approved modification to the REMS program. Used to track program currency and trigger downstream label and communication updates.

business_glossary_term: Last Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Modification Type
description: Classification of the most recent REMS modification as major (substantive change to ETASU or goals), minor (non-substantive change), or administrative (formatting/contact updates).

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Modification Count
description: Total number of FDA-approved modifications made to the REMS program since initial approval. Tracks program evolution and regulatory change history.

business_glossary_term: Next Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Assessment Due Date
description: Date by which the next REMS assessment report must be submitted to FDA per the approved assessment schedule. Critical for regulatory compliance tracking.

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Administrator
description: Name of the organization or vendor contracted to administer the REMS program operations (e.g., patient enrollment, prescriber certification, pharmacy certification, call center). May be an internal team or a third-party REMS administrator.

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Name
description: Official FDA-approved name of the REMS program as registered in Veeva Vault RIM and the FDA REMS database (e.g., 'iPLEDGE REMS', 'TIRF REMS ACCESS').

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Status
description: Current lifecycle status of the REMS program. 'active' indicates the program is fully operational; 'under_review' indicates FDA is evaluating a modification; 'pending_approval' indicates initial REMS awaiting FDA approval.

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Version
description: Version identifier of the current approved REMS program document, incremented with each FDA-approved modification (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Aligns with eCTD submission versioning in Veeva Vault RIM.
reference: 21 CFR 314.520; eCTD Guidance; Veeva Vault RIM

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Number
description: FDA-assigned unique program identifier for the REMS, used for official correspondence and regulatory submissions. Serves as the externally-known business identifier.
Indicates whether pharmacies must be certified or enrolled in the REMS program before they are authorized to dispense the drug. Restricts dispensing to a controlled pharmacy network.
Indicates whether prescribers must be certified or enrolled in the REMS program before they are authorized to prescribe the drug. A key ETASU element for high-risk products.
Indicates whether the REMS program mandates specific laboratory tests (e.g., pregnancy tests, liver function tests, blood counts) as a condition of prescribing or dispensing the drug.
Indicates whether patients must be enrolled in the REMS program (e.g., patient registry or patient agreement) before receiving the drug. Enables tracking of patient exposure and outcomes.

Summary description of the serious risk(s) that necessitate the REMS program (e.g., teratogenicity, QT prolongation, severe hepatotoxicity, opioid addiction and overdose). Derived from the FDA-approved REMS document.
reference: 21 CFR 314.520; FDA REMS Approval Letter; ICH E2A

description: Route by which the drug product is administered to patients (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, transdermal). Informs risk communication and ETASU design.
reference: 21 CFR 201; FDA Route of Administration Terminology; ISO 11239

business_glossary_term: Shared Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program Indicator
description: Indicates whether this is a shared REMS program administered jointly by multiple sponsors (e.g., brand and generic manufacturers) under a single FDA-approved REMS. Relevant for interoperability with generic entrants.
reference: 21 CFR 314.520; FDA Shared REMS Guidance; FDAAA 2007

description: Therapeutic area classification of the drug product subject to the REMS (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Neurology, Rare Diseases, Pain Management). Used for portfolio-level REMS analytics.
Timestamp when this REMS program master record was most recently updated in the data lakehouse. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.
Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM for the current approved REMS program document. Provides direct traceability to the source regulatory document management system.
Unique surrogate identifier for the post-approval variation or supplement record in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Variations (formulation changes, new indications, manufacturing process changes) may affect patent claims or require new patent filings. Business process: patent strategy assessment during variation p

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.site. Business justification: Post-approval variations are frequently site-specific (site additions, manufacturing process changes at a site). Regulatory teams track which manufacturing site a variation applies to for site-specifi

Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Variations (post-approval changes) are submitted to health authorities via formal submissions. The variation record tracks the change request, while submission tracks the filing event. Adding submissi

description: Comma-separated list of eCTD Common Technical Document (CTD) module and section references impacted by this variation (e.g., 3.2.S.2.2, 3.2.P.3.3, 2.7.1). Sourced from Veeva Vault RIM dossier mapping.

description: Total number of days the regulatory review clock was stopped due to requests for supplementary information (List of Questions / List of Outstanding Issues) from the health authority, used for accurate timeline tracking.

description: Free-text field for additional regulatory affairs notes, internal observations, or contextual information relevant to the variation that does not fit structured fields.

description: Pharmaceutical dosage form of the product subject to this variation (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution for injection), as defined in the marketing authorization.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Sequence Number
description: Four-digit eCTD sequence number assigned to the variation submission within the application lifecycle, used for dossier version control and health authority tracking.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Specification

description: Reference identifier linking this variation to a grouped variation package when multiple changes are submitted together under EMA grouping or FDA bundling provisions.

description: Date on which the approved change was operationally implemented in manufacturing, labeling, or other affected business processes.
reference: ICH Q12; 21 CFR Part 314.70; EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008

description: Regulatory or internal deadline by which the approved variation must be implemented, as mandated by the health authority decision or internal policy.
reference: EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008; 21 CFR Part 314.70; ICH Q12

description: Current status of the operational implementation of the approved variation change across manufacturing, labeling, and supply chain.
reference: ICH Q12; Veeva Vault RIM; MasterControl / TrackWise CAPA

description: Indicates whether this variation is submitted as part of a grouped variation package (multiple changes submitted together in a single application under EMA grouping rules).
reference: EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008 Article 7; EMA Grouping Guideline

description: Indicates whether this variation includes a change to the approved product labeling (SmPC, PIL, or US prescribing information), triggering label management workflows.

description: Indicates whether this variation was initiated as a result of a pharmacovigilance signal, safety review, or post-market safety commitment, triggering expedited regulatory processing.

description: Date on which the health authority issued a List of Questions (LoQ) or Request for Information (RFI) during the review of this variation, initiating a clock stop.

description: The specific regulatory guideline, condition number, or legal basis under which the variation is classified (e.g., EMA Annex II condition number, ICH Q12 established conditions, FDA regulation citation).

description: Date on which the company submitted its response to the health authority's List of Questions or Request for Information, restarting the regulatory review clock.
reference: EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008; 21 CFR Part 314.70; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Approved strength or concentration of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) in the product subject to this variation (e.g., 10 mg, 100 mg/mL).
reference: EMA Product Information; FDA Orange Book; ICH CTD Module 3

description: Planned or projected date by which health authority approval of the variation is expected, based on regulatory clock and review timelines.
reference: EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008; 21 CFR PDUFA; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Therapeutic area or disease area associated with the product subject to this variation (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases), used for portfolio reporting.
reference: ATC Classification; ICH CTD Module 1; EMA Product Information

description: Timestamp when this variation record was last modified, supporting audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; GDPR Article 5; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Externally-known unique identifier assigned to the variation or supplement by the health authority or internal regulatory tracking system (e.g., EMA variation number, FDA supplement number, or internal RIMS reference).
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008; 21 CFR Part 314.70

description: Current lifecycle status of the variation or supplement from initial drafting through health authority decision and implementation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|refused|withdrawn|implemented — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

reference: Veeva Vault RIM Variation Management Module; 21 CFR Part 11

business_glossary_term: Drug Master File (DMF) Authorization Letter Date
description: Date on which the most recent Letter of Authorization (LoA) was issued by the DMF holder to the referencing applicant. Null if no authorization letter has been issued.

business_glossary_term: Drug Master File (DMF) Authorization Letter Issued

description: Current workflow state of the submission within the regulatory review lifecycle. Draft = being prepared; Submitted = transmitted to agency; Under Review = agency actively reviewing; Approved = marketing authorization granted; Complete Response = FDA issued CRL requiring additional data; Refused = EMA/MHRA refusal; Withdrawn = applicant withdrew; On Hold = review paused. description: Physical or electronic channel used to transmit the submission to the health authority. Electronic Gateway (e.g., FDA ESG, EMA eSubmission Gateway) is the standard; physical media (CD/DVD/USB) and portal submissions are also tracked.

description: Indicates whether a Pre-Approval Inspection (PAI) of manufacturing facilities is required as part of the review of this submission. PAIs verify GMP compliance and data integrity of manufacturing processes prior to approval. True = PAI required.

description: Special regulatory designation granted to the submission or parent application that confers expedited review or development benefits. description: Calendar date on which the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the submission. Triggers the start of the regulatory review clock and PDUFA/EMA review timeline. May differ from submission_date due to gateway processing.

description: Regulatory review pathway designation assigned to this submission, determining review timelines and procedural requirements. Examples include FDA Standard, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track; EMA Conditional Marketing Authorisation, Rolling Review. reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 312.80; FDA FDASIA 2012; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004 Article 14; ICH E8

description: Name of the health authority review division or committee responsible for evaluating this submission (e.g., FDA CDER Office of Oncology Products, EMA Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), PMDA Office of New Drug I). Determines reviewer assignment and procedural rules.

description: Route by which the drug product is administered to patients as specified in this submission (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical, inhalation). Captured per ICH M4 CTD Module 1 product information.

description: Four-digit zero-padded eCTD sequence number uniquely identifying this submission within the parent application lifecycle (e.g., 0000 for initial submission, 0001 for first amendment). Conforms to ICH M8 eCTD technical specification.

description: Calendar date on which the submission was formally transmitted to the health authority. This is the principal business event date used for regulatory timeline calculations, PDUFA date computation, and compliance tracking.

description: Human-readable external submission reference number assigned by the health authority or internal tracking system (e.g., NDA 123456/S-001, EMA/H/C/000123/0001). Used for correspondence and regulatory tracking.

description: Classification of the submission event within the regulatory application lifecycle. Original = initial filing; Supplement = post-approval change; Amendment = pre-approval change; Annual Report = periodic safety/status update; Response = reply to agency information request; Withdrawal = voluntary withdrawal of application. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original|supplement|amendment|annual_report|response|withdrawal — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.70; ICH M4 CTD; EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008

description: Further classification of the submission type providing regulatory granularity (e.g., Prior Approval Supplement, Changes Being Effected Supplement, Type IA Variation, Type IB Variation, Type II Variation). Jurisdiction-specific categorization used for review timeline determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.70; EMA Variation Regulation (EC) No 1234/2008; ICH M4

description: Regulatory target date by which the health authority is committed to completing its review and issuing a decision. For FDA submissions, this is the Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) goal date. For EMA, this is the Day 210 opinion date. Critical milestone for commercial planning.
reference: FDA PDUFA VI; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; 21 CFR Part 314.100

description: Clinical therapeutic area or disease category addressed by the drug product in this submission (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio analytics, resource planning, and regulatory strategy alignment.

description: Timestamp when this submission record was last modified in the data lakehouse, conforming to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data integrity verification under 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GDPR Article 5(2); ISO 8601

description: Number of technical validation errors identified during eCTD dossier validation. Zero indicates a clean submission. Used to assess submission quality and track remediation effort prior to resubmission.

description: Technical validation status of the eCTD dossier as assessed by the health authority's automated validation tools (e.g., FDA Validation Criteria, EMA eCTD Validation Criteria). Passed = all technical criteria met; Failed = validation errors requiring correction; Waived = validation requirement formally waived.
reference: FDA eCTD Validation Criteria; EMA eCTD Validation Criteria v6.0; ICH M8 eCTD Technical Specification

description: Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system for this submission record. Enables direct cross-reference and deep-link navigation to the source system of record for submission lifecycle management.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each eCTD submission sequence record within the regulatory application lifecycle.

description: Date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the submission sequence. Triggers the start of the regulatory review clock for applicable submission types.

description: Date and time at which this submission sequence record was first created in the data platform, serving as the audit trail creation marker per 21 CFR Part 11 and data governance requirements.

description: Indicates whether a Contract Research Organization (CRO) or Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO) contributed data or documents to this submission sequence. Relevant for supply chain transparency and regulatory accountability.

description: The primary Common Technical Document (CTD) module addressed by this submission sequence. M1 = Regional Administrative; M2 = Summaries; M3 = Quality (CMC); M4 = Nonclinical; M5 = Clinical. Used for content classification and routing.

reference: ICH M8 eCTD Technical Specification v4.0; FDA eCTD Guidance for Industry

description: The version of the Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) specification used to structure and validate this submission sequence (e.g., 3.2.2 for legacy, 4.0 for ICH M8 compliant submissions).

description: Regulatory priority designation applicable to this submission, which may affect review timelines and PDUFA goal dates. reference: 21 CFR Part 314.110; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004; MHRA Guidance on Regulatory Decisions

description: The regulatory procedure under which this submission is filed, particularly relevant for EMA submissions. Centralized = EMA centralized procedure; Decentralized = DCP across multiple EU member states; Mutual Recognition = MRP; National = single country national procedure. Null for FDA submissions.

description: The date on which the regulatory review clock officially begins for this submission sequence, as determined by the health authority. May differ from acknowledgement_date for submissions requiring filing review or completeness assessment.

description: Four-digit zero-padded sequence number assigned to this submission event within the regulatory application, as defined by ICH M8 eCTD specification (e.g., 0000 for original submission, 0001 for first amendment).
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Technical Specification v4.0; FDA eCTD Guidance for Industry

description: Free-text narrative description of the purpose and content of this submission sequence, summarizing the regulatory objective (e.g., 'Response to FDA Complete Response Letter dated 2024-03-15 addressing CMC deficiencies in Module 3').
reference: Veeva Vault RIM Submission Description Field; FDA eCTD Guidance for Industry

description: Current lifecycle status of the submission sequence reflecting the health authority's processing state. Draft = prepared but not yet transmitted; Submitted = transmitted to health authority; Acknowledged = receipt confirmed; Under Review = active regulatory review; Accepted = approved/validated; Refused = rejected by health authority; Withdrawn = retracted by sponsor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|under_review|accepted|refused|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA eCTD Guidance for Industry; EMA eCTD Guidance; ICH M8; Veeva Vault RIM Submission Status Workflow

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the eCTD sequence package was transmitted to or received by the health authority gateway (e.g., FDA ESG, EMA CESP). Distinct from submission_date which captures only the calendar date.

description: Number of submission units (eCTD submission unit packages) included in this sequence. In eCTD v4.0, a sequence may contain multiple submission units each addressing different regulatory activities.

description: Regulatory category of a supplement submission indicating the level of change and required review pathway. PAS = Prior Approval Supplement; CBE-30 = Changes Being Effected in 30 Days; CBE-0 = Changes Being Effected immediately; Class I/II per EMA variation classification. Null for non-supplement submissions.

description: Date and time at which this submission sequence record was last modified in the data platform, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); Veeva Vault RIM Audit Trail

description: Number of technical validation errors identified during eCTD package validation. A value of zero indicates a clean validation pass. Used to triage submission readiness and track remediation progress.
reference: FDA eCTD Validation Criteria; EMA eCTD Validation Rules

description: Technical validation status of the eCTD submission package against FDA/EMA validation criteria and the eCTD specification. Pending = validation not yet run; Passed = all validation checks successful; Failed = one or more validation errors identified; Waived = validation requirement formally waived by health authority.
reference: FDA eCTD Validation Criteria; EMA eCTD Validation Rules; ICH M8 eCTD Technical Specification

description: Actual quantity of material for which goods issue (GI) has been posted at the sending plant. May differ from transfer_quantity in cases of partial goods issue. Corresponds to SAP movement type 351/641 posted quantity.
reference: SAP MM Goods Issue; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Date and time when the stock transfer order record was last modified in the source system. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP MM Change Documents; 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records)

description: SAP inventory management movement type code used for the stock transfer. Key values: 301 (one-step plant-to-plant), 303/305 (two-step transfer order), 351 (goods issue for STO), 641/647 (goods issue with delivery). Determines accounting and inventory impact.
reference: SAP MM Movement Types; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Business priority assigned to the stock transfer order. Critical transfers may relate to patient safety supply continuity, clinical trial supply, or shortage situations. Drives scheduling and resource allocation in supply chain planning.
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse Supply Chain Planning; Blue Yonder Supply Chain

description: Business reason code explaining why the stock transfer was initiated. Examples include demand replenishment, safety stock replenishment, inter-company transfer, clinical trial supply, product recall, quality hold relocation, or returns processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: demand_replenishment|safety_stock|inter_company|clinical_supply|recall|quality_relocation|returns|balance_stock — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; Kinaxis RapidResponse

description: Actual quantity of material for which goods receipt (GR) has been posted at the receiving plant. May differ from issued_quantity due to transit losses, damage, or rejection. Corresponds to SAP movement type 101/105 posted quantity.
reference: SAP MM Goods Receipt; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: SAP storage location code within the receiving plant where the materials will be placed upon goods receipt. Identifies the specific warehouse area, cold-room, quarantine zone, or controlled substance vault at the destination site.
reference: SAP WM Storage Location; SAP S/4HANA WM Module

description: The date by which the receiving plant requires the materials to arrive. Used for supply chain planning, scheduling goods issue, and measuring on-time delivery performance. Corresponds to the SAP STO delivery date field.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; Kinaxis RapidResponse Supply Chain Planning

description: Shipping service level or condition required for the transfer. Determines carrier selection, transit time expectations, and special handling requirements. Corresponds to SAP SD shipping condition configuration.
reference: SAP SD Shipping Condition; GDP (Good Distribution Practice) — PIC/S PE 011

description: Date and time when the stock transfer order was created in the source system (SAP MM). Represents the business event timestamp of order initiation. Used for lead time analysis and supply chain performance reporting.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Externally-known business document number assigned by SAP MM for the inter-plant or inter-site stock transfer order. Corresponds to the SAP Purchase Order number used for STO processing (document type UB or NB).
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: SAP stock type of the material at the sending location prior to transfer. Indicates whether the material is in unrestricted use, quality inspection (QI) stock, blocked stock, or restricted-use stock. Impacts GDP compliance and release status.
reference: SAP MM Stock Types; 21 CFR Part 211.68; EU GMP Chapter 3

description: Required storage and transport temperature condition for the pharmaceutical material. Drives cold-chain logistics planning and carrier qualification. Values: ambient (15-25°C), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (-20°C), deep_frozen (-80°C), controlled_room_temp (20-25°C).
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Testing; USP <1079> Good Storage and Distribution Practices; GDP PIC/S PE 011

description: Distinguishes whether the stock transfer is part of the commercial supply chain or the clinical trial supply chain. Clinical trial transfers require additional traceability and compliance controls per GCP and IND/CTA requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); ICH E6 GCP; GDP (Good Distribution Practice)

description: Carrier-assigned tracking number or waybill number for the shipment associated with this stock transfer order. Enables real-time shipment visibility and chain of custody documentation for GDP compliance.
reference: GDP (Good Distribution Practice) — PIC/S PE 011; SAP MM/WM Shipment Tracking

description: Classifies the nature of the transfer based on the sending and receiving location types: plant-to-plant, distribution center to plant, plant to distribution center, DC-to-DC, or involving a third-party logistics (3PL) provider. Supports network-level supply chain analytics.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; GDP (Good Distribution Practice) — PIC/S PE 011

description: Quantity of the pharmaceutical material ordered for transfer in the base unit of measure. Represents the planned transfer quantity as specified in the STO line item.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; GDP (Good Distribution Practice)

description: Current lifecycle status of the stock transfer order. Tracks progression from creation through goods issue, in-transit, goods receipt, and closure or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|goods_issued|in_transit|partially_received|received|cancelled|blocked — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; GDP (Good Distribution Practice) — PIC/S PE 011

description: Indicates whether the transfer is a one-step transfer (goods issue and receipt posted simultaneously) or a two-step transfer (goods issue posted at sending plant, goods receipt posted separately at receiving plant). Aligns with SAP MM movement types 301/303/305.
reference: SAP MM Stock Transfer Order; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Unique surrogate primary key for the goods issue record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the system-generated identifier for each material outbound movement or consumption event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module – MIGO Transaction

description: Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: goods_issue has clinical_trial_number as a free-text string, but clinical supply issuances should be linked to the structured clinical_supply_order record. Adding clinical_supply_order_id → clinical_s

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: goods_issue.cost_center is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.cost_center enables accurate financial posting of material movements to validated cost centers for manufacturing cost

Foreign key linking to supply.delivery_order. Business justification: Goods issue documents are created as part of delivery order fulfillment. The delivery_document_number (STRING) should be replaced with a proper FK to delivery_order.delivery_order_id. This establishes

Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Goods issue transactions occur at specific distribution network nodes. The plant_code (STRING) should be replaced with distribution_network_id FK to distribution_network.distribution_network_id. This

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: GMP-relevant goods issues in pharma manufacturing must reference the drug product for batch traceability, cost accounting by product, and controlled substance movement reporting. Regulatory audits req

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Goods issue transactions consume inventory from specific lots. The batch_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material identifiers and descrip

Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: goods_issue.issuing_storage_location is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.storage_location enables GMP-compliant goods issue posting from validated storage zones and supports GDP

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Goods issue automatic accounting: every goods issue (material consumption, intercompany transfer, destruction) generates a journal entry for inventory cost accounting (COGS, write-offs). SAP MM-FI int

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Material issuance requires material master data for valuation, specifications, handling instructions, controlled substance schedules. Essential for inventory accounting, GMP compliance, and movement t

Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: goods_issue.profit_center is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.profit_center enables accurate P&L attribution of material movements for segment reporting and intercompany transfer

Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Material issuances to receiving plants require plant master data for GMP status, capacity, regulatory authorization. Receiving_plant is denormalized code; FK enables plant qualification verification a

Foreign key linking to supply.stock_transfer_order. Business justification: A goods issue is the sending-side event for a stock transfer order — when materials leave the source plant/warehouse as part of an inter-site transfer. goods_issue has order_reference_number (string)

Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: goods_issue.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for GMP batch documentation and financial goods m

Indicates whether the issued material requires cold-chain temperature-controlled handling and transportation (e.g., 2-8°C refrigerated, -20°C frozen, -80°C ultra-cold for biologics and mRNA products). When True, GDP cold-chain documentation including temperature monitoring records must accompany the shipment.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; WHO Technical Report Series No. 961 (Cold Chain); 21 CFR Part 211.142

description: The date and time when the goods issue document was first created and posted in SAP (CPUDT + CPUTM in MKPF). Provides the precise audit timestamp for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and GMP batch record documentation. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – CPUDT/CPUTM; 21 CFR Part 11; EU GMP Annex 11; ISO 8601

description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the material cost amount (WAERS in SAP). Represents the local currency of the issuing plant. Required for multi-currency financial consolidation and cross-border supply chain cost reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI – WAERS; IFRS/US GAAP

description: The certificate of destruction document number issued by a licensed waste disposal or pharmaceutical destruction contractor when materials are scrapped or destroyed (movement type 551/552). Required for GMP compliance, DEA controlled substance destruction reporting, and environmental regulatory compliance. Populated only for destruction goods issues.
reference: 21 CFR Part 1317 (Disposal of Controlled Substances); EU GMP Chapter 5; DEA Regulations; EPA RCRA

description: The date the goods issue document was physically created or the actual date of the material movement (BLDAT in SAP). May differ from posting_date when backdated postings occur. Used for audit trail and GMP documentation compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – BLDAT; 21 CFR Part 211.68; GDP Guidelines

description: The externally-known SAP material document number assigned to the goods issue posting (e.g., 4900012345). This is the primary business identifier referenced in GMP batch records, cost accounting entries, and supply chain traceability. Corresponds to MBLNR in SAP MSEG/MKPF tables.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – MIGO; 21 CFR Part 211.188 (Batch Production Records); ICH Q7

description: The fiscal year in which the goods issue material document was posted in SAP (MJAHR). Required in combination with gi_document_number to uniquely identify a material document in SAP MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – MKPF Table; SAP FI Fiscal Year Variant

description: Current lifecycle status of the goods issue document. 'posted' indicates the GI is active and inventory has been reduced; 'reversed' indicates a reversal document has been posted (movement type 262/552/602); 'blocked' indicates the document is on quality hold; 'cancelled' indicates the document was voided prior to posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – MIGO; 21 CFR Part 211.192 (Investigation of Discrepancies)

description: Indicates whether the issued material is a DEA-scheduled controlled substance requiring additional regulatory documentation, DEA Form 222 or CSOS electronic ordering, and controlled substance inventory reconciliation. When True, the goods issue must comply with DEA 21 CFR Part 1304 record-keeping requirements.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1304 (Records and Reports); DEA Controlled Substances Act; 21 CFR Part 211.188

description: Indicates whether this goods issue is subject to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) documentation and traceability requirements. True for materials used in GMP-regulated manufacturing, clinical trial supply, or quality testing. False for non-GMP materials such as office supplies, maintenance items, or non-regulated consumables. Drives GMP batch record inclusion and regulatory audit scope.
reference: 21 CFR Parts 210/211; EU GMP Guidelines; ICH Q7; PIC/S Guide to GMP

description: The quantity of material issued in the base unit of measure (ERFMG in SAP MSEG). Represents the actual amount consumed, shipped, scrapped, or transferred. Drives inventory reduction, cost accounting entries, and GMP batch record material consumption documentation. Must reconcile with production order component requirements or delivery quantities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – ERFMG; 21 CFR Part 211.188; ICH Q7 Section 6; GDP Guidelines

description: The date and time when the goods issue record was last modified in the source system or Silver Layer. Tracks subsequent changes such as reversal postings, status updates, or data corrections. Required for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); EU GMP Annex 11; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: The total cost value of the goods issue calculated as issued_quantity multiplied by the material valuation price at time of posting (DMBTR in SAP MSEG in local currency). Represents the COGS or cost allocation amount posted to the consuming cost center or production order. Critical for financial reporting, profitability analysis, and manufacturing cost tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO – DMBTR; IFRS/US GAAP COGS Recognition; SOX Section 404

description: SAP MM movement type code that classifies the nature of the goods issue (BWART in MSEG). Drives inventory valuation, account determination, and GMP documentation requirements. Key values: 261=Goods issue to production order (consumption), 551=Scrapping/destruction, 601=Goods issue for delivery/customer shipment, 311=Transfer posting within plant. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 261|262|551|552|601|602|311|312|901|902 — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Movement Type Configuration; 21 CFR Part 211.188; GDP Guidelines

description: Human-readable description of the SAP movement type (e.g., 'Goods Issue to Production Order', 'Goods Issue for Delivery', 'Scrapping', 'Sample Withdrawal', 'Transfer Posting'). Supports business reporting and GMP batch record documentation without requiring SAP configuration knowledge.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – T156T Table; 21 CFR Part 211.188

description: The reference number of the consuming or receiving order associated with the goods issue. For production consumption (movement type 261), this is the SAP production order number (AUFNR). For clinical supply, this is the clinical trial supply order number. For customer delivery, this is the SAP sales order number (VBELN). Central to GMP batch record traceability and cost allocation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/SD – AUFNR/VBELN; 21 CFR Part 211.188; ICH Q7

description: Classification of the order type associated with the goods issue. Determines the business process context: production_order/process_order for manufacturing consumption, sales_order for commercial customer delivery, clinical_supply_order for investigational product distribution to trial sites, transfer_order for inter-plant movements, destruction_order for controlled substance or expired material disposal.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/SD/MM; 21 CFR Part 211.188; ICH Q7; GCP Guidelines

description: The accounting and inventory posting date of the goods issue (BUDAT in SAP). Determines the period in which inventory reduction and cost accounting entries are recorded. Critical for period-end closing, GMP batch record dating, and regulatory traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI – BUDAT; 21 CFR Part 211.188; ICH Q7 Section 6

description: The SAP reason code (GRUND) providing additional classification for the goods issue movement, particularly for scrapping, destruction, or non-standard consumption. Examples include: expired material destruction, quality rejection scrap, clinical trial sample, regulatory submission sample, stability testing sample. Supports GMP deviation tracking and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – GRUND; 21 CFR Part 211.192; EU GMP Chapter 5; ICH Q7

description: For intra-plant transfer postings (movement type 311), the destination storage location within the same plant. Tracks material movement between warehouse zones, e.g., from quarantine to released stock, from bulk storage to dispensing area, or from cold-chain to ambient staging.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM – UMLGO; 21 CFR Part 211.68; EU GMP Annex 1; GDP Guidelines

description: External or cross-system reference document number associated with the goods issue (XBLNR in SAP). May reference a purchase order, clinical trial supply request, destruction certificate, quality disposition document, or regulatory sample request. Enables cross-system traceability between SAP and CTMS, LIMS, or QMS systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – XBLNR; 21 CFR Part 211.188; Veeva Vault QualityDocs; MasterControl

description: The SAP material document number of the reversal posting (STBLG) if this goods issue has been reversed. A reversal restores inventory and reverses cost accounting entries. Populated only when gi_status = 'reversed'. Required for GMP audit trail and financial period correction documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – STBLG; 21 CFR Part 211.68 (Audit Trail); EU GMP Annex 11

description: SAP special stock indicator (SOBKZ) identifying whether the issued material belongs to a special stock category. Key values: E=Sales order stock (customer-specific), Q=Project stock, K=Consignment stock at customer, W=Vendor consignment stock. Empty value indicates standard own stock. Critical for clinical trial supply (project stock) and consignment management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – SOBKZ; 21 CFR Part 211.150; GDP Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Stock Type at Time of Issue
description: The quality/availability stock type from which the material was issued (INSMK in SAP). Indicates whether material was issued from unrestricted (released) stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, or restricted-use stock. Critical for GMP compliance — goods issues from quality inspection or blocked stock require QA authorization and deviation documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – INSMK; 21 CFR Part 211.84 (Testing and Approval); EU GMP Chapter 4; ICH Q7

description: The inventory valuation method applied to the goods issue for cost accounting purposes (BWTAR in SAP). Determines how the material consumption cost is calculated: standard cost (standard price from material master), moving average price (MAP), or actual cost. Impacts COGS calculation and financial reporting accuracy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-IM Valuation; IFRS/US GAAP Inventory Valuation; SOX Section 404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each lot/batch-level inventory record in the pharmaceutical supply chain. Primary key for the inventory_lot data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM Module; 21 CFR Part 211.192

description: Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Inventory valuation, expiry management, and revenue recognition in pharma require brand-level aggregation. Enables commercial finance to track inventory by brand for gross-to-net calculations, sample

Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: Each inventory lot of an API or drug product is manufactured under a specific DMF. QA lot release and disposition decisions reference the DMF to confirm GMP compliance and manufacturing site authoriza

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Inventory lots represent physical stock of specific drug products (formulation+strength+dosage form). Essential for batch traceability, GDP compliance, recall execution, and regulatory lot disposition

Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: GCP drug accountability logs (required by ICH E6 and FDA 21 CFR Part 312) track which investigator received, used, and returned each clinical supply lot. inventory_lot has is_clinical_supply flag but

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Every inventory lot must reference its material master for specifications, regulatory data, storage requirements, quality parameters. Material_number is denormalized identifier; FK enforces referentia

Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Lots are manufactured in specific packaging configurations. Required for serialization hierarchy (GTIN/NDC mapping), regulatory compliance reporting, and distribution channel planning where package si

Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: Inventory lots designated for patient access programs (free goods, compassionate use stock) are segregated and tracked separately for program cost accounting, regulatory reporting, and benefit cap man

Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: An inventory lot is created as a result of executing a supply plan (planned production or planned receipt). Linking inventory_lot.plan_id → plan.plan_id connects the physical lot to the supply plan th

Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Inventory lots are held at plants requiring plant master data for GDP authorization, GMP status, capacity management, regulatory inspections. Plant_code is denormalized identifier; FK enforces referen

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: GMP batch traceability and recall management require tracing any inventory lot back to the production order that created it. FDA 21 CFR Part 211 and EU GMP Annex 15 mandate full batch genealogy. No ex

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Inventory lots in pharma are managed at SKU level for market-specific stock allocation, reimbursement tracking, and serialization compliance. sku_code is a denormalized plain attribute; FK to sku enab

Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Lots are stored at specific storage locations requiring master data for GDP compliance, capacity management, temperature zones, DEA authorization. Storage_location_code is denormalized identifier; FK

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Pharma GMP requires traceability of each inventory lot to its supplier for quality investigations, CAPA management, and supplier qualification audits. inventory_lot.supplier_lot_number implies a suppl

Foreign key linking to finance.transfer_price. Business justification: Intercompany inventory valuation: when lots are transferred between legal entities, the approved transfer price governs the intercompany cost applied to that lot. Finance and tax teams reconcile lot u

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Clinical inventory lots (is_clinical_supply=true) are allocated to specific trials. Linking inventory_lot to trial supports IMP accountability reconciliation, expiry management per trial, and regulato

Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: inventory_lot.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures GMP-compliant UOM validation for batch release and regulatory submissions

Reference number of the associated Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in the Quality Management System (QMS) if this lot is subject to an active quality investigation or deviation. Links to MasterControl or TrackWise QMS.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.192; ICH Q10; MasterControl/TrackWise; FDA

description: Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued by the manufacturer or supplier for this lot, confirming that the lot meets all specified quality attributes and release criteria. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory inspections.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.84; EU GMP Chapter 6; ICH Q7; GDP Guidelines

description: Date on which a cold chain break or temperature excursion was first detected for this lot. Populated only when temperature_excursion_flag is True. Used to initiate quality impact assessment and determine lot disposition.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines; USP <1079>; WHO Technical Report Series No. 961

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit_cost valuation (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency inventory valuation reporting across global manufacturing and distribution sites.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 21

description: Number of days remaining until the lot's expiry date, calculated at the time of data extraction. Used for near-expiry inventory alerts, FEFO prioritization, and supply chain risk management. Negative values indicate expired lots.
reference: GDP Guidelines; Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder; ICH Q1A

description: DEA controlled substance schedule classification (I through V) for this lot, if applicable. Determines the level of security, storage, and record-keeping requirements. Null for non-controlled substances.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1308; Controlled Substances Act; DEA

description: Date after which the lot is no longer suitable for use, as determined by stability studies and approved shelf-life specifications. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management, GDP compliance, and regulatory reporting. Corresponds to SAP batch expiry date (VFDAT field).
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.137; EU GMP Chapter 4; ICH Q1A; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Flag indicating whether this lot is designated for use in clinical trials (investigational product supply) rather than commercial distribution. Clinical supply lots are subject to additional regulatory controls under GCP and IND/CTA requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6 (GCP); FDA

description: Flag indicating whether this lot contains a DEA-scheduled controlled substance requiring additional security, storage, and regulatory reporting obligations under the Controlled Substances Act.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301; Controlled Substances Act; DEA

description: Flag indicating whether this lot's storage and handling at the current location is confirmed compliant with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) requirements, including temperature monitoring, security, and documentation standards.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; WHO Technical Report Series No. 957; PIC/S

description: Date of the most recent inventory movement (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer posting, or stock transfer) recorded for this lot in SAP. Used for slow-moving inventory analysis and supply chain performance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM Module; Kinaxis RapidResponse

description: Manufacturer-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the production lot or batch. Enables full lot/batch traceability from raw material receipt through finished goods distribution. Corresponds to SAP batch number (CHARG field). Required for GMP compliance and recall management.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.68; EU GMP Annex 11; ICH Q7; SAP S/4HANA QM/WM Module

description: Current quality and disposition status of the lot in the inventory management system. Drives whether the lot is available for use, shipment, or must be held pending quality review. Maps to SAP batch status (unrestricted-use, quality inspection, blocked). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unrestricted|quality_hold|quarantine|rejected|released|destroyed — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.192; EU GMP Chapter 4; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ICH Q7

description: Date on which the lot was manufactured or the production process was initiated. Used for shelf-life calculation, lot traceability, and regulatory reporting. Corresponds to SAP batch manufacturing date (HSDAT field).
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.68; EU GMP Chapter 4; ICH Q7; SAP S/4HANA PP/QM Module

description: Classification of the material held in this lot. Distinguishes between Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API), excipient, packaging component, drug substance (DS), drug product (DP), and finished dosage form (FDF/finished good). Drives storage, handling, and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: API|excipient|packaging_component|drug_substance|drug_product|finished_good — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q7; ICH Q11; 21 CFR Part 210; EU GMP

description: FDA-assigned National Drug Code in 5-4-2 format uniquely identifying the drug product, including labeler, product, and package codes. Applicable to finished drug products and drug substances registered with the FDA. Null for raw materials and packaging components.
reference: FDA NDC Directory; 21 CFR Part 207; FDA

description: SAP purchase order number associated with the procurement of this lot from an external supplier. Enables financial reconciliation, goods receipt matching, and procurement traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Module; GDP Guidelines

description: Net quantity of the lot available for new allocations, calculated as quantity_on_hand minus quantity_reserved minus quantity_on_quality_hold. Represents the actionable inventory for supply planning and order fulfillment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder

description: Total physical quantity of the lot currently in stock at the storage location, expressed in the base unit of measure. Represents the gross inventory position before reservations. Sourced from SAP inventory management (MARD/MCHB tables).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/WM Module; ICH Q7

description: Quantity of the lot currently blocked due to quality inspection, Out-of-Specification (OOS) investigation, or quality hold pending Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) resolution. Not available for use or shipment.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.192; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; MasterControl/TrackWise

description: Quantity of the lot that has been reserved for specific production orders, sales orders, or clinical trial supply requests but not yet physically consumed or shipped. Reduces available quantity for new allocations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Module; Kinaxis RapidResponse

description: Date on which the lot was received at the current storage location via goods receipt (GR) posting in SAP. Marks the start of the lot's residency at the site and triggers incoming quality inspection workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module (MIGO GR); GDP Guidelines; 21 CFR Part 211.84

description: Timestamp when this inventory lot record was first created in the source system (SAP) or the data lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and data lineage tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA; GDPR Article 5

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this inventory lot record in the source system or data lakehouse. Supports change data capture (CDC), audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and data freshness monitoring.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which the lot was formally released by Quality Assurance (QA) for use in manufacturing, clinical supply, or commercial distribution following successful completion of all required quality control testing and review.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.192; EU GMP Chapter 6; ICH Q7; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Date by which the lot must be retested to confirm continued compliance with specifications before further use or distribution. Applicable primarily to APIs, drug substances, and excipients with defined retest periods per ICH Q7 and approved specifications.
reference: ICH Q7 Section 11.6; ICH Q1A; 21 CFR Part 211.137; EU GMP Chapter 4

description: Code identifying the manufacturing site, distribution center, or third-party logistics (3PL) location where this lot is physically held. Enables multi-site inventory visibility and GDP compliance tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; GDP Guidelines; PIC/S; EU GMP Chapter 3

description: Required storage temperature condition for this lot as specified in the product's approved labeling and storage requirements. Drives cold-chain logistics planning and GDP compliance. Values: ambient (15–25°C), refrigerated (2–8°C), frozen (-20°C), controlled_room_temp (20–25°C), ultra_low (-60 to -80°C).
reference: ICH Q1A; USP <1079>; GDP Guidelines; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Original lot or batch number assigned by the external supplier, Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO), or Contract Development and Manufacturing Organization (CDMO). Enables traceability back to the supplier's manufacturing records and Certificate of Analysis (CoA).
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.84; EU GMP Chapter 5; ICH Q7; GDP Guidelines

description: Flag indicating whether this lot has experienced a documented temperature excursion during storage or transit that may impact product quality and requires quality impact assessment per GDP and cold-chain management procedures.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines; USP <1079>; ICH Q1A; WHO Technical Report Series No. 961

description: Standard or moving average cost per unit of measure for this lot as recorded in SAP. Used for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and financial reporting. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; IFRS IAS 2; SOX; GAAP

description: SAP inventory valuation method applied to this lot for financial costing purposes. Determines how the lot's value is calculated in the general ledger (standard price, moving average price, or actual cost). Relevant for COGS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; IFRS IAS 2; SOX

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the warehouse receipt record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module – Goods Receipt (MIGO)

description: Foreign key linking to supply.clinical_supply_order. Business justification: warehouse_receipt has a clinical_supply_flag boolean indicating clinical supply receipts, but no FK to the clinical_supply_order that triggered the receipt. Adding clinical_supply_order_id normalizes

Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: Warehouse receipts occur at specific distribution network nodes (plants, warehouses). The plant_code (STRING) should be replaced with distribution_network_id FK to distribution_network.distribution_ne

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Goods receipt in pharma requires drug product identification for QP release decisions, storage condition assignment, and GMP-compliant inspection lot creation. A pharma supply chain expert expects war

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Warehouse receipts create or update inventory lot records upon material receipt. The batch_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material attri

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Goods receipt must validate against material master specifications (storage conditions, quality inspection requirements, batch management rules). Critical for GDP compliance, COA verification, and qua
