Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Goods receipts occur at specific plants requiring plant master data for GMP certification status, FDA establishment identifier, capacity validation. Essential for receipt authorization, quality inspec

Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: A warehouse receipt is the physical confirmation of an inbound shipment arriving at a pharmaceutical manufacturing site, warehouse, or distribution center. Linking warehouse_receipt.shipment_id → ship

Foreign key linking to supply.stock_transfer_order. Business justification: A warehouse receipt at the receiving plant is the completion event for a stock transfer order. Linking warehouse_receipt.stock_transfer_order_id → stock_transfer_order.stock_transfer_order_id enables

Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: warehouse_receipt.storage_location is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.storage_location enables GMP-compliant goods receipt posting to validated storage zones and supports GDP co

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: Warehouse receipts must be traceable to the supplying business partner for GMP supplier qualification, incoming inspection management, and purchase order reconciliation. No supplier FK currently exist

Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: warehouse_receipt.unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for GMP goods receipt documentation and reg

The actual date and time the physical materials arrived at the receiving dock or facility. Distinct from the SAP posting date; captures the real-world event time for GDP compliance, cold-chain monitoring, and traceability. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDP – Good Distribution Practice; 21 CFR Part 211.68; ICH Q10

description: The carrier's bill of lading number for the inbound shipment. Provides the legal transport document reference linking the receipt to the logistics carrier record. Used for freight claims, GDP transport documentation, and supply chain traceability.

The name of the logistics carrier or third-party logistics provider (3PL) responsible for transporting the materials to the receiving site. Used for GDP carrier qualification tracking and freight performance management.

Indicates whether the received material is designated for clinical trial supply (investigational product) rather than commercial supply. True = clinical trial supply subject to IND/GCP requirements; False = commercial supply. Determines applicable regulatory framework and handling procedures.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 – IND; GCP; ICH E6

description: The document reference number or identifier for the vendor's Certificate of Analysis (CoA) accompanying the shipment. The CoA confirms the material meets specification. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory traceability. Stored in Veeva Vault or LIMS document management.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.84; ICH Q7; GMP; GDP

description: The document reference number for the vendor's Certificate of Compliance (CoC), confirming the material was manufactured in accordance with GMP and applicable regulatory requirements. Distinct from the CoA which covers analytical test results.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.84; ICH Q7; GMP; GDP

description: Timestamp when this warehouse receipt record was first created in the source system or data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The vendor's delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the shipment. Used for physical receipt verification, discrepancy identification, and document archiving per GMP requirements.

The GDP compliance check status for this receipt, confirming that the material was transported and received in accordance with Good Distribution Practice guidelines. 'compliant' = all GDP checks passed; 'non_compliant' = one or more GDP deviations identified; 'pending_review' = GDP review in progress.
reference: GDP – EU Guidelines 2013/C 68/01; WHO GDP; 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: The SAP Material Document number generated upon posting the goods receipt in SAP S/4HANA MIGO (transaction MIGO/MB01). Serves as the primary business identifier linking this receipt to the source ERP transaction. Format follows SAP material document numbering (e.g., 5000012345).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Document (MIGO); 21 CFR Part 211.68; GDP

description: The fiscal year associated with the SAP GR material document number. Required because SAP material document numbers are unique only within a fiscal year. Combined with gr_document_number to form the unique SAP key.

SAP inbound delivery document number (transaction VL31N/VL32N) associated with this goods receipt, if applicable. Used when the receipt is triggered by an advance shipping notice (ASN) from the vendor. May be null for direct PO-based receipts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM/EWM – Inbound Delivery; GDP

description: The SAP Quality Management (QM) inspection lot number automatically created upon goods receipt posting for materials subject to quality inspection. Links the warehouse receipt to the quality inspection process in SAP QM and LIMS. Null if no inspection is required.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM – Inspection Lot; 21 CFR Part 211.84; GMP

description: The specific line item number within the purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. A single PO may have multiple line items for different materials or delivery schedules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchase Order Item

description: The ordered quantity as specified in the purchase order line item. Used to calculate receipt variance (received vs. ordered) and to identify over/under deliveries requiring vendor notification or tolerance check.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchase Order Quantity

description: The accounting posting date of the goods receipt in SAP, determining the fiscal period in which the inventory and accounting entries are recorded. This is the principal business event date for this transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – MIGO Posting Date; 21 CFR Part 211.68

description: The SAP purchase order number against which this goods receipt is posted. Links the physical receipt to the procurement commitment. Used for three-way matching (PO, GR, invoice) in accounts payable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Purchase Order; 21 CFR Part 211.84

description: Indicates whether the received material has been placed in quarantine status pending quality inspection or investigation. True = material is quarantined and not available for use; False = material is not in quarantine. Triggers quality inspection workflow in LIMS and QMS.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211.84; ICH Q7; GMP; GDP

description: Current lifecycle status of the warehouse receipt. 'posted' = GR document successfully posted in SAP; 'reversed' = GR has been reversed/cancelled; 'blocked' = receipt blocked pending resolution; 'under_inspection' = quality inspection in progress; 'released' = inspection complete and stock released for use.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Stock Status; 21 CFR Part 211.84; GDP

description: The actual quantity of material physically received and counted at the receiving dock, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used for inventory posting, three-way matching, and discrepancy reporting against the PO quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – MIGO Quantity; 21 CFR Part 211.84; GDP

description: The SAP stock type assigned to the received material upon posting. 'unrestricted' = available for use/sale; 'quality_inspection' = held pending QC inspection (default for GMP materials); 'blocked' = blocked stock due to quality issue or hold; 'returns' = returned goods stock.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM – Stock Type; 21 CFR Part 211.84; GMP

description: The required storage condition for the received material, determining the appropriate storage area and cold-chain handling requirements. 'ambient' = room temperature; 'refrigerated' = 2–8°C; 'frozen' = -20°C or below; 'controlled_room_temp' = 15–25°C; 'controlled_substance' = DEA-regulated controlled substance requiring secure storage.
reference: ICH Q1A – Stability Testing; USP <659>; GDP; DEA 21 CFR Part 1301

description: Indicates whether a temperature excursion was detected during transport or at the time of receipt. True = temperature excursion occurred (requires investigation and potential rejection); False = no temperature excursion detected. Critical for cold-chain materials (biologics, vaccines, temperature-sensitive APIs).
reference: GDP – Cold Chain Management; ICH Q1A; 21 CFR Part 211.68; WHO TRS 961

description: Timestamp when this warehouse receipt record was last modified in the source system or data platform. Tracks changes to status, inspection results, or corrections. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Unique identifier for the cold chain monitoring record. Primary key for the cold_chain_record product.

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Cold chain excursions require brand-level disposition decisions and regulatory reporting. Commercial teams need brand-level cold chain deviation metrics for product quality KPIs and customer/regulator

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: cold_chain_record.carrier_name is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.business_partner enables carrier qualification tracking, GDP deviation attribution to specific carriers, and ca

Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: cold_chain_record has location_code (string) and location_type identifying where the temperature reading was taken, but no FK to the distribution_network. Adding distribution_network_id → distribution

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Temperature monitoring requirements and excursion thresholds are drug product-specific (formulation-dependent stability profiles). Product link required for deviation assessment, disposition decisions

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Cold chain monitoring records track temperature/humidity for specific inventory lots. The lot_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Material id

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Cold-chain excursions affecting IMP shipments must be assessed against the specific investigational product's stability profile for QP disposition decisions. GDP regulations require linking temperatur

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Temperature monitoring must reference material storage requirements (min/max thresholds, excursion tolerances) from material master. Essential for excursion assessment, disposition decisions, and GDP
Self-referencing FK on cold_chain_record (preceding_cold_chain_record_id)
The unique identifier of the shipment or delivery if the monitoring occurred during transit. Links to shipment records for end-to-end traceability.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.storage_location. Business justification: Cold chain deviations occurring at a storage location must be linked to the validated storage zone for GDP deviation management, CAPA initiation, and re-qualification decisions. cold_chain_record.stor
The reference number of the CAPA investigation initiated in response to the temperature excursion, if applicable.
The date and time when this cold chain monitoring record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The reference number of the quality deviation or non-conformance record associated with the temperature excursion event.
The category of temperature monitoring device used to capture the reading.
The date when the quality disposition decision was made following excursion investigation and impact assessment.
The documented justification and scientific rationale for the disposition decision, including stability data and risk assessment results.
The quality disposition decision for the product following temperature monitoring or excursion investigation. Determines whether product can be released for use.
The total duration in minutes that the temperature or humidity remained outside the acceptable range during the excursion event.
The date and time when the temperature or humidity returned to within the acceptable range, marking the end of the excursion event.
Boolean indicator whether this reading represents a temperature or humidity excursion outside the acceptable range. True indicates an excursion event.
The severity classification of the temperature excursion based on the magnitude and duration of the deviation from acceptable range.
The date and time when the temperature or humidity first exceeded the acceptable threshold, marking the beginning of the excursion event.
The assessment of whether the cold chain monitoring and handling met GDP requirements and regulatory standards.
The relative humidity percentage recorded by the monitoring device, if humidity monitoring is required for the product.
The date and time when this cold chain monitoring record was most recently modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The category of location where the temperature monitoring occurred, indicating the supply chain segment.
Unique identifier of the temperature monitoring device or data logger used to capture the reading. Required for device calibration traceability.
Business identifier for the temperature monitoring event or data collection session. Used for traceability and audit purposes.
Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or contextual information related to the temperature monitoring event or excursion.
The precise date and time when the temperature or humidity reading was captured by the monitoring device. Critical for excursion analysis.
The date when the temperature excursion event was reported to the relevant regulatory authority, if reporting was required.
Boolean indicator whether the temperature excursion event requires reporting to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EMA, or other health agencies.
Unique serialization identifier for the individual package or pallet being monitored, if serialization is implemented.
The required storage condition classification for the pharmaceutical product as defined in the product specification and regulatory approval.
The specific storage zone, room, or area within the facility where the product was located during monitoring.
The segment of the pharmaceutical supply chain where the temperature monitoring occurred, enabling segment-specific analysis.
The maximum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for the product storage condition. Readings above this trigger an excursion event.
The minimum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for the product storage condition. Readings below this trigger an excursion event.
The actual temperature measurement in degrees Celsius recorded by the monitoring device at the reading timestamp.
The carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment, enabling correlation with carrier systems and proof of delivery.
Unique system-generated identifier for the pharmaceutical product recall event record. Primary key for the product_recall data product in the supply domain.

Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated against a specific regulatory approval — the recall targets the approved product/indication. Regulatory affairs teams track which approval is being recalled for health au

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated based on quality findings documented in batch records. FDA 21 CFR Part 7 recall submissions require direct reference to the implicated batch record. No existing column on

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Recalls are managed at brand level; commercial teams coordinate customer notifications, field force communications, and market impact assessments. Links recall events to brand positioning, promotional

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: Product recalls must be assessed against the active brand plan to quantify commercial revenue impact, update forecasts, and trigger regulatory/commercial communications. FDA recall effectiveness repor

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Product recalls are executed per country with country-specific regulatory notification requirements (FDA, EMA, PMDA). A country FK on product_recall is essential for regulatory notification tracking a

Foreign key linking to market.coverage_decision. Business justification: Product recall regulatory process requires notifying payers and suspending affected coverage decisions. Market access teams must identify which coverage decisions are impacted by a recall for regulato

Foreign key linking to intellectual.drug_master_file. Business justification: During a product recall, regulatory teams reference the DMF to identify manufacturing site details, API specifications, and CMC data relevant to the recall root cause. Recall regulatory submissions to

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Recalls target specific drug product formulations/strengths, not just medicinal product families. Batch-level recall execution requires drug_product specificity for lot identification, customer notifi

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Recall cost collection: product recalls require a dedicated internal order (cost collector) to capture all recall-related costs — logistics, destruction, customer credits, regulatory fees — for financ

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Product recalls are initiated and managed by a specific legal entity responsible for regulatory notifications and liability. Pharma regulatory affairs teams require legal entity attribution for recall

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.line. Business justification: Recalls are frequently scoped to a specific manufacturing line (e.g., contamination event on Line 3). FDA recall scope definition and effectiveness checks require direct line-level scoping on the reca

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_deviation. Business justification: Manufacturing deviations are a primary trigger for product recalls. Regulatory CAPA closure and FDA/EMA recall submissions require direct linkage between the causative deviation and the resulting reca

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.master_batch_record. Business justification: Recalls triggered by a defective MBR version (wrong formulation, incorrect specifications) require direct linkage to the implicated MBR. QA must identify all batches produced under that MBR version fo

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Recalls must link to material master for affected specifications, regulatory data, distribution history. Critical for recall scope determination, effectiveness checks, regulatory reporting, and custom
Reference to the master product record for the pharmaceutical product subject to the recall. Links to the product master data entity for product name, formulation, and therapeutic area details.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Recalls in pharma are often packaging-configuration-specific (e.g., a specific blister pack size with a defective closure). FDA/EMA recall notifications require precise packaging identification. Direc

Foreign key linking to market.patient_access_program. Business justification: When a product recall is initiated, patient access programs dispensing the recalled product must be immediately suspended and enrolled patients notified. Patient safety compliance requires direct trac

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.pv_product. Business justification: Product recalls are often triggered by safety signals (ICSRs, SUSARs) and require coordination between supply chain and pharmacovigilance for regulatory authority notifications (FDA, EMA). Links recal

Foreign key linking to market.reimbursement_policy. Business justification: Regulatory recall notifications must identify affected reimbursement policies for suspension or amendment. Health authorities and payers require traceability from recall events to active reimbursement

Foreign key linking to pharmacovigilance.safety_signal. Business justification: Product recalls in pharma are formally triggered by validated safety signals. Regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) require traceability from recall action back to the originating safety signal. PV and suppl
Reference to the manufacturing site where the affected lots were produced. Enables traceability back to the production facility for root cause investigation and regulatory inspection purposes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 211; SAP S/4HANA PP Module; GDP Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Product recalls in pharma are executed at SKU level — the specific market pack (country, strength, pack size) determines customer notification scope and regulatory reporting. sku_code is denormalized;

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: Recall management requires identifying all active clinical trials using the recalled product/lot. Linking product_recall to trial enables recall teams to notify sponsors, pause dosing, and file regula
Comma-separated list of ISO 3-letter country codes (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU) identifying the countries or markets where the recalled product was distributed and where recall actions must be executed.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; 21 CFR Part 7; EMA Recall Guidance

description: Indicates whether the recalled product requires cold-chain handling (temperature-controlled logistics) during the recovery and return process. Critical for biologics, vaccines, and temperature-sensitive drug products.
reference: GDP Guidelines; WHO Cold Chain Guidelines; 21 CFR Part 211.142

description: The timestamp when the product recall record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and data lineage tracking.
The date on which direct customers (wholesalers, distributors, hospitals, pharmacies) were formally notified of the recall via recall notification letters or electronic communications.
Current status of the customer notification process indicating whether recall notification letters have been sent to all affected customers in the distribution chain.

The level in the distribution chain to which the recall extends. Consumer: product reached end patients/consumers; Retail: product at retail pharmacy level; Wholesale: product at wholesale distributor level; Hospital: product at hospital/clinic level; User: product at healthcare professional level.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.3(k); FDA Recall Depth Definitions

value_regex: Level A|Level B|Level C|Level D|Not Required
description: FDA-defined effectiveness check level indicating the depth and percentage of consignees to be audited to verify recall notification receipt and product return. Level A: 100% of consignees; Level B: some percentage; Level C: 10% of consignees; Level D: 2% of consignees; Not Required: for certain recall types.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.42(b); FDA Recall Effectiveness Check Guidance

business_glossary_term: Expiration Date Range of Affected Lots
description: The expiration date range (e.g., '2024-01 to 2025-06') covering all affected lot numbers included in the recall. Assists customers and distributors in identifying recalled product on shelves and in inventory.

Indicates whether the recall execution is being conducted in compliance with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) guidelines, including proper documentation, chain of custody, and temperature-controlled handling of recovered product where applicable.
reference: EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01; WHO GDP Guidelines; PIC/S

description: Summary of the health hazard evaluation conducted by the company's medical and regulatory teams to assess the risk to patients from the defective product. Supports recall classification and regulatory submission under 21 CFR Part 7.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.41; FDA Health Hazard Evaluation

description: The regulatory authority or company entity that initiated or mandated the recall. Identifies whether the recall was company-initiated (voluntary) or directed by a specific regulatory body such as FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, or NMPA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|NMPA|WHO|Company — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 7; EMA Recall Guidance; MHRA Recall Guidance

description: The timestamp when the product recall record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and enables change tracking for regulatory inspection readiness.

The National Drug Code (NDC) of the affected drug product in the standard 5-4-2 format. Uniquely identifies the labeler, product, and package configuration of the recalled pharmaceutical product as registered with the FDA.

The date on which the company issued a public press release or public notification regarding the recall. Required for Class I and certain Class II recalls to inform consumers and healthcare professionals.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7; FDA Press Release Requirements

description: The approved method for disposing of recovered recalled product. Options include destruction, return to manufacturer, reprocessing, relabeling (where permitted), or quarantine pending further evaluation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7; 21 CFR Part 211.204; GDP Guidelines

description: Total quantity of the affected product that was distributed to the market prior to the recall initiation, expressed in the standard unit of measure (e.g., units, bottles, vials). Used to calculate recall effectiveness and track recovery progress.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.53; FDA Recall Effectiveness Checks

description: Total quantity of the recalled product that has been physically returned, destroyed, or otherwise accounted for as of the last update. Used to calculate recall effectiveness percentage and support regulatory reporting.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.53; FDA Recall Effectiveness Checks

description: Unit of measure applicable to quantity_distributed and quantity_recovered fields (e.g., EA=Each, BT=Bottle, VL=Vial, AMP=Ampoule, PKG=Package, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|BT|VL|AMP|PKG|KG|L — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

FDA-defined classification indicating the severity of the health hazard associated with the recalled product. Class I: serious adverse health consequences or death; Class II: temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences; Class III: unlikely to cause adverse health consequences.

The date on which the recall was formally closed or terminated by the regulatory authority or the company after all recall activities, effectiveness checks, and documentation requirements were completed.

Percentage of distributed product that has been successfully recovered or accounted for, calculated as (quantity_recovered / quantity_distributed) * 100. Key metric for FDA recall effectiveness checks and regulatory reporting under 21 CFR Part 7.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.53; FDA Recall Effectiveness Guidance

description: The date on which the recall was formally initiated by the company or ordered by the regulatory authority. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the recall lifecycle.
Free-text field for additional operational notes, regulatory correspondence summaries, or contextual information relevant to the recall event that is not captured in structured fields.

Externally-assigned recall identification number issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA recall number) or internally assigned by the company for voluntary recalls. Serves as the primary business identifier for tracking and referencing the recall event across regulatory submissions and communications.

Standardized code categorizing the root cause or reason for the recall (e.g., contamination, labeling error, sub-potency, sterility failure, foreign particulate). Aligns with FDA recall reason taxonomy for regulatory reporting and trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Contamination|Labeling Error|Sub-Potency|Super-Potency|Sterility Failure|Foreign Particulate|Stability Failure|Packaging Defect|Microbial Contamination|Other — promote to reference product]
reference: 21 CFR Part 7; FDA Recall Reason Codes

description: Detailed narrative description of the specific defect, violation, or safety concern that triggered the recall. Provides the full technical and regulatory context beyond the recall reason code, including root cause findings from CAPA investigations.

Geographic scope of the recall indicating whether the recall covers the entire national market, specific regions, international markets, or is limited to a specific manufacturing or distribution site.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7; EMA Recall Guidance; GDP Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle status of the recall event. Pending: recall initiated but not yet active; Active: recall in progress with field corrections underway; Monitoring: recall effectiveness checks ongoing; Terminated: FDA has terminated the recall; Closed: all recall activities completed and documented.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.55; FDA Recall Status Definitions

description: Description of the recall strategy approved by the regulatory authority or company, including the depth of recall, communication methods, effectiveness check methodology, and disposition instructions for recovered product.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.42; FDA Recall Strategy Guidance

description: Categorization of the recall initiation type. Voluntary recalls are company-initiated; Mandatory recalls are ordered by a regulatory authority; Market Withdrawal involves minor violations not subject to legal action; Stock Recovery involves products not yet distributed to the market.

The date on which the company formally notified the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA) of the recall initiation. Critical compliance milestone tracked under 21 CFR Part 7 and equivalent international regulations.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7.46; EMA Recall Notification Requirements

description: Reference number or identifier for the regulatory submission (e.g., NDA, BLA, MAA) associated with the recalled product. Links the recall event to the product's regulatory dossier for compliance tracking in RIMS.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; 21 CFR Part 601; Veeva Vault RIM

business_glossary_term: Recall Lot Scope - Recall Lot Scope Id
description: Primary key for the recall_lot_scope association

business_glossary_term: Recall Lot Scope - Inventory Lot Id
description: Foreign key linking to the specific inventory lot included within the scope of this recall action.

business_glossary_term: Recall Lot Scope - Product Recall Id
description: Foreign key linking to the parent product recall event that initiated the scope of this lot inclusion.

The quantity of this specific inventory lot that falls within the scope of the recall. Distinct from product_recall.quantity_distributed, which is the aggregate across all lots. Required for per-lot effectiveness check reporting to the FDA.

The approved disposition method for this specific lot as determined by quality review. May differ from the recall-level product_disposition on the parent recall record if lot-specific conditions (e.g., cold chain break, partial quantity) warrant a different disposition decision.

Indicates whether the holder or custodian of this specific inventory lot has been formally notified of the recall. Tracked per lot because lots may be held at different locations (3PL, distributor, hospital) requiring separate notification actions.

The date on which this specific inventory lot was physically recovered, confirmed destroyed, or otherwise resolved within the recall process. Per-lot date required for FDA effectiveness check documentation.

Current recovery status for this specific lot within the recall. Tracked independently per lot as different lots may be at different stages of recovery simultaneously. Cannot reside on inventory_lot (which reflects current inventory state) or product_recall (which is aggregate).
Primary key for serialization_record

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Serialized units must trace to manufacturing batch. DSCSA and EU FMD regulations mandate linking serial numbers to batch records for product authentication, verification, and targeted recall execution

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: Serialization compliance (DSCSA, FMD) is brand-specific; commercial teams use serialization data for product authentication, counterfeit prevention, and market-specific regulatory reporting. Links tra

Reference to the trading partner (manufacturer, wholesaler, distributor, pharmacy) that currently holds legal ownership of the serialized unit. Updated at each DSCSA transaction event (Transfer of Ownership). Core to DSCSA chain-of-custody tracking.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203) Section 582(b); GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01)

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: Serialization commissioning occurs at plants; plant master data provides GMP certification, FDA establishment identifier, regulatory market authorization. Distinct from manufacturing_site_id (manufact

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: serialization_record.market_country_code is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.country enables EU FMD and DSCSA serialization compliance validation by market country and supports r

Foreign key linking to hcp.master. Business justification: DSCSA track-and-trace and anti-diversion programs require serialized units to be traceable to the dispensing or administering HCP. Role-prefix 'dispensing_' distinguishes from patient_id already on th
Reference to the pharmaceutical finished goods product master record. Links the serialized unit to the product's regulatory approvals, storage conditions, and supply chain attributes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203)

description: Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Serialization records track individual serialized units that belong to specific inventory lots. The lot_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. N

Foreign key linking to clinical.investigational_product. Business justification: Serialized IMP packs must be linked to the specific investigational product for DSCSA/FMD track-and-trace compliance. Regulatory serialization requirements for clinical supplies mandate that each seri

Foreign key linking to product.packaging_configuration. Business justification: Serialization occurs at specific packaging levels (bottle, carton, case, pallet). Packaging configuration defines GTIN structure, aggregation hierarchy, and regulatory serialization requirements (DSCS

Foreign key linking to patient.patient. Business justification: Clinical supply serialization systems track which specific serialized unit (GTIN/serial) was dispensed to which patient. Required for accountability, returns reconciliation, regulatory inspections, an
Reference to the manufacturing or packaging site where the serial number was commissioned. Used for GDP compliance, site-level traceability, and regulatory reporting to FDA/EMA.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161; GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01)

description: Reference to the recall event record when recall_flag is TRUE. Links the serialized unit to the specific recall notification, recall class, and remediation actions. Populated only when the unit is subject to an active recall.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7 (Recalls); FDA Recall Guidance; EU GMP Chapter 8

description: Foreign key linking to commercial.sales_order. Business justification: DSCSA (Drug Supply Chain Security Act) compliance requires tracing serialized pharmaceutical units to the sales transaction. Pharma serialization teams link serial numbers to sales orders for transact

Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: A serialization record tracks the unit-level and case-level serialization status of pharmaceutical finished goods. When a serialized unit is shipped, the serialization record should be linked to the s

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: DSCSA (US) and FMD (EU) serialization regulations require saleable unit identification at SKU level. Serialization records must reference the SKU to support verification, aggregation hierarchy, and re

Reference to the physical location (warehouse, distribution center, pharmacy, hospital) where the serialized unit is currently held. Used for inventory visibility, GDP compliance, and cold-chain monitoring.
reference: GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01); DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); SAP WM Module

description: Foreign key linking to patient.treatment_exposure. Business justification: Links serialized investigational product units to documented treatment administration events in eCRF. Enables verification that lot/serial numbers recorded in exposure logs match dispensed units, crit

Indicates whether this serialized unit requires cold-chain storage and transportation (e.g., 2-8°C refrigerated or -20°C frozen). Drives cold-chain logistics routing, temperature monitoring requirements, and GDP compliance checks.
reference: GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01); WHO Technical Report Series No. 961; ICH Q1A

description: Date and time when the serial number was commissioned (activated and associated with a physical product unit) at the manufacturing or packaging site. Marks the start of the serialized unit's lifecycle. Required for DSCSA and EU FMD event reporting.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161; GS1 EPCIS Standard

description: Date and time when this serialization record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Represents the audit trail creation timestamp for the Silver Layer record, distinct from the commissioning event timestamp.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Reason code for decommissioning the serialized unit. Required for EU FMD decommissioning event reporting to the European Medicines Verification System (EMVS). Supports pharmacovigilance and recall management workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recall|destruction|sample|theft|damage|dispensed|exported|other — promote to reference product]
reference: EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161 Article 40; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203)

description: Date and time when the serialized unit was decommissioned (removed from the active supply chain). Populated when serialization_status transitions to 'decommissioned' or 'destroyed'. Required for EU FMD and DSCSA event reporting.
reference: EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); GS1 EPCIS Standard

description: Pharmaceutical dosage form of the product (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, solution). Used for supply chain classification, storage condition assignment, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|solution|cream|patch|inhaler|suspension — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Dosage Form Codes; EMA Product Information; ICH M5

business_glossary_term: Electronic Product Code Information Services (EPCIS) Event ID
description: Unique identifier of the most recent GS1 EPCIS event associated with this serialized unit. Links to the EPCIS event repository for full event history (ObjectEvent, AggregationEvent, TransactionEvent). Used for DSCSA and EU FMD interoperability.
reference: GS1 EPCIS Standard v1.2/2.0; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161

description: Indicates whether the current handling and storage of this serialized unit is compliant with Good Distribution Practice (GDP) requirements. Set to FALSE if a GDP deviation has been recorded for this unit (e.g., temperature excursion, unauthorized handling).
reference: GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01); WHO Technical Report Series No. 957; MHRA GDP Guidelines

description: 14-digit GS1 Global Trade Item Number identifying the pharmaceutical product at the trade item level. Encoded in the 2D DataMatrix barcode on the package. Used for product identification in DSCSA transaction data and EU FMD verification.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161

description: Date and time when this serialization record was most recently updated in the enterprise data platform. Tracks the latest status change, verification event, or ownership transfer recorded against this serialized unit.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Date and time of the most recent verification event performed against the medicines verification system (NMVS/EMVS). Used to track verification frequency and identify units that have not been recently verified.
reference: EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203)

description: Quantity and unit of the pharmaceutical product contained in the individual saleable unit (e.g., '30 tablets', '10 mL vial', '1 pre-filled syringe'). Used for dispensing verification and supply chain planning.
reference: FDA Labeling Requirements (21 CFR Part 201); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161

description: Level of the packaging hierarchy this serialization record represents. 'unit' is the individual saleable unit (SGTIN); 'bundle' is a multi-pack; 'case' is a shipping case (SSCC); 'pallet' is a pallet-level aggregation (SSCC). Drives aggregation hierarchy logic.
reference: GS1 SGTIN/SSCC Standards; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161

description: Serial number of the immediate parent container in the aggregation hierarchy (e.g., the case serial number for a unit, or the pallet serial number for a case). Enables reconstruction of the full unit→case→pallet aggregation tree for DSCSA and EU FMD compliance.
reference: GS1 SSCC Standard; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161

description: Commercial or brand name of the pharmaceutical product associated with this serialized unit. Included for operational readability and reporting without requiring a join to the product master.
reference: FDA Labeling Requirements (21 CFR Part 201); EMA Product Information

description: Indicates whether this serialized unit is subject to an active product recall. Set to TRUE when the unit's lot number is included in a recall notification. Enables rapid identification and quarantine of affected units across the supply chain.
reference: 21 CFR Part 7 (Recalls); FDA Recall Guidance; EU GMP Chapter 8; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203)

description: Regulatory market authorization code or product registration number applicable to the market country. Links the serialized unit to the specific marketing authorization (NDA/BLA/MAA) under which it was released for that market.
reference: FDA NDA/BLA (21 CFR Part 314/601); EMA MAA; ICH CTD

description: Indicates whether this serialized unit has been returned from the market and assessed as eligible for re-entry into the saleable supply chain. Requires verification against the NMVS/EMVS and GDP assessment before re-release.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203) Section 582(g); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161 Article 13; GDP Guidelines

description: Unique alphanumeric serial number assigned to an individual saleable unit or case during commissioning. Conforms to GS1 SGTIN or SSCC encoding standards. Required for DSCSA unit-level traceability and EU FMD verification at point of dispense.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161; GS1 SGTIN Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the serialized unit within the track-and-trace system. 'commissioned' = activated at manufacturing; 'active' = in supply chain; 'shipped' = dispatched to trading partner; 'returned' = returned from market; 'decommissioned' = intentionally removed; 'destroyed' = physically destroyed.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161; GS1 EPCIS Standard

description: Identifier of the originating serialization system or track-and-trace platform (e.g., SAP Advanced Track and Trace for Pharmaceuticals, Tracelink, Serialized Inventory Management System) that generated this record. Used for data lineage and system reconciliation.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); SAP S/4HANA ATTP Module

description: Required storage condition for the serialized unit as specified in the product's approved labeling and stability data. Drives warehouse zone assignment, cold-chain logistics, and GDP compliance monitoring.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; GDP Guidelines (2013/C 343/01); 21 CFR Part 211.142

description: Indicates whether the serialized unit belongs to the commercial supply chain or the clinical trial supply chain. Clinical trial units require additional IND/CTA traceability. Compassionate use units require special regulatory tracking.
reference: DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203); ICH E6 GCP; 21 CFR Part 312 (IND)

description: Indicates whether a valid DSCSA Transaction Statement has been received and verified for this serialized unit from the upstream trading partner. Required under DSCSA Section 582 for each change of ownership in the pharmaceutical supply chain.

Result of the most recent verification check of the serialized unit against the national or European medicines verification system (NMVS/EMVS). 'verified' = authentication passed; 'failed' = authentication failed (potential falsification); 'pending' = verification in progress; 'not_verified' = not yet checked.
reference: EU FMD Delegated Regulation 2016/161 Article 11; DSCSA (21 CFR Part 203) Section 582

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the clinical supply order record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: QP release for clinical trial supplies requires the batch record to be directly referenced on the clinical supply order. EU GMP Annex 13 and FDA 21 CFR Part 312 mandate batch record traceability for i

Foreign key linking to discovery.compound. Business justification: Clinical trials order specific investigational compounds identified in IND/CTA applications. Essential for clinical supply chain management linking discovery assets to trial execution, regulatory subm

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: clinical_supply_order.courier_name is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.business_partner enables courier qualification tracking, GDP compliance verification, and clinical supply c

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: clinical_supply_order.destination_country_code is a denormalized plain-text field. Linking to masterdata.country enables import permit validation, clinical trial regulatory compliance checks, and coun

Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: clinical_supply_order has supply_depot_code as a free-text string identifying the supply depot from which the IMP is shipped. Adding distribution_network_id → distribution_network.distribution_network

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Clinical supply orders must reference the investigational drug product for IND/CTA regulatory compliance, QP release documentation, and clinical trial supply chain traceability. A clinical supply expe

Foreign key linking to product.formulation. Business justification: Clinical supply orders must specify the exact formulation version for blinding integrity, dose preparation instructions, and regulatory submission (IND/CTA CMC section). Different formulation versions

Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Clinical supply costs (IMP manufacturing, packaging, distribution) are charged to clinical trial internal orders for R&D cost accounting. Required for trial budget tracking, capitalization decisions,

Foreign key linking to supply.inventory_lot. Business justification: Clinical supply orders are fulfilled from specific inventory lots of investigational products. The lot_number (STRING) should be replaced with inventory_lot_id FK to inventory_lot.inventory_lot_id. Ma
Reference to the investigational medicinal product (IMP) master record being ordered. Captures the IMP material code as defined in the clinical supply catalogue and aligned with the IND/CTA filing.
reference: EU Directive 2001/20/EC; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH Q10

description: Reference to the investigational site that will receive the clinical supply shipment. Identifies the physical location (hospital, clinic, research centre) conducting the trial.

Foreign key linking to hcp.investigator. Business justification: GCP/ICH E6 drug accountability requires every clinical supply order to be traceable to the responsible investigator. FDA inspections and drug accountability logs mandate this link. No existing FK cove

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Clinical supply orders for in-licensed compounds must reference the licensing agreement to confirm clinical use is within the licensed field-of-use and territory. IP and clinical operations teams veri

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Clinical supply orders for investigational products are tied to specific patents protecting the compound under study. IP teams track which patents cover clinical-stage compounds to manage prosecution

Foreign key linking to patient.patient_enrollment. Business justification: Supply orders must verify enrollment status (active, consented) before shipping investigational product. Links order to specific trial enrollment context (site, protocol version, treatment arm) requir

Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Clinical supply orders for IMPs and comparators are driven by clinical supply plans. Linking clinical_supply_order.plan_id → plan.plan_id connects clinical supply execution to the supply plan, enablin

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Clinical supply orders drive manufacturing of investigational products. IND/CTA regulatory submissions and QP release require direct traceability from the clinical supply order to the production order

Foreign key linking to discovery.project. Business justification: Clinical supply orders are executed under a discovery/development project for budget allocation, protocol management, and regulatory tracking. Supply teams reference the project for SAP WBS elements a

Foreign key linking to clinical.protocol. Business justification: Clinical supply orders are placed against a specific protocol version. Protocol amendments (dose changes, sample size) drive supply order amendments. Linking to protocol_id allows supply teams to trac

Foreign key linking to clinical.trial. Business justification: All clinical supply orders are placed under a specific trial. A direct trial_id FK enables supply operations to report all orders, quantities, and costs per trial for IND annual reports and clinical s

Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: clinical_supply_order.quantity_unit_of_measure is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and ensures validated UOM master data for IRT system reconciliation
The date on which the clinical supply was confirmed as received at the investigational site. Compared against required_delivery_date to assess supply chain performance and site readiness.

The quantity of IMP or ancillary supply formally approved for fulfilment after clinical supply review. May differ from ordered_quantity due to stock availability, protocol amendments, or QP release constraints.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH Q10; 21 CFR Parts 210/211

description: Specifies the blinding methodology applied to this supply order as defined in the clinical protocol. Determines labelling, packaging, and handling requirements for the IMP to maintain trial integrity.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312

description: Free-text description of the reason for cancellation when order_status is 'Cancelled'. Captures business justification for audit trail and supply planning analysis (e.g., protocol amendment, site closure, stock recall).
reference: ICH Q10; 21 CFR Part 11; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Indicates whether this supply order requires cold-chain logistics (True) due to temperature-sensitive IMP storage and transport conditions. Triggers cold-chain packaging, monitoring, and carrier selection in the distribution workflow.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01; WHO Technical Report Series No. 961 Annex 9; 21 CFR Parts 210/211

description: The date and time when this clinical supply order record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU Annex 11; ICH Q10

description: Description of the dispensing unit configuration for the IMP as packaged for clinical trial use (e.g., 'Blinded kit of 28 tablets', '1 vial 10mg/mL'). Supports site pharmacist dispensing and accountability records.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Parts 210/211; ICH E6(R2)

description: Reference number of the export permit or authorisation issued by the originating country's regulatory authority for the shipment of IMP across international borders for clinical trial purposes.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; EU Regulation 536/2014; GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01

description: Reference number of the import permit issued by the relevant national regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA member state, PMDA) authorising the importation of the IMP into the destination country for clinical trial use.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312; EU Regulation 536/2014; PMDA Clinical Trial Notification; ICH E6(R2)

business_glossary_term: Investigational New Drug (IND) / Clinical Trial Authorisation (CTA) Number
description: The regulatory filing reference number (IND number in the US per 21 CFR Part 312, or CTA number in the EU per Regulation 536/2014) under which this clinical supply order is authorised. Required for import/export permit applications and regulatory compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 312 (IND); EU Regulation 536/2014 (CTA); ICH E6(R2)

description: Reference number or transaction ID from the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) / Interactive Web Response System (IWRS) that triggered or confirmed this supply order. Links the supply event to the randomization and drug management system.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU GMP Annex 13

description: Indicates whether this supply order is subject to blinding requirements (True) or is open-label (False). Used as a quick filter for blinding compliance checks and supply handling procedures.
reference: ICH E9; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Part 312

description: The date and time when this clinical supply order record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for change control audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; EU Annex 11; ICH Q10

description: The calendar date on which the clinical supply order was formally placed or submitted by the clinical supply team. This is the principal business event date for the order.
reference: ICH Q10; SAP MM Purchase Order Date

description: Externally-known, human-readable order reference number assigned by the clinical supply management system (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse or SAP MM) to uniquely identify this supply order across systems and with third-party logistics providers.
reference: ICH Q10; SAP MM Purchase Order Number convention

description: Current lifecycle state of the clinical supply order, tracking progression from initial creation through fulfilment or cancellation. Drives supply chain workflow in Kinaxis RapidResponse / SAP MM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Submitted|Approved|In Preparation|Shipped|Delivered|Cancelled|On Hold — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH Q10; SAP MM Order Status; GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01

description: Classification of the supply order by the nature of the material being ordered. IMP = investigational medicinal product; Comparator = approved reference drug; Placebo = matching placebo; Ancillary = non-IMP study supplies; Open-Label Extension = OLE phase supply; Rescue Medication = emergency/rescue supply.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; ICH E6(R2); 21 CFR Part 312

description: The quantity of IMP or ancillary supply units requested in this order, expressed in the unit of measure defined on the IMP material master (e.g., vials, tablets, kits).
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Parts 210/211; ICH Q10

description: Priority classification of the clinical supply order indicating the urgency of fulfilment. Emergency orders may be triggered by unplanned site demand, protocol deviations, or patient safety requirements.
reference: ICH E6(R2) GCP; ICH Q10; GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01

description: The date on which the Qualified Person (QP) formally certified and released the IMP batch for clinical use. Required for EU GMP Annex 13 compliance and batch disposition records.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; EU Directive 2001/20/EC; EMA GMP Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the IMP batch included in this order has been certified and released by a Qualified Person (QP) as required by EU GMP Annex 13 and Directive 2001/20/EC before dispatch to the clinical site.
reference: EU GMP Annex 13; EU Directive 2001/20/EC; EMA GMP Guidelines

description: The unique kit number assigned by the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) or randomization system to the blinded supply kit. Links the physical supply to the randomization schedule and subject assignment. Critical for blinded trial integrity.
reference: ICH E9; ICH E6(R2) GCP; 21 CFR Part 312; EU GMP Annex 13

description: The date by which the clinical supply must arrive at the investigational site to support the trial schedule. Used for supply chain planning and lead-time management in Kinaxis RapidResponse.
The date on which the clinical supply was physically dispatched from the originating depot or manufacturing site. Used for GDP compliance, transit time tracking, and delivery performance monitoring.
The actual quantity of IMP or ancillary supply physically dispatched from the manufacturing site or depot. May differ from approved_quantity due to last-minute stock adjustments or partial shipments.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01; EU GMP Annex 13; 21 CFR Parts 210/211

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country from which the clinical supply is dispatched. Used for import/export compliance, customs documentation, and GDP route planning.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01; 21 CFR Part 312

description: The maximum permissible storage and transport temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP in this order, as specified in the IMP dossier and CoA. Used to configure cold-chain monitoring thresholds and excursion alerts.
reference: ICH Q1A(R2); GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01; WHO TRS 961 Annex 9

description: The minimum permissible storage and transport temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP in this order, as specified in the IMP dossier and CoA. Used to configure cold-chain monitoring thresholds.
reference: ICH Q1A(R2); GDP Guidelines (EU) 2013/C 343/01; WHO TRS 961 Annex 9

description: The courier or logistics provider's shipment tracking reference number, enabling real-time monitoring of the clinical supply in transit. Supports GDP compliance and cold-chain excursion investigation.
Unique surrogate identifier for the demand plan record in the pharmaceutical supply chain planning system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Kinaxis RapidResponse

description: Foreign key linking to market.access_strategy. Business justification: Demand planning in pharma is directly driven by market access strategy — formulary coverage scope, reimbursement approval dates, and launch sequencing all define the addressable patient population. Su

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: demand_plan.atc_code is a denormalized ATC classification. Linking to masterdata.atc_classification enables therapeutic-area demand aggregation and WHO ATC-based market analysis reports used in pharma

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand. Business justification: S&OP consensus demand planning in pharma is brand-driven. The demand plan must reference the brand it forecasts to reconcile commercial revenue targets with supply capacity. A pharma supply planner wo

Foreign key linking to commercial.brand_plan. Business justification: The S&OP process explicitly reconciles demand plans against brand plan volume and revenue targets. Linking demand_plan to brand_plan enables consensus forecast approval workflows and plan-vs-actual va

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Demand plans are created at country-market level for market access and regulatory forecasting. A pharma demand planner always scopes forecasts by country to align with local regulatory and reimburseme

Foreign key linking to supply.distribution_network. Business justification: A demand plan is a statistical/consensus forecast for pharmaceutical SKUs across specific distribution channels and network nodes. demand_plan has distribution_channel (string) but no FK to the distri

Foreign key linking to intellectual.exclusivity_period. Business justification: Demand forecasts must incorporate exclusivity expiry dates (orphan, pediatric, data exclusivity) as these drive generic entry timing. Critical for commercial forecasting with regulatory exclusivity co

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Demand plans for in-licensed products must reference the licensing agreement to apply contractual volume caps, minimum purchase obligations, and territory-specific demand constraints. Royalty calculat
Reference to the pharmaceutical product (Drug Product/Drug Substance) associated with this demand plan, enabling cross-SKU product-level demand aggregation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ICH Q10

description: Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent_family. Business justification: Demand planning for LOE scenarios requires patent family-level analysis (earliest/latest expiry across jurisdictions) to model demand cliffs accurately. A patent family spans multiple countries; plann

Foreign key linking to intellectual.patent. Business justification: Demand planning must account for patent expiry dates (LOE events) to forecast generic competition impact on volumes. Critical for commercial forecasting and LOE planning cycles in pharmaceuticals.
Reference to the manufacturing or distribution site for which this demand plan is scoped, supporting multi-site supply network planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP/MM Module; GDP Guidelines

description: Reference to the pharmaceutical SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this demand plan is generated. Covers both commercial and clinical supply SKUs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; GS1 Standards

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: demand_plan.uom is a denormalized plain-text UOM. Linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure enforces 3NF and enables consistent UOM conversion in supply planning reports. Pharma planners require validated

Demand quantity after commercial, medical affairs, or supply chain planner adjustments to the statistical baseline. Reflects market intelligence, launch events, tender wins, or clinical trial enrollment changes applied on top of the baseline.
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder; APICS S&OP

description: Statistically generated baseline demand quantity for the SKU in the forecast period, produced by the statistical forecasting engine (Kinaxis/Blue Yonder) using historical sales, shipment, or consumption data without manual override.
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder Demand Planning; APICS CPIM

description: Indicates whether this SKU requires cold chain (temperature-controlled) logistics and storage throughout the supply chain. Critical for biologics, vaccines, and temperature-sensitive pharmaceutical products. Drives GDP-compliant distribution planning.
reference: GDP Guidelines (EU 2013/C 343/01); WHO Technical Report Series 961; FDA Guidance on Temperature-Sensitive Products

description: Date and time when the demand plan was formally approved through the S&OP consensus process. Marks the transition to frozen/authoritative demand signal for supply execution. Compliant with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH Q10; SOX Compliance; APICS S&OP

description: Final consensus-approved demand quantity agreed upon during the S&OP/IBP consensus meeting, representing the authoritative demand signal used for supply planning and inventory positioning. Populated only after plan_status reaches consensus_approved.
reference: APICS S&OP Framework; Kinaxis RapidResponse; ICH Q10

description: Classification of the demand signal by supply chain purpose: commercial (market sales), clinical (investigational product for trials), compassionate use (expanded access programs), sample (promotional/medical samples), or launch (pre-launch inventory build). Drives planning rules and inventory segmentation.
reference: ICH Q10; FDA 21 CFR Part 312; GDP Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Origin of the primary demand signal used to generate or adjust this forecast (e.g., Kinaxis RapidResponse statistical engine, Blue Yonder, SAP APO, manual planner input, CRM pipeline, market research, or tender/contract data). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kinaxis|blue_yonder|sap_apo|manual|crm|market_research|tender — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder; SAP S/4HANA PP; APICS

description: The trade channel through which the pharmaceutical product reaches the end customer (e.g., wholesale distributor, direct hospital supply, retail pharmacy, specialty pharmacy, government/tender, export). Influences demand pattern and supply routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wholesale|direct|hospital|retail_pharmacy|specialty_pharmacy|government|export|e_commerce — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; GDP Guidelines; WHO GDP

description: Measured forecast accuracy for the prior equivalent period, expressed as a percentage (0–100). Calculated as 1 minus the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) for the SKU-period. Used to assess planning reliability and trigger model recalibration.
reference: APICS CPIM; Kinaxis RapidResponse KPI Framework

description: Systematic directional error in the forecast for the prior equivalent period, measured as actual minus forecast quantity. Positive bias indicates under-forecasting; negative bias indicates over-forecasting. Used for continuous improvement of demand planning accuracy.
reference: APICS CPIM; Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder Analytics

description: The time granularity at which the demand forecast quantities are expressed (weekly, monthly, or quarterly buckets). Determines how forecast_period_date is interpreted.
The specific time bucket (month, week, or quarter start date) to which the demand quantities in this record apply. Each row represents one SKU-period combination within the planning horizon.
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder Demand Planning; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Standard manufacturing lot or batch size quantity for the SKU, used to convert demand quantities into production orders and assess capacity requirements. Expressed in the same UOM as forecast quantities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Module; ICH Q7; cGMP 21 CFR Part 211

description: FDA National Drug Code (NDC) for the pharmaceutical product/package, used for US market demand planning, regulatory reporting, and supply chain traceability. Format: labeler-product-package (5-4-2).
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 207; FDA NDC Directory

description: Standardized reason code explaining why the adjusted forecast deviates from the statistical baseline (e.g., new tender win, product launch, competitor entry, clinical trial enrollment change, market withdrawal). Required when adjusted_forecast_qty differs from baseline_forecast_qty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tender_win|product_launch|competitor_entry|clinical_enrollment|market_withdrawal|promotional_event|regulatory_change — promote to reference product]
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder; APICS S&OP; ICH Q10

description: Timestamp when this demand plan record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail start point for the planning record lifecycle.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH Q10; SOX Compliance

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this demand plan record, supporting audit trail requirements and change management tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH Q10.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; ICH Q10; SOX Compliance

description: Externally-known business identifier for the demand plan, used for cross-system reference in Kinaxis RapidResponse, Blue Yonder, and SAP S/4HANA. Follows the enterprise demand plan numbering convention.
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; SAP S/4HANA MM; Blue Yonder

description: Current lifecycle status of the demand plan record, tracking progression from initial statistical generation through consensus approval and freeze for supply execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|consensus_approved|frozen|superseded — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; APICS S&OP Framework; ICH Q10

description: Version identifier of the demand plan, enabling tracking of iterative revisions within a planning cycle (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Supports version-controlled consensus planning workflows.
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder Demand Planning

description: Free-text notes entered by the demand planner documenting assumptions, market intelligence, adjustment rationale, or risk factors influencing the forecast. Supports S&OP review and audit trail documentation.
reference: ICH Q10; APICS S&OP Framework; 21 CFR Part 11

description: The cadence of the demand planning cycle in which this plan was generated (e.g., monthly S&OP cycle, quarterly business review, weekly rolling horizon). Determines the review and approval schedule.
reference: APICS S&OP Framework; Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder

description: The last date of the demand planning horizon covered by this plan. Defines the end of the forecast period, typically 12–24 months for pharmaceutical commercial planning.
The first date of the demand planning horizon covered by this plan. Defines the start of the forecast period for supply planning and inventory positioning.
Inventory level at which a replenishment order should be triggered for this SKU, calculated from lead time demand plus safety stock. Used in SAP S/4HANA MRP and Kinaxis supply planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/MRP; Kinaxis RapidResponse; APICS CPIM

description: Minimum buffer inventory quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions for this SKU in the planning period. Expressed in the same UOM as forecast quantities. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM or Kinaxis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Kinaxis RapidResponse; APICS CPIM; GDP Guidelines

description: Approved shelf life of the pharmaceutical SKU in days from the date of manufacture. Critical for demand planning to ensure inventory is consumed within expiry, minimizing write-offs and ensuring patient safety compliance.
reference: ICH Q1A Stability Guidelines; FDA 21 CFR Part 211.137; EMA Guideline on Shelf Life

description: The statistical forecasting algorithm applied to generate the baseline forecast (e.g., exponential smoothing, ARIMA, Croston, moving average, machine learning ensemble). Sourced from Kinaxis RapidResponse or Blue Yonder model configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exponential_smoothing|arima|croston|moving_average|holt_winters|ml_ensemble — promote to reference product]
reference: Kinaxis RapidResponse; Blue Yonder Demand Planning; APICS CPIM

The date when the brand plan was formally approved by senior management or the commercial leadership team, authorizing execution and budget release.
The core brand positioning statement that defines how the brand is differentiated in the market and the value proposition communicated to Healthcare Professionals (HCPs) and patients.

The date when the brand plan becomes effective and execution begins. Typically aligns with fiscal year start or product launch date.

The fiscal or calendar year for which this brand plan is effective (e.g., 2024). For multi-year plans, this represents the start year.

Description of the target patient population for the brand, including demographic, clinical, and epidemiological characteristics (e.g., 'Adults with advanced NSCLC, EGFR mutation positive').

Version number or identifier of the informed consent form document used. Critical for regulatory traceability and protocol amendment tracking. Applicable for clinical trial consents.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 4.8, 21 CFR Part 312.60

description: Full name of the legally authorized representative or caregiver who provided consent on behalf of the patient. Applicable for pediatric populations or incapacitated patients.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 4.8.15, 21 CFR Part 50.3(l)

description: Relationship of the LAR to the patient. Examples: parent, legal guardian, healthcare proxy, power of attorney, caregiver.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 4.8.15, 21 CFR Part 50.3(l)

description: Reason for re-consenting the patient. Examples: protocol_amendment, new_safety_information, icf_version_update, regulatory_requirement. Applicable when consent_category is re_consent.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 4.8.2, 21 CFR Part 312.60

description: Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.
Timestamp when this consent record was last updated in the system. Audit trail field for change tracking and compliance.
Reason provided by the patient for withdrawing consent. Examples: adverse_event, personal_reasons, lack_of_efficacy, relocation, lost_to_followup. Free-text or controlled vocabulary.

description: Scope of consent withdrawal. Indicates whether patient withdrew from entire study, data use only, biological sample use only, or future contact. Critical for GCP compliance.

description: Full name of the impartial witness who observed the consent process. Required when patient or LAR cannot read, or as per local regulations.

description: Date when the impartial witness signed the informed consent form. Null if no witness was required.
reference: ICH GCP E6(R2) Section 4.8.9, 21 CFR Part 50.27

description: Date when the patient was enrolled in this specific support program. Critical for program eligibility, benefit period calculation, and compliance reporting.

Boolean flag indicating whether the patient is included in the Per-Protocol analysis population. True if the patient completed the protocol without major protocol deviations.
reference: ICH E9 Statistical Principles, CDISC SDTM DM Domain

description: Date the patient was randomized to a treatment arm in a clinical trial. Null for non-randomized studies or support programs.

description: The non-proprietary or generic name of the drug product, typically the International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or United States Adopted Name (USAN).

description: Date when this label version is superseded or no longer valid. Null if the label is currently active with no planned replacement.

description: Full text of the Indications and Usage section from the label, describing the approved therapeutic uses and patient populations for the drug product.

Current lifecycle status of the label. Draft = under development, Submitted = filed with regulatory authority, Approved = authorized but not yet effective, Active = currently in use, Superseded = replaced by newer version, Withdrawn = removed from use.
Type of product label document. USPI = US Prescribing Information, SmPC = Summary of Product Characteristics (EU), PIL = Patient Information Leaflet, IFU = Instructions for Use.
Version number of the product label following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented with each regulatory update or revision.

description: Timestamp when this product labeling record was last modified or updated.

The brand or trade name under which the drug product is marketed. This is the commercial name used in labeling and promotion.
Official approval or authorization number issued by the regulatory authority for this label. Examples: NDA number (FDA), MAA number (EMA), PMDA approval number.

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Required Flag

description: Indicates whether the product requires a prescription (Rx), is available over-the-counter (OTC), or is behind-the-counter (BTC) requiring pharmacist consultation.

Full text of the Use in Specific Populations section from the label, providing information on use in pregnancy, lactation, pediatric, geriatric, and patients with renal or hepatic impairment.
Full text of the Warnings and Precautions section from the label, describing serious adverse reactions, potential safety hazards, and steps to prevent or manage them.

Foreign key reference to the medicinal product (Drug Product, Drug Substance, Finished Dosage Form, or SKU) whose lifecycle is being tracked. Links to the authoritative product master catalog.

description: Date when the formal gate review was conducted to assess readiness for stage transition. Part of the governance process ensuring disciplined portfolio management.

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this lifecycle record is the current active record for the product. Supports temporal tracking and historical analysis of lifecycle transitions.

description: United States Adopted Name assigned by the USAN Council, representing the official generic name used in the United States for the drug substance.

Indicates whether the excipient contains or is derived from known allergens. Critical for patient safety labeling and formulation risk assessment.
Specific allergen classification if allergen_flag is true. Examples include lactose (milk-derived), gelatin (animal-derived), or gluten.
Indicates whether the excipient is derived from animal sources. Important for TSE/BSE risk assessment and religious/dietary considerations.

description: Unique CAS registry number assigned to the excipient for chemical substance identification. Critical for regulatory submissions and safety data sheets.
