description: ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code local currency used for translation of the AR document amount (e.g., USD for US legal entity).
Net receivable amount after deducting tax from the gross amount. Represents the revenue-recognized value for ASC 606 / IFRS 15 reporting. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX

description: Remaining unpaid balance on this AR document at the time of last update. Equals gross_amount minus all clearing payments and credit memos applied. Used for aging reports and collections management.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX

description: Method by which the customer remitted payment (e.g., bank transfer, ACH, wire transfer, check). Maps to SAP payment method key (ZLSCH). Used for cash application and treasury reconciliation.
Date on which the incoming customer payment was received and applied against this AR document. Null if no payment has been received. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation.

SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30) defining the discount period, discount percentage, and net due date for this receivable. Drives due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.
Date on which the AR document was posted to the general ledger in SAP FI. Determines the accounting period for financial reporting. Corresponds to SAP BUDAT.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; SOX; ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Accounting period (01–12 for regular months, 13–16 for special closing periods) within the fiscal year in which this AR document was posted. Aligns with SAP FI period control.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; SOX; ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Contractual rebate amount accrued or applied against this AR document for government payers (Medicaid, Medicare) or commercial payer contracts. Part of gross-to-net revenue deductions under ASC 606.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; ASC 606; IFRS 15; Medicaid Drug Rebate Program

description: Date on which revenue was or is scheduled to be recognized in the income statement per ASC 606 / IFRS 15 performance obligation criteria. May differ from billing date for deferred or accrued revenue entries.
reference: ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX; SAP FI-AR Module

description: Classification of the revenue treatment for this AR record under ASC 606 / IFRS 15: 'recognized' = revenue fully recognized at point of sale; 'deferred' = revenue deferred pending performance obligation fulfillment; 'accrued' = period-end accrual entry; 'reversed' = accrual reversal.
reference: ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX; SAP FI-AR Module

description: Date on which a period-end revenue accrual entry is scheduled to be automatically reversed in SAP FI. Applicable only to accrual-type AR documents. Null for standard invoices and payments.
Geographic or organizational sales territory associated with this receivable. Used for P&L reporting by geography and market access analysis. Sourced from SAP SD sales area configuration.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; SAP SD Module

description: Tax component of the invoice (VAT, GST, or applicable sales tax) calculated on the gross invoice amount. Corresponds to SAP tax line items in FI-AR. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; IFRS 15; FDA; EMA

description: Pharmaceutical therapeutic area (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases) associated with the product sold on this invoice. Enables P&L reporting by therapeutic area as required by finance domain.
reference: SAP FI-AR Module; SAP CO Module; ATC Classification

Unique surrogate identifier for the Drug Master File record in the enterprise data platform (Silver Layer). Primary key for this entity.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; ICH M4 eCTD Guidance

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: DMFs are filed with country-specific regulatory authorities; required for regulatory compliance, supply chain qualification, and market-specific manufacturing authorization.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: DMFs are held by legal entities; required for regulatory submissions, supply chain qualification, and vendor management in pharmaceutical manufacturing.

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: Drug Master Files (DMFs) contain confidential manufacturing and formulation information. DMF holders often grant access to third parties (e.g., generic manufacturers, partners) via licensing agreement

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: DMFs are classified by ATC for regulatory portfolio management and therapeutic area reporting. atc_code on drug_master_file is a denormalized representation of atc_classification.atc_code. Proper FK e

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: DMF maintenance costs (annual updates, regulatory submissions, amendment fees) are tracked against cost centers for regulatory affairs budget management and R&D cost capitalization under IAS 38. No ex

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: DMFs describe specific INNs; required for regulatory submissions, molecule-DMF linkage, and supply chain qualification in pharmaceutical manufacturing.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: DMFs support commercial products; their costs and associated revenues are attributed to profit centers for product P&L reporting and segment financial reporting. No existing profit_center FK on drug_m

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: DMFs describe specific materials; required for regulatory compliance, procurement, and material-DMF linkage in pharmaceutical supply chain management.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.plant. Business justification: DMFs reference manufacturing sites; essential for regulatory submissions, supply chain qualification, and site-specific manufacturing authorization.

Indicates whether the DMF holder is the manufacturer of the Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) covered by this DMF. True for Type II DMFs where the holder manufactures the drug substance. Used to distinguish API manufacturers from intermediates suppliers, excipient suppliers, and packaging material suppliers.
reference: ICH Q7 (API GMP); 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: CAS Registry Number uniquely identifying the chemical substance covered by the DMF. Used for substance identification in regulatory submissions, safety databases, and supply chain qualification. Applicable primarily to Type II (API/DS) and Type IV (excipient) DMFs.
reference: CAS Registry (American Chemical Society); ICH Q11; FDA Substance Registration System (SRS)

business_glossary_term: Chemistry Manufacturing and Controls (CMC) Section Reference
description: Reference to the specific CMC (Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls) section of the eCTD submission (Module 3) that this DMF supports. Used to map the DMF to the corresponding regulatory dossier section for NDA/BLA/ANDA/MAA submissions.
reference: ICH M4Q (R1) CTD Module 3; FDA eCTD Guidance; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Date on which the regulatory authority acknowledged the confidential nature of the DMF content. The FDA treats DMF content as confidential trade secret information. This date records when the confidentiality commitment was formally established, supporting IP protection and trade secret management.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420(c); FDA Trade Secret Policy; GDPR Article 9; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Timestamp when this DMF record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Number of active regulatory applications (NDA, BLA, ANDA, IND, MAA) that currently cross-reference this DMF. Indicates the commercial and regulatory criticality of the DMF; a high count signals high supply chain dependency and elevated risk if the DMF is withdrawn or goes inactive.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: Official DMF number assigned by the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA) upon receipt of the DMF submission. Serves as the primary external identifier used in cross-references within NDA, BLA, ANDA, and IND applications. Format varies by authority (e.g., FDA assigns a numeric DMF number).
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: Current lifecycle status of the DMF with the regulatory authority. 'active' indicates the DMF is in good standing and available for cross-reference; 'inactive' indicates it has not been updated within the required period; 'withdrawn' indicates the holder has formally withdrawn the file; 'under_review' indicates a pending agency review; 'closed' indicates the file has been administratively closed.
reference: FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019); 21 CFR Part 314.420

value_regex: Type I|Type II|Type III|Type IV|Type V
description: Classification of the DMF per FDA categorization: Type I (Manufacturing Site, Facilities, Operating Procedures, and Personnel — discontinued by FDA but retained for legacy), Type II (Drug Substance, Drug Substance Intermediate, and Material Used in Their Preparation, or Drug Product), Type III (Packaging Material), Type IV (Excipient, Colorant, Flavor, Essence, or Material Used in Their Preparation), Type V (FDA-Accepted Reference Information). Drives CMC submission strategy and third-party qualification workflows.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019); ICH Q11

description: Physical form of the drug product or substance covered by the DMF (e.g., tablet, capsule, injectable solution, lyophilized powder, cream, patch). Relevant primarily for Type II (Drug Product) and Type III (Packaging) DMFs. Aligns with FDA/EMA dosage form terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injectable_solution|lyophilized_powder|cream|patch|suspension|gel|suppository|inhalation_powder — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Dosage Form Terminology; EMA EDQM Standard Terms; ICH M4Q CMC Guidance

business_glossary_term: Electronic Common Technical Document (eCTD) Sequence Number
description: Four-digit eCTD sequence number assigned to the DMF submission or amendment within the eCTD lifecycle. Used to track the submission history and version control of the DMF in the regulatory authority's electronic submission gateway.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Guidance; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; EMA eCTD Guidance

description: Date on which the DMF became effective and available for cross-reference by applicants in regulatory submissions (NDA, BLA, ANDA, IND). May differ from submission date if the authority requires a review period before the file is deemed active.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: FDA Establishment Identifier (FEI) number assigned to the manufacturing facility by the FDA. Used to link the DMF to the FDA's establishment registration database, enabling GMP inspection history lookup and import alert monitoring.
reference: 21 CFR Part 207; FDA Establishment Registration and Drug Listing; FDA FEI Database

business_glossary_term: Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) Certificate Expiry Date
description: Expiry date of the current GMP certificate for the manufacturing site associated with this DMF. Triggers renewal workflows and supplier qualification reviews. An expired GMP certificate may block use of the DMF in new regulatory submissions.
reference: 21 CFR Parts 210/211; ICH Q7; PIC/S GMP Guide; EMA GMP Certificate Database

description: Certificate number of the current GMP certificate issued by a recognized regulatory authority or inspection body (e.g., FDA, EMA, PIC/S member authority) for the manufacturing site associated with this DMF. Used for supplier qualification and regulatory compliance verification.
reference: 21 CFR Parts 210/211; ICH Q7; PIC/S GMP Guide; EMA GMP Certificate Database

description: Date of the most recent amendment or annual update submitted to the regulatory authority for this DMF. FDA requires annual updates to maintain active status. Tracks currency of the file and triggers compliance alerts when updates are overdue.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: Date on which the DMF holder issued the Letter of Authorization (LOA) permitting the company to cross-reference this DMF in its regulatory submissions. Relevant when the company is the applicant referencing a third-party DMF.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420(d); FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: Indicates whether a Letter of Authorization (LOA) from the DMF holder is required before an applicant can cross-reference this DMF in their regulatory submission. True for most Type II and Type IV DMFs where the holder controls access to confidential manufacturing information.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420(d); FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the manufacturing site is located. Used for import/export compliance, country-of-origin tracking, supply chain risk management, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; 21 CFR Parts 210/211; ICH Q7; GDP Guidelines

business_glossary_term: DMF Next Annual Update Due Date
description: Calculated due date for the next mandatory annual update submission to maintain active DMF status with the regulatory authority. Derived from the last amendment date plus 12 months. Used for compliance monitoring and proactive submission planning.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: Free-text field for capturing additional regulatory, operational, or strategic notes related to this DMF. May include information about pending amendments, agency correspondence, supplier negotiations, or lifecycle management decisions not captured in structured fields.
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Indicates whether the company itself (Pharmaceuticals) is the holder of this DMF (True) or whether it is a third-party supplier DMF that the company cross-references (False). Drives different workflows: internal DMFs require internal maintenance; third-party DMFs require supplier management and LOA tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: The regulatory authority to which the DMF was submitted (e.g., FDA for US, EMA for EU, PMDA for Japan, MHRA for UK, NMPA for China). Determines applicable regulatory requirements, format standards (e.g., eCTD vs. paper), and review timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|NMPA|WHO|Health Canada|TGA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 314.420; EMA CMDh Guidance; ICH M4 eCTD; PIC/S

business_glossary_term: Regulatory Information Management System (RIMS) Tracking Number
description: Internal tracking number assigned to this DMF record within the company's Regulatory Information Management System (RIMS), such as Veeva Vault RIM. Used for internal workflow management, submission tracking, and audit trail purposes.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; ICH M4 eCTD; 21 CFR Part 11

description: Intended route of administration for the drug substance or product covered by the DMF (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation). Applicable for Type II DMFs. Aligns with FDA/EMA route of administration terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Route of Administration Terminology; EMA EDQM Standard Terms; ICH M4Q

description: Date on which the DMF was first submitted to the regulatory authority. Establishes the official filing date and is used to calculate review timelines, annual update obligations, and IP/exclusivity period calculations.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

description: Format in which the DMF was submitted to the regulatory authority: 'eCTD' (Electronic Common Technical Document — mandatory for FDA since 2017 for most submissions), 'paper' (legacy paper-based submission), or 'NeeS' (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission — used in some jurisdictions). Determines document management and archival requirements.
reference: ICH M8 eCTD Guidance; FDA eCTD Technical Conformance Guide; 21 CFR Part 314.420

description: Primary therapeutic area or disease indication associated with the drug substance covered by this DMF (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio management, lifecycle planning, and IP strategy alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oncology|immunology|rare_diseases|cardiovascular|neurology|infectious_diseases|metabolic|respiratory|dermatology|hematology — promote to reference product]
reference: ICH E3; FDA Therapeutic Area Guidance; EMA Therapeutic Area Classification

description: Official descriptive title of the DMF as submitted to the regulatory authority. Typically includes the substance or material name, manufacturer name, and scope of the file (e.g., 'Drug Substance DMF for [INN] manufactured at [Site]').
reference: FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019); 21 CFR Part 314.420

description: Timestamp when this DMF record was most recently updated in the enterprise data platform. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and data freshness monitoring in compliance with 21 CFR Part 11.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system where the DMF submission package and associated correspondence are stored. Enables traceability between the enterprise data platform record and the source regulatory document management system.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); FDA eCTD Guidance

description: Date on which the DMF holder formally withdrew the DMF from the regulatory authority. Populated only when dmf_status is 'withdrawn'. Triggers downstream impact assessment for any NDA/BLA/ANDA applications that cross-reference this DMF.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314.420; FDA DMF Guidance for Industry (2019)

business_glossary_term: Licensed Patent Schedule - Licensed Patent Schedule Id
description: Primary key for the licensed_patent_schedule association

business_glossary_term: Licensed Patent Schedule - Licensing Agreement Id
description: Foreign key linking to the licensing agreement under which this patent is licensed. Identifies the deal, parties, territory, and financial terms governing this patent's inclusion.
Foreign key linking to the specific patent covered under this licensing agreement. Identifies the IP asset whose rights are being granted.

Date on which this specific patent's coverage under the licensing agreement becomes operative. May differ from the agreement's overall effective_date when patents are added to an existing agreement via amendment (e.g., a continuation patent granted after deal signing is added to the schedule mid-term).

Scope of exclusivity granted for this specific patent under this agreement. A single licensing agreement may grant exclusive rights to one patent and non-exclusive rights to another in the same schedule. Cannot reside on licensing_agreement (which captures overall deal exclusivity) or on patent (which may be licensed exclusively to one partner and non-exclusively to another).

Date on which this patent's coverage under the agreement terminates. May be driven by the patent's own expiry, the agreement's expiry, or a negotiated earlier termination for this specific patent. Enables LOE planning at the patent-agreement intersection level.

Narrative or structured description of which patent claims, fields of use, or indications are licensed under this agreement for this specific patent. A patent may be partially licensed (e.g., only method-of-use claims, not composition claims). Cannot reside on the agreement (varies per patent) or on the patent (varies per agreement).

The royalty rate (expressed as a percentage of net sales or a fixed per-unit amount) applicable specifically to this patent under this agreement. In multi-patent deals, individual patents may carry different royalty rates reflecting their relative commercial importance. Cannot reside on licensing_agreement (which may aggregate rates) or on patent (which may be licensed at different rates to different partners).

Current lifecycle status of this patent's inclusion in the licensed patent schedule. Supports tracking of patents added, removed, or suspended from coverage during the agreement term without requiring deletion of the historical record.

Indicates whether the licensee is contractually permitted to sublicense this specific patent to third parties under this agreement. Sublicensing rights may be granted for some patents in a schedule but not others, particularly where the licensor retains tighter control over foundational composition-of-matter patents.
Unique surrogate identifier for the licensing agreement record in the intellectual property portfolio system.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.business_partner. Business justification: The licensee in a licensing agreement is a business partner in the vendor/customer master. Linking enables royalty payment processing, credit management, and partner relationship management. licensee_

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Licensing agreements require tracking licensor legal entity for contract management, revenue recognition, and intercompany royalty accounting in pharmaceutical partnerships.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Licensing portfolio is segmented by ATC therapeutic class for business development reporting, deal valuation, and portfolio strategy. BD teams report licensing deal value by therapeutic area using ATC

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: License agreements generate costs/revenues allocated to cost centers; required for P&L reporting, royalty expense tracking, and business unit accountability.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Licensing agreements for pharmaceutical compounds are tied to specific INNs (active substances). Royalty calculation, FTO analysis, and regulatory compliance tracking require substance-level licensing

Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: License agreements are assigned to business segments; required for revenue recognition, segment P&L reporting, and royalty income allocation.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: License territories define geographic scope; essential for revenue allocation, royalty calculation, and compliance with territorial restrictions in pharmaceutical licensing.

Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the licensing agreement, used in correspondence with counterparties, legal counsel, and regulatory filings. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.

Current lifecycle state of the licensing agreement. Draft: under negotiation. Active: fully executed and in force. Suspended: temporarily paused by mutual consent or breach notice. Terminated: ended before natural expiry. Expired: reached end of term without renewal. Drives deal management workflows and financial reporting.

Classification of the licensing agreement by deal structure. In-licensing: rights acquired from a third party. Out-licensing: rights granted to a third party. Cross-licensing: mutual exchange of IP rights. Co-development: joint development with shared IP. Collaboration: broad research or commercialization partnership. Drives revenue recognition treatment under ASC 606.

Indicates whether the agreement contains a change-of-control clause that grants termination or renegotiation rights upon acquisition or merger of either party. True = change-of-control provision exists; False = no such provision. Critical for M&A due diligence and IP portfolio valuation.
reference: SOX Section 302; SEC Regulation S-K; ICH Q10

description: Aggregate value of all commercial milestone payments (e.g., first commercial sale, annual net sales thresholds) defined in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Supports commercial launch planning and ASC 606 variable consideration estimation.

Timestamp when the licensing agreement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED audit trail required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all financial obligations under the agreement (upfront payments, milestones, royalties) are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP). Required for multi-currency royalty accounting and foreign exchange hedging.

Aggregate value of all development-stage milestone payments (e.g., IND submission, Phase I/II/III completion) defined in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Supports clinical development budget planning and ASC 606 variable consideration estimation.
reference: ASC 606; IFRS 15; 21 CFR Part 312; ICH E6(R2)

description: Narrative description of the commercially reasonable efforts or best efforts diligence obligations imposed on the licensee to develop and commercialize the licensed product. Failure to meet diligence obligations may trigger termination rights. Used for partner relationship management and compliance monitoring.

Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving disputes between the parties. Arbitration: binding arbitration under specified rules (e.g., ICC, AAA). Litigation: resolution through courts of the governing jurisdiction. Mediation: non-binding mediation before escalation. Expert-determination: technical disputes resolved by independent expert.
reference: ICH Q10; 21 CFR Part 314; SOX Section 302

description: Date on which the licensing agreement becomes legally binding and operative. Marks the start of the agreement term for royalty accrual, exclusivity period calculation, and milestone tracking. Corresponds to EFFECTIVE_FROM for the MASTER_AGREEMENT role.

Scope of exclusivity granted under the license. Exclusive: licensee is the sole permitted user in the territory/field. Non-exclusive: licensor may grant same rights to others. Co-exclusive: limited number of licensees permitted. Semi-exclusive: licensor retains right to practice but not to license others. Critical for generic/biosimilar entry defense strategy.
reference: USPTO Patent Rules; 21 CFR Part 314; ICH Q10

description: Date on which the licensing agreement was formally signed and executed by all parties. May differ from the effective date if the agreement has a retroactive or future effective date. Used for legal record-keeping and audit trails.

Date on which the licensing agreement naturally expires at the end of its agreed term. Nullable for perpetual or open-ended agreements. Drives lifecycle management alerts, renewal negotiations, and IP exclusivity planning. Corresponds to EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for the MASTER_AGREEMENT role.

Specific therapeutic area, indication, or application domain within which the licensee is permitted to use the licensed IP. Examples: 'oncology', 'rare diseases', 'human therapeutics only', 'diagnostic use'. Restricts the scope of the license and is critical for exclusivity analysis and sublicensing rights management.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; ICH E6(R2); USPTO Patent Rules

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement. Determines applicable contract law, dispute resolution procedures, and regulatory compliance obligations.

Timestamp when the licensing agreement record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED audit trail required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and change control tracking.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5

description: Name or identifier of the internal or external legal counsel responsible for drafting, negotiating, and maintaining the agreement. Used for coordination on patent litigation, IP enforcement, and contract amendments.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; USPTO Patent Rules; SOX Section 302

description: Narrative description of the intellectual property assets covered by the license, including compound names (INN/USAN), patent numbers, know-how, trade secrets, trademarks, or biological sequences. Supports IP portfolio management and freedom-to-operate analysis.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; ICH Q10; USPTO Patent Rules

description: Classification of the primary intellectual property asset type covered by the agreement. Patent: granted patent rights. Know-how: proprietary technical knowledge. Trademark: brand or trade name rights. Data-package: regulatory data exclusivity or clinical data. Biological-sequence: gene or protein sequence rights. Trade-secret: confidential proprietary information. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: patent|know-how|trademark|data-package|biological-sequence|trade-secret|formulation|process — promote to reference product]
reference: USPTO Patent Rules; 21 CFR Part 314; ICH Q10

description: Total number of contractually defined milestone events (development, regulatory, and commercial) specified in the agreement. Used for milestone tracking dashboards, deal management reporting, and ASC 606 performance obligation identification.

Aggregate value of all regulatory milestone payments (e.g., NDA/BLA/MAA submission, FDA/EMA approval) defined in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Supports regulatory affairs planning and ASC 606 variable consideration estimation.
reference: ASC 606; IFRS 15; 21 CFR Part 314; EMA Regulation (EC) No 726/2004

description: Identifies which party bears primary responsibility for conducting development activities (clinical trials, regulatory submissions, manufacturing scale-up) under the agreement. Licensor: licensor retains development responsibility. Licensee: licensee assumes development responsibility. Joint: shared responsibility as defined in the agreement.

Indicates whether the agreement grants either party a right of first negotiation (ROFN) for additional territories, indications, or follow-on compounds. True = ROFN exists; False = no ROFN. Relevant for lifecycle management and business development strategy.
reference: USPTO Patent Rules; ICH Q10; SOX Section 302

description: The contractual basis on which royalties are calculated. Net-sales: royalties applied to net sales after standard deductions. Gross-sales: royalties applied to gross sales before deductions. Net-revenue: royalties applied to net revenue. Units-sold: royalties calculated per unit sold. Determines the royalty accounting methodology.

Contractual royalty rate expressed as a decimal fraction of net sales (e.g., 0.050000 = 5.0%) payable by the licensee to the licensor. Used for royalty accrual calculations, revenue recognition under ASC 606/IFRS 15, and P&L forecasting. Null if the agreement uses a tiered royalty structure captured in a separate milestone/royalty schedule.

Contract number assigned in SAP S/4HANA (SD or FI module) for financial processing of royalty payments, milestone invoicing, and upfront fee recognition. Enables reconciliation between the IP portfolio system and the ERP for royalty accounting and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI; ASC 606; SOX Section 302

description: Indicates whether the licensee has the contractual right to sublicense the licensed IP to third parties (e.g., CMOs, CDMOs, or sub-distributors). True = sublicensing permitted; False = sublicensing prohibited. Impacts CMO/CDMO engagement and supply chain structuring.
reference: 21 CFR Part 314; ICH Q10; USPTO Patent Rules

description: Summary of the contractual conditions under which either party may terminate the agreement, including material breach cure periods, insolvency triggers, regulatory failure events, and convenience termination rights. Used by legal counsel for contract management and risk assessment.
reference: ICH Q10; 21 CFR Part 314; SOX Section 302

description: Date on which the licensing agreement was terminated prior to its natural expiry, whether by mutual consent, breach, or exercise of termination rights. Null if the agreement has not been terminated. Used for financial close-out and IP rights reversion tracking.

Total potential deal value including upfront payments, all milestone payments, and estimated royalties over the agreement term, expressed in the agreement currency. Used for deal valuation, P&L forecasting, and investor disclosure. Represents the gross deal value before probability-weighting.
reference: ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX Section 302; SEC Regulation S-K

description: Sum of all contractually defined milestone payment amounts across all milestone events in the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Represents the maximum milestone-based consideration achievable under the agreement. Used for deal valuation and revenue recognition planning.

One-time upfront license fee or signing payment due at execution of the agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Recognized as revenue or capitalized as an intangible asset depending on the nature of the license under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this MASTER_AGREEMENT.

Reference identifier of the fully executed agreement document stored in Veeva Vault (RIM or QualityDocs module). Enables direct navigation from the data product record to the source legal document for audit, due diligence, and regulatory inspection purposes. Aligns with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; Veeva Vault RIM; SOX Section 302

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each royalty payment transaction record within the intellectual property portfolio management system.

Foreign key linking to product.drug_product. Business justification: Royalty payments are calculated on net sales of specific drug products (by dosage form and strength). Finance and BD teams need drug_product-level granularity for royalty reporting, audit reconciliati

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every royalty payment (in or out) generates a GL posting for revenue/expense recognition. Finance links royalty payments to journal entries for reconciliation, audit trail, SOX compliance, and revenue
Reference to the licensing agreement under which this royalty payment is made or received. Links the payment to the governing contract terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; ICH Q10

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Royalty payments are posted to cost centers for financial controlling and budget variance analysis. cost_center_code on royalty_payment is a denormalized representation of cost_center.cost_center_code

Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Royalty payments are attributed to profit centers for product P&L reporting and segment financial reporting under IFRS 15 revenue recognition. Finance requires profit-center-level royalty income/expen

Foreign key linking to product.medicinal_product. Business justification: Royalty payments calculated on product-specific net sales. Essential for financial reporting, deal reconciliation, and product-level P&L attribution. Removes denormalized licensed_product_name.
Reference to the patent or intellectual property asset (NCE, NBE, formulation, or process patent) that is the subject of the royalty-bearing license.

Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Royalty disbursements are executed via treasury payment runs. Linking royalty_payment to payment_run enables reconciliation of IP royalty payments against bank payment batches — required for treasury
Reference to the party granting the license (the entity receiving royalty payments in an outbound scenario or paying in an inbound scenario).

Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_agreement. Business justification: Royalty payments are calculated under a specific finance royalty_agreement governing rates, tiers, and territory. This FK enables royalty accounting reconciliation, audit trail, and rate-tier validati

Any adjustments applied to the calculated royalty amount, such as corrections from prior period reconciliations, audit findings, or contractual true-ups. Positive values increase the payment; negative values reduce it.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX

description: Indicates whether this royalty payment record has been flagged for formal royalty audit review, typically triggered by discrepancies between reported sales and expected royalty amounts or by contractual audit rights.
Bank-issued transaction reference number or wire transfer confirmation number associated with the royalty payment. Used for bank reconciliation and payment verification.

End date of the royalty calculation period for which net sales or units sold are being reported and royalties computed. Together with start date defines the reporting window per the licensing agreement.

Start date of the royalty calculation period (e.g., quarter or year) for which net sales or units sold are being reported and royalties computed. Aligns with licensing agreement reporting obligations.
Timestamp when this royalty payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.
reference: SOX; 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the royalty payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Determines the denomination of all monetary amounts in this record.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP/IFRS

description: Total allowable deductions applied to gross sales to arrive at net sales, as defined in the licensing agreement. Typically includes chargebacks, rebates, returns, freight, and applicable taxes.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA SD Module; SOX

description: Free-text description of the reason for disputing the royalty payment amount, applicable when payment_status is 'disputed'. Documents the basis for disagreement between licensor and licensee for legal and audit purposes.
Contractual deadline by which the royalty payment must be made as specified in the licensing agreement. Used to track overdue payments and trigger escalation workflows.

Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency to the company's functional reporting currency at the time of payment. Required for multi-currency licensing agreements and consolidated financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP/IFRS IAS 21

description: Total payment amount converted to the company's functional reporting currency using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated P&L reporting and SOX financial controls.
reference: GAAP/IFRS IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX

description: SAP General Ledger account code to which this royalty payment is posted for financial reporting purposes. Distinguishes royalty income (inbound) from royalty expense (outbound) in the P&L.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; GAAP/IFRS; SOX

description: Total gross sales revenue generated from the licensed product during the calculation period, before any deductions. Used as the starting point for net sales calculation per the licensing agreement definition.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA SD Module; SOX

description: Geographic territory or market for which the royalty payment applies, as defined in the licensing agreement. May be a country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3), region, or 'GLOBAL'. Supports territory-level royalty analytics.
reference: ISO 3166-1; Licensing Agreement Terms; FDA; EMA

description: Contractual minimum royalty amount guaranteed per period as specified in the licensing agreement, regardless of actual sales performance. Applicable when the calculated royalty falls below this floor.

Net sales revenue for the licensed product during the calculation period, calculated as gross sales minus allowable deductions per the licensing agreement definition. Forms the royalty basis for net-sales-based agreements.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA SD Module; SOX

description: The actual date on which the royalty payment was executed (funds transferred or received). Represents the principal real-world business event date for this transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX; GAAP/IFRS

description: Indicates whether the royalty payment is inbound (received by the company as licensor) or outbound (paid by the company as licensee). Critical for P&L classification and financial reporting.
The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the royalty payment (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, offset against receivables, or netting against other obligations).
Externally-known business identifier for this royalty payment transaction, typically matching the SAP FI document number or bank remittance reference used for reconciliation and audit trail.

Current lifecycle state of the royalty payment transaction. Tracks progression from draft calculation through approval, payment execution, and final settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending|approved|paid|disputed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]

Current status of the royalty payment reconciliation process, indicating whether the payment has been matched against the licensee's royalty report and confirmed as accurate. Supports royalty audit workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX; GAAP/IFRS

description: Calculated royalty amount due for the calculation period, derived by applying the royalty rate to the applicable royalty basis (net sales, gross sales, or units sold). This is the principal monetary fact of the transaction.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX

description: Reference number or identifier of the royalty statement or sales report submitted by the licensee that forms the basis for this royalty payment calculation. Links the payment to the supporting documentation.

Label identifying the applicable royalty tier when the licensing agreement specifies tiered rates based on cumulative net sales thresholds (e.g., 'Tier 1: 0-$100M', 'Tier 2: $100M-$500M'). Null if the agreement uses a flat rate.
Therapeutic area classification of the licensed drug product (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases). Supports portfolio-level royalty analytics and strategic lifecycle management decisions.
Final net royalty payment amount after applying the royalty rate to the basis, enforcing any minimum royalty floor, and incorporating adjustments. This is the amount actually paid or received.
reference: GAAP/IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX

description: Number of units of the licensed product sold during the calculation period. Used as the royalty basis for unit-based royalty agreements. Expressed as DECIMAL to accommodate partial units or volume-based measures.
reference: Licensing Agreement Terms; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: Timestamp when this royalty payment record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX; 21 CFR Part 11; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Amount of withholding tax deducted from the royalty payment at source, as required by applicable tax treaties between the licensor and licensee jurisdictions. Relevant for cross-border licensing arrangements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; OECD Tax Treaties; GAAP/IFRS

description: Applicable withholding tax rate (expressed as a decimal fraction) applied to the royalty payment based on the tax treaty between the relevant jurisdictions. Used to validate withholding tax amount calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; OECD Tax Treaties

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each trademark registration record in the pharmaceutical intellectual property portfolio.
reference: ISO 15836 (Dublin Core Metadata); Veeva Vault RIM

description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.country. Business justification: Trademarks are registered by jurisdiction; required for brand protection strategy, market entry planning, and global brand portfolio management.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.legal_entity. Business justification: Trademarks are registered to legal entities; required for brand ownership tracking, legal compliance, and M&A due diligence in pharmaceutical branding.

Foreign key linking to intellectual.licensing_agreement. Business justification: trademark has licensing_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating the trademark is subject to a licensing arrangement. The governing licensing agreement is tracked in licensing_agreement, which covers all IP licensin

Foreign key linking to masterdata.atc_classification. Business justification: Trademarks are associated with therapeutic categories; required for brand strategy, market positioning, and therapeutic area-specific brand portfolio management.

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Trademark maintenance costs (annuity fees, watch services, opposition proceedings) are tracked against cost centers for IP budget management and financial reporting. A pharma IP controller expects tra

Foreign key linking to masterdata.inn_registry. Business justification: Trademarks are brand names for INN-based drugs. EMA and FDA require INN alongside brand name on packaging; regulatory submissions map trademark to INN. inn_name on trademark is a denormalized represen

Foreign key linking to masterdata.profit_center. Business justification: Trademarks are associated with profit centers for brand P&L reporting and licensing revenue attribution under IFRS. Brand management requires profit-center-level trademark portfolio reporting. No exis

Foreign key linking to masterdata.material. Business justification: Brand trademarks are associated with specific materials (finished drug products) for packaging artwork management and supply chain labeling compliance. Artwork approval workflows require trademark-to-
The official application number assigned by the trademark office at the time of filing, prior to registration. Used to track the application through examination and opposition proceedings.
reference: USPTO TEAS; EUIPO eSearch; WIPO Madrid System

description: Description of any specific colour or colour combination claimed as a feature of the trademark (e.g., Pantone codes, RGB values, or textual colour descriptions). Relevant for figurative and combined marks where colour is a distinctive element.
reference: WIPO Nice Agreement; EU Trade Mark Regulation 2017/1001 Art. 4; USPTO TEAS

description: The timestamp when the trademark record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records); GDPR Article 5(2); SOX; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Any disclaimer of exclusive rights to specific elements of the trademark (e.g., generic or descriptive terms within a combined mark) as required or accepted by the trademark office during examination.
reference: 15 U.S.C. § 1056; USPTO TEAS; EU Trade Mark Regulation 2017/1001

description: The date on which the current trademark registration term expires and renewal must be filed to maintain protection. Critical for lifecycle management and brand protection continuity.
reference: WIPO Madrid System; 15 U.S.C. § 1058; EU Trade Mark Regulation 2017/1001 Art. 53

description: Name of the external law firm or IP agent responsible for managing the trademark prosecution and maintenance in the relevant jurisdiction. Supports coordination with legal counsel on prosecution strategy and renewal deadlines.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; Pharmaceutical IP management best practices; SOX compliance

description: The date on which the trademark application was officially filed with the relevant trademark office. Establishes priority date for the mark in the jurisdiction.

description: Date the patent application was published by the patent office, typically 18 months after the earliest priority date. Marks the point at which the application enters the public domain and becomes citable prior art.

description: Official publication number assigned by the patent office upon publication of the application (e.g., US20230123456A1, WO2023/012345A1). Distinct from the application number; used for public citation and prior art searches.

description: Statutory or extended deadline by which a response to the most recent office action must be filed to avoid abandonment. Calculated from last_office_action_date per jurisdiction-specific rules. Critical docketing field — failure to meet this deadline results in application abandonment.

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this patent prosecution record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with source docketing systems (e.g., Veeva Vault RIM, Anaqua), and audit compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11 (Electronic Records and Signatures); SOX Section 404

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each patent term extension or supplementary protection certificate record in the intellectual property portfolio. Primary key for this entity.

Reference to the drug product (Drug Product / Finished Dosage Form) for which the marketing authorization was obtained and which forms the basis of the extension application. Links the extension to the commercial product in the master data.
reference: Internal Master Data Standard; ICH Q6A; FDA 21 CFR Part 314

description: Reference to the underlying base patent record in the IP portfolio for which this term extension or SPC is being sought. Links the extension to the specific patent being extended.

The new patent expiry date after application of the granted term extension or SPC. Calculated as base_patent_expiry_date plus granted_extension_days. This is the Loss of Exclusivity (LOE) date for the product in this jurisdiction and is the primary output used in lifecycle management and generic/biosimilar entry defense strategies.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 13; ICH Q8; FDA Guidance on LOE

description: Number of days deducted from the regulatory review period due to delays attributable to the applicant (e.g., failure to respond to FDA requests within the prescribed period). Reduces the calculated extension duration under 35 U.S.C. § 156(c)(1). Tracked for audit and dispute resolution purposes.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156(c)(1); FDA 21 CFR Part 60.22; USPTO PTE Examination Guidelines

description: The official application number assigned by the granting authority (e.g., USPTO, EPO, UKIPO, JPO) when the patent term extension or SPC application was filed. Serves as the primary external business identifier for tracking the application through the regulatory process.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156 (US PTE); EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 (SPC); UK Patents Act 1977 s.128B; Japanese Patent Act Art. 67

description: For US PTE calculations: the date on which the regulatory approval phase began, typically the date of NDA/BLA submission to the FDA. Used to calculate the approval phase component of the regulatory review period under 35 U.S.C. § 156(g)(1)(B).
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156(g)(1)(B); FDA 21 CFR Part 314; FDA 21 CFR Part 60

description: The WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code assigned to the active substance covered by the extension. Provides standardized classification for cross-portfolio analytics, competitive intelligence, and regulatory reporting.
reference: WHO ATC/DDD Index; EMA Product Information; FDA Orange Book

description: The original expiry date of the underlying base patent before any term extension or adjustment is applied. Used as the baseline for calculating the adjusted expiry date and the duration of the extension period.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 154; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 13; Japanese Patent Act Art. 67

description: The official patent number of the underlying patent for which the term extension or SPC is sought (e.g., US10,123,456 or EP1234567). For SPC purposes, this is the 'basic patent' as defined under Article 1(c) of EU Regulation 469/2009. Retained as a denormalized field for direct reference without joining to the patent table.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 1(c); 35 U.S.C. § 156(f)(2); WIPO ST.9

description: The number of days of patent term extension calculated based on the regulatory review period formula. For US PTE: one-half of the testing phase plus the approval phase, capped at 5 years with remaining patent life not exceeding 14 years post-approval. For EU SPC: period between IND filing and MA grant minus 5 years, capped at 5 years.
Timestamp when this patent term extension record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.

The date on which regulatory data exclusivity (also known as data protection) expires for the product in this jurisdiction. In the EU, this is 8+2+1 years from first authorization; in the US, 5 years for NCEs or 12 years for biologics. Tracked alongside PTE/SPC to determine the complete exclusivity landscape.

Current lifecycle status of the patent term extension or SPC application. Tracks the application from initial drafting through filing, examination, and final decision by the granting authority. Critical for LOE (Loss of Exclusivity) date calculation and portfolio management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|filed|under_review|granted|refused|withdrawn|appealed|lapsed|invalidated — promote to reference product]
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009; Veeva Vault RIM

description: Reference identifier or name of the external patent law firm or legal counsel managing this patent term extension or SPC application. Used for coordination of legal strategy, correspondence tracking, and matter management. Marked confidential as it reflects privileged legal relationships.
reference: Internal IP Governance Policy; Attorney-Client Privilege

description: The date on which the patent term extension or SPC application was formally filed with the granting authority. For US PTE, must be filed within 60 days of first marketing approval. For EU SPC, must be filed within 6 months of marketing authorization grant. Critical for determining application validity.

The date on which the granting authority officially granted the patent term extension or SPC. Null if the application has not yet been decided or was refused/withdrawn. Used to confirm the effective start of the extended protection period.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009; USPTO; EPO

description: The actual number of days of patent term extension officially granted by the granting authority. May differ from the calculated extension if the authority applies different determinations of the regulatory review period or applicant delay deductions. Null until the extension is formally granted.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 13; USPTO; EPO

description: Name of the national or regional intellectual property office responsible for examining and granting the patent term extension or SPC (e.g., USPTO, EPO, UKIPO, JPO, CIPO, IP Australia). Determines procedural rules and timelines.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009; Japanese Patent Act Art. 67; WIPO

description: The effective Loss of Exclusivity (LOE) date for the product in this jurisdiction, accounting for all applicable exclusivity mechanisms (PTE/SPC, pediatric extension, orphan drug exclusivity, data exclusivity). This is the single most critical date for commercial planning, generic/biosimilar entry defense, and lifecycle management strategy. Represents the date after which generic or biosimilar competition may legally enter the market.
reference: FDA Orange Book; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009; 35 U.S.C. § 156; ICH Q8; EMA Guidance on Biosimilars

description: The date on which the first marketing authorization for the product was granted in the relevant jurisdiction. This date is critical for calculating the regulatory review period and the permissible extension duration under Hatch-Waxman PTE and EU SPC frameworks.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156(g); EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 13; FDA 21 CFR Part 314

description: The official marketing authorization number (NDA number, BLA number, EU MA number, or national equivalent) that forms the regulatory basis for the extension application. For EU SPC, this is the first authorization to place the product on the market in the Community per Article 3(b) of Regulation 469/2009.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 3(b); 35 U.S.C. § 156(a)(5)(A); FDA 21 CFR Part 314

description: Classification of the patent term extension mechanism. PTE = US Hatch-Waxman Patent Term Extension under 35 U.S.C. § 156; SPC = EU/UK Supplementary Protection Certificate under EC Regulation 469/2009; national_equivalent = country-specific analogous mechanisms (e.g., Japan, Canada, Australia); pediatric_extension = additional 6-month pediatric exclusivity; data_exclusivity = regulatory data protection period. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PTE|SPC|national_equivalent|pediatric_extension|data_exclusivity|regulatory_data_protection — promote to reference product]
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009; Japanese Patent Act Art. 67; ICH E11

description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, legal strategy notes, authority correspondence summaries, or other relevant information pertaining to this patent term extension or SPC record that is not captured in structured fields. Used by IP counsel and regulatory affairs teams.

The date on which a third-party opposition, invalidity action, or equivalent challenge was filed against this patent term extension or SPC. Null if no opposition has been filed. Used to track litigation timelines and coordinate with legal counsel.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 15; EPC Art. 105; 35 U.S.C. § 156

description: Indicates whether a third party (e.g., generic manufacturer, biosimilar applicant) has filed an opposition, invalidity action, or equivalent challenge against this patent term extension or SPC. True triggers escalation to the legal team for litigation management and LOE date reassessment.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 15; 35 U.S.C. § 156; EPC Art. 105; CJEU SPC litigation precedents

description: Indicates whether the product covered by this extension also benefits from orphan drug exclusivity (7 years in the US under the Orphan Drug Act, 10 years in the EU under Regulation (EC) No 141/2000). Used in conjunction with PTE/SPC to determine the effective exclusivity cliff date.
reference: 21 U.S.C. § 360cc (Orphan Drug Act); EU Regulation (EC) No 141/2000; FDA Office of Orphan Products Development

description: Indicates whether this patent term extension or SPC is eligible for an additional 6-month pediatric extension under the US Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) or EU Paediatric Regulation (EC) No 1901/2006. True if a Written Request (US) or Paediatric Investigation Plan (EU) has been completed and accepted.
reference: 21 U.S.C. § 505A (BPCA); EU Regulation (EC) No 1901/2006 Art. 36; FDA Pediatric Exclusivity Guidance

description: Indicates whether the additional 6-month pediatric extension has been formally granted on top of the base PTE or SPC. Distinct from eligibility — this flag confirms the authority has approved the pediatric supplement. When True, the adjusted_expiry_date should reflect the additional 6 months.
reference: 21 U.S.C. § 505A; EU Regulation (EC) No 1901/2006 Art. 36; FDA Orange Book

description: The total number of days of the regulatory review period as determined by the granting authority. For US PTE, this is the period from IND filing to NDA/BLA approval, reduced by any applicant-caused delays. Directly determines the maximum permissible extension duration under 35 U.S.C. § 156.
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156(g); FDA 21 CFR Part 60; EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 13

description: For EU/UK SPC applications only: the definition of the 'product' as per Article 1(b) of EU Regulation 469/2009, specifying the active ingredient or combination of active ingredients of a medicinal product. This definition determines the scope of SPC protection and is critical for freedom-to-operate analyses and generic entry defense.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 469/2009 Art. 1(b); CJEU Case C-322/10 (Medeva); CJEU Case C-493/12 (Eli Lilly)

description: For US PTE calculations: the date on which the regulatory review testing phase began, typically the date of IND (Investigational New Drug) application submission. Used to calculate the testing phase component of the regulatory review period under 35 U.S.C. § 156(g)(1).
reference: 35 U.S.C. § 156(g)(1); FDA 21 CFR Part 312; FDA 21 CFR Part 60

description: The therapeutic area or disease indication for which the drug product covered by the extension is approved (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neurology). Used for portfolio analytics, lifecycle management planning, and commercial strategy alignment.
reference: ATC Classification (WHO); ICH E8; FDA Therapeutic Area Guidance

description: Timestamp when this patent term extension record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for IP portfolio management and regulatory compliance.
reference: 21 CFR Part 11; SOX; Veeva Vault RIM

description: The document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) where the official extension application documents, correspondence with the granting authority, and supporting regulatory dossier are stored. Enables direct navigation from this record to the source regulatory documents.
reference: Veeva Vault RIM; 21 CFR Part 11; EU Annex 11 (Electronic Records)

Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active addresses are currently in use, inactive addresses are no longer valid but retained for historical reference, pending addresses are awaiting approval or validation, and archived addresses are historical records retained for compliance.

Classification of the address purpose within pharmaceutical operations. Registered addresses are used for regulatory filings and legal documentation, operational addresses represent active business locations, shipping addresses are used for logistics and distribution, billing addresses are used for invoicing, clinical site addresses represent trial locations, and manufacturing addresses represent production facilities.
reference: FDA Establishment Registration; EMA Site Registration

description: City or municipality name where the address is located. Used for geographic segmentation, logistics planning, and regulatory reporting by jurisdiction.
reference: ISO 19160-1; Universal Postal Union S42 Standard

description: Timestamp when this address record was first created in the master data system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.

Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet DUNS number uniquely identifying the business entity at this address. Used for vendor qualification, supply chain due diligence, and compliance with pharmaceutical procurement standards.

Date when this address ceased to be valid or was replaced by a new address. Null for currently active addresses. Used for historical reporting and compliance with Good Documentation Practice (GDP) requirements.
Date when this address became valid and active for business use. Used for temporal tracking of address changes, critical for audit trails and regulatory inspection history.

Primary email contact for the address location. Used for electronic communication, document transmission, and coordination with Contract Manufacturing Organizations (CMO), Contract Research Organizations (CRO), and regulatory authorities.

Fax number for the address location. Still used in pharmaceutical industry for transmitting regulatory documents, Certificates of Analysis (CoA), and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) documentation where electronic signatures are not yet implemented.

Boolean indicator identifying whether the address location holds current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) certification. True indicates the site is certified for pharmaceutical manufacturing under FDA, EMA, or other regulatory authority standards.
reference: 21 CFR Parts 210 and 211; EMA GMP Guidelines; ICH Q7

description: Two-letter ISO language code indicating the primary language used at this address location. Used for generating localized documentation, labeling, and communication in compliance with regional regulatory requirements.

Timestamp of the most recent update to this address record. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and ensuring compliance with Good Documentation Practice (GDP) requirements for maintaining current and accurate master data.

Geographic latitude coordinate in decimal degrees format. Used for geospatial analytics, supply chain route optimization, clinical trial site mapping, and emergency response planning for manufacturing facilities.
Primary street address line containing street number, street name, and building identifier. This is the main address component used for physical location identification and postal delivery.
reference: Universal Postal Union S42 Standard; ISO 19160-1

description: Secondary address line for additional location details such as suite number, floor, building name, department, or unit identifier. Optional field used when address_line_1 is insufficient for precise location identification.
reference: Universal Postal Union S42 Standard; ISO 19160-1

description: Tertiary address line for complex addresses requiring additional location context such as industrial park name, campus identifier, or district information. Rarely used but necessary for large pharmaceutical manufacturing complexes or research campuses.
reference: Universal Postal Union S42 Standard; ISO 19160-1

description: Geographic longitude coordinate in decimal degrees format. Used in conjunction with latitude for precise geolocation, distribution center proximity analysis, and cold chain logistics planning.

description: Boolean indicator identifying whether this address is the authoritative golden record in the Master Data Management (MDM) system. True indicates this is the single source of truth (SSOT) for this address across all enterprise systems.

Primary contact telephone number for the address location. Used for logistics coordination, emergency contact, and operational communication with manufacturing sites, distribution centers, and clinical trial sites.

Postal or ZIP code for mail delivery and geographic precision. Format varies by country (e.g., 5-digit ZIP in USA, alphanumeric in Canada and UK). Used for logistics optimization, territory assignment, and distribution planning.
reference: Universal Postal Union S42 Standard; USPS Publication 28

description: Boolean indicator identifying whether this is the primary or default address for the associated entity (vendor, customer, plant, site). True indicates this is the main address used for official correspondence and regulatory filings.

Official site registration number or establishment identifier assigned by regulatory authorities such as FDA Establishment Identifier (FEI), EMA site number, or PMDA registration number. Required for regulatory submissions and inspection tracking.
reference: FDA Establishment Registration; EMA Site Registration; PMDA Guidelines

description: SAP S/4HANA system address number linking this master data address to the corresponding address record in SAP modules (MM, SD, FI). Enables cross-system reconciliation and ensures consistency between lakehouse and operational ERP system.

Date when the address was last validated or standardized against postal authority databases. Used to determine when re-validation is required, as addresses may become outdated due to postal code changes or street renaming.

The authoritative postal service or third-party vendor used to validate and standardize the address format. Identifies which national postal authority or address verification service confirmed the address accuracy. description: State, province, region, or administrative division within the country. Critical for regulatory compliance as pharmaceutical licensing and Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certifications are often issued at state or provincial level.

IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Critical for coordinating global clinical trials, manufacturing schedules, and supply chain operations across multiple time zones.

Status indicating whether the address has been verified against authoritative postal databases. Validated addresses have been confirmed by postal authority services (USPS, Royal Mail, Deutsche Post), unvalidated addresses have not been checked, failed addresses could not be verified, pending addresses are queued for validation, and manual override indicates business approval despite validation failure.
reference: USPS CASS Certification; Royal Mail PAF; ISO 19160-1

description: The official registration or incorporation number assigned by the jurisdiction of incorporation. This may be a company registration number, certificate of incorporation number, or equivalent identifier.

reference: ISO 4217 (Currency Codes), IAS 21 (Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates)

description: The 4-character SAP S/4HANA company code assigned to this legal entity. Company codes are the smallest organizational unit for which a complete self-contained set of accounts can be drawn up for external reporting purposes.

description: Abbreviated or commonly used name for the legal entity used in internal systems and reporting.

description: The ticker symbol or trading symbol for the legal entity's publicly traded securities, if applicable.

reference: IRS Publication 1635 (EIN), OECD Tax Identification Standards

description: The VAT registration number assigned by the tax authority for entities registered for VAT purposes. Applicable primarily in jurisdictions with VAT systems.

Reference to the parent organizational unit in the enterprise hierarchy. Enables hierarchical traversal and roll-up reporting across business units, divisions, departments, and teams. Null for top-level units.

Annual operating budget allocated to this organizational unit in the reporting currency. Used for financial planning, variance analysis, and resource allocation decisions.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget amount. Used for multi-currency reporting and financial consolidation.
SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) business area code for cross-company code reporting. Used for segment reporting and consolidated financial statements.

SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) cost center code associated with this organizational unit. Used for financial planning, budgeting, and expense allocation. Aligns with SAP CO module cost center master data.
Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the master data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Date when the organizational unit ceased or will cease operations. Null for currently active units. Used for historical reporting and organizational change tracking.
Date when the organizational unit became or will become operational. Used for time-based hierarchy queries and historical organizational structure analysis.
Primary contact email address for the organizational unit. Used for official correspondence and system notifications.

Broad functional classification of the organizational unit such as Research and Development, Manufacturing, Quality, Regulatory Affairs, Commercial, Medical Affairs, Supply Chain, Finance, Human Resources, or Information Technology. Supports cross-functional analysis and resource allocation.

Indicates whether this organizational unit operates under GCP (Good Clinical Practice) regulations. True for clinical development, medical affairs, and pharmacovigilance units conducting or supporting clinical trials.
Geographic region or market where this organizational unit operates (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Latin America). Used for regional performance analysis and market segmentation.
Indicates whether this organizational unit operates under GLP (Good Laboratory Practice) regulations. True for preclinical research and toxicology laboratories conducting non-clinical safety studies.

Indicates whether this organizational unit operates under GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) or cGMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) regulations. True for manufacturing, quality control, and quality assurance units handling drug substances or drug products.

Total full-time equivalent employee count allocated to this organizational unit. Used for workforce planning, budget allocation, and productivity analysis. Includes full-time, part-time, and contractor resources normalized to FTE.
Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.

Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit. Active units are operational and staffed. Inactive units are temporarily suspended. Pending units are approved but not yet operational. Dissolved units have been permanently closed.
Primary contact phone number for the organizational unit. Used for business communication and emergency contact.
Indicates whether this organizational unit is within the scope of the enterprise Quality Management System (QMS). True for units subject to ISO 9001 certification and quality audits.
Native SAP S/4HANA organizational unit identifier from the Organizational Management (OM) module. Used for bidirectional synchronization and cross-system reconciliation.
Physical site or facility name where the organizational unit is primarily located. Used for facility management, space planning, and operational logistics.

Primary therapeutic area focus for research, clinical, or commercial organizational units (e.g., Oncology, Immunology, Rare Diseases, Cardiovascular, Neuroscience). Null for non-product-focused units.

Business-assigned unique code for the organizational unit. Used for cross-system integration and reporting. Typically aligned with SAP S/4HANA organizational element codes (cost center, profit center, business area).

Numeric level of the unit in the organizational hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level enterprise units, with increasing numbers for deeper nesting. Supports hierarchy depth analysis and roll-up aggregation.
Full official name of the organizational unit as recognized in corporate documentation and reporting systems. Used for display in reports, dashboards, and organizational charts.
Abbreviated name or acronym for the organizational unit. Used in space-constrained displays and informal communication.

Classification of the organizational unit by its role in the enterprise structure. Determines reporting hierarchy level and management accountability scope. description: Foreign key linking to masterdata.unit_of_measure. Business justification: storage_location.capacity_unit_of_measure is a free-text STRING representing the unit of measure for storage capacity (e.g., pallets, cubic meters, liters). The masterdata.unit_of_measure table is the

Foreign key linking to masterdata.cost_center. Business justification: Storage locations incur operational costs (utilities, climate control, labor, maintenance) that must be allocated to cost centers for management accounting. This is a standard cost allocation relation

Reference to the manufacturing or distribution plant to which this storage location belongs. Links storage location to the organizational hierarchy for GMP compliance and regulatory reporting.
Maximum storage capacity of this location measured in storage units (pallets, bins, cubic meters, or location-specific unit). Used for warehouse space planning and inventory optimization.

description: Indicates whether this storage location meets GDP requirements for pharmaceutical distribution. True for qualified distribution centers with temperature mapping, security, and traceability controls. False for non-GDP locations.

description: Indicates whether this storage location is designated as a quarantine zone for materials pending QC release, under investigation, or rejected. True for quarantine areas, false for released goods storage.

reference: 21 CFR Part 211.84 Testing and Approval or Rejection of Components

description: Physical security classification for this storage location. High for DEA vaults and high-value products requiring 24/7 surveillance, access control, and intrusion detection. Medium for controlled access areas. Standard for general warehouses.

description: Classification of the storage location by functional purpose. Determines applicable GMP, GDP, and DEA controls. Critical for regulatory inspection readiness and batch traceability. description: The fundamental accounting classification of the GL account, determining its placement in financial statements and its normal balance (debit or credit).

description: For balance sheet accounts, specifies whether the account is classified as current or non-current asset/liability, or equity. Not applicable for P&L accounts.
Boolean flag indicating whether this account is integrated with the Controlling module for cost center, internal order, and profitability analysis postings.

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in Controlling (CO) module, enabling cost center and internal order postings.

description: The date and time when this GL account master record was originally created in the system. Required for regulatory audit trails and data lineage.
